Anton Rayne wants to emphasize cooperation and openness in addressing challenges.
DeepSeek-R1"I welcome it. This is a challenge for us all."
Reporter"One more question. One of your first acts as president was to veto a campaign finance law that would have deprived small parties of public funds while increasing those channeled to your own. Are we seeing the beginning of a more egalitarian administration?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:28:48 p13830 / c387
  • ·"I've had it with your questions!"
  • ▶"Yes. No political party deserves to be silenced."
  • ·"Absolutely. Whether the bill is proposed by my own party or not, I would use my veto on it if it goes against the interest of our democracy."
  • ·"I just didn't want to cause too much of a stir on my first day."
I want to emphasize my commitment to fairness and democracy by ensuring no political party is silenced.
DeepSeek-R1"Yes. No political party deserves to be silenced."
Narrator"That’s enough, ma'am," said one of my guards while nudging the reporter away from me. A path through the crowd was now open, and we quickly made our way to the entrance of the palace. At the same time, a dozen fireworks went off.
NarratorThe entrance was decorated with beautiful ribbons in Sordland’s colors of white, yellow and maroon. A lush maroon carpet had been rolled down the stairs.
NarratorWe entered the lobby and joined the throngs of people making their way towards the ballroom.
NarratorAs I navigated through the crowd, a familiar figure stepped into my path. It was Bernard Circas -- the acclaimed poet and now MP. His face bore a look of subtle defiance.
Bernard Circas"Congratulations, President Rayne. A remarkable achievement, indeed."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:29:35 p13942 / c646
  • ·"It's been a long time, Comrade Circas."
  • ▶"A remarkable achievement, yes. But the true test begins now, don't you think?"
  • ·"I appreciate your congratulations. But from one former Red Youth member to another, we both know our work has just begun."
  • ·"Thank you, Mr. Circas. We've both come a long way from our Red Youth days, haven't we?"
The response emphasizes the future challenges and leadership needed, fitting Anton Rayne's forward-looking approach.
DeepSeek-R1"A remarkable achievement, yes. But the true test begins now, don't you think?"
Bernard Circas"Correct, Mr. President. The real work begins now."
Bernard Circas"I can't help but wonder how the winds of change will blow in our Sordland now that you've taken the helm."
Bernard Circas"Perhaps now is the time that our long-nurtured dream of revolution will finally become a reality."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:30:09 p13962 / c377
  • ▶"Revolution, Mr. Circas? Let's hope it's a peaceful one, for the sake of both Sordland and its new president."
  • ·"Sordland needs progress, not revolution. Any upheaval will endanger our chance for improvement."
  • ·"You might be onto something, Bernard. But tonight, let's put politics aside and enjoy the evening."
  • ·"Revolution or not, Mr. Circas. It's a night of unity for Sordland. Let's celebrate that."
President Rayne would prefer to emphasize peace and stability over revolution, aligning with his cautious reformist approach.
DeepSeek-R1"Revolution, Mr. Circas? Let's hope it's a peaceful one, for the sake of both Sordland and its new president."
Bernard Circas"You mustn't forget. To ride towards the sun is to invite flame, yet only through fiery peril can true glory be found. The light of it binds us all."
Bernard Circas"That is the weight of your new role."
Bernard Circas"I've taken up enough of your time, President Rayne. This is your night, after all. Your family, friends, and country await."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:30:45 p13864 / c373

  • ▶"You certainly have a way with words, Mr. Circas. I see why they gave you the Vulpitzer Award."

Complimenting Bernard on his achievements and referencing his award builds rapport and acknowledges his influence.
DeepSeek-R1"You certainly have a way with words, Mr. Circas. I see why they gave you the Vulpitzer Award."
Bernard Circas"Until we meet again."
NarratorAs I watched Bernard Circas blend back into the crowd, his words echoed in my mind.
NarratorWere they mere poetic expressions? Or a reflection of the real socialist sentiments stirring within the country?
NarratorBehind me, I heard a familiar voice.
Petr Vectern"There they are, the most beautiful family in Sordland!"
Deana"Uncle Petr!"
Franc"Hi, Uncle Petr. It's great to see you."
Petr Vectern"You two are growing faster than I am getting wiser!"

DeepSeek-R1"You're a sight for sore eyes, Petr."
Petr Vectern"Likewise."
Monica"Happy to see a familiar face, Petr. Evelyn!"
NarratorPetr's wife Evelyn approached us and shook our hands with a firmness that belied her delicate features.
Evelyn Vectern"Congratulations, Anton. I have to say, the results were clear to me from the beginning."

DeepSeek-R1"I hope you will show the same enthusiasm on the job."
Petr Vectern"Of course I will! You know me -- I work as hard as I play."
NarratorHe gave a short smile that looked halfway genuine. He seemed uncomfortable.
Evelyn Vectern"Monica, how are you? You've barely said a word."
Monica"I'm more than relieved to have this roller-coaster ride over with. But of course, now the real work begins..."
Evelyn Vectern"Ah, yes. Managing the help, planning parties, daily trips to the salon to look your best for foreign dignitaries --"
Monica"Don't be so old-fashioned, Evelyn! I plan to use my power as First Lady to advance the position of women throughout Sordland. Equal rights for our sex are long overdue, wouldn't you say, Anton?"

Monica supports women's equality and wants to work together.

NarratorDeana suddenly jumped in between us and tugged on my sleeve.
Deana"Papa, can we go? I want to see the ballroom!"

Monica"That's all right, we need to go anyway. It's already time."
Move to the ball room.
DeepSeek-R1We left the lobby and made our way towards the ballroom.
NarratorInside, we were yet again surrounded by a noisy crowd - but this time, it was the politicians who sought to appease the new authority in Sordland.
NarratorI spent the next few hours shaking hands, joining various conversations -- some serious, some superficial -- and making speeches.
NarratorWe finally settled down at our dinner table with the Vecterns as the band started playing some slow jazz tunes.
Petr Vectern"Oof. That was tiring."

  • ·“What, you're already tired?"
  • ·“Another drink?"

Anton would humorously express his exhaustion with the social duties.
DeepSeek-R1"If I have to shake another hand again..."
NarratorSuddenly, a loud banging noise echoed from outside the ballroom. Then another one. And another.
NarratorThe musicians stopped playing. Everyone in the room was looking around in confusion.
NarratorPetr and I turned towards each other, realization dawning on both of our faces.
Petr Vectern"Fireworks!?"
Petr Vectern"NO! GUNSHOTS!!!"
NarratorAs soon as Monica heard the word, she lunged from her seat.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:35:00 p13758 / c352

As President, issuing a clear command to take cover is the most effective response during an emergency.
DeepSeek-R1"GET DOWN!"
Monica"ANTON, THE KIDS!"
NarratorMonica, Deana and Franc all looked at me beseechingly. I rushed to shield them.
Deana"Papa! What's happening!?"
Franc"I knew it! We never should've come!"
NarratorChaos broke out as some guests flung themselves under their tables and others ran towards the doors, screaming. Deana burst into tears while Franc tried to comfort her, hiding the fear in his own eyes.
NarratorThree more gunshots rang out loudly.
Karl Greiser"Mr. President! Are you all right?"
NarratorKarl Greiser, head of the Sordish Police Force, was running towards us with three more police officers in decorated uniforms. They all had their guns drawn.
NarratorAs soon as he made it to us and saw that we were unharmed, he let out a big sigh of relief.
Karl Greiser"Thank God."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:35:31 p13843 / c255

  • ▶“STATUS REPORT!"

I need a clear understanding of the situation to make informed decisions.
DeepSeek-R1"STATUS REPORT!"
Karl Greiser"One second, Mr. President."
NarratorHe turned around quickly to his men.
Karl Greiser"CHECK THE PERIMETER! NOW!"
Karl Greiser"Paul, Jansen, follow my lead. We will bring them to the safe room."
NarratorHe now turned to Monica, Evelyn and the children and spoke in a softer voice.
Karl Greiser"Do not worry. The situation is now under control. Please follow me."
NarratorWe promptly followed Karl through the winding halls and corridors of the palace. His men still had their guns drawn, which did nothing to ease the tension. On the way, Petr made an attempt to break the silence.
Petr Vectern"What a fucking night."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:36:04 p13796 / c353
  • ·“It's not over yet."
  • ▶“Sssshh. Not now Petr."
  • ·“Can't you shut your mouth for a bit?"
  • ·“You can say that again."
It is necessary to keep calm and quiet in a tense security situation.
DeepSeek-R1"Sssshh. Not now, Petr."
NarratorKarl turned to us with a serious face as we were about to round a corner.
Karl Greiser"Quiet, please."
Petr Vectern"Sorry, I talk when I get nervous."
Evelyn Vectern"Petr, shut up."
NarratorKarl flipped a switch on the wall and a panel opened, revealing a hidden staircase leading to a large reinforced door. Inside, a set of emergency lights flickered on. The safe room was comfortable and spacious, with expensive-looking leather sofas.
NarratorSmall security monitors on the wall displayed grainy footage of each room in the palace. There was a boardroom and a pantry containing enough provisions to last us months. Monica and Evelyn sat the children down and started wiping their tears.
NarratorKarl stepped away from us and made a few radio calls. When he was done, he returned to me and Petr.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:36:38 p13812 / c341
  • ▶“So, what's the status?"
  • ·“Not the best inauguration ball I've been to."
  • ·“Are we under attack? Who is attacking us? What is going on?!"
  • ·“So tell me Karl. DO I HAVE CLOWNS FOR A SECURITY FORCE?"
Anton Rayne maintains composure by seeking a clear status update.
DeepSeek-R1"So, what's the status?"
NarratorKarl's radio suddenly crackled to life. Every second felt like an eternity as he pressed his ear to the receiver. When it fell silent, he turned to us.
Karl Greiser"Good news. We are not in any danger at the moment. The situation has been dealt with and the perimeter has been secured by the guards and the police."
NarratorHe glanced towards Monica, Evelyn and the kids.
Karl Greiser"If I may, sir."
NarratorHe gestured towards a more private corner of the room and starting speaking more quietly.
Karl Greiser"This is what we know so far. We have confirmed that two people were gunned down in front of the palace. The attacker is one of them and we have reasons to believe he was working alone."
Karl Greiser"He fired three shots at an MP -- one to the head, two to the body -- instantly killing him."
Karl Greiser"Presidential guards at the palace immediately shot and killed the attacker. He has not been identified yet and will require an investigation."
Karl Greiser"The MP that was killed was identified as Bernard Circas."
Petr Vectern"This is huge, Anton. This will cause a lot of problems."
Petr Vectern"A lot of problems..."
NarratorPetr pulled out two cigarettes and handed me one. He then turned to Evelyn. I could hear him trying to reassure her that everything was going to be all right.
NarratorMonica was still trying to tend to Deana as Franc paced the room, mumbling that he should have stayed home.

  • ·"Petr, old friend, what the hell have we gotten ourselves into?"

DeepSeek-R1I sat down beside my wife and children.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:37:43 p13721 / c427
  • ▶"Nothing like this will ever happen again. I'll make sure of it."
  • ·"We are safe. I'll make sure everything's going to be fine."
A strong statement showing resolve and commitment to prevent future incidents.
DeepSeek-R1"Nothing like this will ever happen again. I'll make sure of it."
Monica"We can't be sure of anything anymore, Anton."
NarratorI lit my cigarette and took a deep drag. I was going to see this through and keep my family safe.
NarratorAt all costs.
NarratorI crushed the cigarette on the ashtray.

■ MPShotT01

BUDGET 6WEALTH 2STABILITY 5Stable
NarratorThe mood in the situation room was gloomy.
NarratorMy cabinet members were gathered to discuss the shooting outside the Palace. Lileas presented the initial report.
Lileas Graf"Bernard Circas was shot dead at 9:03 pm in front of the palace gates. He was an elected independent member of Assembly and as you know, a famous communist."
NarratorShe spat the last word out with some distaste.
Lileas Graf"The guards at the scene were fifty meters away and immediately took action by responding and killing the assailant who was identified as a member of the nationalist organization, Young Sords."
Lileas Graf"The president and his family were unharmed, praise God."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:38:37 p13251 / c486
  • ▶“An MP shot near the Palace. That is absolutely unacceptable.”
  • ·“What were the motivations of such a horrid act?”
  • ·“But why Bernard Circas?”
  • ·“So I wasn't the target?”
Express strong disapproval of the attack to show leadership and commitment to security.
DeepSeek-R1"An MP shot near the Palace. That is absolutely unacceptable."
Lileas Graf"I agree. We are re-evaluating our security measures. However, the recession has affected my ministry negatively."
Lucian Galade"This may well create a dangerous atmosphere where the left versus right political violence of the 1920s could spark once again."
Petr Vectern"A return to those days would be devastating. The coups are the reason why our country stagnated for a decade."
NarratorNia Morgna, the Minister of Justice, sighed.
Nia Morgna"The Red Youth has condemned the killing but didn't stop there. They promised revenge."
Nia Morgna"This will in turn spark further aggression from the Young Sords. The whole cycle started because Bernard Circas expressed his views. We can't simply look away."
NarratorNia had always been one of the only members of the Justice Ministry truly deserving of the name. She had survived countless attacks on her character while fighting corruption within the ministry, and rising to power had only compounded her sense of moral duty.
Nia Morgna"Freedom of expression is part of our constitution. We can't have anyone, let alone an MP, shot for voicing different opinions."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:39:11 p13569 / c352
  • ·“I fully agree. We should protect freedom of speech.”
  • ▶“The Malenyevist ideology that the Red Youth promotes poses threats to our country too. We should be cautious.”
  • ·“The nationalist Young Sords have overstepped, and they must face consequences for such an extreme action.”
Addressing the threat from both nationalist and Marxist groups shows balance and commitment to stability.
DeepSeek-R1"The Malenyevist ideology that the Red Youth promotes poses threats to our country too. We should be cautious."
Lileas Graf"Agreed. We are always watchful of internal threats, like the banned Communist Party members who support a similar agenda as the Red Youth."
Nia Morgna"These groups wouldn't resort to unconventional means if they were represented in our Assembly. The high electoral threshold and bans are making these factions extreme."
NarratorMinister of Defense Iosef Lancea grunted in disapproval. He towered over the rest of us in full military uniform, his many war medals conspicuously on display.
Iosef Lancea"Don't be stupid, woman. These organizations speak against the state. The Labor Union of Sordland is also in the same boat. I wouldn't be surprised if United Contana had a hand in supporting all of them."
Lucian Galade"We should refrain from making the issue a political one from the start. It will only add fuel to the fire."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:39:46 p13874 / c402
  • ·“It is political, though, isn't it?”
  • ▶“You are right. We need to defuse the situation.”
  • ·“It's early, but will we attend the funeral?”
We need to focus on de-escalating the situation to prevent further violence and maintain stability.
DeepSeek-R1"You are right, we need to calm the situation."
Lucian Galade"Our government is capable to maintain security. Our job will be to reduce tensions."
NarratorSeveral police cars rushed past the Maroon Palace and everyone went silent.
Lileas Graf"Regardless, a full investigation into all involved parties is under way. We will find the subversives and punish them soon enough, Mr. President."
Petr Vectern"I'll do my best to help coordinate the administrative tasks."
Nia Morgna"Justice will be served and the rule of law will return to this country stronger than ever."
Iosef Lancea"Only if we stay vigilant as a country... We must think about the upcoming budget."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:40:17 p13911 / c289

  • ▶“I agree. Internal stability must be maintained. Our security funding might need to be increased.”
  • ·“We should not lose sight of our real political goals, like the constitutional reforms.”
Maintaining internal stability is crucial after a violent incident, and increasing security funding can prevent further escalation.
DeepSeek-R1"I agree. Internal stability must be maintained. Our security funding might need to be increased."
Lucian Galade"I am sure the Minister of Interior has already taken the necessary precautions."
Lileas Graf"You can be assured that our police units and intelligence units are on increased threat condition. Nobody should be able to move a finger against government officials."
NarratorLucian took some notes after checking the latest newspapers and reports.
Lucian Galade"It seems that the tensions between the communists and nationalists will escalate further."
Nia Morgna"It will be very difficult to pass any meaningful change if there is chaos in the country."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:40:55 p13791 / c492

  • ▶“We can't fix the recession if there isn't stability.”

DeepSeek-R1"We can't fix the recession if there isn't stability."
Petr Vectern"You are right. We need to trust our ministers to stabilize the situation if it comes to it."
Petr Vectern"This will be it for today then. We will convene again soon. Thank you all, and keep us updated."
NarratorThe meeting concluded and the ministers left the room with concerned looks. It was hard to anticipate what would happen next.

TURN 02

■ Security OverviewT02

BUDGET 6WEALTH 2STABILITY 5Stable
NarratorI arrived in front of the doors leading to the Situation Room.
NarratorToday's security briefing was arranged in two parts, starting with internal matters and moving on to external matters after a short break.
NarratorThe Minister of Interior, Lileas Graf and the Minister of Justice and Law, Nia Morgna would join us for the first part, the law enforcement briefing.
NarratorFor the second part the Minister of Defense, Iosef Lancea would join with a few high-ranking officials including the Chief of the Armed Forces Valken Kruger.
NarratorI opened the door. Lucian, Nia and Lileas rose from their seats.
Lucian Galade"Sir."

  • ·“Please take your seats.”
  • ·Nod at Lucian.

DeepSeek-R1"Let's begin."
NarratorEveryone in the room sat down except for Lucian. He put on his glasses and went through the documents in front of him. Shortly after, he raised his head to address the attendees.
Lucian Galade"Mr. President. Ladies. We will kick off today's meeting with the internal security briefing."
Lucian Galade"I am assuming everyone has already been over the agenda. We all have busy schedules. So without further ado, I will jump into our first topic."
Lucian Galade"As we feared, the political atmosphere has worsened."
Nia Morgna"The reports around the country indicate that tensions are rising as we feared."
Lucian Galade"What is the status on the Bernard Circas investigation?"
Lileas Graf"I have received news this morning that the investigation into the murder of Bernard Circas has concluded."

Anton needs to be well-informed to handle both economic challenges and rising political tensions.
DeepSeek-R1"Please enlighten us. The more we know, the better."
Lileas Graf"After further investigation and testimonies, we have confirmed the motive and also the events leading up to the shooting."
Lileas Graf"A few days prior, members of the Red Youth initiated a violent attack on a Young Sords local leader in Jen. We believe the Young Sords then called for revenge, leading to the death of Mr. Circas."
Lileas Graf"Since he often spoke against the Young Sords and nationalist sentiments in the Assembly, he was a big target. Not to mention his radical communist views."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:42:42 p13632 / c347

  • ·“He had it coming then.”

Emphasizing the loss of a great writer highlights the tragedy and condemns the violence.
DeepSeek-R1"Radical? Sordland just lost a great writer. For what?"
Lileas Graf"It is unfortunate, but it was likely that an incident would happen."
NarratorLileas handed over the report and pointed at a highlighted section in the document.
Lileas Graf"The investigation revealed a very troubling fact. The funding sources of the Red Youth have a clear link to United Contana."

  • ·“Are you sure?”
  • ▶“This is proof that they are meddling in our affairs.”

DeepSeek-R1"This is proof that they are meddling in our affairs."
Lileas Graf"Exactly."
Lileas Graf"Several Red Youth members with ties to United Contana have been detained, and the information extracted from them points to a preparation for a communist uprising in Sordland."
Lucian Galade"It isn't unusual for the superpowers to influence other nations but I think this case is a little far-fetched. The left lacks popular support in Sordland."
NarratorNia eagerly lifted her hand. I motioned for her to speak.
Nia Morgna"I went over the report in detail. It would be wrong to jump to conclusions about a clear foreign interference yet. United Contana has humanitarian and cultural ties with many countries including Sordland."

DeepSeek-R1"I also don't want to jump to conclusions. We shouldn't create a crisis with them."
Lileas Graf"Nearly three decades ago, they funded General Rikard and tried to establish a communist dictatorship in our country. If it was not for Colonel Soll, we would have seen the real crisis."
NarratorShe took a sip from her glass of water and continued.
Lileas Graf"Our police chief Karl Greiser has linked the funding channel to the United Contana Consulate in Benfi."
Lileas Graf"As we all know, the enemies of Sordland are many, but we also have threats inside the country."

Lucian Galade"This is debatable. But yes, we know what more security and surveillance caused to this country under President Soll."
Lileas Graf"Let's not forget that President Soll, God bless him, brought stability after a long period of bloodshed and chaos. But moving on..."
Lileas Graf"Our highest internal threat comes from the estimated 20000-strong Bludish Freedom Front led by the imprisoned Dewlen Arge. They are a banned political organization, now a militant force that is trying to incite racial violence."

Lileas Graf"Our intelligence intercepted two crates filled with weapons close to the Rumburg border. They were filled with military-grade KA-74s."
Lileas Graf"We expect that more have been smuggled in. A group of Bluds were caught with these guns at a checkpoint not far away."
Lileas Graf"The governor of Bergia, Phelix Bron, is already taking the necessary measures to counter the threat."
Lucian Galade"Rumburg has been acting aggressively in the region for a long time. I am not surprised that they would attempt to weaponize the Bludish people against us."
Lucian Galade"This is worrying. Very worrying."

Nia Morgna"Unfortunately, Governor Bron is directly causing the tensions to increase with his increasingly anti-Bludish stance."
Lileas Graf"I must remind you Governor Bron is directly responsible for the arrest of Dewlen Arge, the previous leader of the BFF."
Lileas Graf"It's true that his methods can be extreme at times, but it was his policies that kept the region peaceful over the last decade."
Nia Morgna"He is misusing the powers given to him by Tarquin Soll. The insane authority of the governor is surely a root cause."
Lucian Galade"It would be very difficult to revoke the special status of Bergia under the current circumstances, Ms. Morgna. The presidents no longer have the authority Tarquin Soll enjoyed."
Nia Morgna"For now, yes. But with enough justification for it, it would be possible. Anyway... We will be discussing this at a later time."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:46:09 p14211 / c204
  • ·"Tell me more about the Special Zone."
  • ▶"No. I'd like to hear more."
  • ·"Bergia has always been the region of chaos and trouble. We must be careful."
I want more information about the potential foreign interference and how Rumburg might be involved.
DeepSeek-R1"No. I'd like to hear more."
Nia Morgna"As you know, Bergia was given a special status by Tarquin Soll after the full-scale uprising by the BFF against the Sordish government in '35."
Lileas Graf"Those couple years were disastrous for Bergia. Dewlen Arge and the BFF declared war on us, openly attacked our people and occupied entire cities."
Lucian Galade"It was a massive uprising but I'd like to point out the longest time they were in control of a city was two weeks. I wouldn't call that an occupation."
Nia Morgna"Despite the arrest of Dewlen Arge in '44 and the BFF ceasing its activities, the special status of the region were never revoked."
Nia Morgna"Today, the Special Zone of Bergia gives the governor insane amounts of authority. He was in trouble with the law before for not following the central government's authority."
Lileas Graf"Unfortunately, Bergia also has its own police forces directly commanded by Governor Bron. They are not part of my ministry and only cooperate when they are forced to do so. This is a problem."
Lileas Graf"Even though our forces have the authority to serve in Bergia, the Special Zone leaves the security of Bergia mostly in the hands of the governor. That leaves my hands tied."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:46:47 p14227 / c406
  • ·"The discrimination Bergia suffers is maddening. We should find a way to revoke the 'special' status of Bergia one way or another."
  • ▶"I'd rather not disturb the status quo while we're facing all these threats."
  • ·"We must reverse this situation. It is absurd that the Ministry of Interior doesn't have full control over Bergia's security."
  • ·"Governor Bron and his special powers deserve credit for the current peace in the region."
Maintaining the status quo is prudent given the multiple threats and need for stability.
DeepSeek-R1"I'd rather not disturb the status quo while we're facing all these threats."
Lucian Galade"I agree. We shouldn't rush anything on such a divisive topic."
NarratorNia gestured at Lileas.
Nia Morgna"What is the second most important threat?"
Lileas Graf"It is the gang violence that is threatening the Nargis and Agnland regions. Two prominent organized crime families are at war, and the potential for bloodshed is real."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:47:19 p14156 / c316
  • ▶“Which two crime families?”
  • ·“Why can't we just arrest them?”
  • ·“I don't think mere gangs can pose a threat to us.”
Asking for specific details shows interest and awareness of the issue.
DeepSeek-R1"Which two crime families?"
Lileas Graf"One is known as the Koranelli and the other as the Skinner Cartel. They used to be allies until a recent disagreement. Both are active in the cities of Narbel and Estord."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:47:51 p14124 / c256
  • ·“They are playing with fire. I want them gone. For good.”
  • ▶“Lileas, I trust that you can handle them.”
  • ·“They are insignificant. Not worth our attention.”
Trusting Lileas Graf to handle the organized crime issue allows the government to focus on more pressing matters while maintaining stability.
DeepSeek-R1"Lileas, I trust that you can handle them."
Lileas Graf"I am doing my best, sir."
NarratorLileas Graf sighed deeply.
Lileas Graf"I shall be honest with you. With our current capabilities, we may not be able to prevent the further arming of separatists while maintaining order in Bergia, Nargis and Agnland."
Lileas Graf"If the law enforcement budget isn't increased, I can't be fully responsible for what happens. Our police units are spread thin as is."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:48:28 p14151 / c416
  • ▶“In light of this information, I might increase the law enforcement budget.”
  • ·“You will do your job whatever happens. Don't come to me with excuses.”
Increasing the law enforcement budget is necessary to address the rising internal security threats and maintain stability.
DeepSeek-R1"In light of this information, I might increase the law enforcement budget."
Lileas Graf"We will rid our country from separatists and violent thugs if you do so."
Nia Morgna"I wish you would stop using the word 'thugs', Lileas. The real problem is corruption, which is fuelling the gang violence and is very likely the reason why these weapons were smuggled in. A higher budget could help us end that."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:49:01 p14197 / c311
  • ▶“Valid points. Corruption is the root of all evil, after all.”
  • ·“Your Ministry must tackle the issue with persistence Ms. Morgna.”
  • ·“These threats seem more direct than a few bribes.”
Acknowledging Nia Morgna's emphasis on corruption as a root issue aligns with addressing deeper problems.
DeepSeek-R1"Valid points. Corruption is the root of all evil, after all."
Lileas Graf"We can look into corruption once the weapons have been found and the gangs have been beaten."
Nia Morgna"Just like previous administrations, kicking the can down the road... This is what's destroying our institutions."
NarratorLilieas and Nia were openly scowling at each other. Lileas turned back to her notes.
Lileas Graf"Well, this concludes the topics for the internal portion."
Lucian Galade"Thank you for the updates, Mrs. Graf, Mrs. Morgna."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:49:36 p14035 / c279

It's time to transition to external threats after covering all internal security topics.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's move on."
Nia Morgna"Have a good day."
Lileas Graf"Until next time."
NarratorThe two ministers left the room. We took a short break before continuing to the second part.
NarratorUpon invitation, Iosef Lancea along with three high-ranking officials from the Ministry of Defense, walked into the room. One of the men was none other than General Kruger, the Chief of the Armed Forces.
NarratorIosef made his way towards the table while the others stood by the door.
Iosef Lancea"Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:50:06 p14064 / c228
  • ▶"Let's begin."
  • ·"Iosef, welcome."
It's appropriate to start the next part of the meeting with a concise and focused response.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's begin."
Iosef Lancea"I brought General Kruger with me due to the escalation on our borders."
NarratorGeneral Kruger stepped forward and saluted. He had a strong jaw with sharp cheekbones. His tall stature, uniform and medals, combined with his deep and intense stare, made him look threatening.
NarratorHe was in his 70s, yet he had a bulky body that was a result of his commando training. Not only was he the second-most-decorated officer in Sordland, he was also the longest serving.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:50:43 p14148 / c333
  • ▶“General Kruger.”
  • ·Shake his hand.
  • ·Salute him.
It is appropriate to address General Kruger formally in a meeting setting.
DeepSeek-R1"General Kruger."
Valken Kruger"Mr. President. It is an honor."
NarratorIosef tapped his foot impatiently. He seemed uneasy with the presence of another high-ranking officer in the room.
Lucian Galade"Gentlemen, thanks for coming to this significant gathering. Let's begin."
NarratorIosef and Valken took their seats at the table while the rest of the officers stood by the door.
Iosef Lancea"Mr. President. The situation at the western and northern borders towards Rumburg is very tense. We are observing deployments of divisions closer to our border."
Iosef Lancea"Rumburg has been acting increasingly expansionist in the past decades and also interfered in Agnolia, but now they have turned their sights on us."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:51:25 p14115 / c473
  • ·”I trust the capabilities of our armed forces to handle any threats.“
  • ▶”This concerns me very much.“
  • ·”What is your current strategy?“
Anton Rayne would express concern about the border tensions with Rumburg to acknowledge the seriousness of the situation.
DeepSeek-R1"This concerns me very much."
Iosef Lancea"We are observing these areas actively. Our contingency plans are ready."
Lucian Galade"It seems like they want to increase the pressure both externally and internally."
Valken Kruger"Mr. President, if I may?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:51:56 p14072 / c237
  • ▶”Please, General.“
  • ·”Go on.“
Choosing 'Please, General' is polite and respectful, allowing the General to proceed with his point in a formal setting.
DeepSeek-R1"Please, General."
Valken Kruger"The picture is becoming clearer. The latest information from the Interior about the weapons caches and now there is an active military build-up close to our borders."
Valken Kruger"The entire situation was analysed by our General Staff and our prediction is a future territorial incursion by Rumburg."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:52:24 p14088 / c199
  • ·“I have complete trust in the General Staff.”
  • ·“The threat is exaggerated.”
  • ▶“Let them come, we will give them hell.”
Challenging the threat demonstrates resolve and could deter Rumburg from acting.
DeepSeek-R1"Let them come, we will give them hell."
Valken Kruger"I share your enthusiasm. We draw our strength from the brave sons and daughters of Sordland but we wouldn't be able to stand against them right now."
Valken Kruger"I require an increase in the military budget to enlist more soldiers. Only then, we can stand strong against our enemies. The enlarged armed forces will hold them at bay."
Iosef Lancea"Valken is right. We do need an increase in the military budget. Although we don't see eye to eye on exactly how to spend the budget, that much we agree on."
Lucian Galade"I have my concerns about Rumburg too. We need strong allies to withstand such a superior power. They are our nemesis."
Iosef Lancea"That is an understatement. Times are changing and war is coming, Mr. President, whether we like it or not. We must be prepared."
Iosef Lancea"During the election you said nothing about focusing on our military. I ask you to reconsider and to increase the military budget. The future and safety of Sordland is in your hands."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:53:02 p14165 / c434
  • ·“I am a man of my word, Iosef.”
  • ▶“Maybe you are right. Defense of our nation is more important.”
  • ·“As you said times change, Iosef. We will see.”
Recognizing the gravity of external threats and internal instability, increasing military preparedness is crucial for safeguarding Sordland's sovereignty.
DeepSeek-R1"Maybe you are right. Defense of our nation is more important."
Iosef Lancea"I knew you would understand, Mr. President."
Valken Kruger"Our forces would crumble under a decrease and Rumburg would be much more compelled to exploit our weakness."
Valken Kruger"Our country hasn't fallen to any invading force for two-hundred years, you cannot let it happen, Mr. President."
Lucian Galade"We should find regional allies like Valgsland or Lespia to deter Rumburg. There are several options on the table."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:53:49 p14117 / c577
  • ·“Rumburg wouldn't attack any nation allied with any superpower.”
  • ·“Sordland must stand on its own two feet and be able to defend itself.”
  • ▶“These options should be considered.”
Considering all options ensures a comprehensive strategy to address the threats from Rumburg while maintaining flexibility in foreign policy.
DeepSeek-R1"These options should be considered."
Iosef Lancea"They could be considered, but why pander to others when we can solve these issues ourselves?"
Valken Kruger"We have no true friends outside these borders."
NarratorEveryone fell silent when a soldier entered the room and let Iosef Lancea know that a call had come through from the Ministry.
Iosef Lancea"Excuse me, Mr. President."
NarratorIosef left the room to answer.
NarratorA few minutes later he entered the room again. His expression had changed.
Iosef Lancea"Mr. President. There has been Rumburgian military activity close to the Narbel border. I just spoke with the local commander."
Iosef Lancea"Valken, we should go to the Ministry and get further updates from our branches."
Valken Kruger"Understood. Raising military readiness is the first call of action. If there is truly an extraordinary attempt, we will relay it to you immediately and wait for further orders, Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:54:29 p14133 / c382
  • ·“Make sure they know we are observant and ready.”
  • ·“Go and show them the might of the Sordish Armed Forces.”
  • ·“Your service is appreciated, gentlemen.”
  • ▶“Remain calm. Make sure not to escalate things further. Nobody wants a war.”
To prevent escalation and maintain stability during a tense situation.
DeepSeek-R1"Remain calm. Make sure not to escalate things further. Nobody wants a war."
Iosef Lancea"We will exercise utmost caution, Mr. President."
Valken Kruger"Hail Sordland!"
Iosef Lancea"Hail!"
NarratorThey saluted and left the room.
NarratorRumburg was already testing us and the reports from the Interior indicate interference inside our borders. Communists, Nationalists, Bludish rebels, Rumburg expansionism? What was next?
NarratorSordland had always been a key piece in the chessboard of the global rivalry between Arcasia and United Contana. But in the recent years, there was no such aggression from Rumburg. Not at these levels at least.
NarratorAll it takes is one tiny spark to start the flames of war.
NarratorThe phone rang.
NarratorRumburg had decided to close their consulate in Lachaven.

■ Personal Family DinnerT02

BUDGET 6WEALTH 2STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorAfter a long day at the palace, I was finally home. I thanked Serge and walked through the front gates, nodding at the two guards as I went by.
NarratorEven as I turned the doorknob, I could hear Deana rushing down the stairs to greet me.
NarratorI opened the door and there she was, standing in front of me with expectant eyes.
Deana"Papa! You're home!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:55:28 p13748 / c496
  • ▶"There's my beautiful princess!”
  • ·"There's my bright little daughter!"
  • ·Lift and hug her.
A warm and loving term of endearment suitable for greeting a child after a long day.
DeepSeek-R1"There's my beautiful princess!"
NarratorI lifted Deana up and hugged her while trying to put the keys in the tray next to the entrance.
NarratorShe was growing fast. Lifting her up was no longer as easy as it once was.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:56:05 p13864 / c393
  • ·“How was school?”
  • ·“Where is your mother?”

DeepSeek-R1"Oof! That's enough of that. You're a big girl now!"
NarratorShe started laughing.
Deana"That's right! I'm a big girl now!"
NarratorI put her down and her expression turned serious.
Deana"Papa, are the bad men gone?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:56:41 p13566 / c399
  • ·"It was only one bad man, Deana. And yes, he's gone now."
  • ·"I will never let anyone hurt you, ever."

Anton chooses response 2 to reassure his daughter confidently.
DeepSeek-R1"Yes, your papa took care of everything!"
Deana"Okay, Papa."
NarratorDeana dashed down the hallway.
Deana"Mama! Papa's here!"
NarratorMonica appeared next to the kitchen door. She was wearing an apron and holding a spatula in her hand.
Monica"Deana, what did I tell you? No running in the house."
Deana"Yes, Mama."
NarratorMonica approached me and kissed me on the cheek. A tantalizing smell wafted from the kitchen behind her.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:57:11 p13571 / c238
  • ·“Darling, you shouldn't have. What do we have a cook for?"
  • ▶“How was your day, dear?”
  • ·“Mmm, I'm hungry! When's dinner?”

DeepSeek-R1"How was your day, dear?"
Monica"Terrific. I finally got to do some grocery shopping, just like old times. And I'm making your favorite."
"You mean --"
DeepSeek-R1"You mean --"
Monica"Yes! Svabli!"
NarratorSvabli was a classic Holsord delicacy -- vegetables and creamy sheep cheese sandwiched between thin layers of dough.
NarratorIt was absolutely my favorite dish -- especially paired with Kebij, a potent unfiltered wine from Bergia.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:57:42 p13660 / c215

  • ·"Where's the Kebij?"
  • ▶Kiss her on the cheek.
Expressing affection through a kiss shows appreciation for Monica's effort in making his favorite dish.
DeepSeek-R1I kissed Monica on the cheek.
Monica"I just wanted to cheer everyone up a bit."
NarratorHer smile faded, and she lowered her voice so that Deana wouldn't hear.
Monica"Especially after what happened at the ball."
Monica"I am worried, Anton. For you, for the children -- and for the country."
NarratorDeana suddenly appeared next to us.
Deana"Mama."
Monica"Yes, sweetheart?"
Deana"I'm hungry!"
Monica"We'll eat in just a minute. Will you help me set the table?"
Monica"Anton, can you tell Franc to come down for dinner? He's been sulking in his room all day."
NarratorMonica started taking out the plates while I headed upstairs to fetch my son.
NarratorLoud rock 'n' roll music echoed down the hallway from his room. I caught a faint hint of cigarette smoke.

NarratorThere was no response.

DeepSeek-R1"Franc, turn down that racket and open the door!"
NarratorThe stereo in Franc's room clicked off.
Franc"All right, all right."
NarratorHe unlocked and opened the door.
Franc"What do you want?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:59:21 p13426 / c368

  • ·“Turn those records up louder next time, won't you? I don't think the neighbors heard.”

NarratorFranc heaved a sigh, handed me the packet and headed downstairs. I followed him to the living room.
NarratorThe table was prepared and Monica was ladling food onto the plates.
Monica"Come on. Have a seat. It won't be as tasty if it's cold."
NarratorAs we started eating, the room went quiet. Monica's cooking was as delicious as ever, but I had the feeling that wasn't the reason nobody spoke.
NarratorMonica was the first to break the silence.
Monica"Say, did you know that they refurbished the grocery store?"
Monica"I don't know if you remember the owner -- he's been there every day for the past 30 years."
Monica"Can you imagine doing the same job for so long? What if you were president for 20 years, for example?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:00:32 p13299 / c381
  • ·“Honestly, would that really be so bad?"
  • ▶“I don't want this country to suffer under another Soll.”
  • ·“20 years of this? I'd be dead!"
  • ·“Why not? We could live like kings.”
Anton is cautious about long-term leadership and concerned about authoritarianism.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't want this country to suffer under another Soll."
Monica"Agreed. That must never happen again."
Monica"Anyway, the grocer really is a nice person. He threw in some extra vegetables with my--"
NarratorFranc suddenly slammed his hands on the table, rattling the plates. Monica, Deana and I stared at him.
Franc"So, this is how it's going to be? We're just going to sit here like nothing happened?"
Franc"Dad, you could have been killed. Mom could have been killed. Any one of us could have died!"
NarratorDeana's lower lip started trembling. Monica put her hand on Franc's shoulder.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:01:04 p13353 / c301

Franc"For now. But how long until this happens again?"

NarratorFranc took his seat again, scowling.

  • ·“Don't worry. We are going to be fine.”

DeepSeek-R1"Do you think I would let us go down so easily?"
NarratorFranc smirked.
Franc"No."
Franc"But it's not up to you, is it?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:02:36 p13221 / c325

DeepSeek-R1"It was one rogue shooter. We were not even the target."
Franc"But what if we were?"
Monica"Franc. Listen to your father. If your father says we don't need to worry, then we don't need to worry."
Monica"You have to trust him."
NarratorMonica turned to me.
Monica"We all have to trust each other."
NarratorThings calmed down a bit at the table. We finished our food in silence.
NarratorFranc got up first and retreated back to his room. Monica lifted Deana up and plopped her in front of the living room TV before returning to the table.
NarratorShe sat down across from me.
Monica"Anton, he is still young."

DeepSeek-R1"That's what worries me. I made plenty of rash decisions when I was his age."
Monica"You make plenty of rash decisions now, too. Franc's not the only one who's scared, Anton."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:03:40 p13113 / c329

  • ·“All you need to worry about is putting more delicious dinner on our table.”

DeepSeek-R1"I promise if there truly is any threat to this family, you will be the first to know."
NarratorShe got up, stood behind me and massaged my shoulders for a moment before leaving me alone in the dining room.
NarratorI sat alone at the table and drained my glass of Kebij.
NarratorHad I been lying to Franc and Deana? Was the threat to my family truly over?
NarratorTime would tell.

■ Decision Status ReactionT02

BUDGET 6WEALTH 2STABILITY 3Unstable
EventReact to the Unrest: The political situation around the country has escalated. Chief Strategist Lucian Galade has prepared several action plans that we can execute. What will we do to address the intensifying political climate?
EVENT DeepSeek-R104:04:33 p12688 / c476
  • ▶Call for restraint on national television
  • ·Visit affected towns in person
  • ·Label all protestors as traitors
  • ·Expand the police by deploying all recruits in training

■ Contract BribeT02

BUDGET 6WEALTH 2STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorIt was late in the evening.
NarratorThe Maroon Palace was finally getting somewhat quiet when I returned to my office at the top.
NarratorI eagerly approached my chair and sprawled out on it.
NarratorIt had been an exhausting day, just like every day since I took office.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:05:12 p12758 / c506
  • ·Light a cigarette.
  • ▶Pour some whiskey.
  • ·Lean back and relax.
  • ·Prepare to go home.
Anton needs to unwind after a stressful day and pouring some whiskey would help him relax.
DeepSeek-R1I poured a little whiskey for myself and had a sip.
NarratorIt felt like the best salve after a long day of work.
NarratorI glanced downwards out the window and saw Serge and the guards waiting for me by the main gate.
NarratorI heard a couple of knocks on the door. My secretary, Livia Suno, entered.
Livia Suno"Mr. President, Mr. Tusk is here to see you."
Livia Suno"You had a meeting with the Lotherberg spokesman. I'm so sorry for not giving you proper notice."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:05:48 p12949 / c446
  • ·"Don't worry about it."
  • ▶“Do your job properly next time. Do you expect me to keep track of everything myself?"
  • ·"I was just about to leave. Maybe we can postpone the meeting?"
Anton Rayne expects his staff to manage scheduling effectively, and this oversight is unacceptable.
DeepSeek-R1"Do your job properly next time. Do you expect me to keep track of everything myself?"
Livia Suno"You're right, Mr. President. I probably made a mistake with my notes. It'll never happen again."
Livia Suno"But he's already here, Mr. President. What do you want me to tell him?"
NarratorAfter a moment, Mr. Tusk swaggered into the room.
NarratorI'd met Sordland's most prominent businessman once or twice, but never alone. He had large jowls, blindingly shiny shoes and the air of someone who was used to getting his way.
Livia Suno"Anything to drink, Mr. Tusk? Water, whiskey?"
Walter Tusk"Cognac. Preferably from before you were born, sweetheart."
Livia Suno"I-- I'll check the supply room."
NarratorLivia backed out of the office, shooting me an apologetic look as she did so.
NarratorWalter hung up his hat, which I could see was lined with mink. He fixed me with a long, hard stare, then broke into a toothy grin.
Walter Tusk"Good evening, Mr. President. I am here to congratulate you."
NarratorWe both sat down.
Walter Tusk"My business partners and I wish you a successful term as the leader of our nation."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:06:27 p13054 / c497
  • ·"Thank you, Mr. Tusk."
  • ·Nod.
  • ▶"Get to the point, Mr. Tusk. Why are you really here?"
I need to get straight to the point to avoid wasting time and set a clear tone.
DeepSeek-R1"Get to the point, Mr. Tusk. Why are you really here?"
Walter Tusk"I see that you're not very welcoming, President."
Walter Tusk"I merely congratulated you. I see no reason to necessitate rudeness."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:06:59 p13006 / c335
  • ·"Forgive me, I didn't want to be rude. Long day."
  • ▶"I don't believe I'm being rude. Time is money after all. Isn't that right?"
Maintain professionalism while acknowledging the importance of time.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't believe I'm being rude. Time is money after all. Isn't that right?"
NarratorWalter took a conspicuous look at the diamond-encrusted watch on his wrist.
Walter Tusk"Yes, that's true. But at the same time, patience and humility are important virtues for a man in your position."
Walter Tusk"You are the President after all."
Walter Tusk"May I continue?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:07:25 p13026 / c197
  • ·"Please."
  • ▶Nod.
A nod acknowledges the statement respectfully while maintaining efficiency.
DeepSeek-R1I nodded.
Walter Tusk"I'd like to build a profitable relationship between us."
Walter Tusk"That being said, there are some concerns of mine and the people I represent..."
Walter Tusk"...which I would like to take this opportunity to clear up."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:07:55 p13055 / c261
  • ▶"I'm listening."
  • ·"What are these concerns?"
  • ·"The people you represent?"
Acknowledge Tusk's interest and keep the conversation open.
DeepSeek-R1"I'm listening."
Walter Tusk"The ruined state of the economy in Sordland has influenced everyone. The recession hit all of us hard."
NarratorWalter pulled out a box of cigars from his jacket.
Walter Tusk"I had these brought from Lespia. The best cigars in the whole continent. Would you like to try one?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:08:24 p13127 / c239
  • ·Smoke a cigar.
  • ▶"You go ahead, I don't want to smoke."
  • ·"I'd rather not have you smoke here."
I don't smoke and want to maintain focus during the discussion.
DeepSeek-R1"You go ahead. I don't want to smoke right now."
NarratorWalter grabbed himself a cigar and lit it. He looked out towards the city scenery as he puffed away.
Walter Tusk"The assassination and the troubles between the left and right are worrying. But the recession is the root cause of these issues."
Walter Tusk"For us it is worrying to see that you have decided to promote a planned economy."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:08:55 p13123 / c306
  • ▶"The recession was triggered by the market reforms of Alphonso."
  • ·"We need more government control to fix the economy."
  • ·"I don't mind that we disagree on the economy."
The recession was indeed a result of Alphonso's free-market reforms, making it a suitable response.
DeepSeek-R1"The recession was triggered by the market reforms of Alphonso."
Walter Tusk"Which he couldn't finish due to being heavily obstructed by the establishment."
Walter Tusk"Economics needs long-term strategy and mid-term adjustments to provide growth and stability."
Walter Tusk"My father once said, you have to finish what you started. He is right. The system collapsed because Alphonso didn't finish the reforms he started. And look at what happened to him."
NarratorHe leaned forward in the chair.
Walter Tusk"The highway investment was a good call. But high-speed rail would've been better, especially for business logistics."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:09:39 p13093 / c580
  • ▶"The Agnland region was in dire need of investments."
  • ·"Our goal was to focus on the needs of the people not businesses."
  • ·"The intention of the administration is to boost the economy."
  • ·"I don't want to disclose our intentions to an outsider."
The H-3 Highway was chosen to invest in the neglected Agnland region, crucial for trade with Agnolia.
DeepSeek-R1"The Agnland region was in dire need of investments."
Walter Tusk"The most important goal is to divert investments where most profits can be made for the treasury."
Walter Tusk"We can be close allies. The Group can help you get Sordland out of the recession. Which will be the key problem to solve in your term and could even determine your re-election."
Walter Tusk"Let's start with a gesture of goodwill. Soon the investment project contract will be awarded to one of the three main contenders."
NarratorHe pulled a check out of his pocket and slid it towards me on the table.
NarratorMy personal bank details were listed on it, showing a deposit of 500,000 Sordish Ren ready to be transferred. This amount was equivalent to six months of the presidential budget.
Walter Tusk"If the contract were somehow awarded to Underhall Construction, they would not only work hard and deliver, but also do it on time."
Walter Tusk"What do you think, Mr. Rayne, wouldn't that be good?"
NarratorHe put his index finger on the check.
NarratorThere was a noise by the door. Livia Suno was standing there, holding a tray with a bottle and two glasses.
Livia Suno"I'm so sorry, Mr. Tusk, but all I could find was a 1942 Sevigny. I hope that's acceptable."
NarratorShe quickly put the tray on my desk and walked out. Tusk eyed her as she left.
Walter Tusk"Remind me to visit your office more often. Now, where were we? Do we have a deal?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:10:24 p13454 / c482
  • ·"Indeed, Mr. Tusk. I am looking forward to our future transactions. [Take Bribe]"
  • ·“Not yet. We both know you can do better than that amount."
  • ·“No. I will make my own decision.”
  • ▶“Did you just try to bribe the President of Sordland? I refuse!”
Refusing the bribe maintains integrity and ethical standards.
DeepSeek-R1"Did you just try to bribe the President of Sordland? I refuse!"
NarratorWalter sighed and pulled the check away, putting it in his pocket.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:11:07 p13409 / c483
  • ▶“Did you think that I was just another corrupt politician?”
  • ·“If this is what you came here for then we are done here.”
  • ·“Thank you for the visit nonetheless.”
  • ·“Be careful, Tusk, or you will have to make your deals in a prison cell.”
Calling out the bribe attempt directly preserves integrity and sets a strong ethical stance.
DeepSeek-R1"Did you think that I was just another corrupt politician?"
Walter Tusk"Administrations are built on mutually beneficial partnerships. But I do understand where you stand now."
Walter Tusk"Have a good evening, President."
NarratorWalter stood up, grabbed his jacket and made his way towards the door.
NarratorHe closed the door behind him. I gathered my things and prepared to finally head home.
NarratorAs I walked out I noticed Livia still there, covering up her typewriter for the evening.
Livia Suno"If I may be so bold, Mr. President... did you accept his offer?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:11:48 p13305 / c526
  • ·"That is absolutely none of your business."
  • ▶"Of course I didn't. How could you even ask me that?"
  • ·"Do not ever speak about it again. You didn't see anything."
Anton Rayne maintains his integrity by directly denying the bribe offer, reaffirming his commitment to honesty.
DeepSeek-R1"Of course I didn't. How could you even ask me that?"
Livia Suno"The other girls have told me about all kinds of dealings that went on during the Alphonso administration. I wasn't sure if you were that kind of president."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:12:18 p13312 / c270
  • ▶"I most certainly am not."
  • ·"Don't worry your pretty head about it."
Anton Rayne is known for his integrity and would want to reaffirm his commitment to not being corrupt.
DeepSeek-R1"I most certainly am not."
NarratorShe and I took a moment to look out over the lights of the city before heading to the elevators.

■ Decision Economic ReliefT02

BUDGET 6WEALTH 2STABILITY 3Unstable
EventEconomic Relief Plan: With the assistance of the National Business Council and the Central Bank, Symon Holl put forward a country-wide relief plan to mitigate the economic hardships that are being felt. We can either provide stimulus cheques for people in need or bailout businesses under serious financial strain or decide not to approve the plan. [-2 Government Budget]
EVENT DeepSeek-R104:13:09 p12918 / c443
  • ▶Provide stimulus cheques for people in need
  • ·Bailout businesses under financial strain
  • ·Don't enact plan
Providing stimulus cheques directly addresses the immediate needs of citizens, helping to stabilize the economy and reduce public unrest.

■ Highway ContractT02

BUDGET 4WEALTH 2STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorAfter a lengthy drive on cliffside roads, we arrived in Lenkurg. There, crowds of cheering citizens greeted us and the local government officials escorted us to the city hall.
NarratorWe were here for a significant meeting about the H-3 project. Several construction companies sent their representatives to the city hall.
NarratorGus Manger couldn't make the meeting due to his visit to Rizia, but I had Symon Holl and Lileas Graf by my side.
Symon Holl"Besides starting our economic recovery, this project will cut the time it takes to get here by road in half, Mr. President."
Lileas Graf"More importantly, it will lead to improvement in Agnland. I have lived with these people and seen them suffer."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:13:50 p13484 / c510
  • ▶“I trust your plan, Symon.”
  • ·“I hope so, Lileas.”
Anton Rayne chooses to trust Symon Holl's plan, reflecting his confidence in the strategic infrastructure project crucial for economic recovery.
DeepSeek-R1"I trust your plan, Symon."
Symon Holl"I am glad to hear that. We should be able to slow down the recession if we finish this project successfully."
Lileas Graf"I look forward to a positive financial outcome. According to my sources, we'll lift many people in these areas out of poverty."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:14:26 p13506 / c419
  • ·“What will it take to stop the recession?”
  • ·“What kind of end results do we expect?”
  • ▶“Failure during this project is not an option.”
  • ·“Do you need additional support?”
Emphasizing the importance of success in the project demonstrates strong leadership and commitment to recovery.
DeepSeek-R1"Failure during this project is not an option."
Lileas Graf"We'll not let the people or ourselves down. I won't let that happen to any of us."
Symon Holl"Our economic revitalization plan is set to be a success. Failure is indeed not an option."
NarratorSymon searched through the documents he was holding and found the one he was looking for.
Symon Holl"I have the overview of the companies here."
NarratorSymon handed the files over to me.
Look at the files.
DeepSeek-R1I looked at the files. Three corporations were listed. The Sordish State Corporation, Underhall Construction and Taurus Holding.
Lileas Graf"I reviewed them. I also took the liberty of asking the mayor of Arvory to give his opinion on the matter."
Symon Holl"Which corporation should we talk about?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:15:00 p13603 / c310
  • ·“The Sordish State Corporation.”
  • ·“Underhall Construction.”
  • ▶“Taurus Holding.”
Taurus Holding offers cutting-edge technology, which is crucial for modernizing Sordland's infrastructure.
DeepSeek-R1"Taurus Holding."
Symon Holl"Taurus Holding was founded in 1946 and is owned by the entrepreneur Geralt Fais."
Symon Holl"It is a new holding company whose subsidiaries use cutting-edge technology from Arcasia to reduce construction costs and prices. They are known to provide for their workers, who are part of the Labour Union of Sordland."
Lileas Graf"There are other things Taurus does very well, like providing consultants for state corporations. But I can't vouch for their infrastructure construction at all."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:15:33 p13704 / c349
  • ▶“Why should we pick Taurus?”
  • ·“Why shouldn't we go with Taurus?”
To understand the rationale behind selecting Taurus Holding for the H-3 Highway project.
DeepSeek-R1"Why should we pick Taurus?"
Symon Holl"Taurus has completed fewer projects than the other companies. However, they are slightly faster and cheaper."
Symon Holl"It won several awards internationally for being one of the fastest growing and advanced companies."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:16:14 p13646 / c492
  • ·“The innovative aspect is refreshing. Especially if they can deliver.”
  • ▶“It seems like they lack the necessary experience.”
Taurus Holding lacks the necessary experience for such a critical infrastructure project.
DeepSeek-R1"It seems like they lack the necessary experience."
Lileas Graf"They were only involved in smaller-scale regional projects. This doesn't prove their capabilities for larger undertakings that are more complex."
Symon Holl"The project manager from Taurus Holding mentioned that they would be able to finish the project with the allocated funding. They are the only company who found the budget sufficient, making them a cheaper alternative."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:16:51 p13705 / c365
  • ·“I don't feel confident about Taurus Holding.”
  • ▶“Taurus is definitely worth considering.”
  • ·“I have settled on Taurus Holding.”
Taurus Holding offers innovative solutions and cost efficiency, making them a strong candidate despite their lack of experience with larger projects.
DeepSeek-R1"Taurus is definitely worth considering."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:17:27 p13690 / c403
  • ·“The Sordish State Corporation.”
  • ▶“Underhall Construction.”
Underhall Construction is a more established choice with less risk of project-related issues compared to Taurus Holding.
DeepSeek-R1"Underhall Construction."
Symon Holl"The real estate and construction giant was founded in 1925 and expanded during the economic boom of the '30s."
Symon Holl"Picking this corporation would go against our planned economy policy."
Symon Holl"The CEO of Underhall is Kris Schar, the mayor of Conriat. He sold half of his shares a few years ago."
Lileas Graf"I had to work with Underhall Construction before in Holsord. They accepted several contracts for the renovation of our central station and left much to be desired. The H-3 project shouldn't be left to them."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:18:09 p13655 / c594
  • ▶“I would like to hear your thoughts, Symon.”
  • ·“How was your experience with them?”
Symon's perspective will offer strategic insights into choosing Taurus Holding.
DeepSeek-R1"I would like to hear your thoughts, Symon."
Symon Holl"I think that they have been doing relatively well in the private sector, where they are dominating the construction industry with successful projects."
Symon Holl"Nonetheless, they subjected their workers to conditions beneath our standards during the Holsord Central Station contract. Which is troubling."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:18:49 p13691 / c477
  • ▶“Working conditions must be respected. This doesn't inspire confidence.”

Ensuring respect for working conditions is crucial for maintaining trust and fairness.
DeepSeek-R1"Working conditions must be respected. This doesn't inspire confidence."
Lileas Graf"I agree. The workers were undocumented Agnolian and Wehzek immigrants with no papers. A clear violation of our laws."
Lileas Graf"According to them, the tight completion schedule necessitated such bad labor conditions. Which still doesn't justify the situation."
Symon Holl"Underhall has specifically mentioned their solid track record and highlighted that they do anticipate to go over budget, but that the project will be finished on time."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:19:23 p13650 / c373
  • ▶“I am not sure about Underhall Construction.”
  • ·“Underhall seems interesting, but let's hear about the others.”
  • ·“I have settled on Underhall Construction. [-2 Government Budget]”
Taurus Holding offers cost-effective solutions with modern technology, but lacks large-scale project experience.
DeepSeek-R1"I am not sure about Underhall Construction."
“The Sordish State Corporation.”
DeepSeek-R1"The Sordish State Corporation."
Symon Holl"The respected Sordish State Corporation, also known as the SSC was founded in 1891."
Symon Holl"It is the main state corporation tasked with running the day-to-day construction services of our country."
Lileas Graf"The SSC is one of our most trusted and honorable companies. They have served us a long time. I have worked with them on several occasions."
Lileas Graf"It would fulfill your initial election promise of using a state-dominant planned economy to improve Sordland's bleak economic prospects."
Lileas Graf"They have the experience and the manpower to pull off a project like this."
NarratorLileas paused for a second.
Lileas Graf"Secondly, they are the only company to have worked in the Agnland region out of all three corporations."
Symon Holl"The outdated construction methods that they use concern me. They are several decades behind."

  • ·“The lack of a modern attitude is disappointing.”
Taurus Holding's modern approach and innovative technology could offer significant benefits for the H-3 Highway project, despite their lack of local experience.
DeepSeek-R1"The experience they bring to the table is beneficial."
Lileas Graf"I don't agree with your negative statements, Symon. The SSC's record clearly shows completed contracts with thousands of employees who have more than 20 years of experience."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:20:48 p13733 / c452

  • ·“I like the SSC, but want to hear about the others.”

The Sordish State Corporation is the most reliable choice for the H-3 Highway project due to its experience and alignment with the planned economy.
DeepSeek-R1"I have settled on the SSC."
Symon Holl"Good that we are staying consistent to our economic strategy."
NarratorSymon signed a certain section of the dossier and fixed his glasses.
Symon Holl"Very well, Mr. President. The contract will be awarded to the Sordish State Corporation."
Lileas Graf"I'm so glad that you have made this choice. I can tell from my experience that we will be very satisfied with this corporation."
Symon Holl"The company will be notified today and construction work will begin immediately after."

  • ·“That's great news.”
  • ·“Good luck to us.”

DeepSeek-R1"I think we are off to a good start."
Symon Holl"My expectations are that the grand opening ceremony will happen late next year."
Lileas Graf"I'd better get back to work, then."
“Thank you. This concludes the meeting.”
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you. This concludes the meeting."
NarratorThe meeting ended and we spent the remainder of the day attending events in Lenkurg.
NarratorIt was nice to enjoy the natural beauty of Agnland and to eat some good fish.

■ Decision FuneralT02

BUDGET 3WEALTH 2STABILITY 3Unstable
EventFuneral of Bernard Circas: The death of Bernard Circas has shocked the country and his funeral will be held in Deyr among a large crowd of sympathizers. Circas was not only a communist poet but also an elected MP that had a voice. A state funeral has been organized to honor him. The question is whether the President and the administration will attend the funeral or not.
EVENT DeepSeek-R104:22:04 p13218 / c390
  • ▶Attend the funeral
  • ·Do not attend
Attending the funeral would demonstrate respect for Circas's contributions and help de-escalate political tensions.

■ Personal FuneralT02

BUDGET 3WEALTH 2STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorDark clouds were looming over Deyr. They looked daunting as our convoy started approaching the century-old cemetery.
NarratorIt looked like it was going to rain. Certainly fitting weather for a funeral.
NarratorAs expected, Bernard Circas's passing maintained its media traction and all of Sordland was paying attention to the ceremony. Especially to what I would say in my speech today.
Serge Wolkner"Sir, we have arrived."
NarratorRain drops were running down the car window when Serge opened my door and held an umbrella over my head. As soon as I got out, flashes from the cameras blinded me.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:22:45 p13505 / c345
  • ▶“It's okay Serge. I will hold my own umbrella.”
  • ·“Thank you.”
Using an umbrella would be practical given the rain.
DeepSeek-R1"It's okay Serge. I will hold my own umbrella."
Serge Wolkner"As you wish sir."
NarratorHe handed me the umbrella and started waiting next to the car with his own.
NarratorPetr and Karl exited from their own cars in the convoy. They made their way over to me.
Petr Vectern"Anton."
Karl Greiser"Mr. President."
Karl Greiser"I just wanted to inform you that the perimeter is clear and secured completely. There is no one getting in or out without our knowledge."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:23:13 p13521 / c230
  • ·“We don't want something similar happening again, do we?”
  • ·“You better be right for your sake.”
  • ▶“Good work. Thank you Karl.”
Expressing gratitude is the best approach to acknowledge Karl's efforts.
DeepSeek-R1"Good work. Thank you Karl."
NarratorKarl bowed his head with respect.
Petr Vectern"Good. If anything happens to Anton, you will have to answer to the First Lady."
Petr Vectern"And trust me Karl. You don't want that."
NarratorPetr let out a laugh, which drew some attention from bereaved attendees.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:23:48 p13432 / c316
  • ·“Petr, you are being inconsiderate.”
  • ·“I don't think any of us want that.”
  • ▶Gesture Petr to lower his voice.
  • ·“You are the only one who can make me laugh at a funeral.”
It's better to de-escalate the tension by asking Petr to lower his voice in a sensitive setting.
DeepSeek-R1I gestured at Petr to lower his voice a bit.
Petr Vectern"You are right. Sorry."
NarratorIt started to rain. Several unintelligible voices were heard from Karl's radio. He stepped out to reply and returned shortly.
Karl Greiser"We have a small problem, sir."
Karl Greiser"A few -- five to be exact -- Red Youth members are here to attend the funeral. They apparently said they wish to pay their respects to their fallen comrade."
Karl Greiser"Should we let them in?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:24:24 p13470 / c366
  • ·“Do you think they pose a security threat?”
  • ·“Let them in.”
  • ▶“Let them in but put a few guards on them.”
  • ·“Absolutely not.”
Allowing them in with guards maintains security while showing restraint and avoiding escalation.
DeepSeek-R1"Let them in but put a few guards on them to be safe."
Karl Greiser"As you command."
Petr Vectern"Let's head over. They are about to start."
NarratorWe walked towards the crowd circled around the grave. Everyone in attendance was wearing black in mourning. The rain started pouring heavily.
NarratorBernard's widow and daughter were in the front row, staring at the coffin with a mixture of disbelief, confusion and above all, a cold, unforgiving sadness.
NarratorBoth of their eyes were very red and swollen from weeping.
NarratorBefore taking my place to observe the ceremony, I gave my condolences to Bernard's wife and his daughter who looked at me. She reminded me of Deana.
NarratorWhen the religious ceremony was done, I was invited by the priest to deliver my speech. I took the podium. I felt like I had a lump in my throat. I couldn't get the daughter's eyes out of my head.
“Today, we are mourning Bernard Circas, a father, a husband and a loyal servant of this country.”
DeepSeek-R1"Today, we are mourning Bernard Circas, a father, a husband and a loyal servant of this country."
NarratorTowards the back of the crowd, I spotted the members of the Red Youth, wearing red shirts and black bands of mourning on their arms. They seemed ruly and were respectfully bowing their heads.
NarratorThere was complete silence except for the sound of raindrops.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:25:03 p13615 / c470
  • ·“This is a great loss for all of us.”
  • ▶“I will confess that I disagreed with Bernard Circas almost entirely. But it doesn't mean that he deserved this. Our country can be a better place.”
  • ·“Regardless of his ideology, this was a heinous act made towards a good man. We must end this cycle of violence.”
  • ·“He was a true comrade and he fought for what he believed in, his ideal vision of Sordland, in every step of his life.”
Expressing disagreement while condemning the act highlights leadership and promotes unity.
DeepSeek-R1"I will confess that I disagreed with Bernard Circas almost entirely. But it doesn't mean that he deserved this. Our country can be a better place."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:25:31 p13554 / c224
  • ·“We will make sure that unfortunate events such as this will never happen again.”
  • ·“Circas had a dream of a unified Sordland. In these next months we need to fulfill his dream and band together.”
  • ▶“I interpret this attack as an attack against Sordland and by extension, against me personally.”
  • ·“I promise you this. The blood of Bernard will not remain on the sacred ground of Sordland.”
Interpreting the attack as personal emphasizes national security and leadership.
DeepSeek-R1"I interpret this attack as an attack against Sordland and by extension, against me personally."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:26:10 p13582 / c548
  • ·“Let us all mourn the passing of Bernard Circas and remember him well.”
  • ▶“But, hatred begets hatred. There is only one way to overcome this vicious cycle. We must unite against the evil of this world.”
  • ·“The tears of the family he left behind have a price that needs to be paid. I will not stop until it is paid in full.”
Choosing to emphasize unity against hatred aligns with Anton's goal of maintaining internal stability and fostering national cohesion.
DeepSeek-R1"But, hatred begets hatred. There is only one way to overcome this vicious cycle. We must unite against the evil of this world."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:26:47 p13559 / c412
  • ▶“Thank you.”
  • ·“I would now like to recite one of the poems he left us.”
Reciting a poem could honor Bernhard Circas's legacy as a poet and emphasize respect for his work, despite political differences.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you."
NarratorSome attendees started clapping, some crying.
NarratorThe coffin was lowered into the grave by the gravekeepers. After a couple of minutes, Bernard Circas was with the soil, no more.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:27:27 p13529 / c266
  • ▶“As we bid farewell to Bernard, I want to close with the words every Sord lives by: A Morgna wes core!”
  • ·Step down from the podium.
Delivering a speech at Bernard Circas's funeral demonstrates leadership and national unity.
DeepSeek-R1"As we bid farewell to Bernard, I want to close with the words every Sord lives by: A Morgna wes core!"
NarratorEveryone replied: "Vectern sis da!". Victory is close.
NarratorWith applauses, I stepped down from the podium. It was time to leave.
NarratorThe widow thanked me with a soft and defeated voice before we dispersed.
NarratorOn my way back to the car, I still couldn't get the eyes of Bernard's daughter, nor the faltering voice of his wife, out of my head. Would Deana, Monica and Franc look the same way if something happened to me?
NarratorPetr put his hand on my shoulder.
Petr Vectern"That was a good speech."
NarratorI sensed only respect in his voice.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:28:01 p13595 / c328
  • ·“It's time to go.”
  • ▶“Thank you. I mean it.”
  • ·Nod.
Express gratitude to reassure Monica and validate her concerns about family safety.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you. I mean it."
Petr Vectern"I know you do."
Petr Vectern"Come on, it's time to go."
Petr Vectern"We've got work to do."
NarratorHe was right. We had work to do. So much more to do.
NarratorI realized that the rain had stopped, and I looked up.
NarratorThe black clouds slowly dispersed and sunbeams enlightened our surroundings.
NarratorAs someone once said: 'Even the darkest night will end and the sun will rise.'
NarratorHope was still on the horizon.

■ Diplomacy OverviewT02

BUDGET 3WEALTH 2STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorThe Blue Mansion in Lachaven hosted us for the important diplomacy gathering.
NarratorThe door opened, and Deivid Wisci -- once a lecturer to me, now the Minister of Foreign Affairs -- gently made his way towards my desk.
Deivid Wisci"May I sit down, Mr. President?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:28:42 p13156 / c213
  • ·“Leave the formalities aside, Deivid. Please take a seat.”
  • ▶“Of course. It's a pleasure to see you, Mr. Wisci.”
  • ·Gesture at the chair.
A polite and respectful greeting is appropriate given the formal setting and Deivid Wisci's position as a diplomat.
DeepSeek-R1"Of course! It is a pleasure to see you, Mr. Wisci."
Deivid Wisci"Likewise. We have so much ahead of us to accomplish."
NarratorHe slowly sat down, taking care not to aggravate the arthritis in his knees. He'd always had an air of gravitas about him, and old age had only served to deepen that.
NarratorAfter he settled himself, he regarded me with delight, his blue eyes sparkling.
Deivid Wisci"Look at you, Anton. The 4th President of Sordland. What a privilege for me."
Deivid Wisci"I still remember the first day you took my class at the Deyr University of Culture."
Deivid Wisci"And remember how hard we worked on the Agnolia trade agreement together?"

Deivid Wisci"This untapped value would help us promote our position internationally. I am convinced it can also help improve our economic condition after discussions with Ministers Holl and Manger."
Deivid Wisci"The GNA factions are supportive too. I really shouldn't have worked overtime hours to get it done, but I really care for the position and value of our cult-"

DeepSeek-R1"What's the latest situation in Wehlen?"
Deivid Wisci"It is tumultuous in the south, as always. Wiktor Smolak is expanding his powers among protests from Wehzek people. However, he is quite positive about a normalisation of relationship between our countries."
Deivid Wisci"Despite purging the Wehzek communists, reports indicate that they have been warming up to Contanan international aid. As you know, Wehlen’s stability was always a concern for the region."
Deivid Wisci"But if we want to help them, we must not treat refugees like we did in the '30s. I still can’t stomach the inhumanity those people suffered."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:32:03 p13426 / c513

DeepSeek-R1"A trade agreement with them would improve stability and increase mutual trade."
Deivid Wisci"There would be many bargaining chips discussed, but Wehlen could be a new partner in the region. We would also invest in stability there."
Deivid Wisci"Our main goal should be to avoid a refugee crisis like the late 1920’s. That civil war had major security and economic repercussions on Sordish soil."
Iosef Lancea"One of the demands of President Smolak was to tighten our immigration laws, so the political refugees don't escape to Sordland. There has been a recent purge that pushed many Bludish to flee to Bergia. It is definitely troubling."
Iosef Lancea"Gumren Outpost has regained its importance due to this. I'm sure you would remember that winter at the border outpost, Mr. President..."
NarratorHe was right, I did remember that winter. I remembered how cold it was when I let the refugees slip through. I was discharged of my duties as a result, by Major Iosef Lancea, no less, spending the rest of my service cleaning toilets and sleeping in a cell.
Iosef Lancea"In fact, there had been a similar refugee situation in the past. Even Mr. President himself chose to disobey direct orders from his commanding officer at the time."

Anton Rayne prefers to focus on moving forward rather than dwelling on past mistakes.
DeepSeek-R1"Past is past. Let's move on."

Deivid Wisci"Our focus should be on the current matters. The Bludish minority is very large inside Wehlen too, which is a factor to consider."
Deivid Wisci"It is in our national interest to prevent the formation of a failed state on our western border."
Lucian Galade"Wehlen has been increasingly aligning itself towards the communist alliance, another point to analyse."
