Iosef Lancea"I highly advise caution and calculated action against Rumburg. Our military must be prepared, the threat is real."
Deivid Wisci"They are strong and have the motivation, as well as the means, to damage us. We shouldn't give them any reasons to pursue aggression."

DeepSeek-R1"What about Lespia?"
Deivid Wisci"Our southern neighbour is aligning itself more and more with Arcasia. Lespia is currently led by Prime Minister Patricio Alvarez."
Lucian Galade"They are the jewel of the continent and the second largest economy in Eastern Merkopa. Though wealthy, Lespia suffers from excessive economic inequality."
Deivid Wisci"Our past relations have been great but during the last decade, their investments to Sordland dwindled due to our instability."

Deivid Wisci"I am certain that the recession has a lot to do with their retraction of investments. Once they see that money isn't in the picture they vanish rather quickly because they are capitalists."

Deivid Wisci"This covers the overview of the current situation. We will move on to our trade choice and the response to Rumburg's diplomatic escalation."
NarratorThe sun began to set over the hills surrounding Lachaven. The team was ready to give advice on the new trade initiative and the response to the latest diplomatic incident with Rumburg.

Petr Vectern"Hey there, folks. Excuse my delay. I'm sure I didn't miss anything we didn't already know."

Lucian Galade"Punctuality is important. We are talking about serious subjects here."

NarratorPetr's tone was more serious.
Petr Vectern"Expect a report on your desk tomorrow."
Deivid Wisci"The Vice President will be part of the trade delegation, so it will be great to have his input on that subject as well as on the Rumburg incident."
Deivid Wisci"Rumburg Consul Sir Bran Harrington announced the closure of the 120-year-old consulate. Now their diplomatic staff is leaving this city to go back to Thornbourgh."
Lucian Galade"He blamed the lack of security in Sordland due to the assassination and the subsequent protests. But it is obvious that they are making excuses."
Iosef Lancea"Bastards! First they close their consulate and then they blame our security!?"

Deivid Wisci"In my entire career I have never seen our Rumburg relations suffer this much. The previous low point was the War of Broken Wills."
NarratorIosef Lancea was furious. He barely kept himself from slamming the desk.
Iosef Lancea"There are worrying developments like the smuggled arms and the build-up near the border. Our military must be buffed up to stand against a great power like Rumburg."
Deivid Wisci"We either respond to this aggressive act with a diplomatic condemnation or we retaliate by closing our consulate in Dome."
Lucian Galade"I want to say that an escalation would be risky this early in our Presidency. We should focus on creating a negative image of Rumburg in the international scene."
Iosef Lancea"The people of Sordland must be protected, no matter the cost. I say we respond equally."

Iosef Lancea"I wish we had reacted more strongly. If we don't confront them, they will keep escalating the situation."
Petr Vectern"I, on the other hand, am glad that we are taking the more cautious road."
Deivid Wisci"There are three trade-related topics to address today. Which topic should we tackle first?"

Deivid Wisci"We have two options with our trade initiative. Starting new negotiations with our old partner Agnolia to the north-east, and forming a new cooperation with Wehlen to the west."

DeepSeek-R1"Tell me about the trade deal with Agnolia."

Deivid Wisci"The point we are at with the early negotiations is around the steel trade. They are demanding that we buy their steel for a higher price. "
Deivid Wisci"That is because they think that the deal is far too unbalanced as it is. "
Deivid Wisci"Their second main demand is a privileged access to our agricultural market."
Deivid Wisci"If we are to fulfil their demands, in return they are offering a full alliance with Sordland against Rumburg."
Iosef Lancea"Looking at the current advances of Rumburg near our borders, having a coalition against Rumburg would prove extremely useful."
Deivid Wisci"Obviously there will be consequences for entering a full alliance. Mainly the issue is around Heljiland. Valgsland has clear intentions on taking over the island one way or another and entering a military alliance with Agnolia will make them furious."
Iosef Lancea"I want to state my support for the decision to stick to our old ally, Agnolia. Even though I disagree with their demand to relax immigration, we have cooperated with them on many issues in the past."
Petr Vectern"Agnolia is a solid partner for us, Deivid and the President worked on the previous comprehensive trade agreement in the 30s."
Petr Vectern"There are obviously subjects that we would still need to tackle like our economies, the traded goods and what to do with immigration."
Deivid Wisci"My opinion is that this is a good deal and we should go for it. The new Agnolian Prime Minister is trying to improve the existing relationship."
Deivid Wisci"The downside is that we would need to make concessions. A reality of any trade deal."

Deivid Wisci"That would be the best outcome."
Deivid Wisci"If you decide that Agnolia is a good option, another point is that they have a very democratic system. After the recent elections the PM has a mandate to arrange a fairer deal with us. Maartin Van Hoorten also is an avid supporter of relaxed immigration."
Deivid Wisci"Since we said we will tighten immigration, he might be reluctant."
Deivid Wisci"Starting the highway construction in Agnland plays a part too."
Lucian Galade"Regardless, if we are going to increase our regional influence to previous levels, we might need to make amends with our neighbours."
Lucian Galade"Ultimately the decision will be up to you as always."

DeepSeek-R1"Tell me about the trade deal with Wehlen."
Deivid Wisci"Dealing with a fractured country like Wehlen has its positives and negatives. Although it will be lucrative in the short term, there are risks associated with dealing with such a country."
Deivid Wisci"They are proposing co-investment projects and a no-tariff agreement. They are also offering oil in return for attracting foreign capital interests from Sordland."
Deivid Wisci"However, it is possible that there will be international ramifications of getting closer with a country like Wehlen."
Deivid Wisci"All in all, this is a very lucrative deal for us. However, we need to think about the consequences."
Iosef Lancea"I certainly don't support the decision to become friendlier with Wehlen. We carried their burdens for decades. Their migrants flooded our country and now they are expecting a deal from us."
Petr Vectern"Maintaining influence with Wehlen could prove useful. Yes, it is risky, but think about our influence on a underdeveloped region like that. We could also sell more products and enter their market easily."
Deivid Wisci"I am of the opinion that it is simply too risky. Yes, we would gain resources and immediate capital, but I'm afraid of the consequences."

Deivid Wisci"Me too."
Iosef Lancea"Agreed."
Petr Vectern"I still think we should give it a try."
Deivid Wisci"Hmm."

Deivid Wisci"Our neutrality strategy might ease the tensions with Rumburg, since their primary concern is the strengthening of the superpower blocs."
Deivid Wisci"The last handful of monarchies in Merkopa are reactive against the influences of Arcasian Capitalism and Malenyevism."
Deivid Wisci"We also need to consider the effects of our economic doctrine plan."
Deivid Wisci"The fact that we are headed towards a planned economy will have an influence."

Deivid Wisci"I believe this gives a brief overview of our current options."
Deivid Wisci"Our trade delegation will begin work towards the end of the year. We expect final negotiations and the signings of the trade agreements to happen next year."

Deivid Wisci"From what we have gathered, there is no clear offer yet. The interest clearly seems to be more control over the gas produced and sold in Sordland. I suspect this must be related to the recent tensions on the coast of Pales."
Deivid Wisci"Mr. Manger is working out the details to get more concrete headline terms soon. Of particular interest are the gas fields in the Narbel area of the Nargis region."
Petr Vectern"That must be related to Gasom operations. Gus has ties with the people around that corporation. Even though there is another CEO, Ewald Alphonso is still chairman."
Lucian Galade"Gasom is a private corporation, a fact that limits our control over any foreign investment. Tarquin Soll's Energy Protection Act of 1932 specifically limits the purchases of Sordish energy company shares by foreign nations to ten percent."
Lucian Galade"Ewald Alphonso adjusted this law in 1948 and increased it to up to 20 percent. Anything above that requires us to change the Energy Protection Act. Ewald holds most of Gasom's cards in his hands still."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:42:26 p14517 / c721

DeepSeek-R1"Remember campaigning for him back in the day? He wouldn't have gotten the USP leadership without us."
Petr Vectern"I've got to say his team was great to work with. Toppling Tarquin Soll was a historic moment in the USP."
Lucian Galade"We had to break the status quo to get into government ourselves. I always thought Alphonso was too optimistic about his grand plans."
Iosef Lancea"His decision not to increase the armed forces budget for the term was unprecedented. President Soll would always fund our troops a little more."
Deivid Wisci"We helped him get to power, but also led him to his fall. Such a peaceful transition with the '53 elections, however, showed the strength of our democracy."
NarratorLucian pulled out a pen and took a few notes.
NarratorDeivid coughed a bit and then continued talking.
Deivid Wisci"From our diplomatic channels, we can tell that Lespia is still very interested in Sordish petrol, whereas Rumburg wants more gas resources."
Iosef Lancea"As a sovereign nation, we must protect our resources. Our energy independence is part of our national security concern here; Gasom handles most of our gas production."

Iosef Lancea"I'm very glad you have chosen a Sordland-centric path, Mr. President."
Deivid Wisci"In theory, centralization works. But it leaves little room for outside help when internal conditions are dire."
Iosef Lancea"If Rizia wants to buy more than 20 percent of shares allowed by the protectionism laws, then the government can step in to make changes to the EPA."
Lucian Galade"Precisely, but we don’t know if this will be the case. There is a scenario in which they might invest within the EPA limitations; we just have to hear more before weighing the options."
Petr Vectern"They never revealed much about their involvement to me, but I always had the feeling that Alphonso's crew was more linked to Gasom than they seemed."
Lucian Galade"I have information that the CEO that replaced Alphonso is a loyalist. Alphonso still is the chairman of Gasom and the de facto key figure. Additionally, National Business Council Chairman Mikail Aven and Minister Gus Manger have shares in the company and preside over the board."

Deivid Wisci"Any further comments, esteemed cabinet members?"

Lucian Galade"I will assist in analyzing the political implications, Mr. Wisci."
Deivid Wisci"Thank you. Our support staff and I will be working towards the next steps soon after receiving more context from Mr. Manger."
Deivid Wisci"Our ambassador in Porte Drazon is in communication with Laurento Esquibel, the Rizian Councilor of Foreign Affairs, already."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:44:54 p14780 / c223
  • ▶“I'd like to know more about Rizia.”
  • ·“I don't need any further information about Rizia.”
As President, staying informed about neighboring countries like Rizia is crucial for national security and foreign policy.
DeepSeek-R1"I'd like to know more about Rizia."
Deivid Wisci"The nation south of Lespia is a centralized constitutional monarchy led by King Romus Toras. The grandson of Queen Lyza Toras, 'the Great', and son of King Valero Toras, 'the Frail'."
Lucian Galade"The kingdom is a developed nation that wielded considerable influence in Southern Merkopa before the reign of Valero. The country has lost some territory due to internal strife."
Deivid Wisci"The Alphonso government signed vital trade agreements with King Romus Toras, benefitting both nations at a time of need. Attempts at further cooperation stalled out due to the King's blood ties to Rumburg."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:45:35 p14899 / c495

DeepSeek-R1"I’m curious how our neighbors Lespia and Wehlen get along with Rizia."
Deivid Wisci"There is some tension with both. It appears that since Lespia's acceptance into ATO in 1950, most of South Merkopa has been heading towards a boiling point."
Iosef Lancea"Not to mention Wehlen's struggles with the BFF, an organization that we've long suspected to be armed by King Romus' in-laws in Rumburg."
Lucian Galade"The relationship between Rizia and Wehlen is more complex than that. They were closely aligned under Valero's rule until Rumburg entered the play."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:46:07 p14917 / c262

NarratorLucian knocked on the table.
Lucian Galade"We should be more concerned with-"
NarratorPetr interjected and reached in his pocket.
Petr Vectern"Actually, a friend of Gus called Rusty gifted me this beautiful piece of full Rizian gold coin. They say it helps you find love. Look."

■ Reform BriefingT02

BUDGET 3WEALTH 2STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorI came a little early to the meeting room before anybody showed up. We had scheduled a meeting to discuss the progress of the reform committee regarding the new Constitution.
NarratorI took a seat on the centre chair in the vacant meeting room as I waited for them to show up.
NarratorThe reforms were slowly becoming the most significant expectation of my government. We would be changing Soll's Constitution of '29.
NarratorMost people nowadays blamed the constitution for the outstanding problems of the country.
NarratorOn the other hand, the reformists in the Assembly had been getting louder about their demands. What I knew for sure was that I needed to do something about this encroaching chaos before it swallowed me.
NarratorMeanwhile, Lucian, Petr and Nia had been leading the committee to prepare an initial draft for the constitutional amendments. I was intrigued to hear their results.
NarratorThere was a knock on the door. It was Lucian.
Lucian Galade"Good afternoon, sir."
NarratorLucian arrived exactly on the hour mark again. His punctuality could almost be described as obsessive.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:48:15 p14530 / c422
  • ▶"Good afternoon, Lucian."
  • ·"Are you waiting behind the door to come at the exact right second?"
Lucian Galade's punctuality is a notable trait, and acknowledging it respectfully fits the formal tone.
DeepSeek-R1"Good afternoon, Lucian."
NarratorHe took a seat on the table and took out a bunch of documents, spreading them on the long table.
Lucian Galade"We should begin the meeting as soon as Mrs. Morgna and Vice President Vectern arrive. We finally have some results from the reform committee."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:48:51 p14674 / c341
  • ·"How does it look?"
  • ▶"What results?"
  • ·"Good."
Anton wants to know the specifics of the reform committee's progress and results.
DeepSeek-R1"What results?"
Lucian Galade"The reform committee already drafted the amendments for the new Constitution thanks to the help of the members."
Lucian Galade"But the situation is still quite complicated politically. We'll go over the details in the meeting."
NarratorA few minutes later, Petr and Nia also arrived.
NarratorAfter a short chat, everybody sat down and took out their notes. I knew it was going to be a long meeting.
Petr Vectern"There is a lot to talk about the future of this process. Let us begin to tackle the points of contention."
Nia Morgna"Mr. President, our committee meetings were really fruitful, and we already started working on the constitutional draft together with the reformists."
Nia Morgna"They demanded a little too much, but we've had success with Frens Ricter to create a realistic draft that could potentially go through the Supreme Court."
Petr Vectern"A *little*? They demanded to rewrite the whole Constitution!"
Petr Vectern"Mr. Ricter's deputy even suggested removing the term 'Sord' from the constitution completely."
Nia Morgna"I also didn't like their theatrics but the requests were definitely not outrageous. They were just not realistic in today's political circumstances."
Nia Morgna"Nonetheless, Mr. Vice President, thanks for your convincing arguments we seem to have reached a common ground already."
Lucian Galade"If we can fully reach an agreement with the reformists, we will be very strong in the Assembly to propose the change."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:49:38 p14879 / c486
  • ·"Can we reach a two-thirds majority in the Assembly?"
  • ▶"Is it possible to pass the Supreme Court?"
Passing through the Supreme Court may be more feasible given the political divisions in the Assembly.
DeepSeek-R1"Is it possible to pass this through the Supreme Court?"
Lucian Galade"They are the biggest obstacle. Even if we pass the Assembly, the Chief Justice will do his best to block our amendments."
Lucian Galade"We need at least 6 of the 11 judges to vote for the proposal. But Mr. Hawker has enormous influence over the judges."
Petr Vectern"We should reach out to the judges in secret. Some of them might be willing to break rank to support us."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:50:28 p14992 / c593
  • ·"All means are to be considered in order to succeed."
  • ▶"They will surely support a just cause for the country and its future."
Anton Rayne prefers a just approach, trusting that a fair cause will gain judicial support.
DeepSeek-R1"They will surely support a just cause for the country and its future."
NarratorLucian looked sceptical.
Nia Morgna"According to the Constitution, I am a member of the Supreme Court, which means we already have one vote."
Nia Morgna"Two of the judges are friends of mine, and of similar opinion about the reforms. Consider them with me."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:51:09 p14911 / c421
  • ·"So, we only have 3 votes out of 11?"
  • ▶"Who else would be willing to support reforms?"
  • ·"I'm sure the rest can be bribed."
Asking for more information on potential supporters aligns with the President's integrity and desire to find legitimate ways to pass reforms.
DeepSeek-R1"Who else would be willing to support reforms?"
Nia Morgna"I think Judge Edmonds can be persuaded. And in extension, Mr. Dalton and Mr. Martin."
Nia Morgna"They weren't really blinded by the Sollist ideology."
Petr Vectern"Yes, those three don't seem to be working directly with the Chief Justice Hawker. If we can get to Isabel Edmonds, we might get the 6 votes needed. That's our best bet."
"Can we reach a two-thirds majority in the Assembly?"
DeepSeek-R1"Can we reach a two-thirds majority in the Assembly?"
Lucian Galade"If you can get Frens Ricter to work with you on this, our only problem would be uniting our own party. We'll never be able to get NFP to work with us."
Lucian Galade"We need the majority of our 130 seats in the Assembly to vote in favour. With PFJP's full support of 70 votes, we will easily surpass the needed 166."
Lucian Galade"But it will be tough to unite the USP with all the divisive wings that have formed. You must make them follow you loyally."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:51:47 p15341 / c342
  • ·"We did it before for the elections. We can do it again."
  • ▶"We will convince the leads."
Anton Rayne's experience in uniting factions suggests he believes they can persuade the necessary leaders.
DeepSeek-R1"We will convince the leads."
Petr Vectern"Albin Clavin and his reformist wing would be supporting you. He already weighed in heavily in the committee meetings."
Petr Vectern"However, the conservatives would be tough to break. The influence of Soll is still living strong in the party."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:52:30 p15339 / c314
  • ·"We will eventually remove his influence."
  • ▶"How many votes do they have?"
  • ·"Do you think they can be persuaded?"
Knowing the exact number of votes the conservative wing holds helps assess how challenging it will be to unite the party behind the reforms.
DeepSeek-R1"How many votes do they have in total?"
Petr Vectern"The conservatives have around 40 seats. There are many unaligned people and some of your "loyalists" might decide to show their true faces too."
Petr Vectern"So we need as many of the conservatives to support us. And there is only one person who is key to make them back us."
Nia Morgna"Gloria Tory. She's well respected by the Sollists. She'll be our biggest obstacle in the Assembly and her demands will be extensive."
Nia Morgna"I also have reasons to think she might be in Orso's pocket. She will be tough to break, but we'll need her help to get the conservative support."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:53:07 p15426 / c285
  • ·"What are the demands?"
  • ▶"Let's go through the draft."
Reviewing the draft will provide clarity on the proposed changes and help assess its feasibility with the Supreme Court.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's go through the draft details."
Lucian Galade"Of course."
Lucian Galade"We'll go through each of the main demands of the committee, and get your input. Mrs. Morgna can explain them further."
Lucian Galade"We'll start with the first *red line*. The new Constitution will fix the loopholes regarding the president's absolute veto powers."
Nia Morgna"Even though Mr. Ricter put on a whole show about why vetoes should completely be removed, the committee has almost unanimously agreed on fixing the loopholes, thus limiting presidential vetoes."
Nia Morgna"My suggestion is to add a clause detailing the methods the Assembly may override a veto with some sort of a super-majority. One person should never be able to block the rest."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:53:43 p15493 / c261
  • ·"I stand in agreement with Mr. Ricter. The vetoes should be removed."
  • ▶"The loopholes give the President too much power."
  • ·"They will not be allowed to dictate the negotiations."
Fixing the loopholes would effectively limit presidential power while maintaining a balanced system.
DeepSeek-R1"The loopholes give the President too much power which could be misused."
Nia Morgna"Whatever you decide it's important to get a balanced picture. We will be making history by changing the '29 Constitution of Soll."
Lucian Galade"The second one is about the judiciary. The new Constitution will remove the Supreme Court's right to vote on legislation."
Nia Morgna"This is yet another point that was unanimously agreed on by all parties. Tarquin Soll established this rule to protect his '29 Constitution. We all know most of the judges are still his pawns."
Nia Morgna"Constitutional amendments will still be very difficult to accomplish with a reform like this, particularly because we will keep the two-thirds vote system to be able to propose a change. However, the judiciary will not have the final say in the amendments."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:54:23 p15488 / c394
  • ·"This is already what we wanted to do."
  • ·"I can't disagree with that."
  • ▶"We need the Court to even pass this change. Why would they support this?"
  • ·"And what good does that do to us?"
Questioning whether the Supreme Court would support removing their legislative vote power highlights potential opposition and challenges to passing reforms.
DeepSeek-R1"We need the Court to even pass this change. Why on earth would they support this?"
Nia Morgna"Most of them won't. But we already talked about my vote and a possible agreement with Isabel which may change things."
Nia Morgna"Apart from these two critical points they have other major demands."
Nia Morgna"They want to reform the appointment procedures of the ministers. Mr. Ricter clarified that the Assembly should have a say in the formation of the council of ministers."
Petr Vectern"That is something that a majority in our party also supports."
Nia Morgna"Well, Mr. Ricter also talked about abolishing the Member of Honor rights of Tarquin Soll that gives him absolute immunity and makes him a permanent member of the Assembly."
Petr Vectern"Well... That one is a controversial one. I doubt that USP would like that."
Lucian Galade"Immunity of the Justices, the impeachment of the president, limiting the power of the presidential decrees... All of this came up during the committee meetings."
Nia Morgna"Yes, reformists expect big reforms while our faction in the USP are not very keen on pushing changes that are too great."
Petr Vectern"I'd say keeping everything in the middle ground is the safest bet. No need to agitate anyone too much."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:55:03 p15535 / c386

  • ·"I agree. Let's keep the changes small and manageable."
  • ▶"They have valid concerns that we need to look at."
It's important to consider all perspectives to ensure balanced and effective reforms.
DeepSeek-R1"They have valid concerns that we need to look at."
Nia Morgna"I'll keep working with them and keep you updated, Mr. President."
Petr Vectern"Well, regardless of what Mr. Rayne decides, we will need to work together to push this agenda."
Lucian Galade"Now we have a better picture of your intentions, sir. I will start reaching out to my contacts to make a draft that works for us."
Nia Morgna"I'll present your input to the committee. In a few months, you would have the draft on your desk to make the final changes yourself."
Lucian Galade"I think this was yet another fruitful meeting, sir."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:55:37 p15528 / c302

  • ▶"Together we will democratize Sordland."

DeepSeek-R1"Together we will democratize Sordland."
Nia Morgna"And you have my full support, Mr. President."
Petr Vectern"Ours too, of course."
Lucian Galade"Yes, sir. Thank you for your time."
Lucian Galade"I think this is where we'll call it, sir."
Lucian Galade"Have a good day."
NarratorThey left the room.

■ Bill Tourism CultureT02

BUDGET 3WEALTH 2STABILITY 3Unstable
Bill for decisionTourism and Cultural Preservation Act — The Sordland Tourism and Cultural Preservation Act aims to amplify the cultural allure and tourism viability of Borin, Ribery, Lachaven, Anrica, and Benfi. The Grand National Assembly is calling a collective push to establish Sordland as a global tourist destination and reduce the effects of the recession. Section I focuses on the cultural elevation of these cities, identifying local food, drink, music, dance, architecture, and historical sites as unique selling propositions. This cultivation of cultural tourism is positioned as a catalyst for economic invigoration. Section II imposes the restoration and preservation of key historical landmarks in Lachaven and Anrica. Concurrently, it proposes a comprehensive renovation of tourist infrastructure in Benfi to enrich the visitor experience and generate steady income for local communities. Section II Subsection I further commits to the safeguarding and development of the Caves of Antera in Ribery and the vineyards of Borin, recognizing their potential as tourism growth-drivers. [-1 Government Budget]
BILL DeepSeek-R104:56:37 p15033 / c621
  • ▶Sign: Tourism and Cultural Preservation Act
  • ·Veto: Tourism and Cultural Preservation Act
Promoting tourism can stimulate economic growth and cultural preservation, aligning with goals to revitalize Sordland despite budget constraints.

■ Autocrat BriefingT02

BUDGET 2WEALTH 2STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorThe administration had been working day and night on the overwhelming amount of issues that were facing the country.
NarratorThe various government departments were sending me an avalanche of documents, all of which required my signature so they could start working on the new plans that had been decided on.
NarratorAfter I'd signed dozens of them, I closed my eyes to relax for a moment.
NarratorAnother long day...
NarratorEven though I couldn't wait to leave the Palace for the day, Lucian had earlier told me that the Chief Justice wanted to speak to me. We were to meet in my office in thirty minutes.
NarratorI waited for him, wondering what he wanted to talk about.

Orso Hawker"I know what goes on."

Heron Garaci"We are protecting the Constitution, because it works, Mr. President. It is efficient and it gives you the authority to lead the nation with swift decisions."
Heron Garaci"Sordland has more important problems and threats that we should face right now instead of nitpicking about some paragraphs of the Constitution. We have a potential war within our borders."

Orso Hawker"Mr. President. I know that you are a busy man, so I will make this quick."
Orso Hawker"We all know that Sordish security forces found stashes of Rumburgian weaponry in the hideouts of Bludish separatists. KA-74's are roaming inside Sordland at this moment."
Orso Hawker"As much as Rumburg poses a direct threat, now it's clear that they are also intervening in our country and weaponising our minorities against us. It's not unprecedented to think that the Bluds will start their terror again with their new toys."
Orso Hawker"Not to mention the fights that broke out between the left and right wing after the shooting of Bernard Circas. Communists are rising with the support of United Contana. The chaos is imminent for Sordland."
Orso Hawker"And sadly we don't see the administration taking the necessary precautions. You said you will not focus on military during your term. Under current circumstances that cannot be tolerated at any cost."

Lucian Galade"What is this all about?"

  • ·"Let the Chief Justice talk, Lucian."
  • ·"You're right. I don't think anything will come out of this meeting."
Anton Rayne's diplomatic nature suggests he would prefer a respectful engagement.
DeepSeek-R1"Please explain, Mr. Hawker."
Orso Hawker"The person in question is Mr. Frens Ricter, himself. The *leader* of the reformists."
Orso Hawker"We have enough information to infer that he has ties with Arcasia."
Orso Hawker"Arcasia is aggressively growing their influence around the world and now we have Mr. Ricter coming up with these ridiculous demands for a new Constitution amidst a chaotic period in Sordland."
Orso Hawker"All the pieces seem to fit perfectly. That is why the Supreme Court will be doing what it takes to stop these reforms and preserve the Constitution."
Lucian Galade"How would you know that Mr. Ricter has ties to Arcasia?"
Heron Garaci"Mr. Galade, we all know he has been in Arcasia many times, because it's been documented. But what would you think if I told you he has been making hidden flights to the country?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:06:38 p15680 / c546
  • ·"I need to know this. Please explain."
  • ▶"He was hiding his flights to Arcasia?"
  • ·"Another conspiracy theory."
Challenging the claim directly questions the validity of their accusation.
DeepSeek-R1"He was hiding his flights to Arcasia?"
Heron Garaci"He was."
Orso Hawker"He was spotted in a hotel in Arcasia last year, where they held the 'Conference on Economic Development in Volatile Regions'. Right around that time, he started to influence the masses into believing that our Constitution is the reason for everything bad."
Orso Hawker"His trip was not documented, nor was he an official guest of the conference. We believe that he had a meeting with President Walker behind closed doors."
Orso Hawker"I also have more photos of him that were taken in Arcasia in different cities. One is from a couple months back. Again, none of it is documented."
Orso Hawker"He also attended another event in Lespia last week. The Arcasian Minister of Foreign Affairs was there too."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:07:19 p15748 / c360
  • ·"How did you find out about this?"
  • ·"If this is true, this is *big*."
  • ▶"How can any of these be undocumented?"
Questioning the validity of their claims by pointing out that all these details are undocumented forces them to explain how they obtained such information.
DeepSeek-R1"How can any of these be undocumented?"
Orso Hawker"We don't know. Even if he used a private plane, we would be able to get the information of his flights. That means he has people helping them from inside."
Orso Hawker"Many Sollists were already following his leads for quite some time, Mr. President. I cannot give away their identities, but I can give you the evidence."
NarratorHe handed me a small file.
NarratorIt contained official documents from the Sordish Border Guard with highlighted dates that showed no trace of Frens Ricter's name.
NarratorThere were clear photos of him that were taken in the conference in Arcasia, as well as remarks by Lespian and Arcasian citizens who claim to have seen him with Arcasian businessmen around the same dates.
NarratorThere was also an official transcript of one of his speeches where he claimed to be in Benfi on the dates of the conference in Arcasia, where he was spotted.
NarratorLucian skimmed through the documents with skepticism which slowly turned into surprise.
Lucian Galade"These seem to be real."
Orso Hawker"Of course they are real, Mr. Galade."
Orso Hawker"These seem to suggest that Arcasia is behind the reformists. Their demands will only benefit Arcasia. They want us to have a weaker administration while we face all these threats."
Orso Hawker"This is clearly a plot against Sordland. I bet Rumburg is part of their plan too. It cannot be a coincidence that they are weaponizing our minorities against us around the same time."
Orso Hawker"Now you understand the reasons for our current stance. And also our suspicions about your advisers, who clearly have not given you this vital information."
NarratorLucian gave him a sharp look.
Lucian Galade"We appreciate the information, Mr. Hawker, but I advise against jumping to conclusions right away. We still need to check the information."
Orso Hawker"There is not much to think or check... This is clearly an emergency. In such cases, the Constitution gives the President the right to use his emergency powers. If we work together, you can be sure that the Supreme Court will not block your declaration of emergency."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:08:04 p15963 / c507
  • ▶"Let's talk about how we can work together."
  • ·"You want me to declare an emergency?"
  • ·"I am still too skeptical about these, Mr. Hawker. I will have to decline."
Anton should consider working together with the Supreme Court to address the external threats while pushing for reforms.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's talk about how we can work together."
Orso Hawker"Of course. We cannot wait for the processes of the Assembly to take care of these issues. You must exercise your presidential powers for a fast and effective solution."
Orso Hawker"Which means you might need to suspend parts of the Constitution to give more authority to our security forces to deal with these issues. We must officially investigate Mr. Ricter as well."
Orso Hawker"You can devise a strong security decree if you can base it on the Articles 57 and 58, which gives the president the right to suspend parts of the Constitution to deal with national security threats."
Orso Hawker"You can directly bypass the Assembly in such a situation. The only thing you need is the Supreme Court to allow the emergency to be declared. And you have our word on that."
Lucian Galade"Sir, I think that is going a bit too far. We shouldn't escalate things so fast."
Orso Hawker"Mr. President, why don't we talk in private, just the two of us?"
Orso Hawker"We can devise a plan together. I know how we can use the Constitution to our advantage. It would be more effective if Mr. Galade and Mr. Garaci leave the room before we discuss it."
Lucian Galade"Sir, I advise against it."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:08:49 p16017 / c495

  • ·"I like your idea, Mr. Hawker but I'd rather plan it together."
  • ·"I'm sorry to say that we won't be cooperating, Mr. Hawker."
  • ▶"Regardless of our situation, I'll be proceeding with the reforms."
I must stay true to my reformist agenda despite external pressures.
DeepSeek-R1"Regardless of our situation, I'll be proceeding with the reforms."
Orso Hawker"Even though you are aware of our national security threats..."
Heron Garaci"You are making a big mistake, Mr. President. We will not allow you to succeed."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:09:29 p15872 / c405

  • ·"ENOUGH!"
  • ▶"Gentlemen, I think it's about time to end this meeting."
It's time to end the discussion and maintain control over the agenda.
DeepSeek-R1"Gentlemen, I think it's about time to end this meeting."
Orso Hawker"Well, thank you for having us, Mr. President."
Orso Hawker"Have a good evening."
NarratorThey both left the room without saying anything else.
Lucian Galade"That was not good. They clearly tried to threaten you."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:10:27 p15952 / c727

DeepSeek-R1"We need to take care of them."
Lucian Galade"Orso will be doing whatever it takes to damage you."
Lucian Galade"We need to be careful. There are still many Old Guard sympathizers in our party. We can't let them divide our party."
Lucian Galade"And he will definitely try."
NarratorAfter a short evaluation of the situation on our hands, Lucian left me in the presidential office.
NarratorIt was obvious that the Old Guards would be my main rivals in the near future.

TURN 03

■ Personal Franc TalkT03

BUDGET 2WEALTH 2STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorIt was a Saturday afternoon, my first break from work in what felt like years, and the weather couldn't have been more perfect. Trying to keep my mind off the upheaval sparked by the Circas assassination, I lit the backyard grill.
NarratorMonica and Deana were out shopping for craft supplies, and I'd given the staff the day off. It was just myself and Franc at home.
NarratorI wasn't sure how he felt about me at the moment. But I did know how he felt about barbecued ribs. Our cook had left some marinating in the refrigerator.
NarratorSure enough, as soon as Franc glimpsed the grill and the meat, he stepped outside to join me.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:11:27 p15704 / c401
  • ·"Well, look who's finally out of his room."
  • ▶"Come to spend some quality time with your old man, have you?"
Option 1 reflects a warm, fatherly tone fitting for a relaxed backyard setting with family.
DeepSeek-R1"Come to spend some quality time with your old man, have you?"
Franc"Sure, if you can spare a few minutes out of your busy schedule."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:12:10 p15851 / c395
  • ·“Children who talk back to their fathers don't get dinner. Fan these flames, will you?”
  • ·"Look, son, I'm preoccupied with matters of national importance."
  • ▶"I'm sorry, I know I haven't been around very often lately. But I'm happy to see you now."
Anton chooses a kind and understanding response to connect with his son despite his busy schedule.
DeepSeek-R1"I'm sorry, I know I haven't been around very often lately. But I'm happy to see you now."
Franc"It's okay. I know you've got the most stressful job in the country. And you've got to feed your family on top of it all..."
NarratorWe both chuckled. I turned to tend to the fire.
Franc"Here, let me try."
NarratorHe grabbed the fan and started quickly waving it over the coals. The fire roared to life.
NarratorHe turned to look at me with a grin on his face, as if he was expecting praise.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:12:58 p15807 / c395
  • ·“Good job.”
  • ▶“Not bad, son, not bad.”
  • ·“Don't act like you've done something special, Franc.”
  • ·Say nothing.
Anton wants to encourage Franc positively while maintaining a warm father-son interaction.
DeepSeek-R1"Not bad, son, not bad."
Franc"Thank you, sir. It is in our utmost interest to fulfill our duties with precision. The grilling shall commence in exactly 1 minute and 32 seconds."
NarratorThat was a spot-on impersonation of Lucian. We looked at each other for a second, then burst out laughing.
NarratorI realized my throat was parched. There was a six-pack of beer in the kitchen.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:13:38 p15778 / c295
  • ·"Franc, can you get me a beer?"
  • ▶"Why don't you bring us a couple of beers?"
  • ·Get up and grab a beer.
Suggesting a collaborative effort to get beers maintains a relaxed and friendly atmosphere.
DeepSeek-R1"Why don't you bring us a couple of beers?"
NarratorHe looked at me in disbelief.
Franc"You mean, I can--? But Mom told me--"
NarratorHe stopped himself, quickly ran inside and returned with two beers. We cracked them open, clinked the bottles and gazed out at the city skyline.
NarratorFor a moment, my thoughts flashed back to my childhood and my relationship with my own father.
NarratorMy father was a hard-working man and always had our best interests in mind -- or at least, that's what he always said. But he had a temper, and would lash out at my mother in fights I could hear from my bedroom. By the time I was Franc's age, we seldom spoke.
NarratorNow that I was the president, I was facing more stress than my father ever had. I would have to work hard not to take my frustrations out on my family.
Franc"Dad, can I ask you something?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:14:20 p15804 / c318
  • ·“Go on.”
  • ▶“We are family. You can ask anything.”
  • ·Wait for him to continue.
Encouraging an open dialogue by reassuring Franc he can ask anything.
DeepSeek-R1"We are family. You can ask anything."
Franc"It's just... I feel like there's so much you've been hiding from me. About your past, and about what's going on right now."
Franc"Like at dinner that night after the ball. You told me everything was under control, but it wasn't, was it?"
Franc"I know what happened in the '20s -- we learned all about it in school. Now, with the protests and the riots, is it going to be the same thing?"
Franc"Dad, is there going to be a civil war in Sordland?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:15:03 p15753 / c353
  • ·“I hope not.”
  • ▶“No. I will never let that happen.”
  • ·Say nothing and avoid his question.
Anton Rayne, as a determined leader, reassures his son by confidently stating he will prevent civil war.
DeepSeek-R1"No. I will never let that happen."
Franc"Good."
Franc"But... what was it like, back then? Uncle Petr said the two of you went through a lot..."
Franc"I'm not a kid anymore. You can tell me."
NarratorBefore I could gather my thoughts, the familiar images started flashing before my eyes.
NarratorImages of soldiers advancing towards me and my friends, weapons drawn. Of people I knew -- friends and neighbors -- being dragged through streets and murdered in alleyways.
NarratorThe shock and grief on the faces of fathers, mothers and orphaned children.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:15:45 p15820 / c422
  • ▶“Those were dark times.”
  • ·“I don't want to talk about it.”
Anton wants to acknowledge the past without burdening Franc, providing reassurance while maintaining honesty.
DeepSeek-R1"Those were dark times."
Franc"It seems like it."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:16:26 p15772 / c456
  • ·“But you're nearly a grown man. Let's talk about it.”
  • ▶“Let's change the subject.”
Changing the subject would help maintain Franc's peace of mind and keep the conversation light-hearted.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's change the subject."
Franc"It's okay. I'm sorry. I shouldn't have asked you about it."
Franc"I know I don't understand how easy my life is sometimes."
Franc"One more thing, though -- I know that you joined Red Youth back then. Do you mind if I ask why?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:17:13 p15778 / c557
  • ▶“Yes, I do mind. I don't want to talk about it.”
  • ·“A communist revolution for Sordland was and still is the only way.”
  • ·“I had to survive somehow in that environment, and I made a choice.”
Anton prefers to avoid discussing past affiliations that could be politically sensitive.
DeepSeek-R1"Yes, I do mind. I don't want to talk about it."
Franc"Okay. I was just curious, that's all."
Franc"Sorry for bombarding you with questions."
Franc"I know I complain sometimes, but my problems are nothing compared to what you went through."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:17:56 p15578 / c435
  • ·"I'm here to listen to them, no matter what they are."
  • ·“What problems?”
  • ▶"You're damn right. Your generation will never understand."
Anton wants to avoid discussing sensitive past topics with his son.
DeepSeek-R1"You're damn right. Your generation will never understand."
NarratorBefore Franc could say anything else, I heard the front door unlock. Deana and Monica's voices and the sounds of rustling shopping bags filled the house.
Franc"Alright. I'll be upstairs until dinner."
NarratorFranc went in and dashed upstairs.
NarratorI took a long sip from my beer, looking at the city and the now-setting sun.

■ Personal Driver TalkT03

BUDGET 2WEALTH 2STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorI was travelling to the snow-covered city of Narbel in the Nargis region for the rural development forum organized by the Ministry of Health and the Ministry of Education.
NarratorThe mountainous city was infamously one of Sordland's poorest. It had a gritty edge, and its people were hardy and wary.
NarratorEven after the discovery of gas and oil in the area, years of neglect by the central government were apparent on the buildings and the general infrastructure. The natural resources were now under the control of Gasom, which elevated the corporation to a place of power.
NarratorMy task in this forum was mainly symbolic. Fake smiles and handshakes with oil barons, meeting with local politicians, but most importantly of all, making sure that Narbel did not feel like it was forgotten by the government.
NarratorThe scenery so far, however, was a reflection of Narbel's neglect. The main roads to the city were not maintained well. There were many bumps and empty discolored spots in the asphalt.
NarratorNavigating and swerving to avoid the inconveniences, our motorcade finally started nearing the city.
NarratorAs if my discomfort from the bumpy ride was apparent to him, Serge rolled down the partition window.
Serge Wolkner"We will be arriving at Hotel West in a few minutes, sir."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:18:58 p15635 / c505
  • ·“Thank you for letting me know.”
  • ▶“Can't say it's been a comfortable ride, Serge.”
  • ·“Good. Whatever gets us out of here quickly.”
Anton Rayne acknowledges the discomfort of the ride truthfully but maintains a professional demeanor.
DeepSeek-R1"Can't say it's been a comfortable ride, Serge."
Serge Wolkner"I am sorry sir. The roads here aren't the best."
NarratorAfter a moment, Serge started to smile under his moustache.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:19:36 p15622 / c325
  • ·“What's that smile for?”
  • ·“How is life, Serge?”
  • ▶Pretend not to notice.
Anton prefers to focus on his agenda rather than engage in small talk during an already uncomfortable journey.
DeepSeek-R1I pretended not to notice and looked out the window.
Serge Wolkner"Sir, I just want to say... It has been great these last two months."
Serge Wolkner"As you know, my wife Suzan recently gave birth to our son. And now my daughter just started at a very good high school in Holsord."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:20:14 p15723 / c252
  • ·“Get ready, Serge. Because life is about to become a lot busier.”
  • ·“That's great news, Serge. I'm happy for you.”
  • ▶“Congratulations.”
Expressing genuine congratulations is the most appropriate response to Serge's personal update.
DeepSeek-R1"Congratulations."
Serge Wolkner"I appreciate it, sir. Truly. I was worried that I wouldn't be able to afford a good private school if she scored less in the entrance exams."
Serge Wolkner"But I shouldn't have made my insecurity get to me. Erica outsmarts me all the time. I'm very proud to have a daughter like her."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:20:55 p15729 / c263
  • ▶“I also hope that Deana will grow to be like her mother someday.”
  • ·“So that means she must have gotten the smarts from her mother.”
  • ·“I do not value insecurity in my employees, Serge.”
A kind and supportive response that acknowledges Serge's pride in his daughter and connects it to his own family.
DeepSeek-R1"I too hope that Deana will grow to be like her mother someday."
Serge Wolkner"I'm sure she will sir. After all she is the daughter of a lion and a lioness."
Serge Wolkner"My daughter looks up to the First Lady for inspiration and it's not just limited to my daughter, sir."
Serge Wolkner"She is an inspiration for almost all women in the country."
Serge Wolkner"But at the same time, it must be hard for the First Lady as well."
Serge Wolkner"All this attention, adjusting to this new high-profile life, and a husband that has great responsibility..."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:21:40 p15734 / c390
  • ·“That is none of your business.”
  • ·“Let's not talk about my family, Serge.”
  • ·“You are right. It is hard for her.”
  • ▶“It is hard for both of us.”
Anton recognizes the difficulty both he and Monica face as high-profile parents.
DeepSeek-R1"It is hard for both of us."
Serge Wolkner"It must be sir. We sometimes forget the important people in life between all the responsibility and rush."
Serge Wolkner"You know... Those we care for..."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:22:21 p15586 / c346

  • ·“I agree.”
  • ▶“Yes, Serge. I get it...”
The response is empathetic and acknowledges Serge's understanding of their challenges, maintaining a positive tone.
DeepSeek-R1"Yes, Serge. I get it..."
Serge Wolkner"Of course, sir."
Serge Wolkner"Yes sir. I am sorry sir."
NarratorThere was a long silence. After several minutes, Serge attempted to break silence.
Serge Wolkner"Have I told you, sir? We named my son Georg."
Serge Wolkner"The doctors said he is very healthy and thankfully so is my wife."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:23:04 p15583 / c369

  • ·“That's good. I hope the hospital had a good service.”

Asking about Suzan allows for deeper personal connection and shows interest in Serge's family.
DeepSeek-R1"Tell me more about Suzan. What kind of a person is she?"
Serge Wolkner"She has a good heart. Her family comes from Sarna, so they are a little more traditional."
Serge Wolkner"She's beautiful, of course. But what I really admire is her selflessness."

DeepSeek-R1"Selflessness is a respectable trait to have."
Serge Wolkner"It should be mutual, that is what matters most."
NarratorSerge sighed.
Serge Wolkner"I've already started thinking about their university education. Especially Erica."
Serge Wolkner"I want to send her to a good private school but with the current state of the economy, it's going to be hard for us."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:24:39 p15663 / c636

  • ·“It's hard for everyone.”
Anton Rayne chooses to comprehensively support Serge Wolkner's family, covering education and other expenses, to strengthen their loyalty and demonstrate his compassionate leadership.
DeepSeek-R1"My driver's family shouldn't want for anything in this world. I'll cover your children's education and any other extra expenses you need."
Serge Wolkner"Mr. President, there is no way I can--."
NarratorWith one look from me, Serge cut his sentence short and nodded at me after removing his hat.
Serge Wolkner"Mr. President, I don't know how I can ever repay you for this."
Serge Wolkner"Thank you so much."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:25:28 p15606 / c426

  • ·“I hope you return the favor by helping someone in need someday. Just like I did.”
  • ▶“No worries, Serge.”

DeepSeek-R1"No worries, Serge."
Serge Wolkner"I think I have decided. I will give your name to my boy. "
Serge Wolkner"Georg Anton Wolkner."
Serge Wolkner"May he grow strong like you."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:26:05 p15473 / c255

  • ·Nod.
Expressing approval of the name choice in a warm manner.
DeepSeek-R1"It has a nice ring to it. I like it."
NarratorThe car hit a major pothole and a bump which lifted us up in our seats for a second.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:26:41 p15443 / c250
  • ▶“This is unacceptable. We need to invest in infrastructure here.”
  • ·“Are you fine?”
Anton would focus on systemic issues like infrastructure to show his commitment to improving rural areas.
DeepSeek-R1"This is unacceptable. We need to invest in infrastructure here."
Serge Wolkner"I agree sir. We still need to drive back tomorrow unless you use one of those new helicopters."
NarratorThe motorcade began approaching the hotel.
NarratorThe Hotel West was supposedly the best hotel in Narbel. The large 25-story main building was undoubtedly one of the taller and more expensive buildings in the city. It towered over the nearby slums and had been a target for protests when it was first built.
NarratorA crowd was gathered in front of the hotel with the welcoming committee at its centre. As we approached the red carpeted entrance I could see the Mayor of Narbel and his top aides.
NarratorSerge got out of the car and opened the door. He bowed his head respectfully and gestured towards the entrance.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:27:15 p15427 / c180
  • ·Exit the vehicle.
  • ▶“Thank you.”
  • ·“See you later, Serge.”
A simple 'thank you' is the most appropriate response after receiving a gift.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you."
Serge Wolkner"Have a good day, Mr. President."
NarratorAs soon as I left the vehicle, the fresh air of Narbel filled my lungs.
NarratorAlmost immediately, everyone present in the crowd flocked over to me with an excessive display of courtesy, smiles and handshakes while I donned the mask of a politician.
NarratorA mask that I was very used to.

■ OverviewT03

BUDGET 2WEALTH 0STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorI arrived at the meeting room for my talk with Ciara and Paskal. Before beginning, I took a moment to appreciate the view from the balcony. The mountains of Narbel were completely covered with snow.
NarratorWhile I was mesmerized by the scenery, Paskal Beniwoll walked up to me.
NarratorMy Minister of Health was, ironically, somewhat portly, having gained a few pounds since his breakthrough as a best-selling writer. But his authority on social affairs could not be questioned.
Paskal Beniwoll"Such a spectacular view."
NarratorCiara Walda, the Minister of Education, joined us on the balcony, dressed in a sky-blue pantsuit. Her preference for trousers over dresses had made her the subject of much Palace gossip, but it didn't seem to bother her in the slightest.
Ciara Walda"Spectacular indeed. But if you look in the opposite direction, you will see what Soll's decades of neglect did to the city."
Ciara Walda"Most of the people living in Narbel are workers, farmers and their wives and children. They're breaking their backs for close to no pay, all thanks to greedy corporations."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:28:25 p15279 / c601
  • ▶“My intention is to help the working class.”
  • ·“Whatever. I'd much rather be in Holsord right now.”
  • ·“We can't really blame the companies. That's just how they work.”
Expressing commitment to helping the working class aligns with policy goals and shows leadership.
DeepSeek-R1"My intention is to help the working class."
Ciara Walda"I am glad we see eye to eye on the issue."
Ciara Walda"If you look past the view, you can see the real problems."
Ciara Walda"Real problems like poverty."
NarratorPaskal nodded gravely. This was a subject he knew quite a bit about himself. His best-known books were about the plight of Sordland's less fortunate, drawing from his own past growing up in squalor.
Paskal Beniwoll"Well put, Ms. Walda. I don't need to tell you about my own experiences with poverty. I'm sure you have similar stories, am I right, Mr. President?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:29:09 p15217 / c411
  • ·“We have all gone through difficult times.”
  • ▶“We knew less fortunate families, even though we belonged to the middle class ourselves.”
Anton Rayne's response emphasizes shared understanding of hardship despite different backgrounds, connecting his middle-class roots to empathy for less fortunate families.
DeepSeek-R1"We knew less fortunate families, even though we belonged to the middle class ourselves."
Paskal Beniwoll"So, you could at least sympathize with the plight of these people."
Ciara Walda"You don't have to have been born poor to sympathize with the plight of the impoverished. Much as you don't have to have been born a woman to recognize Sordland's need for parity among the sexes."
Paskal Beniwoll"Parity among the sexes shouldn't be our first priority. Look at how many high-ranking female politicians our country has."
Ciara Walda"Appearances can be deceiving, Paskal."
NarratorHer voice sounded bitter.
Ciara Walda"Think about how Lileas Graf got to her current position. I don't necessarily believe the rumors about her and Soll, but she never could have risen so high if she didn't parrot his cause."
Ciara Walda"Gloria Tory -- is it an accident that the first female Assembly speaker is such a staunch conservative?"
Ciara Walda"And then there was Alphonso's habit of promoting women to prominent positions to give Sordland a progressive sheen while accomplishing nothing in reality..."
Ciara Walda"While women in places like Narbel are denied any opportunity to advance, the government can use a handful of female politicians from wealthy families -- myself included -- to pretend their only obstacle to success is their own lack of initiative."
Ciara Walda"Meanwhile, those of us who don't use our clout to help powerful men stay in power are denigrated as angry spinsters... I ask you, Mr. President, does anyone care that your strategist Mr. Galade doesn't have a wife?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:29:48 p15564 / c266
  • ·"I've been wondering about that, actually."
  • ·"Leave Lucian out of this."
  • ·"You've veered off-topic, Ms. Walda."
  • ▶"I agree with you completely. Sordland's women deserve better."
Agreeing with Ciara's point highlights the ongoing issues faced by women in Sordland and aligns with the narrative of reform and equality.
DeepSeek-R1"I agree with you completely. Sordland's women deserve better."
Ciara Walda"I'm glad to hear that."
Ciara Walda"As I was saying -- isn't it about time Holsord addressed this country's real issues? Rather than bowing to the wishes of the hawks and fearmongerers in the establishment, and diverting yet more resources to the military and law enforcement?"
Ciara Walda"Our welfare, healthcare and education systems have been decaying since the recession. Sordland's poorer communities are losing hope."
Ciara Walda"Hopelessness and a lack of opportunities can drive people to extreme solutions. We're seeing increased crime, domestic violence, and yes, rising inequality between men and women..."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:30:38 p15490 / c552
  • ·“Lileas Graf will take care of crime. She's an expert on the subject."
  • ▶“Those issues must be tackled from different directions. We need to engage it holistically.”
  • ·“If only we could stop the recession, create jobs and growth...”
  • ·“The capital must be prioritised towards the resources that matter the most.”
A holistic approach addresses the interconnected social and economic issues Ciara raised.
DeepSeek-R1"Those issues must be tackled from different directions. We need to engage it holistically."
Paskal Beniwoll"I'm planning to help by using an increased budget to improve the quality of healthcare."
Ciara Walda"That could prove useful, but we need to be doing a lot more. Education has similar issues."
Paskal Beniwoll"There is another subject I want to mention. I have been working on improving the rights of workers in our country and have proposed a draft bill that is currently being reviewed by our party."
Paskal Beniwoll"Mr. Clavin has already backed me and given his support. Sordland has fallen behind most countries on this subject and it is my responsibility to ensure this is not the case."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:31:20 p15554 / c320
  • ▶“I agree. We need to improve the standards for our workers.”
  • ·“There is always room for improvement and it is good to hear that you are pushing for it.”
  • ·“We shouldn't upset the businesses who are already struggling to stay afloat in the crisis.”
  • ·“Sounds like a costly bill for such a low-priority issue."
Supporting worker rights aligns with broader social reforms and shows commitment to improving societal conditions.
DeepSeek-R1"I agree. We need to improve the standards for our workers."
Paskal Beniwoll"Exactly. Sordland must provide for its own people to move forward."
Ciara Walda"It also is a matter of life and death. Every decade we hear of some horrific accident due to employers' disregard for their workers' safety."
Paskal Beniwoll"So, will you back the bill when it arrives on your desk, Mr. President?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:31:58 p15602 / c317

Anton Rayne would want to evaluate the details of the worker rights bill carefully to determine if it aligns with his priorities and has sufficient support.

Paskal Beniwoll"Understandable. You will receive all the sections in an outline."
Paskal Beniwoll"It's getting a little cold out here. Shall we continue our discussion indoors?"
NarratorWe headed back inside.
NarratorThe meeting room was already prepared for state business. Small gifts for each of us had been placed on the table by the municipality. We took our seats.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:32:40 p15521 / c353
  • ·“Let's start with your health overview, Paskal.”
  • ▶“Tell me more about your plans, Ciara.”
  • ·"No need for the briefing, I'm up to speed."

DeepSeek-R1"Tell me more about your plans, Ciara."
Ciara Walda"With pleasure."
Ciara Walda"Sordish education is free, but we have a very outdated system that I want to reform. The other important issue is the lack of access to education in rural areas, especially for young girls. Your administration has the power to solve both problems."
Ciara Walda"My highest priority is to get enough funding to be able to build schools in rural areas while I cleanse our educational system of its nationalistic indoctrination and sexist teachings."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:33:19 p15515 / c288
  • ·“How many students and teachers do we have?”
  • ·“What is the literacy rate of Sordland?”

The third option directly relates to Ciara's point about rural education access.
DeepSeek-R1"Tell me about the difference between urban and rural education."
Ciara Walda"Urban areas have three times the number of schools per ten thousand people compared to rural areas. Rural areas also suffer from a lack of teachers."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:33:52 p15504 / c157

The ratio highlights a significant disparity that needs addressing.

Ciara Walda"This is one of the most important problems we face, if not the most important. A huge part of the population is forgotten in terms of welfare."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:34:30 p15440 / c301

DeepSeek-R1"Thank you, Ciara. I can fill in the blanks from here."
“I want to hear about the healthcare system.”
DeepSeek-R1"I want to hear about the healthcare system."
Paskal Beniwoll"As you wish, Mr. President."
Paskal Beniwoll"Sordland has a free healthcare system, except for a few private hospitals operating under it. Most of the populace receives adequate treatment. Health issues primarily appear in rural areas due to a lack of quality services."
Paskal Beniwoll"I am doing my best to ensure that citizens of all ages receive the best healthcare they can. I also personally want to solve our high infant and maternal mortality problem."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:35:16 p15334 / c444

DeepSeek-R1"How many doctors and nurses are employed?"
Paskal Beniwoll"We have 31,594 doctors and 73,680 nurses working for the Ministry of Health. This is a high number. What I think is--"
Ciara Walda"I don't want to be rude, but out of those numbers. how many are in urban and in rural areas?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:35:58 p15336 / c365

Paskal Beniwoll"The data shows that per ten thousand people in urban areas there are about eleven doctors, while in rural areas there are only three."
Ciara Walda"In other words, rural citizens with medical emergencies have to wait an embarassingly long time before they are treated."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:36:39 p15353 / c364

DeepSeek-R1"What's the life expectancy and mortality rates?"
Paskal Beniwoll"Our life expectancy is 65 years. The infant mortality rate is a worrying 121 per one thousand births, while our maternal mortality is at 5 per thousand."
Paskal Beniwoll"I assure you that we are doing everything we can to save mothers and their newborns."
Ciara Walda"Those numbers are saddening indeed."

Paskal Beniwoll"We are working very hard on improving the quality of the services."

DeepSeek-R1"How many hospital beds are there per ten thousand citizens?"
Paskal Beniwoll"There are ten beds per ten thousand citizens. Compared to most of our neighbors, this is a very good number."
Paskal Beniwoll"Our amount of beds is higher than the countries of Agnolia and Wehlen, but we obviously can't match Lespia or Valgsland."
Ciara Walda"Agnolia and Wehlen should hardly be used as a standard, but it is good to know we aren't in a massive health crisis. Yet."
“Thank you, Paskal. Your in-depth briefing is much appreciated.”
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you, Paskal. Your in-depth briefing is much appreciated."
“I believe that is everything.”
DeepSeek-R1"I believe that is everything."
NarratorCiara stood up and moved towards the window. She took a deep breath.

Ciara Walda"Look at this impoverished city. The streets full of potholes, the hospital barely functioning, the school half open."
Ciara Walda"This is not just about Soll or even Alphonso failing. This decay in Sordland's forgotten regions has been going on for many decades due to structural corruption, which is fueled by capitalism."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:39:12 p15341 / c196

Ciara Walda"That remains to be seen. I'm working on the new education system, and your support would be welcome."
NarratorCiara sat back down and sighed.
Ciara Walda"One way or another, we need change in Sordland. Greed and unchecked capitalism will not magically provide for the people."

DeepSeek-R1"Tell me more about your plans, Ciara."
Ciara Walda"My plans require an increase in the government budget. I aim to solve the problems we highlighted with the allocated money."
Ciara Walda"By building schools in these less fortunate rural areas and through fundamental changes to the education system, I will unlock the potential of all our children. Boys and girls alike."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:40:32 p15318 / c273
  • ·“Improving the situation in rural areas is indeed a good idea.”
  • ▶“Our election mandate was to improve education. We will deliver on this.”
  • ·"Interesting. I thought our problem was with the quality of education in Sordland as a whole.”
Anton Rayne emphasizes delivering on election promises, making option 1 the best fit as it aligns with his focus on fulfilling campaign goals.
DeepSeek-R1"Our election mandate was to improve education. We will deliver on this."
Ciara Walda"This brings hope to the table, which needs to be followed by action. There is much to be done, but if we're given the budget, we can implement policies that push education to the next level."
Paskal Beniwoll"Wouldn't a promotion of private education help create additional funds?"
Ciara Walda"Yes, but at what cost? I'm not a supporter of the private sector in education. At the end of the day, they are focused on profit first."
“What are you working towards, Paskal?”
DeepSeek-R1"What are you working towards, Paskal?"
Paskal Beniwoll"I personally want to improve the low quality of healthcare in the rural areas."
Paskal Beniwoll"So, I created draft plans to increase the salary of doctors and to upgrade the equipment of the hospitals."
Paskal Beniwoll"I can do more with an increased budget. Additionally, a privatization plan to promote private investments in the healthcare system could allocate extra funds."

DeepSeek-R1"Isn't access to healthcare in rural areas an issue?"
Paskal Beniwoll"Access is an issue, but not as critical as improving the quality in these areas. The lack of experienced personnel and equipment causes poor treatment, resulting in many deaths."
Paskal Beniwoll"I do hope to create competition and increase the quality of healthcare with the privatization effort."
Ciara Walda"But you must realize that privatizing our health care system would increase the price of treatment and make access worse for the average citizen."
Ciara Walda"Besides, it wouldn't fit into the planned economy President Rayne has decided to promote."
Ciara Walda"My expectation from our government is that we understand and focus on the needs of the people."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:41:44 p15404 / c354
  • ▶“We are elected representatives of our people and we will prioritize their needs.”
  • ·“There are other significant issues that we need to focus on, like security.”
  • ·“I appreciate your briefings and plans. We will re-convene at the budget meeting.”
  • ·“Minister Walda, I have bailed out people in need instead of businesses. Trust me, my priorities are correct.”
This choice aligns with Anton Rayne's commitment to a planned economy and public welfare, directly addressing Ciara Walda's concerns about prioritizing people over profit.
DeepSeek-R1"We are elected representatives of our people and we will prioritize their needs."
Paskal Beniwoll"That's reassuring to hear."
Ciara Walda"I don't want to sound cynical, but almost all governments keep making the same promises. We must show we are different through our actions."
Ciara Walda"Did we cover all the necessary subjects?"
Paskal Beniwoll"One more thing. I wish to raise my concerns about your scheduled meeting with the Gasom chairman and former President, Mr. Alphonso."
Paskal Beniwoll"By negotiating with Alphonso and his company, you have an opportunity to secure better labor conditions for blue-collar Gasom employees. This could be a triumph for all of Narbel."
Ciara Walda"What is there to negotiate, Paskal? The standards for Gasom workers are already at rock bottom. President Rayne can and must demand better."
Paskal Beniwoll"Nevertheless, it's worth acknowledging the corporation's significant contribution to job creation in Narbel and other poverty-stricken regions. Persuading the chairman to make concessions might prove to be a formidable task."

  • ·"If we do this right, we can find a solution that advances both the economy and the interests of laborers."
Anton Rayne emphasizes the importance of fair labor conditions, ensuring that job creation does not come at the expense of worker welfare.
DeepSeek-R1"What good is job creation if the workers are treated poorly? Of course I'll demand better from Alphonso."
Paskal Beniwoll"I covered this topic extensively in one of my books. Confrontation, if not handled prudently, could exacerbate the challenges these workers face."
Paskal Beniwoll"This matter is close to my heart. I would personally be grateful if you are able to resolve the concerns raised by Katarina Horten and her fellow labor representatives."
Ciara Walda"It would be difficult to imagine a CEO like Alphonso giving benefits to workers without him personally gaining from it."

A compromise approach recognizes mutual reliance between Sordland and Gasom while addressing labor concerns.

Ciara Walda"The middle way often sways perilously close to the status quo. We must ensure that any negotiations genuinely benefit the people and not just uphold the interests of corporate giants."
Paskal Beniwoll"Thank you for your input, Ciara. But I will stress to President Rayne how important it is that the door to dialogue remain open."
Paskal Beniwoll"Ideology should never get in the way of bringing about tangible, lasting change."

Paskal Beniwoll"We shall keep you no longer. Thank you both for the discussions."
Ciara Walda"I do hope we won't repeat the same mistakes again and again. Have a good day, Mr. President."
NarratorThe ministers left, and I prepared to leave for other official business in the city. The Gasom meeting was just one of the many events I was scheduled to attend over the next few days.
NarratorAs I made my way out, the weather was noticeably cold, but it didn’t keep a group of USP supporters from coming out to cheer us on the streets.
NarratorBy looking at the citizens’ attire, it was easy to understand the current state of Narbel. Some people were wearing clothes a few sizes too large. Some kids did not even have shoes. Yet there was hope in their eyes.
NarratorThey were excited to see their president in their city.

■ Gasom ProposalT03

BUDGET 2WEALTH 0STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorAfter a series of intense meetings with Ciara and Paskal, I decided to take some much-needed time to recharge.
NarratorI checked in with Lucian and Petr back at the capital. They were working hard to unite democratically leaning reformers for the crucial task of drafting a new constitution.
NarratorMeanwhile, Gus Manger had invited me to visit the Gasom headquarters in Narbel. It was situated in Ornobel, a neighborhood on the city outskirts.
NarratorNext to it was the Damenoy gas reserve, a prominent natural gas source consisting of four large fields.
NarratorAs our motorcade made its way through the Ornobel district, I noted the neighborhood's state of disrepair.
NarratorThe roads were bumpy and marked by potholes, while the buildings surrounded us appeared run-down and dilapidated. The community had apparently endured a prolonged period of neglect.
NarratorWe came to a halt at the entrance gates of the facility. A sizeable mural depicting the Gasom logo had been painted on a nearby wall.
NarratorAdjacent to the mural, a group of individuals wearing blue work vests had assembled, holding what looked like protest signs.
NarratorSerge skillfully parked the car, bringing our journey to a smooth and elegant conclusion. Gus greeted us warmly as we stepped out of the vehicle.
Gus Manger"Good day, President Rayne. Welcome to Gasom's Narbel operations!"
Gus Manger"I trust Ministers Walda and Beniwoll didn't overwhelm you with their demands."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:44:32 p15384 / c387
  • ·"It's a relief to have a break from all that, even if just for a little while."
  • ·“I would be surprised if there are no demands or expectations here today.”
  • ▶“Good to see you, Gus. I wanted to talk to you about what we can do to help the development here.”
  • ·"Not exactly the most pleasant location for a meeting, is it?"
Expressing willingness to discuss development aligns with proactive leadership.
DeepSeek-R1"Good to see you, Gus. I wanted to talk to you about what we can do to help the development here."
Gus Manger"Perfect. That's exactly what I hope to accomplish during today's discussions. My job is to improve the conditions of places like these."
Gus Manger"Mr. President, I've been brainstorming a little something. The Gruni Rural Investment Plan. I reckon it's got the potential to breathe new life into our rural communities out in Gruni."
Gus Manger"We could consider a few things. For starters, we could give the farming business a boost with some subsidies. Or, we could steer towards modernizing existing farms with machinery and tech."
Gus Manger"Or here's another thought - we could pour resources into developing crop breeds tailor-made for Gruni conditions. If it works out, we could roll this out across Sordland."
NarratorHe kept talking as he guided me into the Gasom building.
Gus Manger"Mr. President, I get the appeal of that GREEN bill that's been making the rounds in the Assembly."
Gus Manger"But I'm telling you, if we put the brakes on that and funnel those funds into the Gruni plan instead, we could make some real strides without spooking any potential investors."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:45:31 p15607 / c904
  • ▶"Gus, I'm intrigued by your vision. I'll seriously consider your proposal."
  • ·"It's a tough call, Gus. I need to weigh the environmental implications and the economic benefits."
  • ·"I have my doubts, Gus. But I'll give your plan some thought."
Gus Manger's proposal offers a practical approach to boosting rural areas, which aligns with the administration's goal of supporting neglected regions and improving local economies.
DeepSeek-R1"Gus, I'm intrigued by your vision. I'll seriously consider your proposal."
Gus Manger"I appreciate you hearing me out, Mr. President. I genuinely believe this plan could work wonders for our rural folks."
NarratorOur talk was interrupted by a noise coming from outside the building. We looked out the nearest window.
NarratorA sleek helicopter was cutting through the sky, sunlight glinting off its polished surface as it descended towards us.
NarratorOn it was our next appointment, Ewald Alphonso.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:46:06 p15696 / c314
  • ·"Is that a helicopter or just his ego with rotor blades?"
  • ·"Alphonso sure knows how to make an entrance."
  • ·"An extravagant show of wealth, isn't it? Hardly seems necessary."
  • ▶"That kind of display plays well to investors... but terribly to farmers watching on state TV."
Pointing out the helicopter's display as excessive and disconnected from rural concerns aligns with Rayne's focus on practical issues.
DeepSeek-R1"That kind of display plays well to investors... but terribly to farmers watching on state TV."
Gus Manger"Exactly. The optics matter. We just have to decide who we’re trying to impress."
Gus Manger"Spectacle aside… before we sit down with him, there's one more Assembly matter I wanted to raise."
Gus Manger"This Workers' Rights Bill... Look, I'm all for giving hard-working folks what they deserve. But, isn't it a bit hasty to push this while we're still stuck in a recession?"
Gus Manger"The way I see it, short-term wins might end up costing us big time in the long run."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:46:47 p15782 / c442

  • ·"In my book, workers' rights aren't up for negotiation, no matter the economic climate."
  • ▶"Heard a worker from Estord on the radio. Said he's got rights on paper, but nothing in his fridge."
Using a relatable anecdote highlights the necessity of addressing workers' needs beyond just rights.
DeepSeek-R1"Heard a worker from Estord on the radio. Said he's got rights on paper, but nothing in his fridge."
Gus Manger"That kind of thing sticks with you. Still, we’ve got to make sure the fixes don’t break something bigger."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:47:30 p15866 / c524
  • ·"If we overload businesses now, they'll shrink or shut down. Workers lose more than just rights, they lose jobs."
  • ·"Strong labor protections are the foundation of lasting growth. A secure worker is a productive one."
  • ▶"Let’s think of the option that signals progress without rattling the markets. We need both stability and reform."
  • ·"People don’t care about long-term strategy when their kids are hungry now. We fix this for them, or we lose them."
Choosing stability while pushing for reform ensures economic balance and maintains investor confidence.
DeepSeek-R1"Let’s think of the option that signals progress without rattling the markets. We need both stability and reform."
Gus Manger"That’s the sweet spot. You bring the idealism, I’ll help shape the rollout."
Gus Manger"Please come this way. Ewald and I have some exciting news to share with you."
Gus Manger"I have faith that you won't leave us hanging."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:48:06 p15796 / c339
