■ Convince AlbinT04

BUDGET -12WEALTH 0STABILITY 1Danger
NarratorAfter our meeting with Gloria Tory, and further discussions with the party and my cabinet, it was time to talk to Albin Clavin to get the reformist wing to support the proposal.
NarratorI also planned to finally present a more fleshed out proposal and declare our agenda to the party after our meeting.
NarratorI walked into the left wing of the Assembly and entered the quarters of the USP. When I reached the meeting hall, I saw many of our members conversing inside.
NarratorAfter taking a glimpse inside, I went back into the corridor to look for the office of Albin Clavin, which was around the back of the place.
NarratorWhen I reached his room, I knocked on his door and entered.
Albin Clavin"Mr. President! Welcome!"
NarratorThe whole room was surrounded by bookshelves packed with files and papers. It was a small office, with books and dossiers lying on every corner. It felt claustrophobic.
NarratorOn his gesture, I took a seat.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:28:09 p14710 / c395
  • ·"How is everything going, Mr. Clavin?"
  • ▶"Did a bomb drop in this office?"
The office's cluttered state suggests a bomb could have dropped in terms of paperwork.
DeepSeek-R1"Did a bomb drop in this office?"
NarratorHe laughed.
Albin Clavin"You could say that."
Albin Clavin"So much research and preparation needs to be done on the plans we're working on."
Albin Clavin"How is everything going for you, Mr. President?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:28:43 p14816 / c351
  • ·"It's pretty tough."
  • ·"I'm feeling pretty good."
  • ▶"It depends on our meeting today."
The success of the reforms depends on the reformist wing's support, making this meeting crucial for determining the president's feelings.
DeepSeek-R1"It depends on our meeting today."
Albin Clavin"Oh, I see. Then I'll try my best not to make it any worse for you, Mr. President."
Albin Clavin"I wanted to express my gratitude for passing the Workers' Rights Act. It is one of my most comprehensive and successful political efforts."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:29:26 p14866 / c626
  • ·"The worker's revolution will begin with small steps."
  • ▶"It was the right thing to do because our workers were suffering for decades."
  • ·"The party effort had to be supported."

It emphasizes the moral correctness of the act, showing empathy and leadership without overstepping into more controversial territory.
DeepSeek-R1"It was the right thing to do because our workers were suffering for decades."
Albin Clavin"Indeed the people will credit your administration and the party for a positive change in their lives."
Albin Clavin"The businesses will share the burden with the state but it is a change they have to abide by."
NarratorAlbin smiled and knocked on wood.
Albin Clavin"Anyway, sir, let's talk about the proposal. Thanks for coming here for my input because there are some subjects where Gloria and I don't see eye to eye."
Albin Clavin"I have to say that most of the progressives were already starting to warm up to you. Your attempt at changing the constitution created quite a bit of excitement. One could dare to say an excitement that hasn't been felt since the 30's..."
Albin Clavin"I've read the proposal that was finalised together with Mrs. Tory. And surprisingly, it came out pretty good."
Albin Clavin"Look, Mr. President. I'm on the same side as you."
Albin Clavin"I won't create any problems."

  • ·"Thank you, Mr. Clavin. Any help from you is much appreciated."

NarratorHe looked down at the proposal.
Albin Clavin"It's looking good."
Albin Clavin"I will support the proposal and get the needed signatures from our wing."
Albin Clavin"I believe the reformist votes that I have will be very important to you."
Albin Clavin"Who knows? Maybe you'll consider me for a job in the government in the future for this."

TURN 05

■ Decision Penalty Superpower Trade WarT05

BUDGET -12WEALTH 0STABILITY 1Danger
EventSuperpower Trade War: A major naval confrontation between United Contana and Arcasia at the Gulf of Rika caused a global trade disruption. The gulf where half of the global trade flows through was blocked. Following the event Arcasia imposed considerable tariffs on CSP goods which triggered a temporary trade embargo on all Arcasian goods from the CSP. The massive financial impact was felt all around the world.
Troubling news... [-3 Government Budget]

■ Bill Religious HarmonyT05

BUDGET -12WEALTH 0STABILITY 1Danger
Bill for decisionReligious Harmony Bill — In an endeavor to promote religious harmony and unity, the Grand National Assembly introduces the Religious Harmony Bill (R.H.B.), establishing regulations for religious affairs across Sordland. Section I of the R.H.B. mandates that all sermons conducted during the Day of Descension ceremony at the Arch-sanctuary of Deyr will henceforth be held in the Sordish language. This move is aimed at promoting inclusivity and common understanding among worshippers. Section II of the R.H.B. regulates the language of sermons in other sanctuaries across Sordland. It prohibits the use of Bludish for sermons unless explicit approval has been obtained from the Archpriest of Sordland. This measure ensures consistency and unity in religious practices across the country. Section III of the R.H.B. sets forth new regulations for priests seeking state-sanctioned positions. It requires that all applicants must have Sordish lineage to be eligible for their salaries and pensions. This criterion ensures a deeper connection with Sordland's cultural heritage among the religious leaders of the country.
BILL DeepSeek-R108:34:41 p15133 / c551
  • ▶Sign: Religious Harmony Bill
  • ·Veto: Religious Harmony Bill
Anton Rayne decides to sign the Religious Harmony Bill to promote cultural unity and inclusivity, aligning with his vision for Sordland's modernization while maintaining national identity.

■ Immigration PolicyT05

BUDGET -15WEALTH 0STABILITY -4Danger
NarratorWe were in the middle of a meeting in the Palace about our immigration policy.
NarratorThe ensuing discussion quickly became heated.
Iosef Lancea"I don't think so, Mr. Wisci!"
Iosef Lancea"It just doesn't make any sense to me that a foreign citizen should come before a Sordish citizen. In Sordland!"
Lileas Graf"Well said. My analysis is also in line with this. Especially during the economic recession... We are giving Sordish jobs to migrants from other countries. What about our own people?"
Deivid Wisci"Immigrants are the reason our economy is not completely unsalvageable."
Deivid Wisci"Look at Agnolian immigrants in Agnland. They have revitalized the whole region and in turn, boosted the economy of the entire country. Symon can attest to that."
Symon Holl"Statistically speaking, allowing immigrants to become part of the workforce contributes to our economy. But I agree that the current situation is very nuanced."
Symon Holl"With that said, we can't just tighten our immigration policy and expect the economy to stay the same."
Symon Holl"We need to put our economy and the recession at the forefront."
Symon Holl"Especially in the wake of..."
Symon Holl...of this insane fucking trade war between Arcasia and United Contana!"
NarratorI'd never seen Symon this rattled. Everyone in the room turned to him in surprise.
Symon Holl"I'm sorry for losing my cool, Mr. President. I have been working day and night."
Symon Holl"I am currently attempting to minimize the damage this trade war will do to Sordland."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:35:33 p15659 / c429
  • ▶“Be careful. Don't burn yourself out.”
  • ·“I am expecting good results, Symon. Do not fail me.”
  • ·“How is it looking? Is there a way out?”
Expressing concern for Symon's well-being and encouraging him to manage his stress to maintain effectiveness.
DeepSeek-R1"Be careful. Don't burn yourself out."
Symon Holl"I will do my best, but the future of Sordish citizens is at stake."
Deivid Wisci"Gentlemen."
Deivid Wisci"Can we please get back on topic?"
Symon Holl"Right. Sorry, Mr. Wisci."
Deivid Wisci"As I was just saying before I was interrupted..."
Deivid Wisci"Look at the superpowers. Do you see them closing off their borders? Why do you think their economies are in such good shape? They are in their current state because they welcome immigration, and use it to their advantage."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:36:14 p15595 / c415
  • ·“We need to modernize and reach the level of the superpowers.”
  • ▶“I agree that we need to consider the economic impact of immigration.”
  • ·“Sordish citizens must come first.”
Considering the economic impact of immigration is crucial during a recession to balance citizen welfare with potential benefits.
DeepSeek-R1"We need to consider the impact on economy."
Symon Holl"Our money flow should be our utmost concern, if we want to have the capability to accomplish actual change."
Deivid Wisci"We need a decision. But before we move on..."
Deivid Wisci"Mr. President. Do you have any questions about the current state of immigration?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:36:52 p15604 / c411
  • ·“What is the current legal status of immigrants?”
  • ·“How many immigrants live in Sordland?”
  • ·“How do immigrants usually arrive here?”
  • ▶“I don't have any questions. Let's move on to solutions.”
Moving towards solutions would help address the heated discussion and focus on actionable steps.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't have any questions. Let's move on to solutions."
Deivid Wisci"Very well."
NarratorDeivid Wisci opened his dossier stamped with the seal of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Trade.
Deivid Wisci"Before we make any decisions, I have to remind you."
Deivid Wisci"During the elections, we promised to tighten immigration laws. Of course, as you know, I was and I still am against it."
Symon Holl"I agree with Deivid. We can turn immigration to our advantage from a financial standpoint."
Lileas Graf"Advantage? You should go over the amount of disadvantages caused by immigrants. I can provide you with a report from my ministry later."
Lileas Graf"Believe me, it would surprise you."
Iosef Lancea"We need to keep our promises and focus on our own citizens. That is what I think."
NarratorDeivid sighed wearily.
Deivid Wisci"This is going nowhere."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:37:25 p15593 / c292
  • ▶“If we decide to tighten immigration, what does that entail?”
  • ·“What are the consequences of keeping our immigration policy relaxed?”
  • ·“I am ready to make a decision.”
I need to understand the implications of tightening immigration before deciding.
DeepSeek-R1"If we decide to tighten immigration, what does that entail?"
Deivid Wisci"We would have to impose quotas and, connected to that, stricter border control."
Symon Holl"On top of allowing our economy to take a hit."
Lileas Graf"Mr. Holl. Surely the well-being of our citizens and our internal security will more than compensate for any downturn in the markets."
Iosef Lancea"Agreed. Sordish citizens should take priority."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:37:56 p15625 / c263
  • ▶“What are the consequences of keeping our immigration policy relaxed?”
  • ·“I am ready to make a decision.”
Gathering more information is crucial for making an informed decision on such a sensitive issue.
DeepSeek-R1"What are the consequences of keeping our immigration policy relaxed?"
Deivid Wisci"Aside from keeping us on the path to prosperity, modernity and continuous improvement...Not much. Our current system is good for our era. We also have the capacity."
Symon Holl"This will allow us to continue protecting investments from foreign countries, which in turn will keep our trade steady. We would be more trustworthy partners. Stability draws money."
Lileas Graf"Stability? What stability? We're already seeing clashes between our people and foreign interlopers. On the contrary, the only way we can achieve stability is through tightening our immigration policy."
Iosef Lancea"You heard the lady. Sordland first..."
“I am ready to make a decision.”
DeepSeek-R1"I am ready to make a decision."
Deivid Wisci"Yes?"
NarratorEveryone leaned forward, eager to hear my response.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:38:40 p15645 / c447

DeepSeek-R1"We will keep immigration laws relaxed."
Deivid Wisci"I am glad you made the right choice."
Iosef Lancea"We will be forced to handle new waves of migrants from Agnolia. Our border posts aren't ready."
Lileas Graf"Not only that, we've just abandoned the native Sords who need our support the most."
Lileas Graf"Also, this will bring a new tide of refugees from Wehlen. Bergia is already a difficult region to manage."
Iosef Lancea"This is shameful."

Lileas Graf"And now they will compete with foreign immigrants just to put food on their tables."

Deivid Wisci"Anything else, Mr. President?"

■ Personal Petr PartyT05

BUDGET -15WEALTH 0STABILITY -4Danger
NarratorFar from becoming accustomed to my workload as president, I only felt more and more snowed under as the weeks passed. It had been ages since I had any time to myself.
NarratorI was starting to lose my focus, and my temper was growing increasingly thin. Petr was the first to realize I needed a break.
NarratorAfter much cajoling, he finally persuaded me to come to a meeting of his new venture: Gentlemen's Club.
NarratorFor the past few months, he'd been hosting a salon of sorts, not just for politicians and businessmen but also for artists, entertainers -- people of taste, he told me with a smile on his face.
NarratorThere was only one rule: no wives or girlfriends allowed. Hence the name.
NarratorAnd so I found myself in front of his new luxury villa in Erlory. Very loud jazz music emanated from inside. I waited and waited. Finally Petr opened the door. I could smell the whiskey on his breath.
Petr Vectern"Anton! Finally! Now the real party can start. Come on in!"
NarratorAs soon as I entered, Petr closed the door and turned to the small crowd.
Petr Vectern"Gentlemen! A minute of your time please."
Petr Vectern"I now have the privilege to present to you... The man himself... The fourth President of Sordland, Mr. Anton Rayne!"
NarratorI'd known him for long enough to understand that he was more than a little drunk. He made an elaborate mock-curtsy as I passed him and walked into the room. The music stopped, and I felt everyone's eyes on me.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:41:12 p15341 / c264
  • ▶“Thank you, Petr. What's next? A choir? A marching band?”
  • ·“And I present to you, Petr Vectern. The first alcoholic Vice President of Sordland.”
  • ·“Good evening.”
A witty remark fits Anton's composed yet humorous demeanor in this social setting.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you, Petr. What's next? A choir? A marching band?"
Petr Vectern"No. I'm saving those for your funeral."
NarratorPetr laughed very hard at his own grim joke before continuing.
Petr Vectern"Dear members, please give a warm welcome to our very special guest! If I may, Anton. Here, take this."
NarratorHe handed me a glass of whiskey and put his hand on my shoulder.
Petr Vectern"We all know why we're gathered here tonight. To celebrate life. To have a brief escape from our tumultuous professions."
Petr Vectern"I personally don't know any better cure for stress than a little drink."

Petr Vectern"Now. The tradition dictates that as the club president, I have to remind you of our house rules. There are three. No politics. No wives. No one sober! Cheers!"

NarratorThe band started up again.
NarratorAfter greeting the crowd around me, I felt a hand on my shoulder.
Petr Vectern"Let me show you around."
NarratorWe walked through the corridors of his mansion. Statues and paintings lined the stark white walls. An arched window provided a view of the neatly manicured grounds, complete with a swimming pool.
NarratorA grand Lespian marble staircase led up to the second story. Petr gestured at the massive crystal chandelier hanging overhead.
Petr Vectern"That chandelier was made in the 18th century. Can you believe it? There's even a hedge maze in the garden!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:43:16 p15662 / c411

Petr Vectern"He'll also be here tonight, by the way."

NarratorHe averted his gaze for a second before calling over one of the waitresses.
NarratorShe was in her mid-twenties, wearing red lipstick, her blonde hair neatly tied into a ponytail. On her plate were toast points slathered in the Lespian caviar Petr had mentioned.
NarratorI took a bite. It was rich, salty, tasting of pure seaside. I could almost hear the sound of waves and seagulls.
Petr Vectern"Lespian caviar, best in the world! I'm telling you, Anton, there are few pleasures in life that are this..."
NarratorAs the waitress left us to serve another attendee, she flashed a quick smile at Petr over her shoulder. He smiled back, a little too broadly.
Petr Vectern"...Sensual."
NarratorHis eyes were still fixed on her, with an expression I remembered from our many nights out together as students.

NarratorI returned to the party and spent some time mingling with the other guests, engaging them in small talk about their businesses, their children, their relationships.

NarratorI woke up the next day with a pounding headache and more than a few regrets.

■ Personal Festival OpeningT05

BUDGET -15WEALTH 0STABILITY -4Danger
NarratorOur motorcade slowly made its way towards the magnificent Benfi High Arts Center. Night was falling, and the festival was about to begin. I flipped through the brochure that had been given to us at the hotel.
NarratorOn the front page, there was the face of Curtan Leste, mayor of Anrica. He was a Benfi native who had a longstanding relationship with the city's current mayor, and had been personally invited to make tonight's opening speech.
NarratorThe Benfi Festival was one of the biggest events of the year. It originally marked the start of the annual religious pilgrimage, a weeks-long journey still undertaken by devout Nurists. But today it was better known as a cultural celebration that drew thousands of revelers to the city.
NarratorThere were film screenings, concerts, theater and dance performances, art exhibitions, and celebrity-studded nightly galas. The opening ceremonies were broadcast on TV and watched by millions.
NarratorAs the car slowed, a coterie of reporters and security guards rushed to meet us. I turned my attention to Monica.
NarratorSince Gloria had convinced me to cancel her opening speech, things between us had been strained. She'd barely said a word to me all day.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:54:42 p16230 / c328
  • ·Say nothing.
  • ▶“Are you still angry about my decision?”
  • ·"Will you ever forgive me? I told you my reasons."
Anton wants to acknowledge Monica's silence and address her potential anger over his decision.
DeepSeek-R1"Are you still angry about my decision?"
NarratorShe didn't reply at all. She clearly did not want to pursue this topic any further.
NarratorAs soon as Serge opened the door, we were enveloped by the roar of the crowd.
NarratorThe next few minutes were a blur of handshakes, photos and reporters' questions. The onlookers cheered as we walked the red carpet to the newly built arts center.
NarratorWe followed the guards through the cavernous white halls until we came to a set of arched doors leading to the auditorium. We waited outside as security did a quick sweep of the area, then headed in.
NarratorI had been asked to make a quick set of remarks introducing Curtan Leste. I beckoned Monica to join me on the podium.
Monica"I'm going to find my seat."
NarratorMonica brushed past me, took her seat in the front row and crossed her arms. I walked alone to the podium.
NarratorI spoke for just a minute, reading a paragraph my speechwriter had churned out about the importance of the festival and Benfi to Sordland.
NarratorI concluded by inviting Curtan Leste, the Mayor of Anrica, to the podium. I stepped down and joined Monica in the front row. Her arms were still folded.
Curtan Leste"Welcome to the first day of the Benfi Festival."
NarratorHe paused for a moment to let the crowd cheer, and his eyes briefly met mine.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:55:27 p16262 / c494
  • ▶Nod.
  • ·Look away.
Nodding would show respect and maintain a positive public image despite internal tensions.
DeepSeek-R1I nodded.
Curtan Leste"This is a night for the people of Benfi -- indeed, for the people of Sordland -- to put aside their differences and come together in celebration."
Curtan Leste"Yet as we unite for the festivities, we must remember the real reason we are gathered here."
NarratorThe crowd fell silent. The people were giving their full attention to what he had to say.
Curtan Leste"This festival was founded around the principles of our cherished religion, Nurity. Yet over the years, it has been severed from its sacred roots and become something else. Something more perverse."
Curtan Leste"An excuse for public drunkenness and grotesque displays of the flesh. An occasion for young women to parade their bodies and engage in lascivious behavior."
NarratorAs he went on, I could see Monica become increasingly agitated.
Monica"What in the hell is he talking about? Lascivious behavior? What century does he think we live in?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:56:15 p16350 / c516
  • ·“Ssshh. Quiet.”
  • ▶“I don't know. Just let it be.”
  • ·Pretend not to hear her.
Anton chooses to acknowledge Monica's frustration but opts not to address it publicly, allowing the situation to de-escalate without drawing attention.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't know. Just let it be."
NarratorMonica turned back to watching the speech, a frown on her face.
Curtan Leste"Perversion hidden under the guise of art is not befitting the beautiful city of --"
NarratorThe crowd was becoming restless. Many of the younger attendees were murmuring in disagreement, while a few of the older ones were nodding vigorously.
Curtan Leste"As I said, perversion hidden under the guise of art is not be--"
Monica"Enough!"
NarratorThe hall fell silent. Monica rose and continued.
Monica"Mr. Leste, it is not young women who are a disgrace to this fine festival, but you. You and your backwards, puritanical attitudes, which have been holding this country back for far too long--"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:56:58 p16429 / c346
  • ▶“Monica, sit down. You're making a fool of yourself.”
  • ·“Ssshh. Monica. Please sit down.”
  • ·Watch the events unfold.
Anton wants to maintain public decorum and avoid a scene that could damage his image.
DeepSeek-R1"Monica, sit down. You're making a fool of yourself."
Monica"No, I will not sit down this time."
NarratorMonica turned to face the audience, speaking loud enough to be heard without a microphone.
Monica"Women of Sordland, we must not let people like Mr. Leste use religion as a justification to demean and oppress us. Our time has come!"
NarratorShocked gasps ran through the crowd. A few people rose and applauded. Curtan Leste waited to speak.
Curtan Leste"I think Mrs. Rayne is forgetting her place."
Monica"What do you think my place is, Mr. Leste? The kitchen? This festival is not about your retrograde traditions. We are living in modern times now. It's 1954."
Monica"And it isn't *your* place to decide how the people of Benfi -- especially the women -- should be celebrating their own festival."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:57:47 p16351 / c459

  • ·Do nothing.
To prevent escalation and maintain decorum during the festival.
DeepSeek-R1"Monica, that's enough."
Monica"What do you mean, enough? I've barely even started."
NarratorI looked at the podium to see Curtan Leste's piercing gaze on me. There would surely be a price to pay for this.
Curtan Leste"Mr. President, could you please control your wife before she embarrasses herself any further?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:58:22 p16548 / c321

  • ·“I'm so sorry about this, Mr. Leste. Please finish your speech.”
  • ·“You know what, Curtan? My wife is absolutely right.”
A calm and firm approach ensures de-escalation while respecting both Monica's stance and public decorum.
DeepSeek-R1"Monica, we've been over this. Now is not the time. Sit down, please."
Monica"I--"
NarratorThe crowd was in uproar. I had to lean over to catch Monica's next words.
Monica"I'm *so* sorry, Anton. Next time, I'll stay in the kitchen."
NarratorShe sat back down, seething. Curtan's voice filled the hall.
Curtan Leste"Benfi has always been a beacon of culture, tradition and religion. Many battles have been fought over this precious city."
Curtan Leste"Army after army has tried to claim it for their own, but we fought back and won each time."
Curtan Leste"We stand here thanks to the strong men who were able to protect their families, their lands, their way of living."
Curtan Leste"Every day, I praise God that we have succeeded so far."
NarratorScattered applause broke out from some of the older members of the audience.
NarratorI leaned over to Monica.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:59:01 p16401 / c396
  • ·“He's a bit extreme, but he is right. We owe everything to God.”
  • ▶“He sounds more like a priest than a politician.”
  • ·“I praise God he's only allowed to speak for another two minutes.”
Pointing out that Leste sounds more like a priest than a politician subtly criticizes his over-the-top remarks while maintaining neutrality.
DeepSeek-R1"He sounds more like a priest than a politician."
NarratorMonica said nothing, her gaze fixed forward.
Curtan Leste"Yet the traditions that form the very fabric of our society are being tested. Even as I speak!"
NarratorHe looked pointedly at Monica.
Curtan Leste"We must stay true to our values so that Sordland can prevail. So that Benfi can prevail!"
Curtan Leste"With these at the forefront, I am convinced that Sordland will continue to be the greatest nation in the world!"
Curtan Leste"I invite every single one of you to celebrate our roots and our traditions at this holy Benfi Festival!"
Curtan Leste"I hereby declare the festival open! Praise God! Praise Sordland! Praise Benfi!"
Curtan Leste"A Morgna wes core!"
Narrator"Vectern sis da!". Most of the people in the hall finished the decades-old saying, including me. Monica's lips moved, but she didn't say the words out loud.
NarratorHe exited the stage as the band struck up the official festival anthem.
NarratorI spent the rest of the evening alternately watching the festivities and conversing with local politicians. By the time we got back to the hotel, I was exhausted.
NarratorBut thanks to the festivities still raging outside -- and the sound of Monica tossing and turning beside me -- I couldn't sleep at all.

■ Personal Monica TalkT05

BUDGET -15WEALTH 0STABILITY -4Danger
NarratorIt felt like eternity since the Benfi Festival. Had it really only been less than 24 hours?
NarratorI was at home now, drinking an afternoon coffee on my balcony. A stack of newspapers lay by my side. I picked them up one by one, reading and re-reading the articles about the festival.
NarratorSome of the newspapers were merciless against Monica for interrupting Curtan during his speech. A few even called for her to be barred from political matters. Others expressed their support.
NarratorIn any event, I was relieved that the incident hadn't reflected too badly on me.
NarratorI was putting the last newspaper down as Monica joined me on the balcony.
Monica"So, you've seen the news?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:59:59 p16116 / c433
  • ·"Monica, I know. We can talk about this--”
  • ▶“What news?”
  • ·Do not reply.
Anton chooses to acknowledge the news without delving into a potentially awkward conversation.
DeepSeek-R1"What news?"
Monica"Here, I have it memorized: First Lady disgraced. No support from her husband."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:00:33 p16232 / c207
  • ·"You shouldn't have spoken out.”
  • ·Do not reply.
  • ▶“Darling, believe me. My hands were tied.”

DeepSeek-R1"Darling, believe me. My hands were tied."
Monica"Anton, how could you? First you deny me the opportunity to speak at the festival, then you let that dinosaur Leste make a mockery of me and my cause!"
NarratorShe grabbed one of the newspapers from the top of the stack and started reading out what a columnist wrote.
Monica"With her shameful outburst at Anrica mayor Curtan Leste, First Lady Monica Rayne once again proved that politics is an area best left to gentlemen."
Monica"With the President providing no support, her challenge to Leste was quickly defeated."
Monica"The President provided no support."
Monica"You can't even stand Leste, and you took his side! While making me look like a fool!"
NarratorShe started ripping the newspaper to shreds.
Monica"After all these years by your side, playing the doting wife..."
Monica"You know... my mother was right about you."

Monica"Just...tell me why. Couldn't you have said something?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:01:42 p16249 / c329

NarratorThere was a brief silence.
Monica"There is another chance, you know. For you to show your *real* support."
Monica"Perhaps the festival wasn't the proper forum to air my views on women's rights. But I still need to do this, Anton."
Monica"I want to officially adopt this cause as First Lady. To actually get involved in politics, not just give impromptu speeches. I could even help your education minister with those reforms she's working on..."
Monica"I know this is something you're passionate about as well. Can I count on you?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:02:22 p16280 / c320
  • ▶“Of course, my love. I'm looking forward to working closer together.”
  • ·“I'm sorry, but no. Leave politics to the politicians. I promise I'll pass the reforms you want."
Supporting Monica's political involvement strengthens their relationship and aligns with his policies.
DeepSeek-R1"Of course, my love. I'm looking forward to working closer together."
Monica"Oh, Anton. This means so much to me!"
NarratorMonica hugged me tightly, and I felt some of the past day's tension melt away.
Monica"Thank you."
NarratorA noise came from the balcony door. We both turned our heads to see Deana standing there.
Deana"Mama, I'm hungry."
Monica"Lets go to the kitchen, baby. We'll make Papa the best snack ever!"
Deana"Yeah!"
NarratorThey left the balcony and returned half an hour later with a large cookie. Deana had written my name in icing on it.
NarratorAs I sipped my coffee and nibbled the cookie, I thought that I had made the right choice.
NarratorThings were going to get harder, but at least my life at home was somewhat...
NarratorPeaceful.

■ Military BudgetT05

BUDGET -15WEALTH 0STABILITY -4Danger
NarratorTo determine the current situation of the military and its spending policy, we held a meeting in the White Room.
NarratorPetr, Valken and Iosef were seated.
Petr Vectern"General Valken. Mr. Lancea. Let's start with the briefing."
Valken Kruger"Very well, Mr. Vice President."
Valken Kruger"We have prepared a briefing to convey further details about the current situation of our military."
Iosef Lancea"Which branch would you like to know about?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:03:23 p16243 / c529
  • ▶“What is the situation of our army?”
  • ·“Let's talk about our navy.”
  • ·“Can you provide the details on our air force?”
  • ·“I am not interested in the details.”
Focusing on the army aligns with immediate domestic security needs and ongoing internal tensions.
DeepSeek-R1"What is the situation of our army?"
Iosef Lancea"According to the latest reports from Camp Strongarm, currently, we field six armies totalling up to approximately 400.000 active troops."
Iosef Lancea"Which is a good amount. The real issue we are having is the quality, not quantity."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:04:33 p16349 / c431
  • ▶“What is the current equipment status?”
  • ·“Where exactly is our army deployed at right now?”
  • ·“How do we compare to other armies?”
Understanding the current equipment status is crucial to assess the military's effectiveness and identify necessary upgrades.
DeepSeek-R1"What is the current equipment status?"
Valken Kruger"Our army is quite outdated by today's military standards. Most of our equipment is two decades old."
Iosef Lancea"To make up for our lack of modern military equipment, we resort to a large number of soldiers which is not really a long term solution."
Valken Kruger"I have been trying hard to reduce the negative effects of this problem. We don't need a modernisation program. A standardized purchase of lacking equipment would do."
Valken Kruger"We need more funding towards expanding the military size. Otherwise, our army will be seen as weak."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:05:18 p15481 / c420
  • ·“Where exactly is our army deployed at right now?”
  • ▶“How do we compare to other armies?”
  • ·“I am curious about another branch.”
Understanding how Sordland's army compares to others can inform the necessity of modernization and strategic priorities.
DeepSeek-R1"How do we compare to other armies?"
Valken Kruger"Rumburg fields six active field armies and two reserve armies, together surpassing one million soldiers in the field. Lespia maintains five armies. With ATO deployments included, their numbers rise to nearly 800,000."
Valken Kruger"Valgsland fields one active army of around 140,000 troops, a significant portion of them marines. They also maintain three reserve armies that can be mobilized quickly due to their rapid mobilization military structure."
Valken Kruger"Wehlen commands two armies totaling roughly 300,000 soldiers, though only their professional core is considered highly capable. Agnolia fields three armies amounting to 250,000 troops. They are well equipped, but have not seen combat in over three decades."
Iosef Lancea"Rumburg’s sheer size stems from its forced conscription laws. In addition, several ducal regions maintain their own forces at their own expense, further bolstering the crown’s strength. They can sustain this scale thanks to their vast gold reserves, much of which flowed in from Rizia over the past decades."
Iosef Lancea"The Lespian army is the most advanced. Arcasian investment and the Alvarez administration’s expansion of the military-industrial complex have accelerated their modernization."
Iosef Lancea"Valgsland follows closely. Although they prioritize economic development and social welfare in their budget, CSP funding has allowed them to remain competitive, keeping the gap with Lespia narrow."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:05:47 p16013 / c185
  • ·“Where exactly is our army deployed at right now?”
  • ▶“I am curious about another branch.”
I want to understand the state of other military branches beyond just the army.
DeepSeek-R1"I am curious about another branch."
Iosef Lancea"Very well."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:06:20 p16016 / c265
  • ▶“Let's talk about our navy.”
  • ·“Can you provide the details on our air force?”
  • ·“Actually, let's move on.”
Shifting focus to the navy could provide crucial insights into Sordland's maritime defense capabilities.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's talk about our navy."
Iosef Lancea"Our navy currently constitutes 59 vessels with a total of 60.000 sailors and support staff."
Iosef Lancea"As you know, these ships are commanded from our main naval base in Conriat where our flagship, SN Renan is docked."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:06:54 p15948 / c299
  • ·“How does our Navy compare to others?”
  • ·“Which seas does our Navy currently patrol?”
  • ▶“How are our capabilities in power projection?”
Asking about power projection capabilities is crucial for understanding the navy's ability to influence and defend national interests.
DeepSeek-R1"How are our capabilities in power projection?"
Valken Kruger"The truth is, we are limited to our near region due to the size and technological level of our naval ships."
Iosef Lancea"If we had modern support vessels, we could participate in international operations."
Valken Kruger"That's right. We still hold *some* power though. If needed be, we could block the trade at the Markian Sea."
Iosef Lancea"To make up for our weakness, allying ourselves to a naval power could also help protect our seas."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:07:28 p16021 / c289

  • ·“Which seas does our Navy currently patrol?”

Iosef Lancea"Which one?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:08:03 p15923 / c286

I need to understand all branches of the military to make informed decisions.
DeepSeek-R1"Can you provide the details on our air force?"
Iosef Lancea"Our air force currently has 410 planes in the inventory. Out of which only 140 are jet planes that are not in great condition."
Iosef Lancea"30.000 pilots and support staff work in the Air Force and they are commanded from the Mark Air Force Base in Erlory."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:08:35 p15999 / c272

DeepSeek-R1"How does our air force work together with other military branches?"
Valken Kruger"The Air Force is trained as an independent tactical support branch which means it assists other branches but doesn't collaborate."
Iosef Lancea"This does cause some issues like friendly fire and missed close air support opportunities."
Valken Kruger"There are ways to improve collaboration with joint operation rooms. Something to think about surely."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:09:07 p15988 / c240

  • ·“How experienced are our pilots?”

DeepSeek-R1"I would like to know about the other branches."
Iosef Lancea"Any specific one you want to know about?"
“Actually, let's move on.”
DeepSeek-R1"Actually, let's move on."
Iosef Lancea"Let's."
NarratorHe cleared his throat.
Iosef Lancea"The Ministry of Defense is content with the additional funds."
Petr Vectern"Hopefully the allocated money helps our defense needs."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:09:42 p15901 / c329

  • ·“The military is a top priority for our administration and the Sordish people who hold it in high esteem.”

Valken Kruger"Rightfully so."
Valken Kruger"We are surrounded by jackals as our intelligence and General Staff indicates."
Iosef Lancea"Our enemies are both outside and inside the country."
Iosef Lancea"We will do all that is necessary to protect this soil."
Petr Vectern"Should we move on to the spending choice?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:10:22 p15847 / c461

DeepSeek-R1"Sure. I want to hear the suggestions."
Petr Vectern"The increase of the military budget leaves us with big questions about how to utilize it."
Iosef Lancea"There was a lot of debate at the Ministry which resulted in two factions of thought."
Iosef Lancea"The first faction is in favour of enlarging the armed forces by enlisting more men while the second faction is in favour of purchasing better military equipment instead."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:11:09 p15844 / c645

Iosef Lancea"The armed forces are behind in terms of military equipment in comparison to our neighbours."
Iosef Lancea"We need a fundamental modernisation programme."
Valken Kruger"No matter how many advanced weapons and systems we buy, our enemies can overcome them."
NarratorValken had a serious tone.
Petr Vectern"That sounds bleak."

DeepSeek-R1"Quality over quantity, always. A smaller well equipped force can destroy a much larger one."
Valken Kruger"Sir, I will be frank. Rumburg fields three times our force."
Valken Kruger"A modernisation programme won't mitigate our difference."
Iosef Lancea"The era of large assaults and human wave tactics are over."
Iosef Lancea"We are heading to an era of modern combat which requires modern tools to adapt."

  • ·“I have settled on a decision.”

Valken Kruger"Some of our neighbours have larger populations and thus a larger armed force."
Valken Kruger"Using funds to boost our size would minimize their advantage."
Iosef Lancea"Theoretically numbers alone don't determine the strength balance."

■ Wehlen Trade VisitT05

BUDGET -15WEALTH 0STABILITY -3Danger
NarratorSordland One's flight from Holsord to Wraclawitz took approximately five hours. Despite heavy turbulence, I managed to sleep a little.
NarratorSymon, Deivid and I landed at the international airport of the capital city of Wehlen. Our aim here was to finalise the trade deal drafted between Sordland and Wehlen.
NarratorOur plane landed, and the door opened. The sun blinded me for a moment. I stepped outside.
NarratorI saw the soldiers lined up on either side of a red carpet. There must've been at least a hundred of them. A marching band started playing and the soldiers immediately saluted me.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:14:56 p15216 / c267
  • ▶Salute the soldiers.
  • ·Proceed to the red carpet without any gestures.
Saluting the soldiers demonstrates respect and unity during a formal state visit.
DeepSeek-R1I saluted the soldiers.
NarratorAt the bottom of the stairs, Wiktor Smolak was waiting for me next to a military jeep with a few soldiers by his side. There were a few reporters at the edge of the crowd, waiting with their cameras.
NarratorHe was a tall man, almost as tall as me. He was wearing large sunglasses and a regular suit, contrasting with the military attire of everyone else in the field.
Wiktor Smolak"Mr. President. Welcome to Wraclawitz."
NarratorHe immediately motioned for an embrace. We hugged and many cameras went off at the same time. He turned around for a couple of additional poses.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:15:34 p15393 / c397
  • ·“Nice to see you, Mr. President.”
  • ·Pull him aside for a couple of more poses yourself.
  • ▶“Thank you for inviting us.”
Expressing gratitude for the invitation strengthens diplomatic relations and acknowledges the host's effort.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you for inviting us."
Wiktor Smolak"Now that the pleasantries are done, let's go! We will take the jeep for a tour."
NarratorWith another tune from the marching band, we boarded the military jeep. He sat in the driver's seat and I sat next to him.
NarratorAs we started speeding up, the soldiers saluted again. Wiktor saluted back.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:16:10 p15399 / c375
  • ▶Salute the soldiers.
  • ·Do nothing.
Saluting the soldiers is a respectful gesture that acknowledges their presence and fosters good relations.
DeepSeek-R1I saluted the soldiers.
NarratorBoth of our security details started following us immediately, along with a team of reporters.
Wiktor Smolak"We will first go to the city center to see the unveiling of a statue."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:16:49 p15465 / c476
  • ·“Whose statue?”
  • ·“Sounds ...exciting.”
  • ▶“I'd rather not waste time. Let's get straight to business.”
Anton Rayne prioritizes efficiency and getting down to business matters quickly.
DeepSeek-R1"I'd rather not waste time. Let's get straight to business."
Wiktor Smolak"Ah, Mr. President. I must insist. Also, I am in the driver's seat."
NarratorWe kept making small talk about the flight and the weather as we made our way towards the city center.
NarratorOn almost every corner, there were reporters taking photos of us. Aside from them, though, the streets seemed completely empty.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:17:22 p15504 / c294
  • ·“Is Wraclawitz always this empty?”
  • ·“Is this all a big photo op for you?”
  • ▶“How long is this going to take?”
Anton prefers efficiency and would want to minimize delays.
DeepSeek-R1"How long is this going to take?"
Wiktor Smolak"Patience is a virtue. We will be done soon, and then we can discuss our trade deal."
Wiktor Smolak"There we are."
NarratorWe arrived at the center. In the middle of a large plaza, there was a covered statue. Large crowds of people were gathered behind fences. waving both Sordland and Wehlen flags. Wiktor waved back at them.
Wiktor Smolak"My people have been very supportive of this visit, Mr. President. I am sure you are delighted to see them."
NarratorA man came closer to the fences and started yelling at Wiktor. With my limited knowledge of the Wehzek language, I understood the words 'Wiktor' and 'murderer'.
NarratorAlmost instantaneously, two soldiers came up from behind the man and he was taken away.
Wiktor Smolak"Thankfully the sun is out. Today will be a good day for the reveal."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:17:58 p15523 / c368
  • ▶“I see that you know how to handle dissidents. Very good.”
  • ·“What will happen to the man who was protesting you?”
  • ·“Very good.”
Anton would acknowledge Wiktor's effective approach to maintaining order, aligning with his pragmatic leadership style focused on stability.
DeepSeek-R1"I see that you know how to handle dissidents. Very good."
Wiktor Smolak"I am glad you approve. We are of the same mind."
Wiktor Smolak"See, I knew we would get along fine."
NarratorWe took our places in front of the statue. With a drumroll, the statue of Wiktor Smolak was uncovered.
NarratorIt was a fairly good likeness, albeit more muscular than the real Wiktor. He was wearing a cloak and holding a sword in his hand.
Wiktor Smolak"Excellent statue. Very lifelike. What do you think?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:18:37 p15569 / c450
  • ·“Mr. Smolak, I don't have time for these games. Let's get straight to the business.”
  • ▶“Yes, it's very good. Very lifelike.”
  • ·“Our artists can do better in Sordland.”
Maintaining a diplomatic tone to foster positive relations during negotiations.
DeepSeek-R1"Yes, it's very good. Very lifelike."
Wiktor Smolak"Glad you appreciate the art behind it."
Wiktor Smolak"Join me for a walk."
NarratorI followed him through the streets. At every corner, more and more photos were taken.
NarratorWe stopped in front of a pastry vendor on the street.
Wiktor Smolak"Give me two strudels."
NarratorThe man behind the stand smiled and gave us two strudels.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:19:09 p15454 / c282
  • ·“Are you going to pay for it?”
  • ·Pay the street vendor.
  • ▶Wait for him to pay the street vendor.
As a visiting president, it's customary not to pay for such items and let the host handle expenses.
DeepSeek-R1I waited for him to pay the street vendor. He simply took the two strudels and didn't pay anything.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:19:47 p15483 / c463
  • ·“Are you going to pay for it?”
  • ▶Pay the street vendor.
  • ·Keep walking.
Anton chooses to pay the street vendor to uphold ethical conduct and fairness.
DeepSeek-R1I put my hand on my wallet and brought up some money to give the vendor.
Wiktor Smolak"Hold up. Your money doesn't count here. I got this."
NarratorHe handed the money to the street vendor with a huge smile on his face. The cameras flashed again.
NarratorAfterwards, we walked a short distance to the palace.
NarratorPassing the gates, we made our way into the main building made from ivory-colored stones. The old guard towers flanked the magnificent structure from both sides.
NarratorThe inside of the palace was very spacious, with a very large chandelier hanging from the ceiling. Grand staircases made from the same ivory stone had red carpets on them. On the upper levels, guards stood wearing the traditional outfits of the old Wehzek kingdom.
NarratorWe finally reached the office of the President of Wehlen. The door closed behind us. I looked around at the golden ornaments on the walls and the various jewel-encrusted artifacts.
NarratorAs it was customary in Wehlen, we sat down on the carpet.
Wiktor Smolak"Now we can talk about our trade deal."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:20:19 p15240 / c276
  • ·“Finally.”
  • ▶Nod.
Nodding is a natural and polite response when invited to sit and begin discussions.
DeepSeek-R1I nodded.
Wiktor Smolak"But before that."
NarratorHe clapped his hands twice.
Wiktor Smolak"Bring the Khisha."
NarratorA few assistants immediately entered the room, carrying a large ornamented Khisha, the well-known smoking instrument originating from Wehlen. They immediately lit it up and the room was filled with the smell of tobacco.
NarratorWiktor inhaled the smoke and handed me the Khisha.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:20:54 p15158 / c385
  • ·“I don't smoke.”
  • ▶Partake.
Partaking in the Khisha would demonstrate respect for Wehlen's customs and strengthen diplomatic relations.
DeepSeek-R1I inhaled the smoke.
NarratorIt was probably the highest-quality tobacco I had ever smoked. I handed it back.
NarratorHe took another puff, filling the room with more smoke.
Wiktor Smolak"So... Mr. President. Bah, that's too long. Can I call you Anton?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:21:22 p15161 / c253
  • ·“I prefer my official title.”
  • ▶“Sure.”
Using first names can build rapport and ease negotiations.
DeepSeek-R1"Sure."
Wiktor Smolak"Anton."
Wiktor Smolak"Tell me. What do you really think about Rumburg?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:21:53 p15217 / c324
  • ▶“A barking dog doesn't bite.”
  • ·“They are a threat to the region.”
  • ·“They can go to hell!”
  • ·“Not much in particular. Like any other country, they have their needs and their own agenda.”
A barking dog doesn't bite is a diplomatic way to downplay potential threats without offending the host.
DeepSeek-R1"A barking dog doesn't bite."
Wiktor Smolak"I wouldn't be so sure, Anton."
Wiktor Smolak"I assure you their time will come."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:22:28 p15136 / c416
  • ·“In due time, yes.”
  • ·“Are you planning something?”
  • ▶“Regardless, I would rather prevent further conflict in the region.”
Emphasizing peace aligns with Anton Rayne's focus on stability and domestic reforms, fitting his role as a reformist president.
DeepSeek-R1"Regardless, I would rather prevent further conflict in the region."
Wiktor Smolak"You know as well as I do, Anton. They are coming whether we like it or not. They are the aggressor, not us."
Wiktor Smolak"And now, I need to solve some problems they are creating for me."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:22:58 p15136 / c288
  • ·“What is it?”
  • ▶“I'm listening.”
Choosing 'I'm listening' shows openness and willingness to engage with Smolak's perspective, which is beneficial for fostering a positive dialogue during trade discussions.
DeepSeek-R1"I'm listening."
NarratorHe clapped his hands again. Two assistants came into the room with a map of the border regions between Wehlen and Sordland. He pointed at the border.
Wiktor Smolak"Look."
Wiktor Smolak"Bluds and their so called *BFF* are causing problems for me in this region. They are getting increasingly dangerous with Rumburg's active support. I know that they are a problem for you as well."
Wiktor Smolak"I will not allow these traitors to exist within my borders. So I will hit them with all the might of Wehlen. These terrorists need to be destroyed."
Wiktor Smolak"I will cut all their supplies from Rumburg and trap them on the mountains."
Wiktor Smolak"I call it Operation Bear Trap."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:23:33 p15208 / c395
  • ▶“I don't support any military action this close to our borders.”
  • ·“What do you want from me?”
  • ·“I like the sound of it.”
Anton prefers caution to avoid escalating regional tensions.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't support any military action this close to our borders."
NarratorHe lay back on the large pillow behind him.
Wiktor Smolak"Here is the deal. I will give you objectively the *best* trade deal in the region."
Wiktor Smolak"You will give me your assistance crushing the terrorists on both of our borders."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:24:07 p15275 / c420
  • ·“I need specifics.”
  • ·“Absolutely not. I won't meddle in Wehlen's military interventions.”

DeepSeek-R1"Two birds, one stone."
Wiktor Smolak"You read my mind."
Wiktor Smolak"My spies have infiltrated BFF cells and they found caches of KA-74s. We traced them back to Rumburg. This alone justifies my retaliation to protect my country!"
Wiktor Smolak"Worst of all, we also found that similar shipments are going to Sordland. Rumburg is trying to unite the BFF forces in both of our countries."
Wiktor Smolak"However, the main problem is the border security between our nations. BFF militants have been using the mountains between Sordland and Wehlen to escape justice in both of our countries."
Wiktor Smolak"If we don't act and trap them in those mountains, they will keep replenishing their forces in both of our countries and keep waging war against our citizens."
Wiktor Smolak"Yes, Anton. You see. This is not just Wehlen's problem."
Wiktor Smolak"Let's take a step back and talk about what you will get in return."
Wiktor Smolak"I propose you a no-tariff agreement, co-investment projects and oil."
Wiktor Smolak"In return, you will assist me in destroying the armed militants in my country."
Wiktor Smolak"You know that as long as they use the mountains in Wehlen as a base, Sordland and Lespia will equally suffer. This is in the interest of the whole region."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:24:41 p15353 / c360

  • ▶“What kind of support do you need from me? Can't Wehlen handle it by itself?”
  • ·“There might be international ramifications.”
Asking for details about the support needed shows skepticism and interest, while implying Wehlen should handle it themselves.
DeepSeek-R1"What kind of support do you need from me? Can't Wehlen handle it by itself?"
Wiktor Smolak"We can, but these vermin are good at hiding. As I told you, they operate and keep reorganizing inside both of our countries."
Wiktor Smolak"What I want from you in return for all the money and the resources, is only a joint operation between our armies against the BFF."
NarratorI took a moment to think about it.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:25:16 p15413 / c369
  • ·“Deal.”
  • ▶“No deal. It's too risky. I'm afraid I can't accept.”

Accepting Wehlen's proposal would risk escalating regional tensions and divert resources from Sordland's internal challenges.
DeepSeek-R1"No deal. It's too risky. I'm afraid I can't accept."
Wiktor Smolak"Wait, Anton. Wait."
Wiktor Smolak"Normally, I wouldn't offer anything else but, I like you."
Wiktor Smolak"Here is my final offer."
Wiktor Smolak"When the operation starts, with you or without you, there will be stragglers trying to cross into Sordland."
Wiktor Smolak"We just want you to support the operation even if you do not join. Stop the terrorists from fleeing into your country and you will have your deal. Simple, isn't it?"
Wiktor Smolak"So what do you think?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:26:10 p15414 / c857
  • ·“Deal.”
  • ▶“No deal. I'm afraid I can't accept.”
Declining the deal avoids military entanglement and focuses on domestic reforms.
DeepSeek-R1"No deal. I'm afraid I can't accept."
Wiktor Smolak"That's unfortunate. I thought we could be friends."
Wiktor Smolak"But you decided to leave Wehlen to fend for itself."
Wiktor Smolak"Such a shame. I am sure a powerful leader like Soll would've accepted this great offer without a doubt."
Wiktor Smolak"My assistants will show you the way."
Wiktor Smolak"Goodbye, Mr. Rayne."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:26:45 p15391 / c409
  • ▶“Take care of yourself, Wiktor.”
  • ·Nod and leave.
A polite refusal maintains decorum and avoids unnecessary conflict.
DeepSeek-R1"Take care of yourself, Wiktor."
NarratorI returned to Sordland immediately.
NarratorI felt relieved to be back in Holsord.

■ Agnolia Trade VisitT05

BUDGET -15WEALTH 0STABILITY -3Danger
NarratorSordland One's flight from Holsord to Staalport took three hours but went very smoothly. We were about to touch down.
NarratorDeivid left his seat and came over while Symon was enjoying his newspaper.
Deivid Wisci"Looks like we are about to land, Mr. President."
NarratorI looked outside. I could see Heljiland, the island in between Valgsland and Agnolia.
Deivid Wisci"So many conflicts over such a small piece of land. The island can barely sustain itself without outside help."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:27:35 p15210 / c434
  • ·“I know. I was just thinking the same thing.”
  • ·“If I was in the shoes of the Agnolian Prime Minister, I would do anything to protect the motherland.”
  • ▶“I don't blame them. It is in a very strategic position.”
Recognizing the island's strategic significance ties into ongoing concerns about border security and regional conflicts.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't blame them. It is in a very strategic position."
Deivid Wisci"Certainly. If you have Heljiland, you are in control of the whole Valgos Channel."
Deivid Wisci"I suspect it will be one of the topics that Mr. Van Hoorten will bring up as a bargaining chip. We must be careful."
Deivid Wisci"Heljiland might be under the control of Agnolia, but the international community has yet to recognise it. For a good reason, too."
Deivid Wisci"Nobody wants to make an enemy of Valgsland. Especially because of the support of United Contana and the CSP."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:28:11 p15432 / c390
  • ·“I don't care who we're up against. We will cross that bridge when we come to it.”
  • ▶“Me included. I wouldn't want do anything to injure our relationship with Valgsland.”
Choosing to maintain good relations with Valgsland aligns with the goal of avoiding unnecessary conflicts and focusing on domestic stability.
DeepSeek-R1"Me included. I wouldn't want do anything to injure our relationship with Valgsland."
Deivid Wisci"I agree. We should try our best to stick to the middle ground."
NarratorSymon folded his newspaper, got up from his seat and sat right next to us.
Symon Holl"Aside from the point of Heljiland, we need to be careful with the additional requests."
Symon Holl"According to the deal that was negotiated so far, they want to sell us their steel for a higher price and they are requesting easier access to our agricultural market."
Symon Holl"In return, they are promising cash flow and investments, especially in the Agnland region. It's not hard to guess why."
Deivid Wisci"Precisely. Agnolian immigrants in our Agnland region make up a good chunk of the area's economy now."
Deivid Wisci"During the initial negotiations, they brought up the topic of immigration many times."
Deivid Wisci"The fact that we have relaxed our immigration laws will help tremendously."
Deivid Wisci"Let's see if we can shake hands on it."
NarratorThe pilot made an announcement that the descent had started. Deivid and Symon went back to their seats.
NarratorI took another look outside the window. I could see Staalport, the capital city of Agnolia.
NarratorThe large dockyards of the city and the canals -- "Staal" in Agnolian -- appeared closer as our descent continued. Staalport was certainly a fitting name.
NarratorA couple of minutes later, we finally touched down. The plane parked. I took a final look out the window. The welcoming ceremony from Agnolia was waiting along the red carpet outside.
NarratorAfter the guards made their way out, I exited the plane.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:28:40 p15545 / c205
  • ▶Wave at the crowd.
  • ·Proceed without a gesture.
Waving at the crowd will demonstrate friendliness and openness, which is important for international relations.
DeepSeek-R1I waved at the crowd that gathered. There were many camera flashes. I started to make my way downstairs.
NarratorPrime Minister Van Hoorten was nowhere in sight. Instead, I was greeted by the Foreign Minister.
NarratorAfter handshakes and a couple of photos, I moved to the car where Serge was waiting to drive me to the office of Prime Minister.
Serge Wolkner"Good day, sir. I hope the flight was comfortable."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:29:11 p15580 / c291
  • ·“Yes, it was. Thank you, Serge.”
  • ▶“Let's go.”
  • ·Nod.
Anton Rayne would prefer a direct approach after a long flight to get into discussions quickly.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's go."
NarratorI got in the car and we made our way through the foreign streets. Our convoy was protected by Sordish and Agnolian guards in their vehicles.
NarratorWe got on the highway beside the port and saw dozens of dockyards and ships anchored. Some of the boats and machinery looked old.
NarratorThe city itself looked very grey and grim. The buildings had a touch of Rumburg's famous Monarkian architecture with a mixture of Sordish signature domes on the older ones.
NarratorWe were now on the main road to the office of the Prime Minister.
Serge Wolkner"Looks like they are not too happy to see you, sir."
NarratorSerge pointed at the crowd that gathered on both sides of the street. It was a protest. They were shouting and yelling at our convoy. Some of them held signs written in Sordish: 'Sordland Must Pay Its Debts To Agnolia'
Serge Wolkner"You know... I never knew Agnolians were so ungrateful. Not everyone sees a great leader like you everyday."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:29:40 p15616 / c249
  • ·“You are absolutely right, Serge. So ungrateful.”
  • ·“They can yell all they want.”
  • ▶“There have been so many issues in Sordland. I don't know if I can call myself a great leader.”
  • ·“I wonder what propaganda their state is feeding them..."
Anton Rayne is focused on internal reforms and addressing issues within Sordland, making him hesitant to view himself as a great leader while problems persist.
DeepSeek-R1"There have been so many issues in Sordland. I don't know if I can call myself a great leader."
Serge Wolkner"Sir, humility is important but not in this case. They were always scared of Sordland's potential. And now they are afraid that a lion leads Sordland. They are afraid of you."
Serge Wolkner"Not just that, but you are a man who is selfless enough to help your driver's children."
Serge Wolkner"A true leader, I dare say."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:30:10 p15653 / c255
  • ·“How is little Anton doing?”
  • ▶“I treat my people well.”
  • ·“Don't mention it.”
Affirming the compliment confidently reinforces leadership qualities.
DeepSeek-R1"I treat my people well."
Serge Wolkner"I can attest to that myself. I am honored to be your driver. And very grateful."
NarratorThe convoy started slowing down.
Serge Wolkner"We have arrived, sir."
NarratorThe car continued through the gates while the security vehicles went the other way. We finally came to a stop in front of the door.
NarratorSerge left the car and opened the car door. I exited and began walking to the door where dozens of press and the Prime Minister were waiting for me.
NarratorI saw the PM much more clearly as I neared the door. He was shorter than I thought.
Maartin Van Hoorten"Mr. President. Welcome to Agnolia and to the beautiful city of Staalport."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:30:38 p15580 / c192
  • ·“Prime Minister.”
  • ▶“Thank you so much for the warm welcome. It is great to be here.”
  • ·“Good to see you, Maartin.”
Expressing gratitude for the warm welcome sets a positive tone for the visit.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you so much for the warm welcome. It is great to be here."
NarratorHe reached out for a handshake. The press surrounding us had been waiting for this moment.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:31:06 p15601 / c211
  • ·Overpower his handshake.
  • ▶Shake hands.
  • ·Shake hands and hug after.
A professional handshake is appropriate to maintain mutual respect and decorum.
DeepSeek-R1We shook hands and smiled at the cameras as the press took pictures.
NarratorHe gestured to the entrance of the two-hundred-year old Parliament building.
Maartin Van Hoorten"This way, please."
NarratorWe entered and the press followed us inside.
Maartin Van Hoorten"I would like to present you with a gift as a sign of friendship between our countries."
NarratorAn assistant showed up immediately with a large glass case in her hands. It contained a single sheet of parchment inside.
Maartin Van Hoorten"This here is one of the first treaties written between Sordland and Agnolia. Just like the one you prepared with Mr. Wisci before my term. May it mark the renewal of our friendship today as well!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:31:36 p15633 / c264

  • ▶“This is an amazing gift. Thank you.”
  • ·“Very good.”
Expressing genuine appreciation for the historical gift strengthens diplomatic relations.
DeepSeek-R1"This is an amazing gift. Thank you."
NarratorOur photos were taken again. I gestured at one of my retainers who was holding our gift to the PM.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:32:06 p15677 / c264
  • ▶Gift one of the original copies of the first constitution of Sordland.
  • ·Gift a Sordish-bred puppy despite Maartin's known fear of dogs.
  • ·Gift an ornamented ceremonial sword made with Sordish steel.
The original Sordland constitution symbolizes national identity and respect for governance, making it an appropriate and thoughtful gift.
DeepSeek-R1Our gift was one of the original copies of the first constitution of Sordland.
Maartin Van Hoorten"Ah! An excellent copy. I have always wondered what one of the originals looked like. Thank you for this excellent gift."
NarratorThe cameras flashed once again.
Maartin Van Hoorten"Let's go to my office. Follow me."
NarratorI followed him and we entered the office of the Prime Minister. With its minimalist wooden decor, the room was far more modest than I expected.
NarratorWe sat down. He leaned back and made himself comfortable.
Maartin Van Hoorten"Sheesh. I'm thankful to be done with the ceremonies."

Maartin Van Hoorten"Would you like a drink, Mr. Rayne?"

Choosing water demonstrates restraint and a focus on maintaining clear judgment during the discussion.

Maartin Van Hoorten"As you wish."

DeepSeek-R1"A true gentleman. I can respect that about you, Mr. Hoorten."
Maartin Van Hoorten"This is how we do things in Agnolia, Mr. Rayne. I appreciate the kind words, but flattery alone won't sway me this day."
Maartin Van Hoorten"First of all, I would like to say that I have been watching your recent immigration policy changes. I am glad that you have decided to keep it relaxed."
Maartin Van Hoorten"Immigrants are very important for beneficial economical development. We believe that very much in Agnolia."
Maartin Van Hoorten"It is reassuring to see Sordland move in a more modern direction."

Maartin Van Hoorten"So tell me, Mr. Rayne. Is Sordland a reliable trading partner to Agnolia?"

Maartin Van Hoorten"I can assure you. We are."
NarratorHe paused for a moment.
Maartin Van Hoorten"It was a good move to start building that highway. Overall, this will positively affect our trade."
Maartin Van Hoorten"Well done, Mr. Rayne."

Maartin Van Hoorten"I give credit when it's necessary."

Maartin Van Hoorten"First of all, if we shake hands on it, this agreement is going to be extremely beneficial for both of us."
Maartin Van Hoorten"You should be able to solve your recession in no time with the investment and money flow coming from us. This is, as you know, on top of the reduced tariffs for trade between our countries."
Maartin Van Hoorten"Of course in return, as discussed between my people and your people before, you will allow us privileged access to your agricultural market."
Maartin Van Hoorten"You will be buying steel from us for a higher price, which is a small price to pay really. That is the deal."
Maartin Van Hoorten"What do you say, Mr. Rayne?"

Maartin Van Hoorten"You are throwing many months of work down the drain. This is a mistake."

NarratorAs soon as we left the office, I briefed Symon and Deivid on the outcome of the meeting.
NarratorThe rest of our state trip was full of fake smiles, handshakes, incredibly long ceremonies and even longer meetings between our ministers and their counterparts.
NarratorWhen we came to the end of the trip, the way back was mostly silent.
NarratorI couldn't help but let out a sigh of relief when we touched down in Holsord.

■ Reformist SpeechT05

BUDGET -15WEALTH 0STABILITY -3Danger
NarratorI came to the Grand National Assembly of Sordland to deliver a speech regarding the new Constitution and ask for the support of the Assembly before the upcoming vote.
NarratorI walked quickly through the corridors, giving my greetings to people on my way towards the parliamentary hall.
NarratorWhen I entered the hall, the Assembly was in complete chaos, with shouting and cursing between the parties. There was a heavy argument about a proposal made by an MP from NFP.
NarratorI took my seat inside the special area reserved for the ministers. Gloria was shouting from her seat at the centre of the hall as she hit the gavel three times, with each strike heavier than the one before.
Gloria Tory"ORDER! ORDER!"
NarratorI saw Kesaro Kibener standing up from his seat and shouting towards the members of the PFJP.
Gloria Tory"Mr. Kibener! Calm yourself! Show respect to the procedures of this Assembly!"
NarratorDespite her efforts, the argument continued for a couple of minutes before everybody took back to their seats and finally went quiet.
Gloria Tory"Mr. Kibener, next time when I tell you to do so, either take your seat or leave the hall; so we can proceed with the procedures of this Assembly and continue our duties."
NarratorSome MP's from the NFP side shouted back at her in protest.
Gloria Tory"Honorable ladies and gentlemen, if you allow me to continue..."
NarratorShe pointed at me.
Gloria Tory"We have the President here with us today. He has asked to give an important announcement."
NarratorThe Assembly fell silent.
Gloria Tory"You have the floor, Mr. President."
NarratorI walked to the stand as loud clapping started in the USP's side.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:39:38 p15276 / c228
  • ·"Ladies and Gentlemen..."
  • ·"Honorable Members of Assembly..."
  • ▶"Brothers and sisters..."
Using 'Brothers and sisters' reflects Anton's commitment to unity and inclusivity in addressing the divided assembly.
DeepSeek-R1"Brothers and sisters! I am here today to raise important questions regarding our outdated Constitution."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:40:14 p15417 / c424
  • ·"The Supreme Court holds too much power..."
  • ·"Our election threshold has harmed our democracy more than anything..."
  • ▶"Perhaps it's not expected of me to say this, but the President holds too much authority..."
  • ·"Our elected Assembly has always been overshadowed..."
Anton wants to limit presidential powers and increase checks on his own authority.
DeepSeek-R1"Perhaps it's not expected of me to say this, but the President holds too much authority in Sordish politics. In order to strengthen our democracy, we must be brave enough to point out the injustices and faults of our Constitution."
NarratorI continued.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:40:50 p15423 / c448
  • ·"...Therefore, we propose changes to the Constitution to prepare Sordland for a more democratic era!"
  • ·"We must finally rewrite the Constitution of Soll and break our chains of the past!"
  • ▶"I call everyone to stand by us to make historic changes to our Constitution and bring it to the standards of today's world!"
Option 2 is chosen to emphasize unity and progress, appealing to all factions for support in modernizing the Constitution.
DeepSeek-R1"I call everyone to stand by us to make historic changes to our Constitution and bring it to the standards of today's world!"
NarratorApplauses came from USP and PFJP seats.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:41:21 p15398 / c326

  • ▶"We will rebalance the branches of government to ensure a more democratic process."
Rebalancing the branches ensures a more democratic process, supporting long-term stability.
DeepSeek-R1"We will rebalance the branches of government to ensure a more democratic process."
NarratorI focused on...
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:42:04 p15349 / c624
  • ·Unity.
  • ▶Change.
  • ·Peace.
Choosing 'Change' aligns with the proposed constitutional reforms and the shift towards a more democratic structure.
DeepSeek-R1"We must not be afraid of change. The world is changing, and Sordland needs the change it has earned after years of Sollism in the last decades. We will rewrite the old and make a constitution that would reflect the will of the people, and open the way for a democratic Sordland!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:42:45 p15432 / c542
  • ·"We will make Sordland great again!"
  • ▶"Let's embrace the future."
A forward-looking message aligns with Anton's reformist agenda and can unite diverse factions within the assembly.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's embrace the future."
NarratorLoud noises came from USP and NFP seats.
NarratorI took a breath and continued.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:43:13 p15459 / c236

  • ▶"It is time to make a new Constitution together!"

Emphasizing collaboration on a new constitution highlights unity and progress.
DeepSeek-R1"It is time to make a new Constitution together!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:43:42 p15434 / c237

Inviting others to join a new era emphasizes unity and collaboration.
DeepSeek-R1"Be part of a new era for Sordland. Join us and vote for our proposal."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:44:16 p15466 / c388

DeepSeek-R1"Let's write the future together!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:44:53 p15402 / c449

CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:45:22 p15355 / c278

  • ▶"For a democratic Sordland!"
