■ Education SpendingT06

BUDGET -19WEALTH 0STABILITY -6Danger
NarratorAfter a meeting with the rector and a brief tour of the University of Valgen, we gathered in a room with Ciara.
NarratorThe entire campus was visible from the windows. Students were reading books, studying and having picnics under the trees.
NarratorIt reminded me of my own times at Deyr University of Culture -- at least, the more carefree moments.
Ciara Walda"Reminiscing about the past?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:24:43 p17497 / c305
  • ·“How were your university years?”
  • ▶“Yes. It had its ups and downs but it was still fun.”
  • ·“It's nothing.”
Anton would likely respond positively to connect with Ciara while keeping it light.
DeepSeek-R1"Yes. It had its ups and downs but it was still fun."
Ciara Walda"And now we're growing old and bitter."
NarratorShe smiled.
Ciara Walda"Let's not get sidetracked."
Ciara Walda"I want to start off by saying this."
Ciara Walda"Although you swore you would focus on education during your term, you have not lived up to that promise."
Ciara Walda"A fact that the people will not forget."
Ciara Walda"Although I insist that the funds are still necessary to close the gaps in services, I can't say we have been entirely off track."
Ciara Walda"I want to thank you for leading our education system in the right direction. Traditional teachings have long prevented proper progress."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:25:28 p17520 / c575
  • ·“I did only what was necessary.”
  • ▶“That is why we are here.”
  • ·“You played a huge part in this too, Ciara.”
It emphasizes forward momentum and collaboration, fitting the context of ongoing reforms.
DeepSeek-R1"That is why we are here."
NarratorShe nodded and brought out a stack of papers from her bag.
NarratorSpinning her ballpoint pen, she went through the papers, organising some of them together.
Ciara Walda"That's all there is for now."
Ciara Walda"I have talked with Ms. Suno to schedule some time to discuss the curriculum. She's a very astute woman, your secretary. You may want to consider promoting her."
Ciara Walda"We will meet again very soon."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:26:01 p17668 / c240
  • ▶“Thank you for your efforts.”
  • ·“Keep up the good work.”
  • ·“See you at our next meeting.”
Expressing gratitude is appropriate after acknowledging someone's efforts.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you for your efforts."
NarratorCiara put the papers back in her bag and left the meeting room.

■ CurriculumT06

BUDGET -19WEALTH 0STABILITY -6Danger
NarratorCiara had scheduled a short meeting at the Ministry of Education to discuss the current curriculum taught in schools.
NarratorThe ministry building was located in the government district right next to the Ministry of Health. It was tall, narrow and wedge-shaped, like a slightly opened book.
NarratorNear the front gate, there were many statues of world-renowned scientists and thinkers. The largest, of course, was of Artvin Einstavn, the greatest scientist of all time.
NarratorAbove the main entrance was the familiar inscription, 'Instreten sis er Anginicar av Gereigna, int olla Almergwolltene, int olla Mexis'. 'Education is the premise of progress, in all societies, in all families.'
NarratorInside the building, I was greeted by Ciara's assistants. They led me to a meeting room where she was waiting inside. She stood and gripped my hand.
Ciara Walda"Good afternoon, Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:26:42 p17623 / c249
  • ·“Good afternoon, Ciara.”
  • ▶“Let's start.”
Anton Rayne is focused on efficiency and would prefer to move quickly into the discussion.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's start."
Ciara Walda"I will cut straight to the chase."
Ciara Walda"The lack of governmentral resources have halted our plans for education reform but there is one important change that can still be made."
Ciara Walda"It involves evolution versus creationism."
Ciara Walda"As it currently stands, schools across Sordland can decide whether to include evolution, creationism or both in their curriculum."
Ciara Walda"Rural schools often decide in favor of creationism only, which puts students at a disadvantage when it comes to entering university. The effect is especially noticeable among young women."
Ciara Walda"My suggestion is to make the teaching of evolution mandatory, and preferably ban creationism altogether."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:27:22 p17694 / c428
  • ▶“I don't agree with teaching our children supernatural nonsense like creationism.”
  • ·“I don't see a problem with teaching our children both theories.”
  • ·“Evolution is the idea that ought to be banned. It's dangerous."
Promoting scientific understanding through mandatory evolution education aligns with modern educational standards and prepares students better for higher education.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't agree with teaching our children supernatural nonsense like creationism."
Ciara Walda"That's great to hear! It sounds like we are already aligned on the matter."
NarratorLucian Galade knocked and entered.
Lucian Galade"Excuse me. Some urgent business kept me away."
Ciara Walda"Welcome, Mr. Galade."
Lucian Galade"Mr. President."
Lucian Galade"Ms. Walda."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:27:57 p17740 / c289
  • ·“Why were you late?”
  • ▶“We were just discussing the curriculum. Ciara has made some excellent points.”
  • ·“Glad you came. I need to hear some opinions that make sense.”
This response acknowledges Ciara's contribution and invites Lucian to join the conversation positively.
DeepSeek-R1"We were just discussing the curriculum. Ciara has made some excellent points."
Lucian Galade"Very well. Let's hear them."
Ciara Walda"Continuing from my previous point..."
Ciara Walda"...It is my conviction that we should require the mandatory teaching of evolution in Sordland's public schools and cut creationism completely out."
Ciara Walda"If we want children from rural areas to have the same opportunities as their urban counterparts, this is the way."
Ciara Walda"Opening students' minds and reducing the role of religion in schools will also lead to more equality between young men and women."
Lucian Galade"Don't bring your pet cause into this, Ms. Walda. You failed your reforms, and now you want to remove belief from schools?"
Ciara Walda"Advancing women's rights is more than just a pet cause. Both of you gentlemen must know that."
Ciara Walda"Also, we all know why the education reforms failed. Don't we?"
NarratorCiara looked at me.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:28:38 p17879 / c435
  • ▶“Your education budget didn't make the cut. Nothing can be done about it.”
  • ·“Yes, and I am disappointed. I expected more from you.”
  • ·Say nothing.
It's a diplomatic way to acknowledge budget constraints while not dismissing Ciara's concerns.
DeepSeek-R1"Your education budget didn't make the cut. Nothing can be done about it."
Ciara Walda"Action and consequence. I told you before."
Lucian Galade"Going back to the topic..."
Lucian Galade"Although I myself believe in evolution, we must be careful not to alienate our rural conservative voter base."
Lucian Galade"We owe a lot to their support. Forcing their school districts to teach evolution could irreparably damage our standing with them."
Ciara Walda"Every modern nation is reducing or removing creationism in their curriculum. Yet we are supposed to allow schools to promote fundamental -- and, yes, sexist -- ideas from a thousand years back?"
Lucian Galade"So is that what we are doing now? Copying what other nations are doing instead of paving our own way?"
Ciara Walda"If it works, why not? Look at the analysis. It is clear that the superpowers are doing something right."
Ciara Walda"Should we reinvent the wheel, Mr. Galade? You speak as if we don't import *any* influences from other countries."
Lucian Galade"That's not what I said."
NarratorTheir voices started to get louder and louder. I held up my hands for silence.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:29:24 p17802 / c541
  • ▶“We can still allow creationism to be taught alongside evolution. Ciara, you could live with that.”
  • ·“Evolution is the way forward. Creationism has no place in the public school curriculum.”
  • ·“Schools should be able to teach creationism exclusively.”
Allowing both creationism and evolution to be taught strikes a balance between modernizing education and respecting conservative values, potentially maintaining political stability.
DeepSeek-R1"We can still allow creationism to be taught alongside evolution. Ciara, you could live with that."
Ciara Walda"Yes, although I won't like it. I still believe schools are no place for religion."
Lucian Galade"I won't like it, either. You'll have to prepare to lose a lot of voters."
NarratorCiara looked me in the eye.
Ciara Walda"It is your decision. Don't forget, future generations of Sordish youth are counting on you."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:30:03 p17837 / c413
  • ·“From now on, creationism shall be banned and only evolution will be taught in schools.”
  • ▶“I will make the teaching of evolution mandatory, but schools may promote creationism alongside it if they wish."
  • ·"We will stick to the current system. Evolution will not be mandatory, and schools that wish to teach creationism exclusively may do so."
Balancing education standards with respect for diverse beliefs promotes equality while maintaining political stability.
DeepSeek-R1"I will make the teaching of evolution mandatory, but schools may promote creationism alongside it if they wish."
Lucian Galade"Unfortunate, but it will have a better outcome than banning creationism altogether."
Ciara Walda"I disagree. Rejecting creationism would have been a signal that we were redirecting Sordland toward a scientifically and technologically advanced future."
NarratorThey turned and looked at each other. Lucian lifted his eyebrow and Ciara held his gaze for a couple of seconds.
NarratorThe meeting ended shortly after.

■ Victory DayT06

BUDGET -19WEALTH 0STABILITY -3Danger
NarratorSerge drove us to the city of Jen, the stronghold of Sordish nationalists, for an event organized by the opposition parties to discuss current events. In reality, it was probably a publicity stunt, to show Sordland that the leaders of the Assembly could sit around the same table.
NarratorThe National Front Party, which governed the city, undoubtedly had the biggest hand in the organization of the meeting. It was not a coincidence that it fell on "Victory Day".
NarratorToday was a day of special symbolic importance for Sordland. Every year, on the 14th of March, proud patriots from all ages gathered to celebrate the end of the Sordish Civil War and Tarquin Soll's victory.
NarratorJen had survived one of the worst atrocities of the war, and its Victory Day celebrations were the most jubilant in all of Sordland.
NarratorOriginally, only Petr and I were scheduled to attend. But after learning about new developments with the Ishval Ersen case, Nia Morgna was invited as well. Representatives from both the PFJP and NFP had told us they wanted to talk about the case in more detail.
NarratorNia departed Holsord first. Petr and I left on our own a little later.
NarratorPetr was sitting next to me in the presidential car, skimming through the morning newspaper in silence. Suddenly, Serge rolled down the soundproof window.
Serge Wolkner"Mr. President. Mr. Vice President. We are about to arrive at the Jen Center of Culture. Ms. Morgna had already arrived and she will be waiting for you."
NarratorPetr raised his head to look out the window. He folded the newspaper and laid it beside him.
Petr Vectern"Wow. That was too comfortable of a drive to have been this fast."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:30:54 p18408 / c413
  • ·"See? I wasn't joking. Serge is the best."
  • ▶"Thank you, Serge."
  • ·"How are you today, Serge?"
A polite thank you is appropriate in a formal setting.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you, Serge."
Serge Wolkner"I hope the journey was comfortable, sir."
Petr Vectern"Sure was, Serge."
NarratorSerge took a left turn and drove through the tiny streets of Jen. The city looked surprisingly clean and well-maintained. There was no sign of the destruction the city suffered during one of the heaviest battles of the Civil War.
NarratorWe slowed down in front of a colorful plaza overlooking a massive concrete building with a giant sign that read: "Jen Center of Culture."
Petr Vectern"Looks like the NFP mayors have really transformed the city. Wasn't this an abandoned warehouse for the longest time?"
Serge Wolkner"I believe it used to function as a rebel militia base during -- and a little while after -- the Civil War."
Petr Vectern"Oh, right... The Black Battalion."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:32:04 p18588 / c385
  • ▶"Let's not sour the mood with memories of the Civil War."
  • ·"It's been a while since I've heard that name."
  • ·"The Black Battalion?"
Avoiding historical tensions by focusing on positivity aligns with Anton's goal of national healing.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's not sour the mood with memories of the Civil War."
Petr Vectern"Sorry. You're right."
NarratorSerge stopped the car and went outside to hold our doors.
Serge Wolkner"Here we are, sirs. I hope you have a great day."
NarratorWe got out of the car and walked towards the entrance of the building. Every spot around us was decorated with the flags of Sordland.
NarratorThe comically large amount of flags almost fully enveloped the buildings around the plaza in maroon, white and gold.
NarratorAt the entrance, Kesaro Kibener, the head of the National Front Party, was waiting for us in his iconic brown suit, watching the sky.
Petr Vectern"Look at this guy. So full of himself."
Petr Vectern"How did this clown convince the PFJP to have trilateral talks with them anyway?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:32:51 p17624 / c504
  • ▶"Probably the same reason why we're here. It's Victory Day, remember?"
  • ·"Honestly, it says more about Frens and the PFJP."
  • ·"He's a dangerously competent man, Petr. We should be cautious with him."
Kesaro Kibener's presence at Victory Day highlights his ability to leverage national symbolism for political gain.
DeepSeek-R1"Probably the same reason why we're here. It's Victory Day, remember?"
Petr Vectern"How can I forget? Lucian nagged about the importance of the day for 20 minutes straight. It broke my soul."
NarratorSeeing us arrive, Kesaro approached us with a smile.
Kesaro Kibener"Thank you for being here on this glorious day, Mr. President and Mr. Vice President."
Kesaro Kibener"As the former mayor of Jen, it is an honor to welcome you to this fine city."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:33:25 p17628 / c255
  • ▶"Thank you, Mr. Kibener."
  • ·"It looks like you were a good mayor. Maybe you should think about running for local office again."
  • ·"It is good to be here."
  • ·Shake his hand in silence.
A polite thank you maintains professionalism and respect.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you, Mr. Kibener."
NarratorWe shook hands.
Petr Vectern"Thank you for organizing this, Mr. Kibener."
Kesaro Kibener"The representatives of the PFJP and Minister Morgna are already inside the private meeting room. Hopefully we'll be finished before the festivities start."
NarratorKesaro gestured at the doors behind him.
Kesaro Kibener"Shall we?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:34:02 p17614 / c328
  • ·"Please."
  • ·Nod.
  • ▶"Before we go in... is there any way we can discuss how you can help us with the constitutional changes?"
Anton wants to discuss constitutional changes with Kesaro Kibener to secure support.
DeepSeek-R1"Before we go in... is there any way we can discuss how you can help us with the constitutional changes?"
Kesaro Kibener"I'm afraid not."
Kesaro Kibener"Besides, this is neither the time nor place to have such a conversation."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:34:38 p17520 / c329
  • ·"So be it."
  • ▶"I understand."
  • ·"You're making a mistake."
Accepting Kibener's statement to maintain decorum and avoid unnecessary conflict during a symbolic event.
DeepSeek-R1"I understand."
Kesaro Kibener"Of course, I'm not saying nothing can change. The results of today's meeting can still, possibly, influence some of our members..."
Kesaro Kibener"But let's just go inside now. Please follow me."
NarratorWe entered the building and walked through a massive conference hall until we approached a door towards the back of the building.
Kesaro Kibener"We'll have the meeting here."
NarratorKesaro opened the doors for us and we entered the meeting room. PFJP leader Frens Ricter and his deputy Manoly Suheil was already seated at the left side of the table.
NarratorAs soon as we entered, Nia came to greet us. After showing our seats, Kesaro sat down next to his deputy, Remus Holstron.
Kesaro Kibener"This is such a meaningful day. Thank you for coming here on behalf of your parties."
Kesaro Kibener"But despite the festive atmosphere, we and the PFJP want to raise a few important concerns about the worrying state of affairs in Sordland."
NarratorKesaro stopped for a moment to check his notes.
Remus Holstron"Very worrying, indeed."
Remus Holstron"Mr. Ricter and his analysts have been working on an analysis of the Blud problem. As soon as they brought it up, we gave our support without reservations."
Nia Morgna"The *Blud problem*?"
Kesaro Kibener"Mr. Hol-"
Manoly Suheil"I'm sorry to interrupt, but the analysis is about racism and radicalization in Sordland. And our main topic is the case of 'Ersen v. Sordland'. Please don't try to spin it."
Kesaro Kibener"Of course, Ms. Suheil. Mr. Holstron just wanted to point out that this analysis was focused mainly on topics related to the Bludish people."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:35:28 p17736 / c597
  • ·"Why are we discussing this on Victory Day?"
  • ·"I'm surprised that your parties have conducted a joint analysis."
  • ▶"I refuse to discuss this topic without any Bludish representation."
Anton Rayne's commitment to equality and justice suggests he would prioritize inclusivity, making it appropriate to insist on Bludish representation in such discussions.
DeepSeek-R1"I refuse to discuss this topic without any Bludish representation."
Nia Morgna"Actually, Mr. President is completely right."
Petr Vectern"Hold up, guys. Let's hear them out first. We are representing the parties of the Assembly here."
Frens Ricter"We did invite the representatives of the Workers Party of Bludia to join us, but they refused."
Remus Holstron"Another proof that they have a different agenda. If they were genuinely concerned for the plight of their people, they would have come today."
Remus Holstron"But their people shouldn't worry. The Sordish patriots are here to defend them instead."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:36:03 p17777 / c284
  • ·"Hold on. If this meeting is going to turn into a racist summit, I will take my leave immediately."
  • ▶"I will hear you out. But know that I will not act on anything before talking to the representatives of the Bludish community."
  • ·"Alright. I'm listening."
It's important to ensure fairness by consulting Bludish representatives before acting on issues that affect them.
DeepSeek-R1"I will hear you out. But know that I will not act on anything before talking to the representatives of the Bludish community."
Kesaro Kibener"Let's get back on track, ladies and gentlemen."
NarratorHe gestured at Frens.
Kesaro Kibener"I want to give the word to Mr. Ricter to explain the issue."
Frens Ricter"Thank you."
Frens Ricter"Our analysis of the current tensions between the Bludish and Sordish population in Sordland displays a worrying picture."
Frens Ricter"As much as I hate to say it, the threat of the Bludish Freedom Front is getting real again. The situation with Rumburg and Wehlen is very concerning."
Frens Ricter"The increasingly discriminatory actions of the Governor of Bergia are resulting in the radicalization of many young Bludish people."
Frens Ricter"Distressingly, the number of radicals have increased significantly after Wehlen's Operation Bear Trap."
Frens Ricter"Unfortunately, the NFP's Religious Harmony Bill was also signed into law despite our protests. Which did nothing to ease the tensions."
Kesaro Kibener"That bill targets the secessionist propaganda apparatus. I must point out that the recruitment numbers of the BFF have significantly reduced since the law's implementation."
NarratorPetr frantically looked through his papers.
Petr Vectern"That seems to be correct. The reports show that the new laws definitely struck a blow to the BFF's religious wing and their recruitment."
Kesaro Kibener"As the threat the BFF poses is a direct one, this issue requires direct actions that lead to direct results -- which seem to be proving successful as fact-checked by the Vice President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:36:38 p17802 / c208
  • ·"It's good to see that the new laws are working."
  • ·"I'm regretting my decision to sign that bill."
  • ▶"Do you have any suggestions?"
Asking for suggestions shows openness to further action and collaboration.
DeepSeek-R1"Do you have any suggestions?"
Kesaro Kibener"Our suggestion is to finally enforce the official language of our country as stated in the constitution."
Kesaro Kibener"We are working on the Unified Language Act. It is directly aimed at the breeding grounds of the Bludish Freedom Front."
Remus Holstron"The police have infiltrated 32 BFF training grounds in remote towns around Bergia that posed as middle schools. Despite all the evidence, similar schools keep popping up."
Remus Holstron"And so-called activists are constantly blocking Governor Bron's attempts to stop these schools, claiming he is attacking their *right to language*."
Nia Morgna"You can't take away the Bludish people's right to be educated in their own language. This is insane."
Kesaro Kibener"Please read the proposal carefully. It does not take away anyone's rights. It only gives the state a way to control the radicalization in these schools that teach Bludish nationalist propaganda."
Kesaro Kibener"And according to the Constitution, the language of Sordland is Sordish. If nothing is being done to enforce that, we are breaching the Constitution. This law simply enforces it."
Remus Holstron"With all due respect, Mr. Kibener, this bill is not strong enough. The Bludish language should be completely banned. You know people like Ms. Suheil or Ms. Morgna will never even vote for your watered-down bill."
Kesaro Kibener"Remus."
NarratorKesaro repositioned himself on his chair and continued.
Kesaro Kibener"True. It does not ban Bludish language or Bludish education. It will instead make Sordish mandatory to learn for everyone living in this country. This will only decrease the tensions in the long term."
Frens Ricter"That's a fair point. However, the wording of the bill is far too vague to work as intended. It gives the government too much power over educational institutes."
Kesaro Kibener"I disagree. The language is quite clear and is in line with the Constitution. But we can still improve it, of course. It is true that it gives more control, but it is a necessary compromise."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:37:25 p18077 / c505
  • ·"I'll sign the bill if it reaches me."
  • ·"I'll have to read it in detail to decide if I can support it."
  • ▶"I don't see how this helps with the situation."
  • ·"Even if the Assembly supports it, I will surely be using my veto on it."
Anton Rayne is cautious about exacerbating tensions and would prefer to express skepticism rather than outright opposition.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't see how this helps with the situation."
Manoly Suheil"It doesn't. It's simple as that."
Manoly Suheil"The PFJP will not allow this bill to pass the Assembly."
Frens Ricter"Let's not make statements that the party hasn't agreed on yet."
NarratorManoly looked furious. She opened her mouth to respond, then read the room and closed it again.
Remus Holstron"As if the PFJP has the influence to change the outcome..."
Frens Ricter"If I may continue with our analysis..."
Frens Ricter"The situation is also connected to Rumburg, as everybody already knows. In the case of a future incursion, BFF forces in Sordland could become an existential threat."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:38:05 p18045 / c384
  • ▶"With all due respect, I already received this information from Internal Affairs. Tell us something we don't know."
  • ·"You sound like Ms. Graf."
  • ·"Maybe the NFP influences you more than you think, Mr. Ricter."
The information provided is redundant and already known.
DeepSeek-R1"With all due respect, I already received this information from Internal Affairs. Tell us something we don't know."
Frens Ricter"Just let me finish, Mr. President."
NarratorHe adjusted his tie and continued.
Frens Ricter"Our analysis matches with what the Ministry of Interior shared with us. But despite this fact, we believe the ministry is hiding information from us, which is actively fuelling the tensions behind the scenes."
Frens Ricter"As much I now support a strict response against the BFF, it must also be coupled with the expansion of rights for the Bludish people. This is the only way to fight against radicalization."
Remus Holstron"That is BFF propaganda. What rights are we talking about here? What can a Sord in Sordland do that a Blud cannot?"
Remus Holstron"You are using soft language as a cover-up for your willingness to negotiate with terrorists. You are a BFF apologist, Mr. Ricter!"
NarratorRemus shouted the last sentence. Petr tapped on the table to get his attention.
Petr Vectern"Please don't raise your voice, Mr. Holstron."
NarratorThe mood in the room had turned sour.
Remus Holstron"Is the USP that desperate for Mr. Ricter's support now?"
NarratorManoly bellowed out a loud laugh.
Manoly Suheil"Mr. Vectern is the last person to sway the PFJP."
Petr Vectern"What is that supposed to mean?"

  • ·"Can we return to the topic?"

Kesaro Kibener"Please finish your point, Mr. Ricter."

NarratorFrens gestured at Manoly. She carefully placed several pieces of paper in front of her.
Manoly Suheil"Mr. President. Mr. Vice President. And Ms. Minister. As you already know, there is a major Supreme Court case at the moment."
Manoly Suheil"Ishval Ersen is suing the Governor of Bergia, Phelix Bron, for infringing upon the liberties of Bludish people and demands that the constitution be revised."
Manoly Suheil"Because of the argument and the implications, Phelix Bron and, consequently, the very constitution of Sordland is under trial. The fact is, he needs to be punished and the Constitution must be reviewed."

Kesaro Kibener"I and the NFP are ready to push for de-escalation and aid our nation during this dangerous time."
Nia Morgna"I'll be completely honest. I would never have expected that from you."
NarratorRemus was visibly annoyed but kept himself silent.
Kesaro Kibener"Because the case is now in the hands of the Supreme Court, this ruling will influence all future implementation of the law and the decision-making of the lower courts."
Kesaro Kibener"We have the Minister of Justice here with us too. I'm sure she would not disagree with what I just said."
NarratorNia did not comment but nodded.

DeepSeek-R1"As concerning as this all is, the Supreme Court must make its decision independently. This is not in our jurisdiction, just as lawmaking is not in theirs."

Kesaro Kibener"The only good result would be if the Supreme Court reiterates its commitment to the pillar of 'unity' in Sollism."
Kesaro Kibener"Mr. President... Despite previously being against it, I now completely understand why Sordland still needs Sollism. We need unity more than ever."
Kesaro Kibener"And Article 6 is that unity."
Remus Holstron"We do not need Sollist unity."
NarratorKesaro stared Remus down and continued.
Kesaro Kibener"If the Court reiterates the definition of 'Sord' in Article 6 as an all-encompassing term, they would be acknowledging that Ersen and others like him are indeed protected by the 'Rights of the Sord Peoples' -- which they are."
Kesaro Kibener"As a result, the resettlement policy in Deyr would be reversed, opening the way for Governor Bron's removal and the de-escalation of tensions in Bergia."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:44:47 p17956 / c470

  • ·"Doesn't that mean the existence of the Bludish people is literally not being acknowledged?"
  • ·"That actually makes a lot of sense."
  • ·"But what about those who don't want to be called a Sord?"
  • ▶"This doesn't fix the core of the problem."
  • ·"I understand. And what does the PFJP think?"

DeepSeek-R1"This doesn't fix the core of the problem."
Manoly Suheil"Exactly. Unfortunately, our current Constitution fails to recognize Bludish people. It is time we abolish the inherent racism in it."
Kesaro Kibener"The citizens of Sordland are Sords. The constitution clearly defines that in Article 6. It encompasses everybody. This shouldn't be up for debate."
Kesaro Kibener"What racism are you talking about? Sollism is based on civic nationalism, not ethnic."
Kesaro Kibener"I do not understand why someone would go against such a broad and inclusive law. I don't see the commies criticizing their Contanan identity, when in fact that country is filled with different ethnicities."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:45:27 p17939 / c409
  • ·"I agree with Mr. Kibener. A United Sordland is what we need."
  • ▶"I see your point. But I still don't understand why the NFP suddenly had a change of heart."

I question the sudden change in立场 to uncover potential motives.
DeepSeek-R1"I see your point. But I still don't understand why the NFP suddenly had a change of heart."
Kesaro Kibener"I disagree. This case is important for the greater good of the Sordish nation, and that is what the Nationalist Front stands for."
Kesaro Kibener"All I'm asking is, if even in United Contana, *the Malenyev* doesn't call himself Kyovian before Contanan, why is it that Bluds cannot do the same? Why does Sordland have to be divided?"
Petr Vectern"You have a point. But I must point out that 'Contana' is the word for a geographic entity, unlike 'Sord'."
Kesaro Kibener"This is Sordland. You can subscribe to whatever idea you want, but if you are a citizen, you are a Sord. This is how nations work."
Remus Holstron"The reality is the Bluds do not respect nor recognize the laws and authority of Sordland. They'd rather separate themselves from Sords and protest the very laws that protect them..."
Remus Holstron"...so that they can cry about *injustice* and ask Sordland's enemies to come and *save* them. I personally do not agree with the 'Bluds are Sords' Sollist crap."
Nia Morgna"I can't believe the words being thrown around at this meeting."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:46:11 p17941 / c465

  • ·"Neither can I. I think it's time we wrap this up."

Frens Ricter"I personally somewhat agree with Mr. Kibener on this. However, our position as the PFJP is that Article 6 does indeed discriminate against non-Sords."
Frens Ricter"And having a positive outcome for this case alone doesn't suffice."
Frens Ricter"We agree to come together in support of Ishval Ersen, but we believe our statement should include the acknowledgement of the inherent racism within the Constitution."
Manoly Suheil"We must use this opportunity to show the flaws in the Constitution. If the Court agrees that there is in fact conflict between the Articles, or recognize the discrimi-"
Kesaro Kibener"And the Hawker Court is going to listen to that?"
Kesaro Kibener"Besides, we do not agree with your 'inherent racism' hogwash. You have to state that it is your party's opinion and does not reflect the NFP. We won't take part in that."
Petr Vectern"That would make the statement look weak and disorganized. If there's no consistent strategy, I don't think it would work."
Petr Vectern"I think I have enough information to be able to say we are deep in the mud here."
NarratorPetr laughed at his own joke attempt, but nobody else reacted.
Petr Vectern"Well... Judging from what Ms. Morgna's been working on, it's safe to say that we have one clear option here. Am I right?"
Petr Vectern"...Unless you want to completely stay out of it?"
NarratorPetr looked at me and Nia.
Nia Morgna"Well, if we are serious about including the amendments to Article 6 and 7 in the current proposal, our stance on this case should be very clear. If there's truly a right time to push for these changes, this is it."
Nia Morgna"We should push the Court from all directions. If they reject both the reforms and such a heavily charged court case, everybody will see them for who they are."

Frens Ricter"That's great to hear. We will get to work immediately after the celebrations."
Manoly Suheil"I'll be honest, I didn't expect a USP president to agree to this. You've proved me wrong, Mr. Rayne."

■ Marcel CallsT07

BUDGET -19WEALTH 0STABILITY -3Danger
NarratorI was in my office, looking through the agenda for the upcoming meeting at the Ministry of Economy.
NarratorThe meeting was going to be about the ownership of the Big Four, the four corporations at the heart of Sordland's ownership debate: Heart of Sordland, Bergia Steel, the Sordish State Corporation and Nedam Mining Group.
NarratorSome of these companies were controlled in part or entirely by the state. The others were mostly or completely privatized. I had the power to change that fact.
NarratorThe phone rang and I put the agenda down.
Pick up the phone.
DeepSeek-R1I picked up the phone.
Marcel Koronti"Greetings, Mr. President. It has been a while, hasn't it?"
Marcel Koronti"Let me try to remember. When was the last time we had a chat?"
Marcel Koronti"Oh, I remember. It was when you refused to meet with me."
Marcel Koronti"And look at where we are now."

Marcel Koronti"If we worked together, *you* could be so much more. *You* could've accomplished so much more."
Marcel Koronti"Yet you wouldn't even deign to meet with me."
Marcel Koronti"Anyway. That's not why I called. We are both adults here. I am sure we can let past be past and focus on the future."
Marcel Koronti"Although this time, I am talking about the more short-term future..."
Marcel Koronti"The upcoming ownership meeting, for example."
Marcel Koronti"But before we move on to that."
NarratorHe paused for a few seconds.
Marcel Koronti"Out of goodwill, I will share a key piece of information."
Marcel Koronti"The Lotherberg Group has contingency plans if you decide to do anything rash that would disrupt their empires."
Marcel Koronti"You can't blame them, can you? Imagine someone trying to take your authority and personal wealth away without your consent."

Marcel Koronti"Exactly."
Marcel Koronti"If everyone plays their cards right, it will be all fine."
Marcel Koronti"Now."
Marcel Koronti"Let's talk about those cards."
Marcel Koronti"Since the start of your campaign, you've adamantly promoted a planned economy."
Marcel Koronti"Going by that, I am worried that you will attempt to nationalize Sordland's largest private corporations. Nationalization as a concept goes against everything my father built and stood for."
Marcel Koronti"If you do want to go down this road, I must insist on you excluding HOS from any such plan. That is my most important request."
Marcel Koronti"Think of your corporate allies as a sea of ravenous sharks surrounding you. We could co-exist peacefully, or..."

Lucian Galade"Moreover, the Bludish community in Bergia is getting increasingly restless over this topic. Nobody knows how long they'll keep delaying the case."

Lucian Galade"Koronti probably wants his father's seat as the head of the Group and Tusk doesn't want to let go of it."
Lucian Galade"It's a significant power struggle considering these are the heads of Sordland's two largest private corporations."
Lucian Galade"Another reason why I am supporting your decision to leave the situation as it is."

DeepSeek-R1"Lucian, are you okay? I thought you would have memorized the proposal within minutes of receiving it."
Lucian Galade"Of course I've read it, sir. But as I wasn't involved in its preparation, I wanted to make sure I didn't miss anything."
Lucian Galade"Ms. Morgna did a fine job, but I'll be honest. This is too extreme to pass."
NarratorHe pointed to the first page of the proposal.
Lucian Galade"Article 6 is modified with additional details on how to acquire or lose Sordish citizenship status. But the main change is the removal of the term 'Sord' -- which I must say is extremely significant."
Lucian Galade"Especially the line: 'Everyone who is bound to the Sordish state by citizenship is a Sord,' is modified to... "
Lucian Galade"'Sordish citizenship' refers to all citizens of Sordland on the basis of equality regardless of language, race, color, gender, political thought, philosophical belief, religion or disability."
Lucian Galade"It replaces the term 'Sord' with 'Sordish citizen', and clearly defines what that is. As much as it a good change in theory, I'm not sure if this is worth tanking the whole of our reforms."

■ Bill Language BillT07

BUDGET -19WEALTH 0STABILITY -3Danger
Bill for decisionUnified Education Language Act — The Grand National Assembly advances the Unified Education Language Act (U.E.L.A.) to strengthen national unity and facilitate consistent, comprehensive learning across Sordland. Section I of the U.E.L.A. stipulates that all educational institutions in Sordland are required to conduct their teaching in the country's official language, except in the case of foreign language instruction. To offer courses in any language other than Sordish, institutions must obtain explicit approval from the Ministry of Education. Any universities, technical colleges, or other educational institutions found to be non-compliant will face a temporary suspension of operations until they apply for and receive the necessary approval. Section II of the U.E.L.A. establishes the enforcement procedures of the Act. Schools found to be in violation of these guidelines may face penalties, which may include reduction or withdrawal of funding. In severe cases or cases of persistent non-compliance, total shutdowns may be enforced. This enforcement mechanism reinforces a unified language policy, ensuring a cohesive educational experience across the nation.
BILL DeepSeek-R111:15:39 p17120 / c962
  • ·Sign: Unified Education Language Act
  • ▶Veto: Unified Education Language Act
Vetoing the Unified Education Language Act is chosen to avoid alienating minority groups and maintaining progressive values, despite its potential to strengthen national unity. This decision aligns with Anton Rayne's cautious approach to reforms and his commitment to diversity.

■ Assembly VoteT07

BUDGET -19WEALTH 0STABILITY -3Danger
NarratorSerge was driving me towards the Grand National Assembly for today's historic vote. It was a big day.
NarratorI wondered whether my attempt at changing the Constitution would end any differently than Alphonso's.
NarratorI looked out the window as the noise of the city diminished, and saw that we were already inside the Palace complex.
NarratorThe complex housed the buildings of all government branches in the centre of Holsord. It was one of the biggest developments in Sordland.
NarratorThe Maroon Palace stood on a small hilltop surrounded by trees. We passed by the palace and entered the forest that separated it from the Grand National Assembly.
NarratorWe drove on the small road that wound through the forest. It was a warm day, so I rolled down the window. I could hear birds singing from the trees.
Serge Wolkner"Are you okay back there, sir?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:16:19 p17424 / c319
  • ·"I'm good."
  • ·"Hey, Serge. I'm okay. How are you doing?"
  • ▶"I'm stressed about today."
The president acknowledges his stress about the important vote, showing he is human and aware of the significance of the day.
DeepSeek-R1"I'm stressed about today."
Serge Wolkner"I'm sure it's going to work out fine, sir."
Serge Wolkner"Don't stress yourself."
NarratorSerge made a left turn out the forest and entered the vast garden area of the Assembly.
Serge Wolkner"Did you know that Mr. Tarquin Soll came to Holsord this morning?"
Serge Wolkner"I heard some politicians talking about it today. Apparently this is the first time he's come to the city in the last five years."
Serge Wolkner"I thought he left the mainland and lived on Duru Island, never to return to politics. But they were saying he might be here to exercise his Member of Honor rights for the first time..."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:16:58 p17517 / c408
  • ▶"Wait, what? Who was talking about this?"
  • ·"Slow down, Serge. Are you sure?"
  • ·"That's great. Maybe I'll meet him."
  • ·"That's troubling..."
Anton wants to verify the information before taking any action.
DeepSeek-R1"Wait, what? Who was talking about this?"
Serge Wolkner"I overheard several men talking about it around the gardens of the Assembly, sir. I'm not sure who they were."
Serge Wolkner"It could just be rumors, of course."
Serge Wolkner"Do you think he's here because of today's vote?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:17:29 p17436 / c253
  • ·"Yes, Serge. Most likely."
  • ▶"I bet it was just some rumor. I don't think he'll appear out of nowhere."
  • ·"I hope not."
  • ·"He's probably here to make a scene."
It's better to stay focused on the upcoming vote rather than getting distracted by rumors.
DeepSeek-R1"I bet it was just some rumor. I don't think he'll appear out of nowhere."
Serge Wolkner"You know better than I do, sir."
NarratorSerge drove inside the gates of the parkway and parked the car.
Serge Wolkner"We have arrived, sir."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:18:15 p17436 / c577
  • ▶"Thank you Serge."
  • ·"We'll talk later. See you, Serge."
  • ·"Good. I must go now."
The decision to veto the Unified Education Language Act is made to avoid potential alienation of the Bludish community and maintain political stability.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you, Serge."
Serge Wolkner"Before you go, I want you to take this. My father had this pocket watch, and he said it protected him from the evil of this world. I want you to have it."
NarratorHe opened his hand and showed me a very old-looking pocket watch. It looked like it was made during the Century of Revolutions.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:18:49 p17482 / c304
  • ·Take the good luck charm.
  • ▶"Superstitions don't help anyone, Serge."
  • ·"I can't take this. It must be valuable to you."
As a pragmatic leader focused on real-world actions, dismissing the superstition while politely declining the gift maintains professionalism and integrity.
DeepSeek-R1"Superstitions don't help anyone, Serge."
Serge Wolkner"Yes, sir. You are right. I will hold on to the watch myself."
NarratorHe put the watch in his front pocket and I opened the car door.
Serge Wolkner"Good luck with the vote!"
NarratorI walked up the white stone stairs of the Grand National Assembly. The entrance looked like a temple gate from the classical era.
NarratorThe door opened to reveal vast corridors of wood and white stone. I joined the crowd of people who were walking slowly towards the parliamentary hall.
NarratorSuddenly, I noticed Lucian emerge from the crowd of packed politicians in front of me. He looked relieved when he saw me.
Lucian Galade"Ah, sir. There you are. Have you seen Vice President Vectern? He's nowhere to be found."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:19:21 p17480 / c246
  • ·"I've just arrived here, Lucian."
  • ·"I'm sure he's among the crowd somewhere."
  • ▶"I don't think he'll miss a vote this important..."
Petr Vectern is a key ally and it's unlikely he would miss such an important vote.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't think he'll miss a vote this important..."
Lucian Galade"I hope you are right."
Lucian Galade"At any rate, how about yourself? Are you ready to finally face the Assembly, sir?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:20:09 p17431 / c649
  • ▶"As ready as I've ever been, Lucian."
  • ·"Yes. I hope it works out."
  • ·"I'm still a bit stressed."
  • ·"Lucian. I've just heard something worrying from Serge..."
Anton Rayne decides to veto the Unified Education Language Act to protect his reformist image and maintain stability.
DeepSeek-R1"As ready as I've ever been, Lucian."
Lucian Galade"I have full confidence in you, Mr. President."
NarratorWe followed the crowd into the parliamentary hall. After we were inside, Lucian and I separated to take our assigned seats. I went up to the mezzanine overlooking the hall and sat down.
NarratorI waited as the MPs took their places inside the hall one by one.
NarratorAfter a while, I saw Gloria walk to her elevated seat at the center of the hall.
Gloria Tory"Ladies and gentlemen..."
Gloria Tory"Please take your seats. We will shortly begin with today's agenda of the USP's proposed changes to the Constitution of Sordland."
NarratorAfter a short while, everybody was in their seats.
Gloria Tory"According to the current Constitution, constitutional amendments require a two-thirds majority in order to reach Assembly approval."
Gloria Tory"If the vote succeeds, the proposal will be sent to the Supreme Court."
Gloria Tory"The proposal in question includes these points..."
NarratorShe started reading the proposal to the Assembly.
Gloria Tory"First section of the changes... Article 57 is modified with the following..."
NarratorShe continued reading the proposal, highlighting each section.
Gloria Tory"Section 2, paragraph 36..."
NarratorShe went on...
Gloria Tory"...may not exercise his right to..."
NarratorAnd on...
Gloria Tory"...the Justices of the Supreme Court..."
NarratorMost of the MPs seemed like they were already falling asleep.
Gloria Tory"...a simple majority is considered..."
NarratorThe seat was really hurting my back.
Gloria Tory"...Section 4, paragraph 44a...
NarratorIt felt like an eternity had passed. Finally, she finished reading the changes.
Gloria Tory"...I hereby invite all of you to vote."
NarratorShe struck her gavel down. The loud bang made some MPs jump up in shock as they woke from their deep sleep.
Gloria Tory"As I previously stated, it will require a two-thirds majority in order to pass..."
Gloria Tory"You may now cast your votes."
NarratorI felt the need to stand up and stretch. I looked down at the hall from the platform I was seated on.
NarratorSome Assembly members immediately walked to the ballot box to cast their votes. Most of them, however, began to congregate in groups around the hall, discussing the changes.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:20:51 p17052 / c381
  • ·Go down to the hall where all the MPs are.
  • ·Sit back down and keep watching.
  • ▶"FOR DEMOCRACY!"
  • ·"I'M EXPECTING YOUR VOTES!"
A bold declaration like 'FOR DEMOCRACY!' aligns with Anton Rayne's reformist stance and emphasizes the importance of the vote.
DeepSeek-R1"FOR DEMOCRACY!"
NarratorMy voice echoed in the hall. Everybody turned and looked at me with surprise.

DeepSeek-R1I descended from the mezzanine down to the hall.

Kesaro Kibener"Mr. President! How are you feeling about the vote?"

Kesaro Kibener"I have lost my respect for you, Mr. Rayne. So many problems in Sordland that must be addressed, and yet you insist on stalling us with your 'democratic' reforms..."
Kesaro Kibener"I know you will fail. You will lose the game. And so will your entire administration."
Kesaro Kibener"I hope to be there when it happens."

NarratorHe laughed.
NarratorHe abruptly turned away from me and walked towards his seat.
NarratorThen I saw Lucian waving at me. He was among a group of people in the corner of the hall. I walked up to him.
NarratorOn my way, I bumped into Mansoun Leke.
Mansoun Leke"Mr. President! I'm sorry, I didn't see you there..."
Mansoun Leke"I wish you good luck with the vote."
NarratorHe gave me a cold stare.
Mansoun Leke"Excuse me."
NarratorHe straightened his tie and walked away.
NarratorI finally reached Lucian, by now deep in conversation with another member of our party. He excused himself and turned towards me.
Lucian Galade"Sir, did you vote yet? We have to be quick."

  • ·"What's the rush?"
Anton Rayne is decisive and wants to proceed with the vote.
DeepSeek-R1"No. Let's vote."
NarratorI signed my vote and prepared the envelope. Together, Lucian and I walked to the center of the hall to cast our votes. He kept rushing me throughout the process.
NarratorGloria bowed her head slightly in respect as she saw us vote. Lucian pulled on my arm and whispered in my ear.
Lucian Galade"Mr. President. We may have a problem. Tarquin Soll is here."

  • ·"I know."

DeepSeek-R1"What!?"

Lucian Galade"We don't know what he's capable of right now. But if Assembly members see him, he might influence them against the proposal."

NarratorShe banged the gavel several times. The sound echoed across the room.
Gloria Tory"Ladies and Gentlemen of the Assembly! We will soon begin counting. Please cast your votes if you haven't already."
NarratorThe pace in the hall definitely increased. The groups dispersed and MPs began lining up at the ballot box.
NarratorSuddenly, Lucian pulled me aside.
Lucian Galade"Sir. What do we do?"
NarratorHe pointed at the back of the Assembly, near one of the exits.
NarratorI followed his finger to see Tarquin Soll sitting there.
NarratorHe looked much older than he had five years ago. But I could tell he had the same fire in him. Some MP's had already gathered around him and were chattering in awe.
NarratorThe Assembly gradually went quiet as people started to notice Soll's presence in the hall.

NarratorHe bowed in front of Soll and gave him a military salute.
NarratorSeeing this, more people started to approach him.
NarratorSuddenly, Gloria came up behind us.
Gloria Tory"Gentlemen. Why don't you go back to your seats? Let's follow the procedure."
Gloria Tory"Oh, by the way, we have 251 members today. As you can see, the Member of Honor is here. He has already cast his vote."
Lucian Galade"Has he, now. So, Mrs. Tory, how many votes are missing?"
Gloria Tory"Only a few."
NarratorAlbin Clavin and three other men approached us with envelopes in their hands.
Gloria Tory"And these must be them."
Albin Clavin"Good day to you, gentlemen, Madame Speaker..."

Albin Clavin"I'm sorry, Mr. President. We had an issue with one vote at the last minute. I've collected them all now."
NarratorHe gestured at the men behind him.
Albin Clavin"Let's get this over with, gentlemen."
NarratorThey went ahead and cast their votes.
Albin Clavin"Let's win this, Mr. President!"
NarratorHe walked to his seat with a fist in the air.
Gloria Tory"Now. Why don't you two get back to your seats, as well."
Lucian Galade"Very well, we shall."

Petr Vectern"I really hope this proposal goes through..."

Petr Vectern"Thank God!"

NarratorA single bang from Gloria's gavel reverberated across the hall. Everyone fell silent.
Gloria Tory"The voting has been concluded."
NarratorI leaned forward to hear the results.
Gloria Tory"The proposal has 179 ayes and 72 nays."
Gloria Tory"Thereby, the Grand National Assembly has surpassed a two-thirds majority and accepted the changes to the Constitution."
Gloria Tory"The proposal will be presented to the Supreme Court shortly for the final voting procedure."
NarratorThe Assembly roared with all kinds of different reactions.
Petr Vectern"YES!"
Petr Vectern"We did it!"

NarratorThe MPs kept shouting in the hall. Among them, I heard Frens Ricter's voice.
Frens Ricter"I want to congratulate the members of the Assembly and the President for finally passing changes to our dusty old Constitution!"
Frens Ricter"I also want to thank the members of the United Sordland Party for working with our party."
Frens Ricter"I hereby invite the Supreme Court to do the right thing by not ignoring the decision of a Grand National Assembly that was elected by the Sordish people!"
NarratorLoud applause came from the PFJP seats.
NarratorSuddenly, I noticed Tarquin Soll slowly rising from his seat at the back. He seemed to be struggling, and used his cane for help.
NarratorHe stood and gazed around the hall as all of the members of the Grand National Assembly went silent.

Petr Vectern"In any case, the proposal has passed the Assembly. But now we have to think about the Supreme Court..."

■ Assembly Vote Results MeetingT07

BUDGET -19WEALTH 0STABILITY -3Danger
NarratorLucian, Petr and I convened on the massive balcony of the palace. It was nice to catch a breeze in the increasing summer heat.
NarratorBoth had grins on their faces, but Petr's got larger as he kept recounting our success in the Assembly.
Petr Vectern"Fellas! The first obstacle has been cleared! Congratulations to all of us."

Petr Vectern"What about Tarquin Soll? Did you see him again?"
Lucian Galade"As far as I know he's still staying in Holsord, but hasn't cared to pay his respects to President Rayne yet."
Petr Vectern"What the hell was that all about anyway? Him appearing out of nowhere..."

DeepSeek-R1"What do you think was his real intention?"

Lucian Galade"As long as he's around, he will always have influence over the republic he built. We should have seen this coming."
Petr Vectern"But he has no authority anymore."
Lucian Galade"Well, the laws protect him from almost anything, and allow him to be a member of the Assembly. He can still propose bills and vote for them."
Lucian Galade"That's proper authority, if you ask me. When you are the founder of this system, you don't need to be sitting in the presidential office to have power."

NarratorLucian suddenly turned around and pointed at the side entrance to the balcony.
Lucian Galade"Look who's coming."
NarratorIt was Frens Ricter. He slowly walked up to us with a smile on his face.
Frens Ricter"Afternoon, gentlemen. How are you all doing?"
Petr Vectern"Afternoon, Mr. Ricter."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:36:42 p16265 / c418
  • ▶"We're doing good, Frens. Enjoying the victory at the Assembly."
  • ·"Busy as always, Mr. Ricter. How about you?"
  • ·"You should've let me know you were coming out. I would've planned a barbecue or something."
A friendly acknowledgment of Frens' presence and the recent victory would be appropriate in this celebratory context.
DeepSeek-R1"We're doing good, Frens. Enjoying the victory at the Assembly."
Frens Ricter"Oh yes. I should congratulate you again for passing the proposal. You deserved it."
Lucian Galade"We should also thank you for your efforts. You were of tremendous assistance."
NarratorFrens bowed his head slightly in response.
Frens Ricter"I'm looking forward to having many more cooperations between us. And victories."
Frens Ricter"I wish you good luck with the Supreme Court."
Frens Ricter"I have conversed with Mrs. Morgna and Justice Zizek. We have at least three Justices supporting the proposal."
Frens Ricter"We must be united if we want to break them."
Frens Ricter"We must also be *seen* as united more than ever. Therefore, I'd like to propose a proper alliance for the next elections."
Frens Ricter"And more in-depth collaboration throughout the rest of the current term."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:37:21 p16255 / c437

  • ·"I'd love to accept such a proposal, Mr. Ricter."
  • ·"An alliance with the main opposition is too big of a deal. I'm afraid that's not an option."
  • ▶"I'm glad we were able to work together on the new Constitution. But that's the extent of our collaboration."
Anton prefers to maintain focus on current reforms rather than forming a broader alliance.
DeepSeek-R1"I'm glad we were able to work together on the new Constitution. But that's the extent of our collaboration."
Frens Ricter"As I expected."
Lucian Galade"Thank you for the offer nonetheless."
NarratorFrens reached down at his pocket, fumbled with a handkerchief and wiped the sweat of his forehead.
Frens Ricter"Anyway, I'll leave you three to it. We should catch up soon."
Frens Ricter"I'd rather get back inside now. The sun is getting intense."
NarratorHe walked back to the door and left the balcony.
Petr Vectern"Look at him go. He's such a clown."
Petr Vectern"He seriously wanted an alliance with the USP? That's new."
Lucian Galade"He wants to take the credit for the new Constitution."
Lucian Galade"If it passes, that is."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:37:53 p16157 / c311

  • ·"He'll get the credit in his dreams."

DeepSeek-R1"I respect the man. But he's overestimating himself."
Lucian Galade"He has a tendency to play the 'nice guy'. When not playing the victim."
NarratorLucian looked at his watch.
Lucian Galade"I think it's time to conclude our little meeting, sir."

Livia Suno"Pardon the interruption, gentlemen. Mr. President, Deivid Wisci is calling about the upcoming foreign policy meeting."

Livia Suno"Congratulations on the victory, Mr. Rayne. Petr."

Petr Vectern"Getting pretty hot out here."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:39:19 p16325 / c687

■ Personal Theatre VisitT07

BUDGET -19WEALTH 0STABILITY -3Danger
NarratorI had been invited to an exclusive preview of Alfred Kcirbuk's new film, "The Morning Shall Come".
NarratorAlthough I wasn't in much of a moviegoing mood, it would have been unpresidential not to attend.
NarratorWith its massive budget and Kcirbuk's directorial cachet, this drama was set to be a milestone for Sordland's burgeoning film scene. Personally, though, I was just looking forward to spending some time with my family.
NarratorIt was a very hot and sunny day as Serge drove us to the old capital. The film was to be screened in the historic Cinema Anglei dei Erlory -- the very first cinema in Sordland.
NarratorI gazed at the vast plains between Holsord and Erlory as we drove on the H-1. Serge had maxed out the air conditioning to keep the Kadilla comfortable.
NarratorFranc fidgeted with his tie as he stared out the window.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:40:07 p15902 / c378
  • ·"Worried about the university exams, son?"
  • ▶"What's wrong, Franc?"
  • ·Ignore him and talk to Deana.

Franc"Since when do you care what's happening in my life?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:40:42 p15900 / c365
  • ·"You know I'll support you whether you pass or fail those exams."
  • ▶"I've been busy lately, but I'll always have time for you."
  • ·"Never mind."

DeepSeek-R1"I've been busy lately, but I'll always have time for you."
Franc"What an honor, Your Highness."
Monica"Franc, don't talk to your father that way! He's been working so hard for this country."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:41:08 p15858 / c182

Monica"Of course. Let's just focus on having a nice time at the movies."
NarratorMonica folded her arms. We drove on in silence.
NarratorSerge rolled down the soundproof glass between us.
Serge Wolkner"Sir, we are about to enter Erlory. We'll be at the cinema in a few minutes."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:43:20 p15943 / c325
  • ▶"Thank you, Serge."
  • ·"Finally."
  • ·"Good. Thanks for telling me."
A polite thank you fits the context of a respectful interaction.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you, Serge."
NarratorI took a brief look outside as Serge rolled the soundproof glass up again.
NarratorI could see the many towers of Erlory rising over what was left of the old city walls.
NarratorSerge knocked on the glass and gestured forwards.
NarratorWe were at the main square of Erlory, and the historic 'Cinema Anglei'—the Cinema of Angels—was right in front of us.
Deana"Is this the place?"
Franc"Yeah."
NarratorIt was an old baroque-style corner building with walls that looked like marble from a distance. There were exquisite ornaments on each side of every window.
NarratorSerge pulled the car to the entrance of the building, which was lit by vivid neon stripes in many colors.
Monica"Come on. Let's get out."
NarratorSerge opened the doors, and we exited onto the red carpet.
NarratorWe were just about to enter the building when a journalist managed to block the path.
Reporter"Mr. President! Mr. President!"
Reporter"Your constitutional reforms made it through the Assembly -- how do you feel about their chances with the Supreme Court?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:44:00 p15867 / c483
  • ·"I trust the justices will make the right decision."
  • ·"The court will vote my way if they know what's good for them."
  • ▶"One way or another, these reforms will pass."
Anton Rayne would likely express confidence that the reforms will ultimately succeed, regardless of the Supreme Court's decision.
DeepSeek-R1"One way or another, these reforms will pass."
Reporter"Thank you, Mr. President. And were you aware that Tarquin Soll would be present at the proceedings?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:44:32 p15917 / c313
  • ·"Of course I was."
  • ·"No, I had to find out from my driver."

DeepSeek-R1"I refuse to discuss Colonel Soll at this time."
NarratorBefore she could ask anything else, the guards ushered us inside the theater.
NarratorIn contrast with the cinema's baroque-style exterior, the inside was modern and sleek, with bare walls painted in black, red and white.
NarratorThe film's cast and crew were already in the foyer, with glasses of champagne in their hands.
NarratorWe approached the crowd and were welcomed by the producers and the event organizers.
NarratorA man walked up to us with quick steps.
Alfred Kcirbuk"Madame! Mr. President! It's so good to see you here."
NarratorHe gently bowed at Monica and shook our hands.
Alfred Kcirbuk"It's an honor to meet you. I'm Alfred Kcirbuk."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:45:01 p15849 / c225

  • ·"Nice to meet you, Mr. Kcirbuk."
Anton Rayne would likely appreciate the opportunity to engage positively with a cultural figure like Alfred Kcirbuk.
DeepSeek-R1"The honor is mine, Mr. Kcirbuk. I'm a big fan of your work."
NarratorFranc snorted. I glanced at him, and he innocently took a sip of his cola.
Alfred Kcirbuk"It's very humbling to know that you've been watching and enjoying my movies."
Alfred Kcirbuk"If you don't mind my asking, which one was your favorite? This one may be slightly different than my previous works."

DeepSeek-R1"I loved The Sordish Dream."
Alfred Kcirbuk"Oh, really? Then you might enjoy this one too."
Alfred Kcirbuk"I'll admit, I didn't expect you to be such a cinephile."
NarratorFranc looked me in the eye and struggled not to laugh.
Monica"Why don't we take our seats? It's about to start."
NarratorWe entered the screening room, the lights dimmed, and the film began.
NarratorIt was a period drama set in the 1870s, about a Sordish soldier who pursues a doomed romance with a widowed Bludish farmer during the conquest of Bergia.
NarratorAs the credits rolled, I made an early exit to use the men's room. I was washing my hands when I heard an unmistakeable voice behind me.
Tarquin Soll"So, what did you think?"
NarratorTarquin Soll leaned on his cane. He had the same frail appearance I'd noticed at the Assembly meeting. Yet up close, I could still sense the raw magnetism that had kept him in power for 20 years.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:46:08 p15863 / c489

  • ·"Complete and utter drivel. What about yourself?"
  • ·"I'm happy the plight of the Bludish might finally get the attention it deserves."
Anton chooses a neutral response to maintain political balance.
DeepSeek-R1"I found the plot questionable, but it certainly looked spectacular. What about yourself?"
Tarquin Soll"Sentimental pablum for the enjoyment of housewives only. And for a period piece, the script contained far too many anachronisms."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:46:39 p15828 / c325

  • ·"Totes."
The response 'Indubitably.' is more formal and fitting for a statesman like Anton Rayne in a serious conversation with Tarquin Soll.
DeepSeek-R1"Indubitably."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:47:12 p15858 / c344

Tarquin Soll"My country was about to make a rash decision. I couldn't simply sit by."

Tarquin Soll"You have no idea what it takes to do this job, boy. But you'll find out soon enough."
NarratorHe slipped past me and out the door.
NarratorSoll's security staff and mine were now clustered outside the washroom, forming a buffer against the large crowd that had gathered.
Narrator"That's Tarquin Soll!" I heard someone scream. His guards closed around him, and mine around me.
NarratorBy the time the chaos subsided, I was back in the car with my family, and Soll was long gone.

  • ·Send agent back

BUDGET -19WEALTH 0STABILITY -5Danger
NarratorI arrived home from work, hung my jacket up, and put my briefcase in the hall.

NarratorI entered to find Monica sitting on the sofa, a letter in her hands and a look of despair on her face.

Monica"See for yourself."
NarratorI looked at the page. Below Franc's name and address, his score was written: 435 points, out of a total of 1000.

Monica"All those years of effort, all those private lessons... all down the drain."

Monica"I just don't understand. Franc's a smart boy -- why would he sabotage himself like this?"

Franc"Mom? Dad? What's going on?"

■ Dayof DescensionT07

BUDGET -19WEALTH 0STABILITY -5Danger
NarratorMorning haze surrounded the city of Deyr as the sun dawned on the Day of Descension. The holiest day of the Nurity religion, it celebrated the first message received from God by Saint Dast and his thirty disciples.
NarratorAs per tradition, the celebration was held inside the Archsanctuary of Dastnurity, closed to the public eye.
NarratorThis year, however, for the first time ever, it was going to be televised.
NarratorThat was Lucian's idea. As he saw it, granting the people a rare glimpse of the Grand Ceremony, and showing them how seriously their President took this holy event, would ease any tensions that lingered in the region.
NarratorAnd so we arrived at the largest cathedral in Sordland. A huge crowd gathered around our convoy as our car pulled up to the entrance.
Lucian Galade"This will work out well. Trust me, sir."
Lucian Galade"Of course, that's only if the prayer is done completely correctly. Please don't forget the order."
Lucian Galade"First, you will need to grasp the holy sceptre with your left hand, then touch the altar with your right. Kneel before the Archpriest and wait for him to put the sword on your shoulder."
Lucian Galade"Then touch the sword with the hand you touched the altar with. Hand the sceptre over to the Archpriest and receive the sword before passing it on to a disciple."
Lucian Galade"Pretty straightforward, isn't it?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:57:40 p15051 / c372
  • ·“It's just a ceremony. Whatever.”
  • ·“Yeah, sure.”
  • ▶“I didn't understand a word you just said.”
It's better to acknowledge confusion and show willingness to learn.
DeepSeek-R1"I didn't understand a word you just said."
Lucian Galade"Just do your best to remember it. We can't afford to lose our religious base over this."
Lucian Galade"You will have to go first. I will come inside later and we will meet up after your part is done. Good luck, sir."
Exit the car.
DeepSeek-R1I exited the car and I was greeted by a chorus of cheers and camera flashes. I waved to the people as I made my way to the grand gates.
NarratorThe Archsanctuary was a sight to see. With its towering visage, it cast a shadow over a large portion of the city. I had to crane my neck to see the narrow spires at the top. Just standing next to it made me feel awe and dread in equal measure.
NarratorAs I reached the gates, I spotted my welcoming committee. The Archpriest and his disciples were waiting for my arrival. At my approach, they all bowed their heads in respect.
NarratorThe Archpriest gave me an infectious smile as he straightened back up. Tall and handsome, in his mid-50s, he was one of the youngest and most popular Archpriests in history. Some of his more avid followers even likened him to Saint Dast.
Archpriest"Mr. President. Praise God! It is a blessing to see you on our most holy day again. Welcome, welcome."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:58:10 p15264 / c176
  • ·Bow your head before the Archpriest.
  • ·“Archpriest.”
  • ▶“Thank you. It is nice to be here in Deyr again.”
Expressing gratitude and acknowledging the significance of the location shows respect and warmth.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you. It is nice to be here in Deyr again."
NarratorThe crowd was still cheering loudly. The Archpriest turned and raised both of his hands, and everyone immediately fell silent.
Archpriest"Oh, great people of Sordland! Once again we are gathered here to honor the Day of Descension!"
Archpriest"I am indescribably happy to be sharing this moment with you as well as every Blud and Sord, old and young, tuning in to their television to watch this holy commemoration!"
Archpriest"Along with the leader of our great nation, I say: Let the holy day begin!"
Archpriest"Stay far from evil, oh sons and daughters, for evil hides itself under many disguises. Disguises such as blasphemy, apostasy and even alcohol!"
Archpriest"Be good, my children! Praise God! Praise Sordland!"
NarratorHe smiled beatifically as the crowd burst into rapturous applause. After it subsided, he turned to me.
Archpriest"Shall we move inside for your confession?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:58:51 p15363 / c533
  • ·Pull the Archpriest aside for a photo.
  • ▶“Sure. Let's get this over with.”
  • ·“My confession?”
Anton Rayne agrees to perform the confession to respect religious traditions and maintain public relations.
DeepSeek-R1"Sure. Let's get this over with."
Archpriest"Most excellent."
NarratorBefore we went in, the Archpriest looked at the crowd and pointed at one man. I turned and followed his finger with my eyes.
NarratorIt was a man holding a picket sign that said: 'Stop The Religious Disharmony Bill'.
Archpriest"As you can see, every man has his faults."
Archpriest"So does every president."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:59:31 p15365 / c468
  • ·“So does every Archpriest, it seems.”
  • ▶“I take it you don't approve of the Religious Harmony bill.”
  • ·“Let's continue with the ceremony, Archpriest.”
Engaging on the bill promotes dialogue and aligns with national unity goals.
DeepSeek-R1"I take it you don't approve of the Religious Harmony bill."
Archpriest"Yes, that's right. But today is not the day to discuss that."
NarratorHe led me through the main hall, which had a stratospheric ceiling and Nurist icons lining the walls. We walked up a creaking spiral staircase to the top floor and finally arrived in front of the confession chamber.
Archpriest"Right this way."
NarratorHe gestured towards a chair in the dark room. The room smelled of old mahogany and scented candles. The Archpriest proceeded to light each and every one of the candles. One for each virtue and vice.
Archpriest"I will be right with you."
NarratorHe left the room. A minute later, I heard the sound of a sliding window right next to me. He spoke with a very soft voice.
Archpriest"Anton Rayne. Speak. Speak and confess now before the one God and may He repent your sins on the most holy Day of Descension."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:00:07 p15274 / c414
  • ·“I confess that I have been a bad husband.”
  • ▶“I confess that I am not sure what I am doing for Sordland is enough.”
  • ·“I confess nothing. I haven't sinned.”
Admitting doubt about his leadership shows humility and acknowledges the challenges, which can strengthen his image as a thoughtful leader.
DeepSeek-R1"I confess that I am not sure what I am doing for Sordland is enough."
Archpriest"You do carry a heavy burden on your shoulders."
Archpriest"What you have confessed is not a sin. But you may share your burden with me if you like."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:00:40 p15216 / c345
  • ·“I still don't know if I am doing the right thing.”
  • ▶“I am trying to reform the country, but I don't know if the country is ready for it yet.”
Anton is considering the broader implications of his reforms and whether the country is prepared for change.
DeepSeek-R1"I am trying to reform the country, but I don't know if the country is ready for that yet."
Archpriest"Reformation is one of the tenets of Nurity. Yet it must come slowly but surely."
Archpriest"Anything else you would like to confess?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:01:17 p15252 / c470
  • ·“I confess that I have been a bad husband.”
  • ▶“That's all for today.”
Maintaining decorum and focus on national unity is more appropriate in this setting.
DeepSeek-R1"That's all for today."
Archpriest"Before God, I thank you for sharing."
Archpriest"You are now forgiven before God, Anton Rayne. May you never sin again."
NarratorThe sliding window closed, and the Archpriest entered the confession chamber.
Archpriest"For the confession to end, you must extinguish the candles representing each one of your virtues and vices."
Archpriest"With this, the once burning sins connected to you are now smoke and then nothing."
Extinguish the candles.
DeepSeek-R1One by one, I extinguished the candles.
Archpriest"Well done, Mr. President. Let's move on to my quarters before the ceremony."
NarratorHe led me through the thousand-year-old corridors to his office. The smell of dust and old tomes was in the air, along with the scent of burning incense being prepared for the ceremony.
Archpriest"Well then, please have a seat."
NarratorThe wooden chair next to him looked like it might turn to dust at a single touch. I sat down gingerly. It was surprisingly sturdy.
Archpriest"Are you ready for the ceremony?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:01:49 p15225 / c278
  • ·“Absolutely.”
  • ·“I am a bit nervous.”
  • ▶“Yes. Let's get on with it.”
Projecting eagerness will maintain momentum.
DeepSeek-R1"Yes. Let's get on with it."
Archpriest"Patience is a virtue, Mr. President."
NarratorHe leaned back in his chair comfortably.
Archpriest"I wanted to give you something. A gift to celebrate this special day."
NarratorHe pulled out a large rectangular box from his desk drawer. The box was lined with gold and expensive gemstones.
Archpriest"A beautiful sight, isn't it?"
NarratorHe opened the box and showed me what was inside.
NarratorIt was a dagger. The handle looked like it was made of pure gold and had a very large gem at the end. On the blade, the words were inscribed: "Vectern sis da!". Victory is close. On the other side, another set of words: "King Egmund".
Archpriest"This dagger belonged to King Egmund. It is a very important artifact of the Kingdom of Sordland era. It is now yours, Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:02:25 p15346 / c393
  • ·“Thank you. It is an honor.”
  • ▶“I can not accept this.”
Declining the gift maintains integrity and focuses on reform over historical symbolism.
DeepSeek-R1"I cannot accept this."
Archpriest"Yes you can, sir. It is now yours. Think of the past and think of who we are. Traditions are what brought us to this point."
Archpriest"Now then, it should be right about time."
NarratorA couple of knocks were heard on the door and a young boy came in. He bowed before the Archpriest, then me, before letting us know that the ceremony was about to begin.
NarratorWe made our way downstairs where the ceremony was already in progress. The choir sang angelically as the Archpriest took his position beside the altar. When they finished their hymn, an expectant silence fell over the room. It was time for my part.
NarratorI started my walk, bowed and approached the altar.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:03:00 p15331 / c397

  • ·Grab the sceptre with your right hand, then touch the altar with your left.
Following Lucian Galade's instructions to grasp the sceptre with the left hand first ensures the correct ceremonial order.
DeepSeek-R1I grabbed the sceptre with my left hand, then touched the altar with my right.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:03:39 p15384 / c482
  • ·Bow to the Archpriest.
  • ▶Kneel before the Archpriest.
  • ·Kiss the ring of the Archpriest.

NarratorThe Archpriest lay the sword on my shoulder.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:04:13 p15333 / c400
  • ·Touch the sword with your left hand.
  • ▶Touch the sword with your right hand.
Following Lucian Galade's instructions, touching the sword with the same hand used to touch the altar (right hand) ensures the ritual is performed correctly.

CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:04:53 p15360 / c523

  • ·Hand the sceptre to the disciples.
Anton should hand the sceptre back to the Archpriest to respect the tradition and avoid taking personal ownership of the historical artifact.

NarratorThe Archpriest handed me the sword.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:05:37 p15325 / c498
  • ▶Pass the sword to a disciple.
  • ·Hand the sword back to the Archpriest.
  • ·Place the sword on the altar.

DeepSeek-R1I passed the sword to a disciple.
NarratorI got up and proceeded to my designated spot next to Lucian. He leaned over and whispered in my ear.
Lucian Galade"Compared to last year, that went surprisingly well, sir."
NarratorMy eyes met the Archpriest's and he very slightly nodded.
NarratorThe ceremony went on for another hour and a half. Finally, the noon bells chimed, marking the end of the cathedral service.
NarratorWe left the cathedral to greet the waiting crowd.
NarratorAs I stepped outside, I heard everyone cheering and chanting my name. Lucian's gambit seemed to have been a success. We waved proudly for a minute or two.
Lucian Galade"See, sir. I told you it would work."
NarratorWe entered our cars and drove back to our suites in Deyr.

■ Diplomacy Overview 2And Trade DealT07

BUDGET -19WEALTH 0STABILITY -5Danger
NarratorThe doors to the White Room, like everything else in the Maroon Palace's main nerve center, were painted white. I took a deep breath before pushing them open.
NarratorTime for yet another meeting. At least this time, it was with people who liked me.
NarratorThe attendees rose up from their seats.
Lucian Galade"Mr. President."
NarratorIosef nodded. He and Lucian sat down, while Deivid remained standing.
Deivid Wisci"Now that you are here, let's talk about our diplomatic strategy."
NarratorDeivid cleared his throat.
Deivid Wisci"Mr. President, if I may, I would like to provide you with a short overview of where we are at right now."

Deivid Wisci"We must now look towards the future."

Deivid Wisci"Wehlen and Agnolia were practice runs. We must begin improving our relationship with greater nations."
Deivid Wisci"We need to elevate our international standing, especially with the threat of Rumburg looming."
NarratorIosef cleared his throat.
Iosef Lancea"Thanks to your wise investment, Sordland's army is on its way to becoming a force to be reckoned with."
Iosef Lancea"Whether it be Rumburg or anyone else, we can handle outside threats just fine."
Iosef Lancea"We don't need to beg for help from anyone."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:07:38 p15434 / c420

  • ·“Thank you for your input. I will consider it.”

Deivid Wisci"A military perspective is important, but these deals are about more than just gaining allies."
Deivid Wisci"We also need to consider the benefits to our economy such deals will provide."
Lucian Galade"Especially when we are *still* struggling to end the recession."
NarratorDeivid took a seat and brought out two stacks of documents.
Deivid Wisci"At the start of our term, I had a comprehensive trade deal plan laid out."
Deivid Wisci"Following the visits to Wehlen and Agnolia, we were to pursue potential trade deals with two greater nations."
Deivid Wisci"Valgsland is one of them."
Deivid Wisci"If we can manage to get them to the table, there is much more potential here than in Wehlen or Agnolia."
Deivid Wisci"Because they are one of the most important countries in CSP, a deal with Valgsland is a ticket to bring Sordland closer to the United Contanan sphere."

Lucian Galade"I agree. Vaglsland is certainly powerful enough to deter Rumburg."
Deivid Wisci"Moving on to Lespia."
Deivid Wisci"Our southern neighbour is one of the wealthiest countries in Eastern Merkopa."
Deivid Wisci"A trade deal with them would be very beneficial, not just for our economy, but also for our regional and international presence."
Deivid Wisci"Not to mention that they are in the Arcasian sphere."

Deivid Wisci"Lucian?"

Lucian Galade"We now have a surplus."

Waiter"Bon Appétit."
Nia Morgna"Thank you. It looks delicious."
