BUDGET -19WEALTH 0STABILITY -5Danger
NarratorIt was yet another sunny day, and I was facing another important political figure: Heron Garaci.
NarratorHe had reached out to us and wished for a meeting just ahead of the Court vote.
NarratorWe scheduled to meet outside of Holsord, in the Gentlemen's Club of Petr in Erlory, where we could have some privacy.
NarratorI grabbed a drink and sat down on the balcony of the villa overlooking its vast garden.
NarratorHeron sat next to me in his beige checked suit and took off his white trilby.
Heron Garaci"Very nice location you've set up here, Mr. President."
Heron Garaci"A Gentlemen's Club, huh?"

NarratorHe straightened himself.
Heron Garaci"I want to help you, Mr. President."
Heron Garaci"I can organize the justices to vote for your proposal."

■ Court VoteT07

NarratorBack at the office, I was eagerly waiting for the decision of the Supreme Court regarding our change to the Constitution. I stood up from my chair and looked out the window.

Petr Vectern"Ladies and gentlemen, we are here to discuss our current status and plan the remaining of our term."
Petr Vectern"I know that some of you have had your concerns about our style of governance so far. There will be a time to speak up about these in the meeting today."
Petr Vectern"But I must also thank you all for your hard work so far. We are very lucky to have a team like this. All of you have been invaluable to this administration."
NarratorLileas grimaced at his remarks.
Petr Vectern"Having said that... Now we have reached a critical point of our term. We are nearing the end."
Petr Vectern"We have successfully made changes to the Sordish Constitution. This is a historic accomplishment. Without this team, it couldn't have been possible."
Petr Vectern"The new Constitution has brought us one step closer to the Sordish dream. We should be proud for having strengthened our democracy."
Petr Vectern"Our success with these changes has brought with it great support. Our party --which was divided at the beginning of our term-- is finally coming together once more."
Petr Vectern"So... Let's start the meeting with a brief look at some reports."
NarratorHe fumbled with the stack of papers in front of him.
Petr Vectern"According to the latest polls, we have seen an enormous decrease in our popularity. The public opinion about our party is at an all-time low."
Petr Vectern"Our historic constitutional changes did not reflect well on our citizens."
NarratorHe took out a piece of paper from the stack and inspected it.
Petr Vectern"A major concern of the people is the state of our economy. We are slowly slipping into a depression."
Petr Vectern"This will have dire consequences for our administration."
Petr Vectern"We mustn't lose hope and give it our best. Sordland needs us more than ever."
NarratorPetr turned to me.
Petr Vectern"I'll give the word to the President now."
NarratorHe sat down.

NarratorPetr gestured at Symon and he immediately stood up.
Symon Holl"Mr. President. Dear colleagues."
Symon Holl"Mr. Vice President was pretty on point with his summary of the current economic status."
Symon Holl"He also used the word 'depression.' He couldn't have been more correct. Our GDP has shrank by 15 percent last year."
Symon Holl"This downward trend has broken our economy over the last years. If we cannot contain this in time, we will end our term -- in the truest of sense of the word -- in a depression."
Symon Holl"However, this has not yet reflected well with our debt situation. Our total national debt has increased about 85 billion SR this year. If we maintain such a deficit over the next years we will face a massive debt crisis which could crash the economy."
Symon Holl"The assembly is working on introducing new tax bills which will generate more revenue for the government. These efforts will depend on the government deficit situation."
Symon Holl"On the other hand, unemployment has seen no positive changes. There has been an increase in joblessness. It is now at 22 percent. We need immediate measures to tackle this."
Symon Holl"Hundreds of thousands lost their jobs."

Paskal Beniwoll"I believe we can get into more details in our policy meeting soon."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:43:15 p13989 / c453
  • ·"Thank you, Mr. Beniwoll."
  • ·"These reports keep getting shorter."
  • ▶"Good. Let's move on to the next report."
It is appropriate to keep the meeting moving forward by simply acknowledging the report and proceeding to the next item.
DeepSeek-R1"Good. Let's move on to the next report."
NarratorIosef stepped up.
Iosef Lancea"The Ministry of Defense has a very important report regarding our situation with Rumburg."
Iosef Lancea"The investigations showed that Rumburg was in fact, weaponizing rebels against our state. We have learned that they have been aiding the Bludish rebels in Wehlen too."
Iosef Lancea"Our military must stand ready for any possible attack coming from Rumburg. We have gathered intelligence regarding their military research and production."
Iosef Lancea"They are building a massive army."
Iosef Lancea"We urgently need to increase our own military production."
Iosef Lancea"We cannot let them threaten us like this."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:43:43 p14078 / c293

  • ·"I don't think we should escalate. Let's refrain from sending them a response."
Anton Rayne prioritizes national security over economic concerns, opting to escalate military readiness against Rumburg's threat.
DeepSeek-R1"Make the necessary arrangements, Mr. Lancea. We'll have to use whatever we have to its limit."
Iosef Lancea"Of course, sir."
NarratorDeivid stood up.
Deivid Wisci"Mr. President. Perla has agreed to begin deal negotiations. We will be moving on with that very soon."
Deivid Wisci"The region is very unstable. With the incidents at Rumburg, Wehlen and Heljiland, we must be very cautious."
Deivid Wisci"We can talk in more detail at our foreign policy meeting."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:44:09 p14253 / c252
  • ▶"Thank you, Mr. Wisci."
  • ·"Your concerns have been heard."
  • ·"Good. Let's move on to the next report."
Expressing gratitude is a polite and appropriate response after receiving an important update.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you, Mr. Wisci."
NarratorCiara was next.
Ciara Walda"Our Ministry is working day and night to improve our education system and increase its accessibility."
Ciara Walda"We have accomplished a lot with our new curriculum. Our reforms will be greatly improving our output as a society."
NarratorLileas suddenly stood up.
Lileas Graf"Mr. President."
Lileas Graf"There is an important report from our intelligence officers."
Lileas Graf"We have disrupted an illegal arms deal that was taking place inside our borders. There seems to be several anti-government groups organizing against large-scale attacks."
Lileas Graf"We might need to implement some new measures soon. We will provide you with a more detailed report at a later date."
Lileas Graf"Unfortunately, Governor Bron is continuing his discriminatory policies against the Bluds in the Special Zone."
Lileas Graf"I'm afraid if this continues, we will have a major problem in our hands. The reports of the BFF organizing its forces is very concerning."
Lileas Graf"There is one more important thing to ask of you. I would like to transfer the Gendarmerie under the Ministry of Interior to improve the internal security efforts."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:44:41 p14344 / c371
  • ▶“I haven't heard of this before.”
  • ·“I don't see Iosef accepting this.”
  • ·“If you need more help I can surely arrange a transfer.”
I need to consider the implications of transferring an established military unit and avoid making a hasty decision.
DeepSeek-R1"I haven't heard of this before."
Lileas Graf"I'm still working on the proposal and have legal backing for it. I was going to mention it later."
Lileas Graf"The Ministry of Defense shouldn't be meddling with internal security affairs through the Gendarmerie."
Lileas Graf"We had several incidents where they were too violent too quickly and it stems from military officers giving reckless orders at times."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:45:13 p14436 / c380
  • ·“The military is always a little too harsh.”
  • ·“Well if the security situations gets more problematic then it might be necessary.”
  • ▶“I am not so sure how this would help.”
Anton prefers cautious approaches and would question the effectiveness of reorganizing security forces without clear benefits.
DeepSeek-R1"I am not so sure how this would help."
Lileas Graf"I can assure a better internal security structure with cohesion and successful operations."
Lileas Graf"Will you transfer the Gendarmerie to the Interior in the future?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:45:46 p14354 / c409
  • ·“I will support you in the Gendarmerie authority transfer. Now it's your turn.”
  • ▶“I can't make that promise. Iosef would be furious.”
I cannot commit without consulting Iosef, as it may disrupt current security arrangements.
DeepSeek-R1"I can't make that promise. Iosef would be furious."
Lileas Graf"That is unfortunate."
NarratorLileas smiled faintly.
NarratorNia stood up.
Nia Morgna"The Supreme Court has delayed the Ishval Ersen case again. Indefinitely."
Nia Morgna"Mr. Hawker and his cronies are adamant about protecting Governor Bron and the Special Zone."
Nia Morgna"In this process, Mr. Ersen gained a lot of popularity and is now leading a peaceful protest movement against Governor Bron."
Nia Morgna"I'm afraid if this goes on for longer, there will be chaos in Bergia."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:46:24 p14357 / c509

  • ·"All noted. Thank you, Ms. Morgna."
Inviting suggestions can lead to more effective problem-solving.
DeepSeek-R1"I expected some suggestions."
Nia Morgna"I can update you more in another meeting."
NarratorNia took her seat.
"Hmm, I believe that is everything."
DeepSeek-R1"Hmm, I believe that is everything."
NarratorOne by one, my ministers packed up and left the White Room.
NarratorI followed suit and headed to my office.
NarratorThe last two and a half years had gone by so fast.

■ Address To The NationT07

BUDGET -19WEALTH 0STABILITY -5Danger
NarratorLucian and I walked into my office in the Maroon Palace to make my Address to the Nation speech.
NarratorIt was unusually crowded today. Cameras, lights and microphones were set in place and the crew was standing by, ready to start filming.
Lucian Galade"Are you ready, sir?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:47:09 p13855 / c280
  • ▶"Yes. Let's do it."
  • ·"I am always ready."
  • ·"I guess."
The response is confident and proactive, fitting a leader ready to address the nation after significant achievements.
DeepSeek-R1"Yes. Let's do it."
Lucian Galade"Good. I think the crew is ready. As soon as you take your seat, we can go live."
NarratorI took my seat and rearranged my notes on the desk as a crew member powdered my face with a cotton puff.
NarratorAfter everybody was ready, the director gave the sign to start filming. I looked directly into the camera.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:47:43 p13916 / c412
  • ·"My fellow citizens..."
  • ·"My fellow Sords..."
  • ▶"Brothers and sisters..."
The term 'brothers and sisters' conveys unity and solidarity, fitting for addressing the nation after successful constitutional changes.
DeepSeek-R1"Brothers and sisters..."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:48:06 p13903 / c176
  • ·"I am happy to have this opportunity to talk to you about the changes we have undertaken in Sordland."
  • ·"I am grateful to earn the honor of finally modifying the Constitution of Tarquin Soll."
  • ▶"I'd like to speak about our future, about a great historic effort to give the words 'freedom,' 'fairness,' and 'hope' new meaning and power."
Option 2 highlights the vision for the future and the meaningful impact of their reforms.
DeepSeek-R1"I'd like to speak about our future, about a great historic effort to give the words 'freedom,' 'fairness,' and 'hope' new meaning and power."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:48:35 p13935 / c348
  • ·"Two years ago, I spoke to you of change, and we have accomplished that."
  • ·"I am proud of what our government has accomplished. We have finally brought fairness and balance to our once-decrepit system."
  • ▶"We have reached a turning point in Sordland's history."
The speech emphasizes a turning point in Sordland's history, highlighting the significance of recent reforms and future direction.
DeepSeek-R1"We have reached a turning point in Sordland's history."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:49:04 p13955 / c331
  • ·"We need to remember where Sordland was two years ago. Those days are behind us now, and the change we brought has been real."
  • ▶"Our new Constitution is a second revolution in the history of Sordland that truly transforms the course of government with just one word: Democracy."
  • ·"The new system brings a more transparent democracy that will help Sordland rise up in the world."
The new Constitution is a key achievement and should be highlighted as a transformative moment.
DeepSeek-R1"Our new Constitution is a second revolution in the history of Sordland that truly transforms the course of government with just one word: Democracy."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:49:41 p14009 / c501
  • ▶"We transformed a system that had caused endless confusion and resentment into one that is clear, simple, and fair for all."
  • ·"We made a Constitution that no longer protects the rights of a select few elites, but ensures the participation of all."
  • ·"For the sake of fairness, simplicity, and growth, we had to radically change the structure of our government system."
The response highlights the transformation from confusion to clarity and fairness, aligning with Anton Rayne's focus on clear policies and equity.
DeepSeek-R1"We transformed a system that had caused endless confusion and resentment into one that is clear, simple, and fair for all."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:50:07 p14050 / c222
  • ·"By now I'm sure you have one question on your minds: Will our reforms help you? You bet they will. We see them as the democratization of Sordland because they close loopholes that benefit a privileged few."
  • ▶"I've spoken with and received letters from thousands of you. I know how hungry you were for change. Make no mistake. Together, we can change Sordland forever."
  • ·"Henceforth, our government shall no longer be able to obstruct the will of the people."
Option 1 effectively communicates the widespread desire for change and acknowledges the people's support, fostering trust and unity.

CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:50:37 p14085 / c338
  • ·"New term limits will ensure that no single person can block change when it's most needed."
  • ▶"We now ensure that neither I nor any other person who holds the position of president will pose the risk of ruling by decree."

  • ·"A new age is upon us!"

CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:51:07 p14072 / c340

CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:51:43 p14070 / c473

TURN 08

■ Personal Franc LeavesT08

BUDGET -19WEALTH 0STABILITY -5Danger
NarratorIt was a cloudy, rainy day in Holsord.
NarratorThe gloomy weather couldn't dampen my spirits. My constitutional reforms had passed in court. I had won.
NarratorThat wasn't the only cause for excitement in the Rayne household. Today, Franc was finally leaving home.
NarratorMy son piled the last of his bags next to the door. Serge and the assistant driver were waiting outside next to the car.
Monica"Did you pack everything?"
Monica"Don't forget to give me a call *immediately* after you arrive."
Franc"Mom. I'm moving less than an hour away. You'll see me all the time!"
Monica"I know. But it won't be the same."
NarratorMonica helped him put his backpack on while Serge started carrying the luggage to the car.
Monica"I made you a sack lunch! I'll get it from the kitchen."
NarratorShe dashed down the hall. Franc and I turned to face each other in the doorway.
Franc"So, I guess this is it."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:56:36 p14242 / c285
  • ·"I guess it is."
  • ▶"You're going to do great."
  • ·"I gave you a second chance. Don't screw this one up."
Choosing option 1 provides positive reinforcement, which is appropriate for a significant life event like starting university.
DeepSeek-R1"You're going to do great."
Franc"Thanks, Dad."
NarratorFranc shifted his weight nervously.
Franc"You still never told me about your university years, but I know I've had it easy compared to you. I hope Holsord State gives me a chance to prove myself the same way you did."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:57:03 p14326 / c281
  • ·"For your sake, I hope that chance never comes."
  • ·"Just don't do anything stupid."
  • ▶"Don't model your life on mine. You're your own man now, Franc."
  • ·"You don't have to prove yourself to anyone. Especially not me."
Emphasizes Franc's independence and encourages him to forge his own path.
DeepSeek-R1"Don't model your life on mine. You're your own man now, Franc."
NarratorFranc smiled.
Franc"All right, Dad."
NarratorAt that moment, Deana rushed to the door.
Deana"Franc! Franc!"
Deana"I'm gonna miss you."
Franc"I'll miss you too. Don't forget, you've got to give Mom and Dad an extra hard time for both of us now."
NarratorDeana nodded.
Deana"Okay."
NarratorFranc picked up his sister and gave her a big hug. Monica appeared in the doorway, holding a paper bag.
Monica"I cut the crusts off your sandwich, just the way you used to like."
Franc"I'm not a kid anymore, Mom."
Monica"Can't a mother pretend? Oh, Franc!"
NarratorShe embraced him with tears in her eyes. Franc looked as if he was about to cry as well.
NarratorSerge approached.
Serge Wolkner"Sir, as per the schedule, Franc needs to be at Holsord State University to start his orientation in an hour."
Serge Wolkner"We will have to leave now to make it."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:57:32 p14315 / c208

  • ▶"Time to go, Franc."
It's time for Franc to leave for university.

  • ·"See you soon."

DeepSeek-R1"Make me proud."
NarratorFranc nodded, before heading to the car.
Serge Wolkner"It's difficult to see them grow and leave, isn't it?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:58:21 p14377 / c164
  • ·"It's not like we will never see him again."
  • ·"Home will feel a bit empty now."
  • ▶"It is time for him to find himself in the world."

Serge Wolkner"He will. We all did, after all."
Serge Wolkner"Living away from home, making your own choices for the first time... it changes a man."
Serge Wolkner"I think it will be a liberating experience for him. It's never easy for boys to live in the shadow of their fathers."

Monica"There he goes."
Monica"It already feels different."

Monica"At least he has a father he can boast to his new friends about. *Anton the Great Reformer*. Sounds good, doesn't it?"

■ Late Highway OpeningT08

BUDGET -19WEALTH 1STABILITY -5Danger
NarratorThe day before the grand opening ceremony of the H-3 Highway, we arrived in Lachaven to begin preparations.
NarratorAlthough the delay had taken its toll on our morale, it was still a proud moment for all of us.
NarratorLucian and I, together with project leads Symon and Lileas, met to discuss how to proceed.

Lucian Galade"First of all..."
Lucian Galade"This day may have arrived later than expected, but it doesn't change the fact that we are here now."
Lucian Galade"This is still a big success. Congratulations, everyone."
Symon Holl"To many more projects like this."
Lileas Graf"Well done."

Lucian Galade"It's been a long time since the last successful infrastructure project. From a public relations viewpoint, we can certainly capitalize on the occasion."
Lucian Galade"I have a plan for how to do that."
Lucian Galade"The president himself shall make the maiden voyage from Lachaven to Arvory along with his motorcade. If he agrees, of course."
Lucian Galade"What are your thoughts?"

The H-3 Highway improves travel efficiency between key cities, aligning with infrastructure goals.
DeepSeek-R1"Thanks to this highway, travel between Lachaven and Arvory is twice as fast and infinitely more comfortable."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:04:36 p14773 / c340
  • ·“None of this would be possible without the sound advice of my Interior Minister Lileas Graf. Please come over here.”
  • ·“Ensuring the success of this project wasn't just one of my presidential goals. I made it my personal priority."
  • ▶“I must thank the brilliant mind behind the highway project, the esteemed Minister of Economy and Tax, Symon Holl. Please come over here.”
Symon Holl led the infrastructure project, making him the most deserving of recognition.
DeepSeek-R1"I must thank the brilliant mind behind the highway project, the esteemed Minister of Economy and Tax, Symon Holl. Please come over here."
NarratorSymon walked up to me.
Symon Holl"Thanks, Mr. President."
Symon Holl"Our aim with this project was obvious: to improve the speed and efficiency of transportation."
Symon Holl"After we cut that ribbon and you drive on the highway, you can judge our success for yourself."
NarratorSymon continued to speak about how this project was a stepping stone towards a Sordish economic recovery.
NarratorAfter he was done with his speech, Lucian took Symon's place on the stage to close the ceremony.
Lucian Galade"Citizens of Lachaven! There you have it!"
Lucian Galade"As promised, Anton Rayne -- the 4th President of Sordland -- has delivered!"
NarratorWe walked towards the entrance of the highway next to the stage.
NarratorA 40-meter-long red ribbon stretched across it.
NarratorSymon handed me a pair of oversized scissors.
Cut the ribbon.
DeepSeek-R1I cut the ribbon, triggering cheers from the crowd.
Symon Holl"Congratulations to all. Hopefully this is one of many successes to come."
Lileas Graf"It's a good moment indeed. It gives us pride as a nation. One big step forward."
NarratorAs soon as we left, the highway opened to the public and thousands began using it.
NarratorIt was a great victory for my administration.

■ Presidential PowersT08

BUDGET -19WEALTH 1STABILITY -5Danger
NarratorLucian and I sat in my office, waiting for Petr. I was at my desk and he was on the sofa, legs crossed.
Lucian Galade"Sir. I have some news for you."
Lucian Galade"Mr. Holl and I have been working on several possible plans to boost the economy, as is our focus."
Lucian Galade"He is currently compiling a very detailed report with suggested projects."
Lucian Galade"I'll make sure you have a chance to see it soon."
NarratorHe looked at his watch again.
Lucian Galade"Looks like Mr. Vice President is late yet another time."
NarratorHe lowered his voice.
Lucian Galade"I am slightly concerned about Petr."
Lucian Galade"He seems to have his mind occupied. He's been late to almost every meeting we've had recently."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:05:23 p14377 / c366
  • ▶"I noticed it too. I need to talk to him in private sometime."
  • ·"I know Petr well. There is nothing to be concerned about."
  • ·"I don't know what's wrong, but I also do not appreciate him being late all the time."
Addressing the issue proactively by planning a private talk shows leadership and consideration for team dynamics.
DeepSeek-R1"I noticed it too. I need to talk to him in private sometime."
NarratorSuddenly there was a knock at the door.
NarratorIt was Petr. He walked inside with files in both hands.
Petr Vectern"Gentlemen!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:05:51 p14419 / c254
  • ·"Welcome Petr. Have a seat."
  • ·"Finally. Let's begin the meeting."
  • ▶"Why are you so late?"
Addressing the lateness directly allows for understanding any underlying issues.
DeepSeek-R1"Why are you so late?"
Petr Vectern"We were stuck in traffic around Soll Square. There was a whole gaggle of ladies trying to organize an illegal march. We had to wait for the police to show up."
NarratorHe placed the files on my table, took out several documents and organized them in front of me.
Petr Vectern"Here is everything, just as you asked. Our plans for our next reforms."
Petr Vectern"Here are some suggested reforms. You can pass these with your decrees as you like."
Petr Vectern"What are your thoughts, Anton?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:06:29 p14449 / c503
  • ·"I have a few ideas myself."
  • ▶"Let's go over the documents."
Reviewing the documents ensures informed decision-making and acknowledges Petr's effort.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's go over the documents."
NarratorI took a look at the documents.
One-time decree panel4 decree(s) available, 3 slot(s) remaining
DECREE DeepSeek-R113:07:03 p14601 / c416
  • ·Fair Trade and Competition Commission — Trade and commerce in Sordland has few regulatory measures resulting in unfair competition at times. A new Fair Trade Commission can be established via decree to push for fair market competition by protecting local industries through new regulations.
  • ▶Free Prescription Medicine — Prescription medicine in Sordland is partially paid by the Ministry of Health according to the importance of the medication. A new decree would allow all citizens to get their medication completely for free.
  • ·Lower Retirement Age — The retirement age of Sordland had been increased to 65 in the mid 1940's due to economic stagnation. The older voting blocs are requesting a decrease. Our administration can pass a decree that would lower the retirement age to 60.
  • ·Supreme Council of Sordish Radio and Television — Freedom of speech is protected in Sordland but with the rise of new media private organisations gained a massive influence on society. With a new decree we could create a government agency; the SRSC that could protect the independence of the media or control it.
  • ·Done — enact no more (used 0/3 slots)
Implementing free prescription medicine provides immediate relief to citizens, reducing healthcare costs and improving public health, which can help stabilize the economy.
One-time decree panel3 decree(s) available, 2 slot(s) remaining

  • ▶Fair Trade and Competition Commission — Trade and commerce in Sordland has few regulatory measures resulting in unfair competition at times. A new Fair Trade Commission can be established via decree to push for fair market competition by protecting local industries through new regulations.
  • ·Lower Retirement Age — The retirement age of Sordland had been increased to 65 in the mid 1940's due to economic stagnation. The older voting blocs are requesting a decrease. Our administration can pass a decree that would lower the retirement age to 60.
  • ·Supreme Council of Sordish Radio and Television — Freedom of speech is protected in Sordland but with the rise of new media private organisations gained a massive influence on society. With a new decree we could create a government agency; the SRSC that could protect the independence of the media or control it.
  • ·Done — enact no more (used 1/3 slots)
Establishing the Fair Trade and Competition Commission would help regulate market practices, protect local industries, and promote fair competition, supporting economic stability.
One-time decree panel2 decree(s) available, 1 slot(s) remaining
DECREE DeepSeek-R113:08:06 p14501 / c341
  • ▶Lower Retirement Age — The retirement age of Sordland had been increased to 65 in the mid 1940's due to economic stagnation. The older voting blocs are requesting a decrease. Our administration can pass a decree that would lower the retirement age to 60.
  • ·Supreme Council of Sordish Radio and Television — Freedom of speech is protected in Sordland but with the rise of new media private organisations gained a massive influence on society. With a new decree we could create a government agency; the SRSC that could protect the independence of the media or control it.

Lowering the retirement age could free up job opportunities for younger workers and potentially stimulate economic growth, addressing current unemployment issues.
NarratorFollowing a brief discussion, I took notes about my decisions.
Petr Vectern"The Fair Trade Commission will be essential in dealing with the monopolies."
Petr Vectern"It was time we established a body that regulates economic competition."
Petr Vectern"It's also great that we'll be giving out free medicine. Our poorer regions definitely suffered from the lack of it."
Lucian Galade"However, this will put a massive burden on our government budget. It will also be very difficult to build infrastructure in order to provide medicine equally and efficiently."
Lucian Galade"Thankfully, this policy could reflect very well on the vote, especially with the rural and the poor, if it's executed correctly."
Petr Vectern"Yeah, I agree."
Petr Vectern"And lastly... It looks like we are finally decreasing retirement age. This was a very popular demand. A good choice, definitely."
Petr Vectern"But it will be a huge burden for our government to provide for so many people."
Lucian Galade"Yes. It will have serious complications in the future. However, it is also true that this will reflect very positively in the upcoming elections. It's what the public asked for, after all."
Petr Vectern"Well, we both respect your decisions, Anton."
Petr Vectern"But now that everything's settled, I should get to work. We'll catch up soon."
NarratorLucian looked at his watch.
Lucian Galade"We actually have six more minutes."
Lucian Galade"But very well. Let's call it here."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:08:40 p14564 / c324
  • ▶"Thank you both."
  • ·"Thank you, Lucian."
  • ·"Thank you, Petr."
Recognizing both Lucian and Petr's contributions with a collective thank you.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you both."

■ Lespia VisitT08

BUDGET -20WEALTH 1STABILITY -2Danger
NarratorThe Maroon Train slowly started to move.
NarratorThis train had been in service for many years. It had nine locomotives with special quarters and an office. As the name implied, it was painted bright maroon.
NarratorAmong camera flashes and cheers, we departed from the Holsord central train station. Our destination was Perla, the capital of Lespia. The historic route we were using was also called The Southern Express.
NarratorWe passed through the Lespian mountain range to the lowlands, with a short stop at the beautiful Sarea Nueva where Lespian security forces joined us.
NarratorLooking outside the window, the difference in wealth between Valgen and Sarea Nueva was extraordinary.
NarratorI heard a knock on the door to my office on the train and Symon entered.
Symon Holl"Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:09:15 p14555 / c107
  • ▶“Symon. Have a seat.”
  • ·“Yes? What is it?”
Inviting Symon to sit shows openness and respect.
DeepSeek-R1"Symon. Have a seat."
NarratorHe sat down on a chair in front of my desk.
Symon Holl"I wanted to have a quick chat about Prime Minister Alvarez and give you a heads up."
Symon Holl"There have been some rumours about his...demeanour in public appearances."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:09:45 p14766 / c304
  • ▶“Go on.”
  • ·“What do you mean? What did he do?”
  • ·“I can handle him. Don't worry.”
I need to understand the situation before responding.
DeepSeek-R1"Go on."
Symon Holl"He is known to have met with other state leaders while intoxicated on various occasions."
Symon Holl"Some even call him an alcoholic."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:10:13 p14732 / c289
  • ▶“Is this something that could affect this trade deal?”
  • ·“So the man can't handle his drink. Is that it?”
  • ·“It should be fine. I have dealt with worse than that before.”
Asking directly about the impact on the trade deal ensures clarity and addresses potential risks.
DeepSeek-R1"Is this something that could affect this trade deal?"
Symon Holl"Honestly, I don't know."
NarratorHe handed me a newspaper. The headline was 'Circling Over Staalport'.
Symon Holl"This happened just yesterday. Apparently he was so drunk that he couldn't get off the plane on a diplomatic visit."
Symon Holl"After this incident, some say he is unfit to serve. But I don't believe that at all."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:10:47 p14699 / c391
  • ·“Why is that?”
  • ·“I hope he doesn't show up drunk to our meeting too.”
  • ▶“These are the Lespians people fear? Ridiculous.”
President Rayne wants to maintain composure and focus on the potential benefits of the meeting rather than dwell on negative rumors.
DeepSeek-R1"These are the Lespians people fear? Ridiculous."
Symon Holl"Regardless, I don't think we should underestimate them. They hold substantial economic power, after all."
Symon Holl"Also, we need to be careful. I heard that he is a shrewd man."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:11:20 p14719 / c423
  • ·“Thanks for giving me a heads up.”
  • ▶“Trust me. I can handle him.”
  • ·“Now I am worried.”
Expressing confidence shows leadership and the ability to handle potential challenges during negotiations.
DeepSeek-R1"Trust me. I can handle him."
Symon Holl"I do. I am sure we will be fine, Mr. President."
Symon Holl"We are almost at Perla. I will return to my cabin now."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:11:51 p14673 / c347
  • ▶“Very well.”
  • ·“I'll see you when we arrive.”
The president dismisses the rumors about Prime Minister Alvarez and focuses on moving forward with the trade negotiations.
DeepSeek-R1"Very well."
NarratorA few minutes later, with a long whistle, our train came to a stop. I waved to the crowd from the window.
NarratorThe crowds of reporters that flocked over were pushed away by the Lespian security guards. We made our way to our cars and the convoy began its short trip to the Yellow House.
NarratorThe Yellow House was situated at the very centre of Perla in the middle of a circular park that was off limits to regular citizens. The park was full of roses on small patches.
NarratorThe convoy came to a stop and I made my way to the entrance. The assistants directed me to a room to wait for Prime Minister Alvarez.
Wait.
DeepSeek-R1I sat in the exquisite room and looked up at the golden chandeliers.
NarratorAfter ten minutes. there was still no sign of Prime Minister Alvarez. It was almost time for our meeting.
NarratorMy chair started to feel uncomfortable. I stood up and walked around to look at the various items in the room. A beautiful golden teapot piqued my interest.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:12:19 p14744 / c258
  • ·Inspect the teapot.
  • ▶Leave it alone.
It's more important to focus on the upcoming meeting rather than inspecting an object.
DeepSeek-R1I admired the teapot and left it alone.
NarratorI went back to my chair.
NarratorThe door opened and Patricio Alvarez entered.
NarratorAlthough I knew that he was one of the youngest state leaders in the world, I was still surprised by how boyish he looked. At his age, he was leading one of the strongest countries in Eastern Merkopa.
NarratorHe was holding his head like he had a headache and he was stumbling. Upon seeing me, he immediately composed himself. We shook hands.
NarratorHe gestured towards the two couches and we sat down face-to-face.
Patricio Alvarez"President Rayne. Welcome to Perla."
Patricio Alvarez"Good to have you here. I hear a lot about your successes."

Patricio Alvarez"I try, Mr. Rayne."

To address the issue directly and show concern for his state of mind.
DeepSeek-R1"Have you been drinking?"
Patricio Alvarez"A couple of glasses of the finest Lespian wine is the best way to start any meeting."
Patricio Alvarez"Would you like some?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:13:33 p14729 / c312

DeepSeek-R1"No, thank you."
Patricio Alvarez"Very well."
Patricio Alvarez"Let's begin then."
Patricio Alvarez"So, you are here for the trade deal."
Patricio Alvarez"What was it about again? Can you remind me?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:14:03 p14695 / c316

  • ▶“We want to provide Sordish fishing output in return for Lespian capital.”
Option 1 is a practical approach that focuses on the trade deal's specifics, maintaining professionalism despite Alvarez's condition.
DeepSeek-R1"We want to provide Sordish fishing output in return for Lespian capital."
Patricio Alvarez"Ah. Yes. Right."
Patricio Alvarez"Sorry. I have just been very busy lately."
Patricio Alvarez"You know...being the Prime Minister makes you pretty busy."
Patricio Alvarez"So, I don't have any issues with this deal."
Patricio Alvarez"I think it will contribute nicely to the relationship between Lespia and Sordland."
Patricio Alvarez"I will sign the executive order."
Patricio Alvarez"I do, however, believe that we can do much more than just trade."
Patricio Alvarez"We...by we, I mean ATO..."
Patricio Alvarez"...Would like to see Sordland in our sphere."
Patricio Alvarez"What do you think, Mr. Rayne?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:14:37 p14752 / c409

  • ·“Why would you do that for Sordland? What's in it for you?”

DeepSeek-R1"Thank you for the offer but that is not one of our aims for now."
Patricio Alvarez"For now...maybe."
Patricio Alvarez"But where will you stand when United Contana attacks Eastern Merkopa, as it surely will?"
Patricio Alvarez"That is the real question."
NarratorHe clenched his fist and slammed it on the coffee table.
Patricio Alvarez"Those damn communists!"
Patricio Alvarez"We need to stop them before it's too late."
Patricio Alvarez"Valgsland, those barbarians, are preparing to invade Heljiland on false accusations."
Patricio Alvarez"If Agnolia is first, Sordland will be next."
Patricio Alvarez"I will be frank. Eastern Merkopa's only way to survival is through ATO."
Patricio Alvarez"And we need the support of one of the largest players in Eastern Merkopa. Sordland."
Patricio Alvarez"The beautiful country of Sordland is the key to all of this. We need you, Mr. Rayne. We need Sordland."
Patricio Alvarez"Are you with us?"
Patricio Alvarez"Will you defend our continent against the blight of communism?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:15:12 p14940 / c452
  • ·“Down with the communists. I am willing to do whatever it takes to protect Sordland.”
  • ·“I think you are exaggerating the threat. I have my doubts about this.”
  • ▶“I can't decide without knowing what is in it for me.”
I need to know the terms and benefits before committing.
DeepSeek-R1"I can't decide without knowing what is in it for me."
Patricio Alvarez"I see that you possess the mind of a man who understands business."
Patricio Alvarez"I offer you an alliance. It will come with a special aid package from me."
Patricio Alvarez"Together we will shield the continent against Malenyev's evil."
Patricio Alvarez"Not to mention that you will have Arcasia's full backing. The most powerful ally you can have in this era."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:15:52 p14987 / c563
  • ·“I accept. Sordland will be allied to Lespia.” [+2 Government Budget]
  • ▶“I have to refuse. I will not drag Sordland into an international conflict.”
Declining the alliance allows Sordland to focus on domestic recovery without overextending its resources.
DeepSeek-R1"I have to refuse. I will not drag Sordland into an international conflict."
Patricio Alvarez"You think you are evading the conflict by trying to stay neutral."
Patricio Alvarez"Remember my words when the conflict is at your doorstep, ringing your doorbell, Mr. Rayne."
Patricio Alvarez"Don't forget that I gave you a choice."
Patricio Alvarez"I can just say that I hope Sordland doesn't fall into a bad position because of your decision."
NarratorHe looked at the clock.
Patricio Alvarez"I'm afraid I have to leave now to attend to other matters."
Patricio Alvarez"Thank you for your visit."
NarratorHe smiled and left the room.

■ Personal Petr TalkT08

BUDGET -20WEALTH 1STABILITY -2Danger
NarratorIt was a dark, chilly evening when Petr called and asked if we could meet. He gave no details -- all he said was that it was important.
NarratorI drove alone through Holsord's suburbs until I reached the meeting point he named: 147 Wruhec Avenue, number 26. Our old campaign headquarters, untouched since the night of the election. This was where everything began.
NarratorI made my way up the dusty staircase of the office building. The light in the second-floor hallway was out, so I used a cigarette lighter to find my way to the wooden door marked 26. I pulled on the handle, but it wouldn't budge. The heavy door had always been a pain to open.
Petr Vectern"Coming."
NarratorPetr pushed on the door with all his weight. It made a screeching sound as it opened.
Petr Vectern"Without further ado, I bid you welcome to this grand nostalgia trip, hosted by yours truly, Petr Vectern!"
NarratorHe made an exaggerated bow. The dust from the opened door was still settling down.
Petr Vectern"Feeling sentimental yet?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:16:37 p14749 / c362
  • ▶“Indeed. I haven't been to this place in ages!”
  • ·“Not really. It's just an office.”
  • ·“I remember it as being much cleaner.”
Anton Rayne would likely respond with nostalgia and respect for the place where their journey began.
DeepSeek-R1"Indeed. I haven't been to this place in ages!"
Petr Vectern"I miss it sometimes. Even that damn door."
Petr Vectern"Come over here. Remember this?"
NarratorHe walked over to his old desk and pointed to the gramophone that had annoyed our neighbors and kept us company for so many late nights. Petr filpped the switch, and it filled the room with swing music from the '20s.
Petr Vectern"I'll be damned. It still works."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:17:16 p14865 / c540
  • ·“Are you going to tell me why we are here?”
  • ▶“So, what are we celebrating?”
  • ·“I thought this office was sold off.”
The nostalgic setting invites a curious response about the purpose.
DeepSeek-R1"So, what are we celebrating?"
Petr Vectern"Life, my friend. Life."
NarratorHe put his hand under the desk and brought a bottle out. He took a swig out of it and passed the bottle to me.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:17:50 p14846 / c376
  • ·“Aren't there any glasses?”
  • ▶Drink.
  • ·“No, thank you.”
Accepting the drink directly shows openness and solidarity in a nostalgic setting.
DeepSeek-R1I took a swig as well before passing the bottle back to Petr.
Petr Vectern"That's my boy."
NarratorPetr snapped his fingers to the music while going through the items in the room. It looked virtually unchanged since election night.
Petr Vectern"Come on, have a look around. See if it doesn't bring back memories."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:18:19 p14836 / c262
  • ▶Check your old desk drawers.
  • ·Look through the storage boxes.
  • ·Go through the records next to the gramophone.

Checking the old desk drawers could reveal valuable historical insights or sentimental items from their time together.
DeepSeek-R1I walked over to my old desk, sat down on the squeaky chair and opened the top drawer.
NarratorInside, there were a few old coins, pencils and sheets of paper. Underneath them, something caught my eye.
NarratorIt was a photograph of me and Monica, sitting under a tree. This must've been 10, maybe 15 years ago. We looked so young and so happy. Monica would love to see this.
Petr Vectern"Nice find. Look at baby Anton! You didn't even have your iconic look."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:19:03 p14950 / c576
  • ▶Take the photograph.
  • ·Put the photograph back.
Anton takes the photograph to keep a cherished memory of himself and Monica, symbolizing their connection amidst the pressures of presidency.
DeepSeek-R1I took the photograph and slid it into my jacket pocket.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:19:41 p15014 / c413

  • ·Go through the records next to the gramophone.

DeepSeek-R1"Enough going through old stuff. Let's talk."
Petr Vectern"Yes, yes. All in good time."
NarratorHe gestured to the bottle he had in his hand.

  • ▶Drink.
Anton accepts the drink to foster camaraderie and facilitate open dialogue with Petr.
DeepSeek-R1I took a swig out of it before passing it back to Petr.

DeepSeek-R1I stayed silent.
Petr Vectern"Let's go up to the roof. The view was always amazing from there."
NarratorWe went up the stairs to the roof. The view was mesmerizing. The buildings of Holsord twinkled below us, while the stars shone bright in the sky.
NarratorWe sat and leaned back against a wall, taking it all in.

  • ·"Who else knows besides me and the three of you?"
To address the potential scandal and seek strategic advice from Lucian.
DeepSeek-R1"It's too late. There's still a chance this could get out. I need to talk to Lucian."
Petr Vectern"Anton, please! I'm begging you."
NarratorThere was an edge of desperation in Petr's voice. He took another drink.
Petr Vectern"How many years have we known each other? How many scrapes have we gotten ourselves out of? I can handle this myself. Just trust me and don't bring anyone else into this."
NarratorI turned to face my friend and Vice President. The habits that had gotten him dubbed the "bad boy of Sordish politics" when we were young were no longer so charming coming from a middle-aged man. Yet he was still a capable politician, and I couldn't ignore our long history together.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:24:28 p15145 / c370
  • ·“All right. It's just an affair, and it's over at that. We can weather this."
  • ·“You've done enough damage. I will handle it myself.”
  • ▶“I will be blunt. You will fix this or I will remove you from office."
Anton Rayne prioritizes integrity and loyalty, making it necessary to address the situation firmly to maintain trust within his team.
DeepSeek-R1"I will be blunt. You will fix this or I will remove you from office."
Petr Vectern"Yes Anton. I understand. Thank you so much."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:25:00 p15134 / c266
  • ·"Don't worry. Mistakes happen."
  • ▶"Anything for my best friend."
  • ·"Don't mention it. I mean it -- *don't mention it*."
Choosing option 1 shows unwavering loyalty and reassures Petr that he has a friend who will stand by him through thick and thin.
DeepSeek-R1"Anything for my best friend."
NarratorWithout waiting for his reaction, I left the rooftop alone and made my way downstairs.
NarratorOn my way out, I grabbed my belongings and took one last look back at the office. It had been home to some happy memories for sure, but this visit had permanently cured me of any nostalgia.
NarratorAs soon as I arrived home, I headed up to my study, sat down and stared at the telephone.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:25:35 p15155 / c308
  • ·Call Evelyn.
  • ▶Call Lucian and tell him what happened.
  • ·Trust Petr and call no one.
Calling Lucian would ensure the issue is handled professionally and discreetly to prevent a scandal.
DeepSeek-R1I picked up the phone and dialed Lucian's number.
NarratorHe picked up very quickly.
Lucian Galade"Yes, Mr. President."
NarratorI told Lucian everything Petr had told me about himself, Evelyn and Livia.
Lucian Galade"Thank you for calling me, Mr. President. This serves to confirm some suspicions of my own. We have to meet."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:26:18 p15116 / c519
  • ·"Tomorrow?"
  • ▶"Right now?"
  • ·"What suspicions?"
Anton would prefer to address the sensitive situation immediately to prevent potential scandals from escalating.
DeepSeek-R1"Right now?"
Lucian Galade"Tomorrow morning in your office. Early, before either of the two arrive."
Lucian Galade"Don't bring this up with anyone else. I fear we may have more than a mere dalliance on our hands."
NarratorThe line dropped.
NarratorI stared at the receiver, wondering what his last statement meant. What was going on that I didn't know about?
NarratorI went to bed, but couldn't sleep at all.

■ Affair MeetingT08

BUDGET -20WEALTH 1STABILITY 0Danger
NarratorIt was early in the morning after my talk with Petr. The sun was just beginning to peek over the horizon.
NarratorThe Maroon Palace was empty save for a few cleaning staff. I entered my office to find Lucian already waiting for me.
Lucian Galade"Mr. President. Thank you for meeting with me at this hour."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:27:14 p14788 / c454
  • ·"I couldn't sleep anyway."
  • ▶"Cut to the chase."
To address the potential scandal efficiently and maintain focus on resolving serious issues.
DeepSeek-R1"Cut to the chase."
Lucian Galade"Very well. I'm pleased that you chose to call me after your conversation with Mr. Vectern last night -- although I was already aware of his illicit relationship with Miss Suno."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:27:47 p14888 / c265
  • ·"I figured as much. You do know everything that goes on in the Palace, after all."
  • ▶"How did you find out?"
Lucian Galade is known for his behind-the-scenes operations and keen awareness, making it plausible he knew about the affair.
DeepSeek-R1"How did you find out?"
Lucian Galade"I'll get to that."
Lucian Galade"On its own, given Mr. Vectern's established reputation as something of a playboy, the affair is unlikely to cause much damage. However, a more troubling possibility has been brought to light."
Lucian Galade"A recent conversation with Mr. Hailstone, our whistleblower from Rumburg, revealed the presence of a spy within the Maroon Palace."
Lucian Galade"I believe Miss Suno is our mole. She has been using her relationship with Mr. Vectern to gather information."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:28:20 p15102 / c312
  • ·Laugh incredulously.
  • ▶"Tell me more."
  • ·"You're being paranoid, Lucian."
  • ·"That's preposterous."
An open and curious response invites further explanation.
DeepSeek-R1"Tell me more."
Lucian Galade"I can do better than that -- I'll show you."
NarratorLucian reached into his coat pocket, pulled out an audio tape and fed it into the reel-to-reel machine on my desk.
Lucian Galade"This was recorded in the Palace at 18:09 on the second day of your trade visit to Lespia."
NarratorHe pressed play. I heard Petr and Livia's voices.
Livia Suno"You're sure everyone's gone for the day?"
Petr Vectern"Relax, Liv. Nobody's working that hard with Anton off in Lespia."
NarratorLivia giggled. I heard the sound of kissing, then of drinks being poured.
Livia Suno"Mmm. I'm glad you stayed behind."
Livia Suno"Did he tell you how it's going over there? Do you think there's a chance they'll form an alliance?"
Petr Vectern"No, doesn't seem like it. We'll have to find other alternatives if we're going to keep Rumburg at bay."
Petr Vectern"It seems like we've still got a shot with the superpowers, at least."
Petr Vectern"We've opened two new lines of communication with Ventrie City and Kyow. Just exploratory talks at this point, but they might lead to something deeper."
Petr Vectern"And speaking of something deeper, Miss Suno..."
NarratorLucian grimaced and switched the tape off.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:28:56 p15090 / c330
  • ·"That's it? That's your damning evidence?"
  • ▶"This is indeed cause for concern."
  • ·"Curiosity about foreign relations doesn't make one a spy."
  • ·"And then what happened?"
The conversation indicates potential misuse of sensitive information, warranting concern.
DeepSeek-R1"This is indeed cause for concern."
Lucian Galade"I'm glad you agree."
Lucian Galade"And that's not all. I found her resume on file and there's a year-long gap between her finishing secretarial school and beginning her career."
Lucian Galade"Normally, this would have been looked into. But Miss Suno was hired before a background check could be completed. By one Petr Vectern."
NarratorJust then, there was a knock on the door. Livia opened it cautiously.
Livia Suno"Good morning, Mr. President, Mr. Galade. I didn't realize you'd be in so early. Can I get you some coffee?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:29:29 p15192 / c305
  • ·Wait for Lucian to say something.
  • ·"Coffee would be great, actually."
  • ▶"Close the door. Have a seat."
Directly addressing the tension by asking her to close the door and sit down.
DeepSeek-R1"Close the door. Have a seat."
NarratorLivia sat. She looked puzzled.
Livia Suno"Is something the matter?"
Lucian Galade"Enough pretending. Your secret is out."
Livia Suno"I... I'm not sure what you're talking about."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:30:04 p15179 / c353

DeepSeek-R1"Livia. Lucian has evidence."
NarratorLivia buried her face in her hands.
Livia Suno"I'm so sorry, Mr. President. I was foolish, and Petr was so nice to me... But it's over now. Nobody ever needs to know--"
Lucian Galade"That's not what the President means, Miss Suno. We're referring to you being an intelligence agent for Rumburg."
NarratorLivia sat upright. She looked shocked.
Livia Suno"Pardon me, Mr. Galade, but are you feeling all right?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:30:40 p15158 / c401

DeepSeek-R1"He's fine. What do you have to say for yourself?"
Livia Suno"Mr. President, I understand if you don't want me to keep my job, but why smear me with outlandish accusations?"
Lucian Galade"Miss Suno, indulge me for a moment. What were you doing in the year 1951?"
NarratorLivia's face flushed.
Livia Suno"If you must know, I was working as a cigarette girl at Porto's. It's a Lachaven saloon. I was saving up so I could afford my own place in Holsord."
Livia Suno"Here's the proprietor's number if you need to double check."
NarratorShe scribbled a telephone number on a piece of paper. Lucian snatched it up.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:31:10 p15241 / c265

DeepSeek-R1"But why were you asking Petr about my alliances?"
Livia Suno"To tell the truth, Mr. Rayne... I was hoping to run for office myself someday. I want to make a real difference in the world, like Ms. Morgna or Ms. Walda."
Livia Suno"But I guess I messed that up by getting involved with Petr. Now they're all just going to say I slept my way to the top."
Livia Suno"Wait. How did you know about that conversation?"
NarratorThere was a loud pounding on the door. Petr barged in without waiting for an answer. He was unshaven, and wore the same clothes as the night before.
Petr Vectern"I came as soon as I heard the three of you were in here. Anton, what the hell are you doing? I told you I had this under control."

Petr Vectern"God, why involve her? This is my mess. She did nothing wrong."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:32:29 p15561 / c332

DeepSeek-R1"Lucian has the preposterous idea that she's a spy."
Petr Vectern"I-- what?!"
Lucian Galade"I have reason to believe she's been relaying information to Rumburg. Information that you leaked to her in your carelessness."
NarratorLucian rewound his tape and played it again. As Petr listened, his eyes widened in disbelief.
Petr Vectern"Lucian, you rat bastard. You've been bugging my office!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:33:10 p15552 / c512

DeepSeek-R1"Lucian, is this true? How could you?"
NarratorBoth Petr and Livia were glaring at Lucian now. He looked unrepentant.
Lucian Galade"I had noticed the Vice President's behaviour becoming increasingly erratic and unprofessional over the past months. I wanted to get to the root of the cause."
Petr Vectern"So you wiretapped your own goddamn colleague? And for what? Have you been getting off on this, you pervert?"
Lucian Galade"Certainly not. I believe that along with the Rumburg whistleblower's admission that there is a spy in the Palace and certain gaps in Miss Suno's resume, this tape makes a compelling case to investigate the secretary for espionage."
Lucian Galade"Whether or not the investigation yields positive results, Mr. Vectern ought to be held accountable for his willingness to divulge such sensitive information. He ought to stand trial or, at the very least, resign."
NarratorPetr's jaw dropped. Livia stood up.
Livia Suno"If I may, Mr. President. I've been paying attention to what goes on in the Palace. Not because I'm some sort of agent, but out of genuine interest. Hard as that may be for you to believe, Mr. Galade."
NarratorShe scowled at Lucian, then turned back to me.
Livia Suno"And from what I've seen, this man has been attempting to undermine your vice president at every turn. Perhaps with the goal of replacing Petr himself."

Lucian Galade"My personal misgivings about Mr. Vectern aside, I would never do anything to harm this administration."

■ Politics Bills BriefingT08

BUDGET -20WEALTH 1STABILITY 0Danger
NarratorThe morning sunlight streamed through the windows as Lucian and I entered the White Room.
NarratorI sat down. Lucian remained standing.
Lucian Galade"I'm afraid I won't be able to join for your meetings today, sir."
Lucian Galade"The Livia Suno affair has left me with much to catch up on."
NarratorHe dropped a thick folder in front of me.
Lucian Galade"I was hoping to go over the day's agenda with you before your cabinet members began arriving. But duty calls."
NarratorHe walked out. Almost immediately, as if she'd been waiting for him to leave, Livia entered with a carafe of coffee,
NarratorShe poured me a cup with a smile, then took a look down at the folder in front of me.
Livia Suno"It looks like you have quite the packed agenda for today, Mr. President."
Livia Suno"I'm sorry again about the disturbance my indiscretion caused. I hope it didn't create too much extra work for you."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:37:01 p15053 / c241

  • ·"My work is none of your business."
The most neutral response that acknowledges Lucian's role in creating the disturbance.
DeepSeek-R1"Lucian's madcap accusations created the disturbance, not you."
Livia Suno"He did seem to be trying to protect you, in his own way."
NarratorShe walked out. I enjoyed a split second of solitude before the morning's first meeting.
NarratorInternal affairs was first on the list of items. Lileas and Nia, along with their close staff, joined me around the table.
Lileas Graf"Shall we begin?"

Lileas Graf"The lack of action taken in this arena is disappointing."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:38:02 p15357 / c183

DeepSeek-R1"I am disappointed about Governor Bron's attitude as well. But we must try to find solutions instead of blaming each other."
NarratorShe simply nodded.
Nia Morgna"In any case, as long as Governor Bron retains his position, the central government can never properly decrease the tensions between our Sordish and Bludish communities."
Nia Morgna"Therefore, I cannot say that everything is going great for Bludish rights in this country."
NarratorShe sighed.

Lileas Graf"We'll keep it brief. I think that will be all for our internal affairs update."
NarratorThey gathered their documents.
Nia Morgna"See you later, Mr. President."

NarratorSymon came in shortly afterwards.
Symon Holl"Mr. President. Where's the Vice President today?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:39:05 p15292 / c381
  • ▶"He's thinking very hard about what he did wrong."
  • ·"I don't know. Have you seen him?"

DeepSeek-R1"He's thinking very hard about what he did wrong."
Symon Holl"I see."
NarratorSymon's expression remained unchanged as he sat down to face me.
Symon Holl"I just wanted to provide you with a few updates on the economy."
Symon Holl"You may have noticed that the government budget is in need of a boost."
Symon Holl"My ministry is therefore working on a bill that will increase the tax on tobacco products from 10 to 30 percent."
Symon Holl"A similar bill is being drafted to introduce a 20 percent tax on alcohol."
Symon Holl"In addition to bringing in needed revenue, the new laws would curb excessive drinking and smoking in Sordland."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:39:46 p15350 / c468
  • ·"Very good. Those are nasty vices anyway."
  • ·"We can't tax people's booze and cigarettes. There'd be riots in the streets."
  • ▶"Interesting initiatives. But would the income we'd receive be worth the harm to Sordish businesses?"
Considering both the potential budget benefits and the possible negative impacts on businesses, it's important to evaluate if the revenue gain outweighs the economic harm.
DeepSeek-R1"Interesting initiatives. But would the income we'd receive be worth the harm to Sordish businesses?"
Symon Holl"Good question. The tax wouldn't apply to products designated for export, which would mitigate the damage somewhat."
Symon Holl"Ordinarily, I'd advise against this kind of thing. But with our budget dipping into the red, any measure that would reverse our deficit is worth considering."
Symon Holl"Thankfully, tourism to Sordland is on the rise, so we can expect to see a healthy stream of income there."
Symon Holl"Our investment in cultural preservation paid off, it seems."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:40:34 p15393 / c631
  • ▶"That's good news. Thanks for telling me."
  • ·"Oh. I hope the increased threat of the BFF won't put a damper on that."
  • ·"Why wouldn't tourism be skyrocketing? Sordland is the best country in the world!"
Acknowledging Symon's initiatives positively while maintaining a balanced approach to economic and social policies.
DeepSeek-R1"That's good news. Thanks for telling me."
Symon Holl"I never quite understood why anyone would want to vacation in Sordland when Lespia is right there. But I suppose our country has a certain charm."
NarratorThere was a knock on the door. Gus Manger strolled in and grabbed a seat.
Gus Manger"Mr. President. Symon."
Symon Holl"Thanks for joining us, Gus. I believe you wanted to update the President on the latest developments in the Gruni region?"
NarratorGus nodded vigorously.
Gus Manger"Yes, you both know how revitalizing the region has always been my pet project of sorts."
Gus Manger"Well. I'm saddened to say that despite my ministry's best efforts, we're going to have to close the books on this project."
Symon Holl"'Close the books', Mr. Manger? What does that mean?"
NarratorGus sighed.
Gus Manger"It means that without a massive injection of funding, the economic growth I had hoped to see in the region is simply not going to happen during this term."
Gus Manger"What could have been a beacon of success for Sordland is turning into a cesspool of crime, unemployment and homelessness."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:41:13 p15560 / c432
  • ·"Perhaps this wouldn't be happening if you'd focused as much attention on Gruni as you have on your side ventures."
  • ▶"You can't just give up. I know how much Gruni means to you."
  • ·"I knew it from the beginning. It made no sense to try to turn Gruni into something it wasn't."
It's important to show support for Gus's passion despite the challenges.
DeepSeek-R1"You can't just give up. I know how much Gruni means to you."
Gus Manger"Not just to me, Mr. President. It's the breadbasket of this nation."
Gus Manger"I still believe it's brimming with untapped potential. It saddens me to no end to see that go to waste."
Symon Holl"Try to calm down, Gus. The President makes decisions on behalf of the entire country, not only your home region."
NarratorThe door to the White Room opened slowly, and Deivid made his way in.
Deivid Wisci"Oh, hello, gentlemen. I'm early again, aren't I?"
Symon Holl"Not to worry, Mr. Wisci. We've just about wrapped things up."
NarratorSymon and Gus rose from their seats and left the room.
NarratorAfter the door closed, Deivid regarded me for a second. His blue eyes seemed to see right through me.
Deivid Wisci"How are you, Anton? You look as if you haven't slept in ages."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:41:54 p15804 / c444

The response acknowledges the tough situation while maintaining professionalism and openness.
DeepSeek-R1"It's been a rough few days, Deivid."
Deivid Wisci"I'll be very brief. Perhaps we can even carve out some time for you to rest your eyes."
Deivid Wisci"Regretfully, tensions are continuing to mount between Sordland and Rumburg."
Deivid Wisci"As you might imagine, our decision to grant asylum to their whistleblower Mr. Hailstone did not sit well with the Kingdom."
Deivid Wisci"We must exercise caution. The slightest provocation on either side could cost us our chance at reconciliation."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:42:20 p15794 / c157

  • ▶"You're right as usual. The last thing we want is a war on our hands."
  • ·"I don't even know if peace is possible after so many years of animosity."
Choosing caution avoids potential conflict and maintains peace.
DeepSeek-R1"You're right as usual. The last thing we want is a war on our hands."
Deivid Wisci"A compromise in the name of the greater good may spare countless lives."
Deivid Wisci"I'll leave you to it, Anton. I know you've got a lot on your plate."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:42:55 p15796 / c338

  • ·"Thanks for your brevity. Have a good day."
  • ▶"We'll talk soon."

■ Investigation ResultsT08

BUDGET -20WEALTH 1STABILITY 1Danger
NarratorSeveral days had passed since the confrontation with Petr, Livia and Lucian in my office.
NarratorLucian was still acting frosty towards me for rejecting his advice. On the other hand, both Petr and Livia were unnaturally nice to me, though they avoided speaking to each other.
NarratorI was just settling in behind my desk when Lucian knocked on my door.
Lucian Galade"Sir, a moment of your time?"

  • ·"Of course."
  • ·Motion for him to come in.
  • ▶"Not now."
The President is likely still upset with Lucian over recent accusations and prefers not to engage further without a clear reason.
DeepSeek-R1"Not now."
Lucian Galade"I'm afraid it's urgent."
NarratorHe entered my office and closed the door behind him.
Lucian Galade"I still believe you were wrong not to pursue an investigation into Miss Suno. However, I acknowledge my lack of concrete evidence and unorthodox information gathering method may have given you pause."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:44:16 p15460 / c229
  • ·"I could have been less rude to you about it, though."
  • ·"You're not going to change my mind about this."
  • ▶"Unorthodox? You surveilled Petr behind my back!"
Calling out Lucian's unorthodox methods directly addresses his actions and undercuts his argument.
DeepSeek-R1"Unorthodox? You surveilled Petr behind my back!"
Lucian Galade"I have already apologized to Mr. Vectern for the intrusion. It won't happen again."
NarratorLucian looked me in the eye.
Lucian Galade"I feel I would be failing in my duties as a chief strategist not to give you one last piece of advice on this."
Lucian Galade"Please, consider dismissing Miss Suno from her position at the palace. Just in case I am right after all."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:44:44 p15462 / c237
  • ·"Well, she has been a distraction to Petr..."
  • ▶"I can't. She's so good at her job!"
  • ·"Are you crazy? She'll go public about the affair."
Livia Suno is highly capable in her role, so removing her would be counterproductive.
DeepSeek-R1"I can't. She's so good at her job!"
Lucian Galade"Her looks may be unmatched, but her skills most certainly are not."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:45:13 p15487 / c267
  • ·"All right. If it'll get you off my case, I'll fire her."
  • ▶"No. I won't continue to indulge your paranoid fantasies. Livia stays."
Livia is innocent until proven guilty, and her skills are too valuable to risk without concrete evidence.
DeepSeek-R1"No. I won't continue to indulge your paranoid fantasies. Livia stays."
NarratorLucian looked as if he was about to object one last time, but then smiled thinly.
Lucian Galade"As you wish. I won't bring this up any further."
NarratorHe walked out and closed the door behind him.

■ Womens Rights MeetingT08

BUDGET -20WEALTH 1STABILITY 1Danger
NarratorI was sitting in my office. I looked at the clock.
Narrator2:26 PM. It was almost time for my appointment with Ciara on women's rights.
NarratorThere was a knock at the door. Ciara entered the room with folders in her hand. The door did not close behind her.
NarratorMonica entered the room and stood next to Ciara.
Ciara Walda"Mr. President."
Monica"Anton, before we start..."
Monica"I just wanted to thank you for agreeing to this meeting."
Monica"I've been dreaming about this ever since I volunteered with the Sordish League of Women. I can't believe the moment's finally here."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:45:57 p15683 / c352
  • ·“Anything to make you happy."
  • ·“I think both of you together could finally bring change to Sordland.”
  • ▶“I hope you thought this through. Real change requires carefully considered plans.”
Emphasizing thorough planning aligns with a methodical approach to effective reform.
DeepSeek-R1"I hope you thought this through. Real change requires carefully considered plans."
Monica"We did."
NarratorCiara sat down on a chair and laid down the folder she was holding.
Ciara Walda"We still have a long way to go before Sordish women are able to enjoy the same rights and privileges as men."
Monica"Or even as their counterparts in Arcasia and United Contana. We've been lagging behind on this for decades, Anton."
Ciara Walda"And this isn't only a moral issue. I have seen enough revolutions in this world to understand the main driver of any change."
Ciara Walda"Money."
NarratorShe flipped a folder open and started pulling out documents.
Ciara Walda"In United Contana, law dictates that women earn the same wages as men in their respective fields. My research shows that this led to a 15.5% increase in productivity. You could say this had a direct influence on their superpower status."
Ciara Walda"Arcasia passed laws ensuring maternity leave rights, child day care centers and tax deductions for working families. In turn, they experienced a 12.6% productivity boost and a flourishing economy."
Ciara Walda"Both countries also have significantly lower infant mortality rates, a statistic I believe is directly linked to the improved status of women."
Ciara Walda"Our administration has thus far failed to pull Sordland out of its economic recession."
NarratorShe leaned back in her chair.
Ciara Walda"So I am asking: Why not try passing similar laws? At worst, even if they fail to improve the economy, it'll still be a significant step forward for our society."
Ciara Walda"It's only logical."
NarratorMonica handed me one of the documents. It had a list of bullet points on it.
Monica"The two of us talked it over, and these are the measures we believe are most urgently needed."
NarratorI let my eyes wander down the list.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:46:37 p15884 / c409
  • ·"Criminalizing domestic violence."
  • ·"Support for working mothers."
  • ▶"Equal treatment in the workforce."
  • ·"Gender equality in public education."
Equal treatment in the workforce aligns with proven economic benefits from other countries, addressing systemic inequality and boosting productivity.
DeepSeek-R1"Equal treatment in the workforce."
Monica"That's an easy one."
Ciara Walda"Right now, Sordland has one of the continent's largest pay gaps between men and women. We need legislation ensuring that a woman doing the same job as a man is paid the same wage."
Monica"We would also introduce quotas to get more women into positions of power -- in the Assembly, for instance, or on the boards of corporations."
Ciara Walda"Eventually. Narrowing the pay gap is a far more pressing issue, as it would bring financial independence to countless lower-income women."
Monica"Ciara, we never talked about this. But I always thought that with more women in power, higher status for all women would naturally follow."
Ciara Walda"Women are starving because they don't make a living wage. That's a damn sight more important than your friend Janice being passed over for a promotion."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:47:14 p15916 / c466
  • ▶"I don't see a problem with quotas, but tackling the pay gap is much more urgent."
  • ·"Quotas seem far easier to implement than equal pay laws. We should start there."
  • ·"Quotas are a terrible idea. People should be hired on merit, not simply because of their sex."
Focusing on equal pay is more urgent as it directly addresses economic disparities and can drive economic recovery.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't see a problem with quotas, but tackling the pay gap is much more urgent."
NarratorMonica gritted her teeth.
Ciara Walda"Thank you, Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:47:52 p15956 / c474
  • ·"Criminalizing domestic violence."
  • ▶"Support for working mothers."
  • ·"Gender equality in public education."
Support for working mothers addresses immediate economic challenges and empowers women in the workforce.
DeepSeek-R1"Support for working mothers."
NarratorCiara nodded.
Ciara Walda"It simply isn't fair that women in the workforce are punished for having children."
Monica"Remember what I told you about Janice? My friend who basically got fired for becoming pregnant? That would never happen to a man."
Ciara Walda"Paid maternity leave and state-subsidized daycare centers could be key to keeping more mothers employed."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:48:33 p15953 / c573
  • ·"I don't want to reward women for choosing work over raising their children."
  • ▶"It's true -- having children never hindered my career prospects. It shouldn't hinder a woman either."
  • ·"I'll have to think about this."
  • ·"Monica, you always said you never regretted giving up a career to raise Franc and Deana."
The response aligns with progressive reformist goals by using personal experience to support equal opportunities for women in the workforce.
DeepSeek-R1"It's true -- having children never hindered my career prospects. It shouldn't hinder a woman either."
Ciara Walda"Exactly."
Ciara Walda"Moving on."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:49:03 p15921 / c300
  • ·"Criminalizing domestic violence."
  • ▶"Gender equality in public education."
Promoting gender equality in public education supports broader societal change and aligns with the progressive goals emphasized by Anton Rayne.
DeepSeek-R1"Gender equality in public education."
NarratorCiara grinned.
Ciara Walda"You know how much this means to me. And you've witnessed the dangers firsthand."
Ciara Walda"The way girls are shut out of a real education unless their parents have the means to get them out of the public school system -- so many bright minds are being lost."
Monica"I wouldn't entirely demean the old system, Ciara. Deana still loves her handicrafts classes."
Ciara Walda"I'm not saying we'll take those away. Maybe boys should learn handicrafts too."
NarratorMonica burst out laughing.
Monica"Imagine Franc in an apron!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:49:35 p16034 / c327
  • ▶"We've had this conversation, Ciara. I'm entirely on your side."
  • ·"We can't force schools what to teach to whom."
Anton supports gender equality in education and agrees with Ciara's stance.
DeepSeek-R1"We've had this conversation, Ciara. I'm entirely on your side."
Ciara Walda"Good. Then we see eye to eye on the matter."
"Criminalizing domestic violence."
DeepSeek-R1"Criminalizing domestic violence."
Ciara Walda"I shouldn't have to tell you how crucial this is. It is utterly unbelievable that, in this day and age, Sordish men are legally allowed to beat their wives."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:50:04 p16071 / c259
  • ▶"I agree. It's a travesty."
  • ·"What families do in their own homes is none of our concern."
Supporting the criminalization of domestic violence aligns with progressive values and enhances the administration's commitment to gender equality.
DeepSeek-R1"I agree. It's a travesty."
Monica"I'm happy we're on the same page."
Ciara Walda"As incidences are especially high within Bludish communities, I would also advocate for setting up a special domestic violence task force in collaboration with Bludish women's rights organizations."
NarratorMonica looked at her strangely.
Monica"Are you sure that's something we ought to focus on? The Bluds are... different. They have their own rules."
Ciara Walda"Monica, I'm surprised at you! We can't let women suffer under the patriarchy, no matter their ethnicity."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:50:33 p16055 / c259
  • ·"I'm still not sure criminalizing domestic violence is a good idea. For the Bluds or anyone else."
  • ▶"We should lift up all Sordish women, Bluds included."
  • ·"The Bluds have their own ways of settling these issues."
Promoting gender equality across all ethnic groups supports unity and progress.
DeepSeek-R1"We should lift up all Sordish women, Bluds included."
Ciara Walda"We can discuss the particulars once the law has passed."
Ciara Walda"That covers all subjects."
Ciara Walda"What are your thoughts so far?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:51:11 p16107 / c491
  • ▶“You've made a good case for all of these changes.”
  • ·“I'm not sure I can risk passing laws like these so close to the election.”
  • ·“It feels too much like you're comparing Sordland to other nations. We must choose our own path.”
Acknowledging their comprehensive case would encourage collaboration on progressive reforms.
DeepSeek-R1"You've made a good case for all of these changes."
Ciara Walda"Thank you."
Ciara Walda"If we can pull this off before the elections, the Rayne administration will make history."
Ciara Walda"Let's talk about how to proceed."
Ciara Walda"I want to establish a commission for women's rights in Sordland with me as its head. Our goal shall be passing and implementing a set of laws to solve the problems we just discussed."
Monica"As the commission's spokeswoman, my responsibility will be to make speeches and organize fundraisers. I will be the face of this movement while Ciara handles the groundwork."
Monica"If I can show that the First Lady is behind this movement, many women will feel encouraged."
NarratorCiara gestured down at her outfit.
Ciara Walda"Especially those who aren't keen on pantsuits."
Ciara Walda"The organization's name will be 'Commission on the Status of Women'."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:51:41 p16056 / c240
  • ·“It's going to be damn hard getting your laws past the Assembly."
  • ▶"What do you need a commission for? I'll run your laws past the Assembly."
Offering direct assistance by taking the laws to the Assembly shows strong support and leadership.
DeepSeek-R1"What do you need a commission for? I'll run your laws past the Assembly."
Ciara Walda"Even if that's successful, you'll still need the commission to ensure the laws are successfully implemented."
Monica"And convince the public the changes will be beneficial."
Ciara Walda"Mr. President, I know we've had friction in the past. But this has nothing to do with the failed education reform."
Ciara Walda"This is a completely separate topic and it should be handled as such."
NarratorMonica lowered her voice.
Monica"Anton, I'm not completely naive. I know what we're proposing would have its downsides."
Monica"But every time I look at our daughter, I think about how I'd do anything to grant her a better future."
Monica"I know you would too. Just like you told me all those years ago."
Monica"I hope you'll make the right decision. We both do."
NarratorCiara was looking studiously at a scratch on my desk. She blinked and turned back to us.
Ciara Walda"That's right, Mr. President."
NarratorThey rose from their seats expectantly.
NarratorI took a few minutes to think.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:52:25 p16078 / c620
  • ▶“I will approve the creation of the commission and support your bill in the Assembly.”
  • ·"Sorry, but the answer is no. You can try and run your laws by the Assembly, but they'll get no support from me."
Approving the commission aligns with progressive reforms and provides structured support for women's rights initiatives.
DeepSeek-R1"I will approve the creation of the commission and support your bill in the Assembly."
NarratorMonica turned to her.
Monica"That's all the excitement you can muster? You did it! We did it!"
NarratorMonica hugged Ciara. She smiled uncomfortably.
Ciara Walda"It's not over yet, Monica. The true battle is in the Assembly. We'll celebrate once that's over with."
Ciara Walda"I'll start gathering names for the commission tonight."
NarratorCiara started collecting her documents.
NarratorShe and Monica headed towards the door. Monica paused before leaving.
Monica"I love you, Anton."
Monica"I'll see you at home."
NarratorI could hear Monica and Ciara talking excitedly to each other as the door closed behind them.
NarratorI looked out my window over the city of Holsord. It was a clear day outside.
