■ Gendarmerie DisputeT08

BUDGET -20WEALTH 1STABILITY 1Danger
NarratorI arrived in the city of Vesord with Petr after receiving an emergency meeting request from Iosef and Lileas.
NarratorI had seen the reports of a recent issue that rose between the Sordish Police Forces and the Gendarmerie units at the city.
NarratorThe report said that an operation against a drug trafficking gang in Vesord failed due to miscommunication.
NarratorIt was not hard to guess what this meeting was going to be about.
NarratorWe entered the city hall and made our way to the second floor. I could hear Iosef and Lileas's voices echoing through the hall.
NarratorI opened the doors to the meeting room. The two ministers did not even realise.
Iosef Lancea"This is unacceptable!"
Iosef Lancea"How dare the police meddle in the affairs of the Gendarmerie?"
Lileas Graf"It was *your* men who started this ridiculousness and meddled with an ongoing police investigation."
NarratorTheir voices were getting louder and louder.
Iosef Lancea"Rural security is my jurisdiction and your men were way outside of Vesord."
Petr Vectern"Please calm down and let's discuss this."
Petr Vectern"What is going on?"
Iosef Lancea"Apologies, but Ms. Graf here fails to understand her place."
Lileas Graf"No, Mr. Lancea. You are the one who does not understand his place."
Lileas Graf"If you continue to do so, you will pay for it one day."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:53:50 p16152 / c276
  • ·Slam the table.
  • ▶“That's enough. Calm down, both of you.”
  • ·“Don't worry. We will solve this conflict of interest.”
As President Rayne, maintaining order and respect in the meeting is crucial, so telling both ministers to calm down and address the issue constructively shows leadership.
DeepSeek-R1"That's enough. Calm down, both of you."
Lileas Graf"I am trying *really* hard to stay calm, Mr. President."
Iosef Lancea"Ms. Graf, you are pushing your boundaries."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:54:27 p15120 / c377
  • ▶“Lileas. Explain what happened.”
  • ·“Iosef. Let's hear your side of the story.”
Starting by asking Lileas Graf will provide immediate context from her ministry's perspective.
DeepSeek-R1"Lileas. Explain what happened."
Lileas Graf"Mr. Greiser was tasked with the investigation of a drug trafficking ring in downtown Vesord."
Lileas Graf"We found the evidence of a drug stash located in the outskirts of the city."
Lileas Graf"We had to act quickly to put an end to the criminals we have been watching for some time."
Lileas Graf"So we immediately sent our police units to catch them and find the drugs in the compound."
Lileas Graf"Mr. Lancea and his Gendarmerie did not notify us that they were also moving against the same target."
Lileas Graf"As a result, criminals got away and we lost months of progress!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:55:00 p15245 / c337
  • ·“You went out of your jurisdiction to pursue these criminals.”
  • ·“So, the Gendarmerie meddled and caused the raid to fail.”
  • ▶“Isn't there a line of communication between you?”
Asking about communication highlights the systemic issue causing the failure.
DeepSeek-R1"Isn't there a line of communication between you?"
Lileas Graf"There is, but the Minister of Defense decided to avoid notifying the police department about the planned raid."
Iosef Lancea"The same could be said about you because the reports show that you knew about the gang and their stash in the countryside."
NarratorI started tapping on the table with my fingers.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:55:33 p15286 / c363
  • ▶“How do we solve this issue so that it never happens again?”
  • ·“What I hear is that you are both incompetent.”
  • ·“We are getting nowhere. We need to find a middle ground.”
Focusing on solving the issue for the future is constructive and addresses the root cause of the conflict.
DeepSeek-R1"How do we solve this issue so that it never happens again?"
Lileas Graf"I think this was the last drop."
Lileas Graf"Internal security matters should be handed to Ministry of Interior completely."
Lileas Graf"We are called the Ministry of *Interior* for a reason. We can't let any more screw ups like this happen again."
NarratorShe looked at me.
Lileas Graf"I want the Gendarmerie to be transferred to the Ministry of Interior."
Iosef Lancea"What?"
Iosef Lancea"Woman, are you out of your mind?"
NarratorIosef turned red with anger. He composed himself after a moment.
Petr Vectern"Can't these branches just try to improve their communication?"
Petr Vectern"Just saying."
Lileas Graf"I believe I just told you the fix to the problem, Mr. Vice President."
Petr Vectern"Well, but-"
Lileas Graf"With all due respect, there is not other way."
Iosef Lancea"Ridiculous! This is absolutely ridiculous. I won't hand over Gendarmerie to her. Out of the question."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:56:04 p15285 / c306
  • ·“Why? Because it makes sense?”
  • ·“I agree. The Gendarmerie has been a part of the Ministry of Defense for years.”
  • ▶“Do you have a better solution, Iosef?”
Asking for an alternative solution shows willingness to find a middle ground.
DeepSeek-R1"Do you have a better solution, Iosef?"
Iosef Lancea"Whenever operations overlap, we should engage at a lower rank with the local officers on both sides."
Lileas Graf"We could work on establishing that but it still doesn't change the wrong system that exists now."
Lileas Graf"No matter what we need to eliminate this grey area and modernise our internal security."
Iosef Lancea"Now I see what is going on here."
Iosef Lancea"Lileas set us up to create this incident."
NarratorLileas bit her lips.
Petr Vectern"That is going a bit too far. What proof do you have?"
Iosef Lancea"There is no way that the police has arrived at the right time of a month long search on the exact day and minute of the Gendarmerie raid."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:56:32 p15289 / c209

  • ·“This is conspiracy theory territory.”
  • ▶“Let us take it down a notch with the accusations.”
It's better to de-escalate the tension by acknowledging that unfounded accusations can lead to more problems.
DeepSeek-R1"Let us take it down a notch with the accusations."
Lileas Graf"This kind of an accusation is unheard of."
Lileas Graf"It's nonsense."
Lileas Graf"If I were you, I wouldn't believe a word Mr. Lancea says, Mr. President."
Iosef Lancea"Heed my word, Mr. President."
Iosef Lancea"When dealing with these situations, you need to always look at who gets what out of it."
Lileas Graf"I won't stand idle by these utterly ridiculous and baseless accusations. I don't have time for this."
Lileas Graf"What is your decision?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:57:10 p15227 / c477

  • ▶“There will be no change related to the Gendarmerie operations.”
To maintain stability and avoid potential internal conflict, it's better to keep the current structure of security operations while improving communication between the Police Forces and Gendarmerie.

Lileas Graf"You are making a mistake."
Iosef Lancea"Thank you for protecting the structure and authority of the Ministry of Defense."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:57:47 p15244 / c425

  • ▶“From now on work together and don't fight among each other. I want capable and effective members of cabinet.”

Encouraging collaboration will prevent future conflicts and ensure effective governance.

Lileas Graf"We will do our best. Right, Mr. Lancea?"
Iosef Lancea"That's right, Ms. Graf."

■ Health Overview And PandemicT08

BUDGET -20WEALTH 1STABILITY 1Danger
NarratorI had a meeting scheduled with Paskal to receive the annual healthcare report.
NarratorHe arrived at my office with stacks of papers underneath both arms.
Paskal Beniwoll"Mr. President."

Paskal Beniwoll"I know this seems like a lot."
Paskal Beniwoll"Reviewing it shouldn't take more than a day."

Paskal Beniwoll"Or maybe two."

Paskal Beniwoll"Of course."

Paskal Beniwoll"So, we have concluded our report for the year."
Paskal Beniwoll"Thanks to the allocated funds from the state, we have improved the healthcare in the country overall. I have used the resources to begin a comprehensive health reform."
Paskal Beniwoll"We have more patients treated, less waiting times and higher treatment quality than the years before."

Paskal Beniwoll"This wouldn't be possible without your wise decision, Mr. President. So, thank you."
Paskal Beniwoll"We are certainly on the right path to challenge even Lespia or Valgsland in the matter but we are not there yet."
Paskal Beniwoll"However, give it a few more years and we might even surpass them."

Paskal Beniwoll"The quality of care increased due to the upgraded equipment and we were able to hire more healthcare staff."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:00:45 p15049 / c508

Paskal Beniwoll"Yes. They have been neglected for a long time due to political reasons. Not anymore."
Paskal Beniwoll"All these efforts have resulted in a clear increase of quality in health services provided to our citizens."

Paskal Beniwoll"Now that I have given you the short summary..."
Paskal Beniwoll"There is a very important matter to discuss."

Take a look.

Paskal Beniwoll"For now it seems to be under control. We have put a few villages under quarantine. The total number of infected is currently 1467. The death toll is at 23 so far."
Paskal Beniwoll"If we have more outbreaks, the results will be terrible."
Paskal Beniwoll"Thankfully we have received the additional funding to our healthcare."
Paskal Beniwoll"This should curb the infection ratio. Although, I am afraid even that might not be enough."
Paskal Beniwoll"This is a very serious matter, Mr. President. The lives of Sordish citizens are at stake."

  • ·“How bad can it be? Don't worry about it. It will stop. I know it.”
  • ·“What can we do about it?”
Anton Rayne would respond with determination, showing strong leadership and commitment to addressing the public health crisis.
DeepSeek-R1"I will do whatever it takes to eliminate this threat."
Paskal Beniwoll"With that attitude, I have no doubt that we will succeed."
Paskal Beniwoll"We have done our research."
Paskal Beniwoll"There is a cure. A vaccine was recently invented in United Contana, and the formula has been made available for free across the entire world."
Paskal Beniwoll"No matter what people think about United Contana, if the same thing had happened in Arcasia, we would be paying a steep price for it."
Paskal Beniwoll"Thanks to the very good decision made by you to increase funding for our healthcare, we have the capability to produce this vaccine locally."
Paskal Beniwoll"But I'm afraid simply having the vaccines won't help. We need to make sure they are administered."
Paskal Beniwoll"We need to implement a nationwide mandatory Polio vaccination policy."
Paskal Beniwoll"With your order, I can immediately get to work to ensure we are doing whatever it takes."
Paskal Beniwoll"This might seem excessive however, it is absolutely necessary."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:02:26 p15200 / c355
  • ▶“Whatever it takes to protect Sordland. Do it.”
  • ·“Let's observe the situation first. We will be fine without it for now.”
Immediate action is necessary to protect public health given the severity of the Polio outbreak.
DeepSeek-R1"Whatever it takes to protect Sordland. Do it."
Paskal Beniwoll"Excellent."
Paskal Beniwoll"Now, if you will excuse me, I must get to work immediately."
NarratorHe got up from his seat.
Paskal Beniwoll"Thank you for your time, Mr. President."
NarratorHe left my office, leaving me alone with a lot of paperwork to go through.

■ Personal Family SceneT08

BUDGET -20WEALTH 1STABILITY 2Danger
NarratorAs I stepped through the front door of our stately residence, a sense of tranquillity washed over me. The house was quiet -- almost too quiet, without Franc blasting his music from upstairs.
NarratorI entered the grand study and let the warm glow of the room envelop me. Monica was sitting by the fireplace with a very old-looking book in her hands.

  • ·"I must say, you seem to have quite the fondness for these dusty tomes."

Monica"Oh. I didn't realize you were home."
Monica"Is everything really going well at work?"
Monica"Evelyn said Petr's finally stopped drinking -- or most likely, stopped drinking so very much."
Monica"Apparently, he's been coming home a lot more often, and spends most nights in his study, working on documents."

Monica"I see."
NarratorShe sighed.
Monica"I don't know, Anton. Both of you seem to be under a massive amount of stress lately."
Monica"Maybe we can make some time for a little getaway."
Monica"We can have a weekend retreat out in the countryside. What do you think?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:04:33 p15128 / c260

Monica"Great!"
NarratorShe grinned.

NarratorDeana appeared by the door with a piece of folded paper clutched in her hand.
Deana"Mama, Papa, my school report came in!"
NarratorMonica and I immediately turned our attention to her, our curiosity piqued.

Deana"I know! But I still feel kinda bad..."
Deana"I'm sure I would've been at the top if I'd done better in handicrafts."

Deana"And then there's that stupid blonde boy Markus. He never even studies, but the teachers always give him higher grades than everyone else."
Monica"Don't call him stupid, baby. Who is this Markus?"

■ Bill Tobacco TaxT08

BUDGET -20WEALTH 1STABILITY 2Danger
Bill for decisionLess Smoke Bill — The assembly passes an amendment to the Tax Bill of 1944 in order to increase tax revenue and reduce the excessive amount of tobacco consumption in the general populace. Section I of the T.B of '44 which had imposed a 10% tax on all tobacco packages, will be increased to 30% on all tobacco products sold in the country. Section II will protect local industries by giving them a full tax exemption if the products are destined for export to other countries. [+1 Government Budget]
BILL DeepSeek-R114:07:30 p14964 / c370
  • ▶Sign: Less Smoke Bill
  • ·Veto: Less Smoke Bill
The Less Smoke Bill increases tobacco taxes to reduce consumption and boost government revenue, with an export exemption to protect local industries.

BUDGET -20WEALTH 1STABILITY 2Danger
EventSupport Aschraf Anniversary?: The remembrance rally of the Aschraf anniversary is about to begin in Erzaren. The Bludish movement is holding their largest rally yet to display their demands and strength. Due to the activity of the BFF in the area, the security detail advises to take caution. The administration can go to Erzaren to support the event with or without an armored convoy or not attend it at all.
EVENT DeepSeek-R114:08:07 p15054 / c341

■ Aschraf AnniversaryT08

BUDGET -19WEALTH 1STABILITY 1Danger
NarratorOn our way to Erzaren, we stopped in Deyr to meet with the Governor of Bergia, Phelix Bron, to discuss the security situation within the Special Zone. He wished us luck at the event and told us to be careful before bidding us farewell.
NarratorAfter a few hours' drive, we finally arrived in the ancient walled city of Erzaren.
NarratorThe city had been of particular importance to the Bludish people ever since the Izzam Incident and the death of Watani Aschraf over two decades ago. Today, we were here to commemorate the anniversary of those events.
NarratorThe ceremonies were to be held in the aptly named Izzam Square, several blocks away from Lake Soll and the sunken ruins of the village of Izzam.
NarratorWe entered the historic center wearing flak vests, transported by an armored personnel carrier. The streets were littered with broken glass and the walls defaced with graffiti. I sensed a heavy regional police presence.
NarratorOur massive convoy could barely fit through the narrow streets, disrupting local traffic and creating noisy chaos in the usually tranquil city. Pedestrians stared at us in fear as we and our heavily armed security forces drove towards the square.
Lucian Galade"I must say this was a good decision, sir. This will help ease the tensions. But I hope bringing this much security won't cause any problems."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:08:44 p15736 / c357
  • ▶“Better safe than sorry.”
  • ·“I hope so.”
  • ·“That was my intention all along.”
President Rayne prefers a cautious and calculated approach, emphasizing safety over other concerns.
DeepSeek-R1"Better safe than sorry."
Lucian Galade"I agree."
Lucian Galade"We are almost there. I can hear the crowd."
NarratorI heard the chants and shouting from the outside. Lucian leaned closer to the window, listening to their voices.
Lucian Galade"They seem to be chanting 'Yollak Bluderat'. I believe that means 'Free Bludia' in Bludish, or something of that sort."
NarratorA large crowd had assembled in the square. There were probably at least ten thousand people.
NarratorOur vehicle came to a stop.
NarratorI noticed an excessively high amount of regional police, as well as the gendarmerie and local police. They formed a human barricade around the square, staring intimidatingly at the crowd.
Lucian Galade"Must be the governor's doing."
NarratorThe security detail left their vehicles with their machine guns openly displayed. I followed them. The crowd seemed to be riled up at the sight of guns.
NarratorWe were greeted by Mansoun Leke. He wore a coat over his suit which was marked with the red and turquoise stripes of the Bludish movement's flag. He moved forward to greet us.
Mansoun Leke"Welcome to Erzaren, Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:09:29 p15841 / c568
  • ·“No need for pleasantries. I want this over with as quickly as possible.”
  • ▶Take his hand and raise it in a display of unity.
  • ·Shake his hand.
Raising Mansoun Leke's hand in a display of unity would be a bold but meaningful gesture to show support for the Bludish community and address historical tensions.
DeepSeek-R1I held our clasped hands up so that the crowd could see them.
NarratorI heard some applause from the crowd.
NarratorHe pointed at the security guards with guns.
Mansoun Leke"I am not sure how bringing your personal army will help."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:10:04 p15729 / c377
  • ·“They are not my personal army. They protect the government.”
  • ·“They are here for protection.”
  • ▶“Let's *go*.”
The choice aims to shift focus back to the event, emphasizing action over explanation.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's *go*."
Mansoun Leke"On a day like this, when you want to show support for the murdered Bludish people... You bring men with guns."
Mansoun Leke"I suppose the governor's huge army wasn't enough. Bergia has already become a police state. A truly *special* zone."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:10:46 p15769 / c532
  • ·“Let's just move on, Mr. Leke.”
  • ·“Watch your words. You are speaking to the president.”
  • ▶“My only intention is to stand in solidarity with our Bludish people.”
Emphasizing solidarity aligns with previous efforts in addressing Bludish discrimination.
DeepSeek-R1"My only intention is to stand in solidarity with our Bludish people."
NarratorHe raised an eyebrow.
Lucian Galade"Mr. Leke."
Mansoun Leke"Mr. Galade. Welcome. Shall we move on to the Sanctuary, then?"
Lucian Galade"Let's go."
NarratorWe made our way to the Erzaren Sanctuary, which stood at the end of the square.
NarratorA much smaller replica of the Archsanctuary in Deyr, the ancient building had a mystical aura emanting from its blackened walls. We entered the building through the ornamented large metal gates.
NarratorAs we walked, the noise of the crowd faded out. Everyone fell silent for the ceremony. Thousands of candles were burning inside the Sanctuary, one for each person that died in Izzam. The red candle in the center was for Watani Aschraf.
NarratorMansoun gestured towards the candles.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:11:20 p15763 / c352
  • ▶Pick up a candle.
  • ·Pick up the red candle.
  • ·Do not pick a candle.
Picking up a candle shows solidarity with the event, aligning with Rayne's goal of neutrality and support.
DeepSeek-R1I picked up one of the candles.
NarratorHe picked up the candle for Aschraf and started walking out. Many others followed suit, including us.
NarratorThe atmosphere outside had completely changed. Not a single noise came from the crowd.
NarratorWe slowly walked towards Aschraf's grave. On the way, hundreds of people joined us.
NarratorWhen we arrived, he pointed at a huge standing torch above the grave.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:11:57 p15614 / c414
  • ·Leave the candle next to it.
  • ·Light the torch.
  • ▶Light the torch together with Mansoun.
Lighting the torch with Mansoun Leke symbolizes unity and collaboration.
DeepSeek-R1We lit the torch together.
NarratorI turned around and realized the sheer amount of people who were following us. They were all looking at me, waiting to hear what I would say.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:12:30 p15612 / c351
  • ·“Brothers and sisters.”
  • ·“My fellow citizens.”
  • ▶“Free people of Bludia.”
Addressing them directly as 'Free people of Bludia' resonates with their identity and struggle, showing strong solidarity.
DeepSeek-R1"Free people of Bludia."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:13:08 p15605 / c445
  • ▶“We are here to mourn Watani Aschraf and many others who have lost their lives.”
  • ·“We are here to mourn those who, tragically, lost their lives in Izzam.”
Choice 0 is more appropriate as it specifically acknowledges Watani Aschraf, making the statement more meaningful.
DeepSeek-R1"We are here to mourn Watani Aschraf and many others who have lost their lives."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:13:42 p15631 / c401
  • ▶“I am not standing before you today as the President. I am standing here as one of you.”
  • ·“They were brave men and women who only wanted to protect their land.”
  • ·“I condemn the events that led to the loss of these lives.”
The statement emphasizes unity and solidarity, which is crucial for addressing the Bludish community's concerns.
DeepSeek-R1"I am not standing before you today as the President. I am standing here as one of you."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:14:23 p15704 / c518
  • ·“Despite attempts to divide us, our people will stand as one. We are Sord. We are Blud. We are all united under one flag.”
  • ▶“I share your hatred and anger. I feel it too, I assure you. Like a red-hot iron, it takes a long time for it to cool. But I look at the lost lives and think, what would they want?”
  • ·“I assure you, this administration will do its absolute best to mend the wounds.”
Expressing shared anger and reflecting on the loss aligns with President Rayne's empathetic approach while maintaining political pragmatism.
DeepSeek-R1"I share your hatred and anger. I feel it too, I assure you. Like a red-hot iron, it takes a long time for it to cool. But I look at the lost lives and think, what would they want?"
NarratorAn old man from the crowd yelled: "He is right!"

  • ▶“Let me tell you one thing. I dream of a truly united Sordland. A country where being a Blud or a Sord does not matter.”

CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:15:32 p15783 / c505

  • ▶“Let this be a testament of our unity for future generations! Let's stand together as Blud and Sord.”
  • ·“Mistakes were made. The innocent people of Izzam paid the price. But we cannot let the past divide us.”
Emphasizing unity between Blud and Sord highlights national cohesion and forward-looking solidarity.
DeepSeek-R1"Let this be a testament of our unity for future generations! Let's stand together as Blud and Sord."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:16:07 p15723 / c367

  • ▶“The responsibility falls on all of our shoulders. Because we don't have anyone but each other.”
  • ·“Thank you.”
Emphasizing shared responsibility reinforces the message of unity and mutual effort needed for Sordland's future.

CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:16:41 p15772 / c355

DeepSeek-R1"To our unity!"
NarratorThere was no reaction from the crowd. Only silence.
NarratorOne man broke the silence and shouted "Do not listen to murderers!", and threw a tomato. It hit the ground right in front of me.
NarratorThere was a moment of complete silence. Then, the whole crowd erupted.
NarratorLucian looked at me worriedly. People started coming closer and closer to us, chanting "Yollak Bluderat."
NarratorI was immediately surrounded by the local police forces. They had drawn their guns and were pointing them at the approaching crowd.
NarratorHearing the chaos, regional police forces and the gendarmerie also rushed to our aid. Suddenly, the entire crowd was surrounded by an army.
Lucian Galade"Sir, I think we must leave."
NarratorAs the security forces pushed everyone away, the presidential guard escorted us back to our vehicle.
Lucian Galade"That was close."

BUDGET -19WEALTH 1STABILITY 1Danger

Petr Vectern"Anton. Morning."

Petr Vectern"Symon told me that he's almost done with the new economic plan."
Petr Vectern"He said he still needs some time with it. Personally, I think he's just trying to avoid human contact for as long as possible, but what do I know?"

Petr Vectern"Next on the docket..."

Petr Vectern"Lucian insisted on being here for this part."

Lucian Galade"Mr. Vectern, I'll let you take the lead on this."
Petr Vectern"Uh... I will, Lucian. Thanks."
Petr Vectern"I just received the results from a voter poll I commissioned earlier this year."
Petr Vectern"To put it in plain language..."

Petr Vectern"Looking at the demographics of the voters polled..."
Petr Vectern"Our support among conservatives has plummeted. We can no longer count on their vote at all."
Petr Vectern"The liberal base appears divided, but you do have a considerable number of supporters there."
Petr Vectern"For better or worse, you certainly aren't popular with nationalists."
Petr Vectern"If you ever had any support from socialists, you've completely lost it now."

TURN 09

■ Bill Strong Womens RightsT09

BUDGET -20WEALTH 1STABILITY 1Danger
Bill for decisionWomen's Liberation Act — In collaboration with the Commission on the Status of Women, the Grand National Assembly presents the Women's Liberation Act (W.L.A.), aimed at promoting gender equality and women's rights across Sordland. Section I of the W.L.A. takes decisive action against domestic violence perpetrated against women. It unequivocally criminalizes such acts, strengthening the legal framework for the protection of women's rights. Subsection I.A of the W.L.A. establishes a system of state protection for victims of domestic abuse. This includes counseling, temporary shelter arrangements, and legal support, ensuring a comprehensive safety net for affected women. Section II of the W.L.A. addresses the issue of wage disparity. It mandates equal pay for women, and to support this transition, it provides subsidies to businesses to offset any resulting expenditure differences. Further, it mandates the adjustment of salaries for all female government personnel to ensure pay parity. Section III of the W.L.A. highlights the importance of education in fostering equality. It mandates that the education system must provide equal treatment to both genders, supporting an environment of fairness and mutual respect from an early age. Section IV of the W.L.A. makes significant strides towards supporting working mothers. It introduces state-paid maternity leave and establishes state-subsidized daycare centers, ensuring mothers receive the support they need to balance their professional and familial responsibilities. [-2 Government Budget]
BILL DeepSeek-R114:26:36 p15491 / c339
  • ·Sign: Women's Liberation Act
  • ▶Veto: Women's Liberation Act
The Women's Liberation Act presents significant social reforms but requires a financial allocation that may strain current economic conditions. Considering the ongoing recession recovery and political challenges, vetoing the bill prioritizes immediate economic stability over social reforms.

■ Bill Child BenefitT09

BUDGET -20WEALTH 1STABILITY -4Danger
Bill for decisionChildren for Future Act — In continuation of the efforts to promote social progress and upliftment, the parliament introduces the Children for Future Act (C.F.A.). This act focuses on providing tangible support to families in need, with particular attention to the welfare of children. Section I of the C.F.A. outlines a direct financial support mechanism for families not falling within the medium or high-income brackets. The state will issue a fixed payment for every child in these families, offering much-needed financial relief and ensuring that the welfare of the child is not compromised due to economic circumstances. Section II of the C.F.A. unlocks additional state support specifically aimed at the children of unemployed families. It provides financing for each child's education and any special healthcare needs that may arise. This proactive approach aims to create a safety net for the most vulnerable, ensuring that every child, regardless of their family's employment status, has access to education and healthcare. [-1 Government Budget]
BILL DeepSeek-R114:31:49 p15603 / c472
  • ·Sign: Children for Future Act
  • ▶Veto: Children for Future Act
Given the current economic instability and negative government budget, vetoing the Children for Future Act is necessary to avoid further financial strain and prioritize immediate economic stability.

■ Decision Penalty Debt CrashT09

BUDGET -20WEALTH 1STABILITY -4Danger
EventThe Sordish Debt Debacle: The mounting government budget deficit and unsustainable borrowing have led to concerns among investors and analysts about the nation's ability to service its debt. Sordland now faces a serious debt crisis. This lack of confidence has resulted in increased borrowing costs, a reduction in foreign investment, and the risk of a potential default, which could have far-reaching consequences for the Sordish economy.
Debts can be paid...

■ Bill Ban ProstitutionT09

BUDGET -20WEALTH 1STABILITY -4Danger
Bill for decisionHuman Dignity Bill — The Human Dignity Bill (H.D.B.) has been presented to the assembly to address an unintended consequence of the 1923 Legal Transactions Act. Section I of the H.D.B. revises and amends Section III of the 1923 Act, which previously permitted transactions involving non-registered businesses or other entities, including individuals, to be legalized if proper documentation was submitted to the financial authority. The H.D.B. specifically nullifies the legality of such transactions if they pertain to activities that violate public morality. Section II addresses the loophole inadvertently created by the 1923 Act, which allowed for the unregulated continuation of prostitution. This section of the H.D.B. explicitly declares prostitution an illegal activity, regardless of whether it occurs within a registered or non-registered entity, thereby reinforcing our dedication to preserving societal decency and moral standards. Section III outlines penalties for violations of the bill's provisions. It classifies prostitution as an illegal offense subject to penalties, including fines, imprisonment, or both, depending on the nature and frequency of the offense.
BILL DeepSeek-R114:32:39 p15901 / c653
  • ·Sign: Human Dignity Bill
  • ▶Veto: Human Dignity Bill
Considering the budget constraints and the potential societal challenges in enforcing this bill, it's prudent to veto the Human Dignity Bill to avoid exacerbating economic pressures and unintended social issues.

■ TalksT09

BUDGET -20WEALTH 1STABILITY -4Danger
NarratorA storm was on the horizon outside my window.
NarratorThe inside of my office, however, was very warm and cosy.
NarratorI straightened myself on the sofa next to the fireplace and looked at Mansoun, who was sitting on the couch across from me. He was here representing all eight Bludish members of the Assembly.
NarratorHe had dark creases under his eyes from lack of sleep.
Mansoun Leke"As I said earlier, we've been working on this bill for a long time, Mr. President. I've always done my best to represent my people... only to keep getting blocked in the Assembly."
Mansoun Leke"We can't have the same happen again."
Mansoun Leke"I've always brought my people's concerns to the Assembly and raised my voice whenever our rights were in question. All we ask is that the Assembly at least have a proper discussion."
Mansoun Leke"But all that we get is disrespect. MPs shouting racial slurs at us, not allowing us to speak. And it's not only the National Front..."
NarratorHe stopped for a moment and repositioned himself on his seat.
Mansoun Leke"Having said that, there has been a lot of new developments since we last spoke."
Mansoun Leke"Unfortunately, the lack of cooperation from your side has been enabling the hardline nationalists to get bolder about their racist tirades."
Mansoun Leke"And Governor Bron is still in charge of the Special Zone... Free to continue his oppressive policies against the Bludish community."
NarratorHe sighed.
Mansoun Leke"And something must be done about this."
Mansoun Leke"I don't even remember a time when Mrs. Tory allowed me to finish a speech."
Mansoun Leke"The Assembly does not take us independents seriously."
Mansoun Leke"And you, Mr. President, have approved anti-Bludish policies."
Mansoun Leke"I know, or want to believe, that you only did this to appease your base."
Mansoun Leke"I am here to ask you to reconsider your stance. We can make a difference in Sordland. Together."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:33:16 p16219 / c310
  • ·"What makes you think that I only had political reasons?"
  • ·"I don't believe I've done anything against the Bludish people."
  • ▶"It's true that I have some regrets over my actions. But how can I help you?"
Expressing regret and willingness to help shows openness to dialogue and collaboration.
DeepSeek-R1"It's true that I have some regrets over my actions. But how can I help you?"
Mansoun Leke"We should start by undoing your regrets, Mr. President."
Mansoun Leke"You were not only silent about the racism in this country, you enabled it."
Mansoun Leke"You targeted our faith and banned our language and our priests from the sanctuaries."
Mansoun Leke"What are these laws if not an attempt at cultural genocide?"
Mansoun Leke"It is time to undo these mistakes, Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:33:47 p16222 / c251

Mansoun Leke"Then please help us, Mr. President."
Mansoun Leke"Talk to your fellow USP members and put your support behind our bill. Our proposal will give amnesty to those who were falsely arrested for having ties to the democratic Bludish Freedom Party."
Mansoun Leke"It will revoke the current ban and allow both the Worker's Party of Bludia and the Bludish Freedom Party into politics once more."
Mansoun Leke"And it will form a Commission for Minority Rights."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:34:24 p16518 / c397

DeepSeek-R1"Okay, I'll do it."
Mansoun Leke"Thank you, Mr. President."
NarratorHe rose and walked up to me. He extended a hand.
Mansoun Leke"This means a lot. Thank you."
NarratorWe shook hands.
NarratorSuddenly, we heard a knock on the door and Lucian entered.
Lucian Galade"Sir."
Lucian Galade"I'm sorry. I thought your meeting was over."
Mansoun Leke"Mr. President has already decided. We can call it over, Mr. Galade. I was already leaving."
Lucian Galade"Great. Then we must go to the meeting room, sir. One of our new campaign advisors has some plans for the election. He's waiting for us."

■ Bill Alcohol TaxT09

BUDGET -20WEALTH 1STABILITY -4Danger
Bill for decisionAlcohol Tax Act — The Grand National Assembly introduces the Alcohol Tax Act (A.T.A.) as a dual measure to curb excessive alcohol consumption and boost tax revenues. Section I of the A.T.A. mandates a 20% tax on all alcohol products sold within the country. This measure serves not only as a deterrent to excessive alcohol consumption by raising the cost of alcoholic beverages but also establishes a consistent source of government revenue. Section II of the A.T.A. promotes domestic industry by making local alcohol production destined for export exempt from the aforementioned taxation. This provision seeks to bolster the competitiveness of local producers in the international market while maintaining the aim of reducing domestic alcohol consumption. Section III of the A.T.A. applies a 25% import tax on foreign alcohol products imported from countries that do not currently have any trade regulations imposed on alcohol-related goods. This policy aims to level the playing field for domestic producers and increase the overall tax revenue. [+1 Government Budget]
BILL DeepSeek-R114:35:23 p16185 / c700
  • ▶Sign: Alcohol Tax Act
  • ·Veto: Alcohol Tax Act
Signing the Alcohol Tax Act would boost government revenue, support local industries through export exemptions, and potentially improve public health by reducing alcohol consumption. While it may face public backlash, the economic benefits align with ongoing recovery efforts.

■ Bill Luxury TaxT09

BUDGET -20WEALTH 1STABILITY -4Danger
Bill for decisionFair Luxury Tax Act — In an endeavor to enhance the equity of the taxation system and augment tax revenues, the Grand National Assembly introduces the Fair Luxury Tax Act (F.L.T.A.). This act proposes an amendment to Section VI of the Tax Code of 1949. Section I of the F.L.T.A. determines a 15% tax rate on luxury goods, as categorized by the Ministry of Economy. The definition of luxury goods under this act includes, but is not limited to, jewelry, furs, sports cars, yachts, and private planes. The implementation of this tax is intended to ensure that those with the means to purchase such items contribute proportionally to the state's revenue. Section II of the F.L.T.A. establishes a 10% estate tax applicable in situations of purchase, sale, and property transfer, particularly in cases of inheritance. This measure seeks to promote fairness in the tax system, ensuring that significant wealth transfers contribute appropriately to the state's revenue, while also deterring large-scale wealth accumulation through inheritance. [+2 Government Budget]
BILL DeepSeek-R114:35:55 p16454 / c219
  • ·Sign: Fair Luxury Tax Act
  • ▶Veto: Fair Luxury Tax Act
The Fair Luxury Tax Act may increase government revenue but risks upsetting wealthy citizens and businesses, potentially harming the economy further given its current downturn.

■ Rumburg IncidentT09

BUDGET -19WEALTH 1STABILITY -5Danger
NarratorI was woken up in the middle of the night by a call from the Ministry of Defense. Due to the sensitivity of the information, I had to be urgently briefed in person.
NarratorI put on my clothes as fast as I could.
NarratorI rushed downstairs and opened the door. Serge was waiting for me.
Serge Wolkner"Mr. President. This way to the car, please."
NarratorWe got into the car and made our way to the Ministry of Defense building without a word.
NarratorI arrived and was directed downstairs. I came to a set of large doors and quickly read the sign above them.
NarratorThe sign read 'War Room'.
NarratorThe dimly lit room was filled with cutting-edge equipment -- the latest video screens, long-range radio electronics and other devices used for communication, presentation and planning.
NarratorThe centerpiece of the room was a large round table with the coat of arms of Sordland engraved on it.
NarratorDeivid, Lucian, Valken and Iosef were already deep in discussion.
Lucian Galade"Mr. Lancea. I advise caution. Going to war wouldn't help anyone."
Deivid Wisci"We must maintain peace in Sordland and Eastern Merkopa at all costs."
Iosef Lancea"What do you expect us to do? Sit around and do nothing?"
Iosef Lancea"They are testing us."
Valken Kruger"We must respond immediately. Otherwise, they will think we are weak."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:36:40 p16414 / c507
  • ·“We must respond immediately to what exactly?”
  • ·“What is going on here?”
  • ▶“Status report. Now!”
Anton Rayne would assert his leadership by demanding a swift status report to handle the urgent situation effectively.
DeepSeek-R1"Status report. Now!"
Iosef Lancea"Mr. President. Thank you for coming in at this late hour. There is a situation."
Iosef Lancea"Valken, please go ahead and provide your report."
Valken Kruger"Mr. President. Approximately 20 minutes ago, Rumburg downed one of our planes with anti-aircraft fire from the ground. The pilot was declared KIA."
Valken Kruger"The last recorded position of the plane was inside Sordish air space."
Deivid Wisci"General Kruger. I mean no offense but are we absolutely sure that is the case? Are you sure this happened within our borders?"
Valken Kruger"No offense taken. Here are the locations on the map and the report from the air base."
NarratorHe pointed to several markers on the map.
Valken Kruger"To answer your question, yes. We are absolutely sure. You can see here on the map that the plane was downed approximately 10 kilometers inside our borders."
Valken Kruger"We have calculated the trajectory of the gunfire and they started firing when our plane was within our border. The plane was downed near Halgen."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:37:07 p16547 / c140
  • ·“This is terrible. What should we do?”
  • ·“How dare they? This is an attack against our sovereignty!”
  • ▶“We need to be cautious. A war with Rumburg is the last thing we want right now.”
Maintaining caution is crucial to prevent escalating tensions and avoid potential conflicts that could harm Sordland's stability.
DeepSeek-R1"We need to be cautious. A war with Rumburg is the last thing we want right now."
Deivid Wisci"I completely agree."
Lucian Galade"It looks like they are trying to lure us into their trap."
Iosef Lancea"We have enough military power to contest against this violation of international law."
Iosef Lancea"We should attack and down one of their planes in retaliation."
Iosef Lancea"Otherwise, we will look weak and it will lead to more casualties."
Valken Kruger"We can't look weak. If we appear weak, they will jump on the opportunity."
Valken Kruger"We must order a military retaliation. Immediately."
Iosef Lancea"I agree with the General."
Valken Kruger"Mr. President. Our army is ready to do whatever it takes to protect Sordland."
Deivid Wisci"Gentlemen. Before it comes to that point, we can prevent further bloodshed by using diplomacy."
NarratorThe room fell silent before Iosef finally spoke up.
Iosef Lancea"Mr. President. What are your orders?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:37:41 p16561 / c328
  • ·“Start the Sordish war machine and prepare to invade Rumburg! They will pay for their crimes.”

Lucian Galade"Thank you, sir."
Deivid Wisci"I will immediately begin working on it."
Lucian Galade"With Mr. Wisci's capabilities, I am sure that we will de-escalate the situation successfully."
Lucian Galade"We will keep you updated on our progress."
Iosef Lancea"We should've retaliated. I hope the diplomatic response makes sure that further incidents like this are prevented."
Iosef Lancea"If there is a next time, I'm afraid that it will be war."
Valken Kruger"We are showing weakness. Of course it will be a war the next time around."
Valken Kruger"I am frankly disappointed, Mr. President."
Valken Kruger"If we can't even retaliate after such an attack, what will become of this country in the future?"
Deivid Wisci"Peace must be maintained at all costs. I am saying this as a man who has seen too many years of war."
Valken Kruger"We are not the ones that are attacking the peace."
Valken Kruger"Regardless, it looks like your decision has been made, Mr. President."
Valken Kruger"I know when I am not needed."
Valken Kruger"I will take my leave."

Iosef Lancea"I have an idea, Mr. President."
Iosef Lancea"I think we should hold a military parade to display our strength."
Deivid Wisci"I am with Mr. Lancea on this one. This should work well as a deterrent and it will come in handy as a tool to de-escalate the situation."

Iosef Lancea"I will keep you updated on the situation."
Iosef Lancea"For now, have a good night's sleep."
Iosef Lancea"You will need it."

■ Economic Overview And ActionT09

BUDGET -17WEALTH 1STABILITY -8Danger
NarratorI arrived at the Ministry of Economy and proceeded upstairs. An assistant waiting by the door to the meeting room took my jacket.
NarratorI opened the door and entered the largest boardroom in the building. Symon was sitting alone with a glass of water and his documents laid out before him.
NarratorHe stood up when I entered and approached me. After a quick handshake I took a seat.
Symon Holl"Good afternoon, Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:51:52 p16760 / c292
  • ·“Good afternoon.”
  • ▶“How is it going?”
  • ·“Good to see you.”
Asking how things are going shows interest and engagement in the ongoing economic challenges.
DeepSeek-R1"How is it going?"
Symon Holl"I'm pretty swamped with work. Barely getting any time with the family."
NarratorThe glee in his voice was barely disguised.
NarratorA knock was heard on the door and Petr entered the room.
Petr Vectern"I hope I'm not late."
Petr Vectern"Did you start yet?"
Symon Holl"Mr. Vice President. No, not yet."
Symon Holl"Now that everyone is here... let's begin."
Symon Holl"As you all know, we're here to review the economic progress we've made over the past term and decide how to proceed."
Symon Holl"I will brief you quickly on what we have done so far. Just as a refresher."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:52:24 p16816 / c263
  • ·“Very well. Let's hear it."
  • ▶Gesture for him to begin.
A gesture for him to begin shows readiness and control.
DeepSeek-R1I gestured for Symon to start.
NarratorHe gathered the documents in the pile in front of him and adjusted his glasses.
NarratorFrom the pile, he produced two booklets and handed them to us.
Symon Holl"Our extra focus on the economy has resulted in some small-scale positive growth. The extra resources have been a boon to my ministry."
Symon Holl"It would be great if we followed up on this focus during our next term. Let's take a deep-dive into the details now."
Symon Holl"Please turn to page 5."
Turn to page 5.
DeepSeek-R1I turned to page 5.
Symon Holl"Let's start off with our economy plan."
Symon Holl"I would like to say we are on track, but unfortunately, I cannot. Our decisions so far have not been in line with our initial intention for a planned economy."
Symon Holl"It looks like we are going for a mixed approach instead, something between a planned and a market economy."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:53:06 p16774 / c525
  • ▶“I don't see a problem with that. It is sometimes necessary and justified to deviate from a plan.”
  • ·“It is unfortunate that we could not follow through. I blame the circumstances."
  • ·“I know. There is no need to rub it in, Symon.”
Anton Rayne's pragmatic approach aligns with accepting necessary deviations to maintain progress.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't see a problem with that. It is sometimes necessary and justified to deviate from a plan."
Petr Vectern"I agree. Plans change all the time."
Symon Holl"Just pointing out the facts here."
Symon Holl"Let's move on with the report."
Symon Holl"Please turn to page 16."
Turn to page 16.
DeepSeek-R1I turned to page 16, titled "Status of Trade and the Effects of Foreign Policy".
Symon Holl"I am sad to say that our trade has decreased since the beginning of our term."
Symon Holl"Our economy has the potential to stagnate unless we open up further and chase after more trade deals."
Symon Holl"Please turn to page 17."
Turn to page 17.
DeepSeek-R1I turned to page 17.
Symon Holl"In our attempt to repair Sordland's economic system, we have mostly gone against the wishes of big business in favour of the requests of Sordish citizens."
Symon Holl"This caused us to take on some losses in order to make the people happy."
Symon Holl"Thankfully, living standards are steadily increasing. We've been receiving fewer and fewer complaints about our welfare systems."
Petr Vectern"That's great. For our election prospects, I mean."
Symon Holl"Exactly."
Symon Holl"Some of our industries have also been knocked down to their knees by our recent taxations. The demand for these goods has decreased drastically which led to businesses in these industries being shut down."
Symon Holl"Some people are calling the government out for infringing on their personal liberty. Apparently, some even filed lawsuits saying the taxes violate their 'human dignity'. Their lawyers argued for them that they need it for 'mental health treatment'."
Symon Holl"Now, to page 34."
Turn to page 34.
DeepSeek-R1I turned to page 34.
Symon Holl"Production in our country has nearly doubled since the start of our administration. It is a great success."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:53:38 p16791 / c210
  • ▶“I couldn't have asked for more. Good work.”
  • ·“Doubled? It's not enough. We need to triple our production. No. Quadriple!”
  • ·“Let's not forget about the sacrifices we had to make to achieve this.”
Recognizing Symon Holl's efforts and achievements is crucial for maintaining team morale.
DeepSeek-R1"I couldn't have asked for more. Good work."
Petr Vectern"You're a wizard, Symon."
Symon Holl"Teamwork makes it happen."
Symon Holl"Moving on to logistics...."
Symon Holl"We have an infrastructure problem, and the economy won't truly flourish unless we solve it."
Symon Holl"We need to make new roads and improve the existing ones. People and businesses alike have been complaining."
Symon Holl"Smoother commutes, easier access and faster delivery times would help everyone out."
Symon Holl"We are very close to the end. I want to talk about a few numbers. Please turn to page 57. I have prepared a chart."
Turn to page 57.
DeepSeek-R1I turned to page 57.
Symon Holl"Our inflation rate is currently at a staggering 49%, way up from 10%. It might be the highest in Sordland's history."
Symon Holl"The Sordish ren has been wildly fluctuating as a result, and consumer prices have gone up."
Petr Vectern"Which means that Sordish citizens are angry at us."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:54:12 p16830 / c313
  • ·“Rightfully so. We have failed them. We need to take responsibility.”
  • ·“If we are not re-elected, we can't help them.”
  • ▶“We need to fix this. There is nothing more important than the wellbeing of our citizens.”
Prioritizing the citizens' wellbeing by addressing the high inflation rate directly.
DeepSeek-R1"We need to fix this. There is nothing more important than the well-being of our citizens."
Symon Holl"Agreed."
Symon Holl"Mr. President. Do you have any questions so far?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:54:42 p16872 / c264
  • ▶“What is our debt situation?”
  • ·“How are we doing with unemployment?”
  • ·“What are the latest numbers on our GDP?”
  • ·“No. Let's move on.”
Understanding the debt situation is crucial for addressing the high inflation rate and ensuring economic sustainability.
DeepSeek-R1"What is our debt situation?"
Symon Holl"Our current debt is at 611 billion SR, up from 427 billion SR at the beginning of our term."
Symon Holl"This debt is due to government borrowing and the interest on our previous debts."
Symon Holl"As a result, our government's budget has decreased substantially."
Symon Holl"Due to our reckless spending in the first term, I am sorry to say that we have entered a debt crisis. According to my calculation, we won't be able to pay up our government debt without the assistance of a third party."
Symon Holl"It has even disrupted fundings to our welfare plans. Some construction projects and programs needed to be put on hold because of that."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:55:17 p16874 / c382
  • ▶“How are we doing with unemployment?”
  • ·“What are the latest numbers on our GDP?”
  • ·“Those are all the questions I had.”
Unemployment rates are crucial for understanding the broader economic impact of inflation and debt.
DeepSeek-R1"How are we doing with unemployment?"
Symon Holl"The unemployment rate remains steady at 16%."
Symon Holl"The stagnation is mostly due to the lack of investments and average economic planning."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:55:51 p16863 / c336
  • ·“What are the latest numbers on our GDP?”
  • ▶“Those are all the questions I had.”
The meeting has covered key economic aspects, and President Rayne feels all questions have been addressed.
DeepSeek-R1"Those are all the questions I had."
Symon Holl"Very well."
Symon Holl"I want to quickly highlight our budget expenditure."
Symon Holl"My numbers indicate that overall, we have been generously spending our government budget."
Symon Holl"Money that is not spent is just pieces of paper, so that's good for us."
Symon Holl"Now, going into the elections and hopefully a second term, we need to decide on the way forward."
Symon Holl"One thing is clear. We need a bigger budget."
Symon Holl"We shouldn't start our next term with a budget deficit."
Symon Holl"What should we do, Mr. President? Any ideas?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:56:24 p16715 / c315
  • ·“We will take an international loan from MFI.”[+2 Government Budget]
  • ▶“Nothing comes to my mind right now. We will have to make do with what we have.”
Avoiding additional debt is preferable given the current budget deficit and economic instability.
DeepSeek-R1"Nothing comes to my mind right now. We will have to make do with what we have."
Symon Holl"Very well, sir."
Symon Holl"Now that that's out of way..."
Symon Holl"During our first term, we have faced many challenges in the midst of a recession."
Symon Holl"We have worked hard to fight against the odds but it was not enough."
NarratorSymon paused to wipe his glasses. He swallowed before continuing.
Symon Holl"Mr. President, you may have surmised from the numbers I have given you that the economy has worsened considerably over the course of our administration."
Symon Holl"I wanted to prepare you for the truth. This is no longer a mere recession. We've entered a period that historians might reasonably refer to as the Sordish Great Depression."
Symon Holl"We have broken our promises and failed our people. My only hope is that future generations will be able to forgive us when they look back."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:56:57 p16721 / c307
  • ·“All of the blame falls on your shoulders as the Minister of Economy, Symon. You will resign before the next term.”
  • ▶“We have done all we could. Now is the time to look to the future and try to fix our mistakes.”
  • ·“This is all my fault. I made the decisions that led us here.”
It's constructive to acknowledge challenges and focus on future solutions.
DeepSeek-R1"We have done all we could. Now is the time to look to the future and try to fix our mistakes."
Petr Vectern"Yes! Some things can't be undone. But for the economy, at least, there's still hope."
Symon Holl"We will get through this together."
Symon Holl"That is all for our meeting today."
Symon Holl"Thank you for coming."
NarratorThe meeting dispersed. I hurried to the cafeteria to get a cup of coffee.

■ Decision Penalty Sordish DepressionT09

BUDGET -17WEALTH 1STABILITY -8Danger
EventSordish Great Depression: The current economic climate in Sordland has taken a severe downturn, leading to what is now being referred to as the 'Sordish Great Depression'. The sharp rise in unemployment coupled with a steep decline in Gross Domestic Product displays the gravity of the situation. Prevailing concerns about government overspending have further aggravated the crisis, giving rise to financial instability.
Our nation will recover...

■ Television InterviewT09

BUDGET -17WEALTH 1STABILITY -8Danger
NarratorThe makeup crew had just finished their preparations for the interview.
NarratorLucian stopped me on my way out to the balcony of the Blue Mansion, where the filming crew was standing ready.
Lucian Galade"Are you ready for the interview, sir?"

Lucian Galade"Good. I have done my best to make this happen. This is very important for our administration."
Lucian Galade"The crew is prepared to start filming. Mr. Sacker is excited to interview you."
Lucian Galade"As I explained before, it would be bad press to decline this offer. They are the most prestigious interview program in the world."
Lucian Galade"And they are from Kyrute. The country is known for its neutrality. I don't think there will be too much pressure."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:58:29 p17021 / c222

  • ▶"It's fine. I can handle it."

DeepSeek-R1"It's fine. I can handle it."
NarratorI walked to the balcony and sat on the seat prepared for me, directly across from Mr. Sacker.
Tuomas Sacker"Mr. President. Welcome to One-On-One."
Tuomas Sacker"Thank you so much for taking the time to speak with us."
Tuomas Sacker"Let us begin with the latest incident between Sordland and Rumburg."
Tuomas Sacker"An aircraft of the Sordish Air Forces was shot down by Rumburgian anti-air guns a few weeks ago. Officials from the Rumburgian government proclaimed that the aircraft was flying inside the airspace of Rumburg."
Tuomas Sacker"Your government has been silent apart from a few statements which claimed that your plane was indeed in Sordish airspace when it was shot down. Do you believe the Rumburgian forces initiated an intentional attack?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:59:04 p16029 / c277
  • ·"We have reason to believe that, yes. Rumburg has become increasingly aggressive over the last years."
  • ▶"I don't want to think that they attacked us intentionally. It must have been a mistake."
  • ·"Is that even a question? Rumburg is a threat to the whole region. This was an act of war."
President Rayne would prefer to maintain a peaceful image despite Rumburg's actions.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't want to think that they attacked us intentionally. It must have been a mistake."
Tuomas Sacker"Does that mean your side is willing to have diplomatic talks with Rumburg?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:59:36 p15921 / c324
  • ▶"Of course. Rumburg is our neighbor, and we want to have good relations with all of our neighbors."
  • ·"If they're willing to, yes."
Choosing option 0 maintains a diplomatic tone and emphasizes good neighborly relations.
DeepSeek-R1"Of course. Rumburg is our neighbor, and we want to have good relations with all of our neighbors."
Tuomas Sacker"Do you believe Queen Livingston shares the same sentiment?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:00:07 p16063 / c281

Maintaining diplomatic neutrality is key to avoiding further escalation with Rumburg.
DeepSeek-R1"I can't say that for sure. We'll have to ask the Queen herself."
Tuomas Sacker"This week, both the Arcasian and United Contanan governments have condemned the airplane incident."
Tuomas Sacker"Do you think they would be willing to serve as mediators in the case of a broader conflict between your countries?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:00:37 p16211 / c239

NarratorHe stopped for a moment and took a brief look at his papers.
Tuomas Sacker"Let's continue on that note."
Tuomas Sacker"I want to expand a little more on your foreign policy."
Tuomas Sacker"Let's talk about the ongoing conflict around Heljiland Island."
Tuomas Sacker"Sordland is not officially taking any sides between Agnolia and Valgsland."
Tuomas Sacker"So far, you have refrained from making any statements about the conflict."
Tuomas Sacker"According to reports from yesterday, the Valgslandian navy has finally attacked the Agnolian fleet at Heljiland Island."
Tuomas Sacker"Today, Prime Minister Van Hoorten confirmed the invasion. He said Valgslandian troops have landed on the island."
Tuomas Sacker"So, here's my question. What is Sordland's role in all this, and what will it be going forward?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:01:18 p16361 / c469

  • ·"We stand together with the Agnolian government."

DeepSeek-R1"Sordland has no role in this conflict, nor do we plan to have one. We are not taking any sides."
Tuomas Sacker"Would Sordland also get involved if the situation gets out of control?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:01:50 p16299 / c297

DeepSeek-R1"No. Sordland has no intention of intervening in other nations' conflicts."
Tuomas Sacker"Okay, let's move on to another topic."
Tuomas Sacker"Last year, a huge conflict took place close to your borders with Wehlen."
Tuomas Sacker"When the Wehzek forces partook in a massacre of Bludish civilians under the guise of eliminating the Bludish Freedom Front..."
Tuomas Sacker"Sordland decided to stay out of the conflict. In fact, many refugees fleeing from the massacre came to Sordland."
Tuomas Sacker"Knowing that Sordland also had its troubles with the Bludish independence movement, how did Sordland cope with the influx of Bludish refugees coming in from Wehlen?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:02:30 p16453 / c443
  • ·"I think we've handled it pretty well. Most of those refugees have already been relocated to major Sordish cities."
  • ·"It was definitely tough. Sordland has its own problems and this put extra pressure on our economy."
  • ▶"I don't understand the question. The Bludish movement has nothing to do with this. They could have been Sords or Wehzeks as well. First of all, they are human beings."
The response emphasizes empathy and humanity, aligning with Rayne's progressive stance.
DeepSeek-R1"The Bludish movement has nothing to do with this. They could have been Sords or Wehzeks as well. First and foremost, they are human beings."
Tuomas Sacker"The world has been watching this humanitarian crisis with utmost attention. And I have to give you credit for allowing these people to enter Sordland."
Tuomas Sacker"Unlike your neighbour Lespia, Sordland took this responsibility."
Tuomas Sacker"Do you feel the burden for being the only country who opened their borders to their refugees?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:03:01 p16429 / c262
  • ▶"To some extent, but we can't complain. In an ideal world, all surrounding nations would take the responsibility. But Sordland stands alone yet again."
  • ·"Definitely. I deplore Prime Minister Alvarez for not doing anything about the humanitarian crisis right next to us."
  • ·"Unfortunately, our economy is still in recession. It would be a lie if I said we don't feel the burden."
Anton Rayne would emphasize the moral responsibility to aid refugees while acknowledging the ideal of shared burden.
DeepSeek-R1"To some extent, but we can't complain. In an ideal world, all surrounding nations would take the responsibility. But Sordland stands alone yet again."
Tuomas Sacker"I understand. Can you say with certainty that these refugees can adapt and find a better life in Sordland?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:03:35 p16485 / c350
  • ▶"I must be honest. If they can integrate in our society, yes. Otherwise, they will certainly face problems."

Anton Rayne's cautious approach towards integration aligns with acknowledging challenges while maintaining hope for adaptation.
DeepSeek-R1"I must be honest. If they can integrate in our society, yes. Otherwise, they will certainly face problems."
Tuomas Sacker"But how about-"
NarratorOn the gesture of the director, he stopped before finishing his sentence.
Tuomas Sacker"Well... It seems that's all the time we have for today."
NarratorThe director shouted at the crew to stop recording. We both stood up and shook hands.
Tuomas Sacker"Thank you for your valuable time, Mr. President."
Tuomas Sacker"It was a pleasure."

NarratorI went inside the building.

■ Lileas DiscussionT09

BUDGET -17WEALTH 1STABILITY -8Danger
NarratorThe historic Graf Manor lay on the outskirts of Arvory, at the end of a long, winding drive. Nearly two centuries old, it was as formidable as Lileas herself.
NarratorLileas had invited me here for a private dinner, just the two of us.
NarratorI wasn't sure of her motivations, but was happy to have an excuse to escape the chaos back in Holsord.
NarratorSerge dropped me off at the door.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:04:52 p16010 / c318
  • ▶Knock.
  • ·Go inside without knocking.
Knocking shows respect for the host's privacy and aligns with social norms.
DeepSeek-R1I used the heavy brass door knocker to signal my arrival. The butler answered and showed me into the parlor.
NarratorGas lanterns lent the wood-paneled room a cosy orange glow. Lileas was sitting on the sofa. Above her hung a nearly life-sized portrait of a sallow, unhappy-looking young woman in a lace ball gown.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:05:34 p16073 / c497
  • ·"Is that you?"
  • ·"Is that your mother?"
  • ▶"Is that your grandmother?"
The portrait is likely of an ancestor, and given the manor's age, it makes sense to refer to her grandmother.
DeepSeek-R1"Is that your grandmother?"
Lileas Graf"My mother, actually."
Lileas Graf"I was her only child, you know. Giving birth to me left her medically unable to conceive."
Lileas Graf"My father never could forgive her for not bearing him a son. And she never forgave me."
NarratorShe took a measured sip of one of the two glasses of sparkling wine she was holding, and handed me the other.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:06:05 p16190 / c290
  • ·"Sounds like a painful childhood."
  • ▶"Get to the point, Lileas. Why did you invite me here?"
Lileas Graf is known for her straightforward nature, and cutting through the chase aligns with her character.
DeepSeek-R1"Get to the point, Lileas. Why did you invite me here?"
Lileas Graf"Given how your wife spends her time these days, I thought you might be in need of a home-cooked supper."
NarratorThe butler silently stepped into the parlor and rang a silver bell.
Lileas Graf"Ah. Speaking of which."
NarratorShe led me into the dining room, where a glittering chandelier hung from the high ceiling. Beneath it was a long table set for two. A woman in an apron and chef's hat, Wehzek by her appearance, ceremoniously presented a roast rack of venison.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:06:37 p16153 / c288
  • ·Start eating.
  • ▶"Smells delicious."
  • ·"A home-cooked supper, huh?"
Complimenting the meal is a polite and engaging response that shows appreciation.
DeepSeek-R1"Smells delicious."
Lileas Graf"The groundskeeper shot it this morning."
NarratorLileas bowed her head and said a quick prayer, then motioned for me to dig in.
NarratorIt was some of the best meat I'd ever had. We ate in silence for a while, then Lileas pensively laid down her cutlery.
Lileas Graf"Anton, I wish you had made national security more of a top priority during your term."
Lileas Graf"Likewise, I wish you had heeded my request to place the Gendarmerie under my jurisdiction."
Lileas Graf"And I regret that you caved to Ms. Walda and mandated the teaching of that evolution nonsense in schools."
Lileas Graf"Not to mention feeling the need to pump our children full of strange chemicals in response to some unconfirmed health threat."
Lileas Graf"Regardless, I'm looking forward to working more closely together during your next term."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:07:26 p16443 / c587
  • ·"Me too, if it means more excellent dinners like this."
  • ·"I'll drink to that."
  • ▶"God willing."
Choosing 'God willing' is a diplomatic response that acknowledges her perspective without committing to any policy agreement.
DeepSeek-R1"God willing."
NarratorLileas raised her glass and smiled.
NarratorWe clinked glasses.
NarratorBy the end of the meal, I was pleasantly full and a little tipsy. The butler had to lead me back through the manor's winding corridors until I found the door.
NarratorI waved at Lileas out the window as Serge pulled away. The coming campaign would be turbulent, but at least I had her on my side.
NarratorOr so I hoped.

TURN 10

■ FCAnrica MatchT10

BUDGET -17WEALTH 1STABILITY -8Danger
NarratorToday, the management of FC Anrica had invited me to attend a pivotal football match. At Lucian's urging, I accepted.
NarratorThe team was representing Sordland in the international arena. According to Lucian, it would have been un-presidential not to put in an appearance.
NarratorFC Anrica was finally in the Merkopan Champions Cup -- the most prestigious football tournament in the world. For the first time ever, a Sordish team had managed to qualify for the Knockout phase.
NarratorMillions of Sordish people were waiting to watch FC Anrica compete against the legendary Rizian team Real Montaklar. Despite all odds being stacked against the team, the mood was full of hope.
NarratorA win today would bring Anrica to the quarter finals, a feat that no Sordish team had ever managed to achieve.
NarratorAnd of course, all presidents of Sordland -- current and former -- were invited to watch the historic competition.
NarratorI was told that both Tarquin Soll and Ewald Alphonso would be joining me in the lounge reserved for us.
Lucian Galade"How are you feeling, sir?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:08:18 p16725 / c217
  • ▶"I'm fine, Lucian. Thank you."
  • ·"I'm just a little worried."
President Rayne would want to project confidence and composure during a significant national event.
DeepSeek-R1"I'm fine, Lucian. Thank you."
NarratorWe made our way towards the Maroon Stadium. The arena was situated in one of the most crowded quarters of Anrica, surrounded by dense urban development.
NarratorAs we wound our way through the streets, I noticed the name of Tarquin Soll everywhere. Since I'd last been here, markets, restaurants and even pubs named after the Colonel had all popped up.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:08:57 p16655 / c326
  • ·"Why is Anrica so crazy about Soll all of a sudden?"
  • ▶"Am I imagining things, or did we just pass a pub called 'Alco-Soll'?"
The pub name 'Alco-Soll' humorously highlights Soll's widespread influence beyond politics.
DeepSeek-R1"Am I imagining things, or did we just pass a pub called 'Alco-Soll'?"
Lucian Galade"I'm afraid we did, sir."
Lucian Galade"I've heard it is part of an ad campaign for Anrica. An attempt to draw tourists, or at least Sollist ones, to the city. Soll's legacy still lives on for millions, after all."
NarratorLucian's pocket radio crackled. After responding, he turned to me.
Lucian Galade"Sir. The security gave us the clear and informed us that Tarquin Soll has already arrived at the lounge."
Lucian Galade"Good luck. I know he's quite the imposing presence."
Lucian Galade"Please follow the guards to the top floor. You will be seated in the VIP lounge."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:09:36 p16794 / c328
  • ·"Are you sure you can't join me?"
  • ▶"Any advice before I leave?"
  • ·"That's a pity. I'll save you some snacks."
Seeking Lucian's advice will help navigate the conversation strategically.
DeepSeek-R1"Any advice before I leave?"
Lucian Galade"You know the guests of honor more than I do, sir. If all else fails, just focus on the match. It shouldn't be difficult."
Lucian Galade"Even just the fact that you are here will surely help with our campaign image."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:10:21 p16801 / c421
  • ·Follow the security.
  • ·"I hope so, Lucian."
  • ·"Sure, Lucian."
  • ▶"My thoughts exactly."
  • ·"I just hope FC Gelsord fans don't hold it against me."
Anton agrees with Lucian's advice to focus on the match, showing unity and confidence.
DeepSeek-R1"My thoughts exactly."
NarratorI followed the security. We entered beneath a giant sign that read: "Welcome to the Hall of Martyrs."
NarratorThe corridors inside were painted almost completely in maroon. Adorning them were various depictions of the Sordish goat and numerous jerseys, awards and team photographs.
NarratorOn our way up, I was greeted by the president of the club. Beaming with pride, he immediately shook my hand.
NarratorBehind him, Mayor Curtan Leste and Gus Manger were waiting to greet me as well. All of them had FC Anrica pins attached to their collars.
Curtan Leste"Welcome to Anrica, Mr. President."
Curtan Leste"You are always welcome in Anrica, and it is great to see you show support in person."
Curtan Leste"Thanks for being here. This match means a lot for all of us Anricans."
Gus Manger"And all of Sordland! Never before has a Sordish team reached this stage in the Merkopan Cup."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:11:02 p16913 / c293
  • ·"It is great to be here in Anrica."
  • ▶"I have high hopes for today. This is a big achievement for Sordish football."
  • ·"Honestly, I don't know much about football. But I'm here for the players and for my country."
Expressing high hopes for the match highlights its significance and boosts morale.
DeepSeek-R1"I have high hopes for today. This is a big achievement for Sordish football."
Gus Manger"Football is of rapidly expanding importance in Sordland and beyond. It's good that we see the opportunity in it."
NarratorThe president of the club walked in, handed us gift bags full of FC Anrica merchandise and gave a speech stressing the importance of today's match.
NarratorAfter delivering his best wishes, he kindly pointed us towards the lounge, which was surrounded by heavily armed men.
NarratorOnce I finally found myself inside, I was greeted by an amazing view of the entire pitch through floor-to-ceiling windows.
NarratorTarquin Soll was already seated on the leather sofa, casting his hawk-like gaze out onto the field. He did not react when I entered.

Choosing a polite and curious response sets a respectful tone in the formal setting.
DeepSeek-R1"To what do we owe this pleasure, Colonel?"

Tarquin Soll"Please. Join me."
NarratorI approached the sofa and sat next to him.
Tarquin Soll"I didn't expect you to show up today."
Tarquin Soll"It is heartening to see that you're a supporter of FC Anrica and Sordish sports."

Ewald Alphonso"Well, Colonel? How do you feel about being Anrica's new poster boy?"

Ewald Alphonso"I was happy to find out that the royals over there appear to be in favor of pursuing friendlier relations with Sordland."
Ewald Alphonso"I still don't think they will do anything to harm their connection with Rumburg, but cooperating with them was the right call."
Tarquin Soll"You're fond of the monarchists now, Ewald? They must be filling your pockets with Rizian gold."
Ewald Alphonso"For Dast's sake, Mr. Soll, try to see the positive in what I do. For once."

NarratorIn his outstretched hand, he held a whimsical compass, glowing with a soft golden light. Surrounding him were people from all walks of life and cultures.

Narrator"Ladies and gentlemen, we bring you a message from the Alphonso Foundation, dedicated to spreading kindness and hope to the less fortunate."
Narrator"Picture a Sordland where compassion shines brighter than the stars in the night sky, where the power of generosity uplifts those who need it most. That Sordland can exist, thanks to the Alphonso Foundation."
Narrator"Led by the charismatic and esteemed President Ewald Alphonso, this foundation is on a mission to make a difference in the lives of our fellow citizens, one act of kindness at a time."
Tarquin Soll"You call this kindness?"
Narrator"Every Sordish Ren you donate to the Alphonso Foundation helps us provide shelter, food, and a chance for a brighter future to those without a home, without hope."
Narrator"Thanks to the donations of President Ewald Alphonso and thousands of Sordish citizens, the Alphonso Foundation has already lifted hundreds out of homelessness."
Tarquin Soll"A couple of hundred people? Congratulations to you both. This is how Sordland will eradicate poverty."
Narrator"Won't you join us in this noble endeavor? Just call the number, 1-600-ALPHONSO, to make your contribution today. Your support will help us extend a helping hand to those in need."
NarratorThe recording ended with a few seconds of upbeat jazzy background music that slowly faded out.

Tarquin Soll"And now you're wasting your time on crap like this."
