  • ▶"Gentlemen. Why don't we calm down a little? We are here to watch football."
  • ·Don't comment.
Keeping the peace and refocusing on the event is the best approach.
DeepSeek-R1"Gentlemen. Why don't we calm down a little? We are here to watch football."
NarratorNeither of the two men seemed to care. They continued without stopping to hear what I said.
Tarquin Soll"Instead of blaming the party, why don't you take a look at yourself? That same party voted me out on their own volition in preference of *you*."
Tarquin Soll"According to you, their only *correct* decision must have been to elect *you* as leader. And the rest are completely wrong. Do you think everything is a problem that is specifically created to block *you*?"
Tarquin Soll"Take a look at yourself. You are the personification of what you're trying to criticize."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:23:25 p16621 / c425
  • ·"You have a point, Mr. Soll. But don't act like you're above all of us."
  • ▶"That's an empty argument. The party and the government were in that state due to your actions over 20 years."
  • ·"This is getting out of hand. I think both of you should calm down."
  • ·"Oh, please. We know that you are still trying to pull the strings behind the scenes, Mr. Soll."
Anton counters Tarquin's critique by holding him accountable for past actions affecting the party.
DeepSeek-R1"That's an empty argument. The party and the government were in that state due to your actions over 20 years."
Tarquin Soll"Correct. They built a republic within two decades in the harshest political climate, and they reached a state of being able to elect and oust their own leaders."
Tarquin Soll"Oh, what a disastrous state to be in."
NarratorSuddenly, we turned our heads to the field as a massive roar shook the stadium.
NarratorThe second half was already in full swing and Anrican players were aggressively attacking, weaving in and out of defenders with speed.
NarratorSuddenly, the ball was passed to the famous Sordish striker, Hakon Zlater, who was in front of the goal. I noticed Soll shifting in his seat, his gaze suddenly locked on the field.
NarratorThe Sordish crowd went crazy as Zlater's powerful shot brought the ball to the back of the Rizian net.
Ewald Alphonso"YES!"
NarratorEwald jumped from his seat.
NarratorSuddenly, the cheering was interrupted by the linesman raising the flag -- making an offside call.
Ewald Alphonso"Bullshit! That wasn't an offside."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:24:01 p16658 / c298
  • ·"That referee is trying to sabotage us. He was clearly in front of the defenders when he received the ball."
  • ·"Do you think the Rizians could have bought the referees?"
  • ▶"To be fair, nobody here cares."
  • ·Do not comment.
Anton prefers to avoid escalating conflicts and focus on broader issues.
DeepSeek-R1"To be fair, nobody here cares."
Ewald Alphonso"Well... I still do."
Tarquin Soll"Halt your endless bickering. The linesman made the right call."
Ewald Alphonso"The angle was all wrong. A decision like that shouldn't be made without proper evaluation."
Tarquin Soll"What next? You're going to install cameras and analyze every position from different angles?"
NarratorThe excitement in the stadium reached a fever pitch as the Sordish striker dribbled the ball past two defenders and took a shot at the goal again. The crowd held its breath as the ball sailed through the air, but the Montaklar goalkeeper made a miraculous save.
Ewald Alphonso"Damn. That was close."
NarratorHe suddenly pointed to a group of people who were holding signs in the front row.
Ewald Alphonso"Do you see that?"
NarratorIt was a group of Rizians who were holding signs in the Sordish language: "No To War In Merkopa. Peace Between Rumburg and Sordland."
Ewald Alphonso"Looks like Rizians are also scared of a possible war between us and Rumburg."
Tarquin Soll"The Toras family is a puppet of Queen Livingston. If the queen wants war, Rizians will follow them."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:24:35 p16745 / c234
  • ·Stay silent.
  • ·"They're just a bunch of hippies. Who cares what they say?"
  • ·"Do you think Rizia would aid Rumburg in a war with us?"
  • ▶"Well, they're right. Nobody wants a war with Rumburg."
Agreeing with the protesters' sentiment helps maintain peace and avoids escalating tensions.
DeepSeek-R1"Well, they're right. Nobody wants a war with Rumburg."
NarratorSuddenly, a wave of shouting made us focus back on the field. An Anrican player was shown a yellow card.
NarratorWith only a few minutes left on the clock, it looked as though the match would end with a 1-0 loss for FC Anrica.
NarratorBut suddenly, everything changed. Number 10 of Real Montaklar dribbled expertly towards the goal and effortlessly scored as Anrican defenders watched him in shock.
Ewald Alphonso"I knew it."
NarratorWe watched in silence as the scoreboard was modified to display: "FC Anrica: 0 - Real Montaklar: 2."
NarratorThe Rizian side broke into massive applause as the referee blew the final whistle.
Tarquin Soll"Well then... That settles it."
Ewald Alphonso"We should go down and console the boys."
NarratorWe left the lounge and went downstairs to congratulate the team for their efforts.

■ Alliance Of NationsT10

BUDGET -17WEALTH 1STABILITY -8Danger
NarratorI, Deivid Wisci and the rest of the Sordish delegation to the Alliance of Nations arrived in Kyrute. The local police escorted us to the Alliance's headquarters in downtown Konstantium, immediately recognizable by its shining dome.
NarratorOn entering the building, we were met by a parliamentary guide who briefed us about the procedures. After a strict security screening, the staff ushered us to the third floor.
NarratorWe entered the massive circular assembly hall and took our designated seats. Deivid sat next to me.
Deivid Wisci"All of the world's most powerful people under one gleaming dome."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:25:37 p16421 / c473
  • ▶"One wrong word and the world will go to hell."
  • ·"I'm glad to be among them."
  • ·"It's amazing how someone has managed to fill a room with people that absolutely loathe each other."
Anton Rayne's cautious and diplomatic nature leads him to emphasize the importance of careful speech in a room filled with powerful figures.
DeepSeek-R1"One wrong word and the world will go to hell."
Deivid Wisci"High pressure, I know. Just believe in yourself, Anton. From what I have seen, you will do just fine."
Deivid Wisci"How are you feeling, Anton? Being the subject of so much media attention must take a toll on you and the family."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:26:15 p16563 / c279

  • ▶"We're managing... somehow."
  • ·"I'm managing... somehow."
  • ·"It comes with the job. Monica and I already came to terms with the risk after the inauguration ball."

Deivid Wisci"Making harsh decisions and dealing with their consequences."
Deivid Wisci"I wish I could have supported you more, but time has been a cruel mistress."

Express gratitude and acknowledge Deivid's support.
DeepSeek-R1"What you have done so far is more than enough. Don't treat yourself so harshly."
NarratorFrom a distance, we could see the president of the general assembly, Mr. Nines, approaching his designated position.
Deivid Wisci"Well, the session is about to start. Let's get comfortable. It's going to be a long day."
NarratorAfter a short opening speech, President Nines called the first speaker to the floor.
Ephraim Nines"It is my great pleasure to invite the Prime Minister of Lespia to address the assembly."
NarratorPatricio Alvarez stood and walked up to the podium as the assembly applauded.
Patricio Alvarez"Mr. President, it is my pleasure to return to this podium."
NarratorDeivid leaned in and spoke in a low voice.
Deivid Wisci"Let's see how crazy this year's session will get."

  • ▶"Let's see..."
  • ·"I'm surprised he isn't drunk."

A neutral response that maintains professionalism in a formal setting.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's see..."
Patricio Alvarez"When I last spoke here, the message I gave was of encouragement and support to this organization and the vital role it plays."
Patricio Alvarez"Of all the challenges Eastern Merkopa and the whole world has faced since then, one has grown larger and more imminent than any other."
Patricio Alvarez"The threat of the so-called 'socialist' states and the actions taken by their authoritarian rulers with no regard to international law."
NarratorA loud noise came from the table of Valgsland. I saw Emmerich Hegel pounding his fists on his table in protest.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:28:00 p16910 / c284

  • ·"Looks like the chancellor is already mad."

DeepSeek-R1I kept watching.
Patricio Alvarez"...Today, the need to come together as the leaders of the free world is more important than ever."
Patricio Alvarez"The CSP, under the leadership of United Contana, has been fuelling Valgsland's military-industrial complex and its expansionist aims in Eastern Merkopa."
Patricio Alvarez"The world turned a blind eye when the armies of Valgsland occupied Heljiland. And they did not stop there."
Patricio Alvarez"According to our intelligence reports, United Contana-made nuclear missiles were quickly deployed to the island."
NarratorHe pulled out a photograph. Even though it was hard to see from my position, I recognized it as an aerial image of Heljiland.
Patricio Alvarez"We demand that the Chancellor speak the truth. As you can all see, we have evidence of the construction of silos."

DeepSeek-R1I kept listening.
Patricio Alvarez"No Eastern Merkopan nation can accept the installation of weapons of mass destruction in the middle of our Markian Sea!"
NarratorEmmerich Hegel stood up and kept pounding his fists.
Patricio Alvarez"We hereby call for the immediate dismantling and withdrawal of all missiles and other offensive weapons from Heljiland."
Patricio Alvarez"We invite all the member states to support our call for a resolution in accordance with Article 11 of the Falenco Treaty."
NarratorA lot of noise came from the assembly hall in response. Most of the Western Merkopan delegates were clapping, while others either shouted in protest or sat in stony silence.
Patricio Alvarez"Let us also not forget about the recent events that transpired in an another state with socialist teachings. A state that wages war against its own innocent citizens. Wehlen."

Patricio Alvarez"Lespia was affected not only by the large wave of refugees coming from Wehlen, but by the subsequent government seizure of all of our companies that were active in the country."
Patricio Alvarez"We hereby call the member states to action against Wehlen and its rogue government."
Patricio Alvarez"Thank you."

NarratorWiktor turned to President Nines and bowed slightly.
Wiktor Smolak"Mr. President."
NarratorHe then turned back at us and stared out into the hall for a few seconds.
Wiktor Smolak"Fellow delegates."
Wiktor Smolak"It is true that the world today faces a multitude of challenges."
Wiktor Smolak"In the midst of these challenges, the world's most powerful leaders gather here in Kyrute."
Wiktor Smolak"And what do they do? They make threats. Promote hate and spread lies."
Wiktor Smolak"Is this the Alliance of Nations we all want? Where is the cooperation and friendship? Where is the unity?"
Wiktor Smolak"I want to remind you of the wars that the West fought on our soil... Of the foreign troops who occupied our country and seized our valuable resources."
Wiktor Smolak"I want to remind you how they stole the oil from our sacred soil. And they did not know when to stop."
Wiktor Smolak"The ATO soldiers left but their interference never ceased. They financed armed rebel groups whose only purpose is to create terror inside our country."
Wiktor Smolak"So, fellow delegates! Don't you dare speak about Wehlen before you acknowledge your involvement in our civil war... Before you acknowledge your own dark past."

Deivid Wisci"What did you expect, Mr. President?"
Deivid Wisci"It's President Smolak..."
Wiktor Smolak"As is well known, after the tremendous tragedy of the Wehlen Civil War, Arcasia made a deal with United Contana, where they agreed upon the withdrawal of their troops along with the removal of half of our army."
Wiktor Smolak"Not only did they weaken us, we were left alone in facing the genocidal Bludish Freedom Front, which the ATO funds together with Rumburg."
Wiktor Smolak"Just like the delegate before me, I will show you some of the evidence of foreign interference."
NarratorHe picked up a large stack of papers and waved them back and forth.
Wiktor Smolak"I have clear evidence of direct aid from Rumburg to the Bludish Freedom Front. They smuggled tons of KA-74s in Wehlen. They smuggled in their spies. Smuggled in mercenary commanders."

DeepSeek-R1I kept listening.
Wiktor Smolak"These documents will be published everywhere. Copies have already been sent to the Investigative Affairs of the Alliance of Nations."
Wiktor Smolak"I expect this organization to do its task and investigate this properly, instead of leaving Wehlen behind as they have always done."
Wiktor Smolak"Coming back to the claims of the Lespian representative... First, there is no evidence of civilian massacres by the Wehzek forces, nor so-called purges of minorities."
Wiktor Smolak"Second, the operation was limited only to the militant BFF, not the Bluds in general."
Wiktor Smolak"Third, we know your government is also implicated in the funding of the BFF. And that your oil companies aided their militants during the operation."
Wiktor Smolak"Fourth, as the state, we only took what was ours. The companies the delegate spoke of were not only aiding militants, they were brought to Wehlen by force."
Wiktor Smolak"Know that Wehlen will no longer recognize the right of any other country to determine what type of weapons we are permitted to possess or what type of policies we follow within our borders."
Wiktor Smolak"Wehlen stands stronger and more united than ever. Our people have spoken. The enemies inside our borders are crushed."
Wiktor Smolak"Now, I ask every delegate here to read our report on the connections of the West with the armed militants in Eastern Merkopa. And I ask the Alliance of Nations to do what's right."
Wiktor Smolak"Wehlen only wants peace. Our people want peace. They are counting on the support and understanding of all the nations of the world."
Wiktor Smolak"Their proclamation is, 'Free Wehlen or Death'."
NarratorThe assembly roared, with particularly loud applause coming from the seats of Rikan nations.

Maartin Van Hoorten"Mr. President. Fellow delegates of the Assembly, and friends."
Maartin Van Hoorten"It is an honor to be with you today."
Maartin Van Hoorten"I wish we could meet under different circumstances."
Maartin Van Hoorten"Once again, the CSP nations -- and in particular Valgsland -- have shown us what they're willing to do to achieve their political aims."
Maartin Van Hoorten"I must sadly say that the world has left us alone."
Maartin Van Hoorten"We were one of the first countries to propose proper cooperation among Eastern Merkopan nations."
Maartin Van Hoorten"Yet when we were threatened by Valgslandian expansionism, nobody came to our side."
Maartin Van Hoorten"Although Mr. Alvarez voiced his indirect support for us earlier in today's session, I can't keep myself from wondering where he was when our lands were under invasion by the very *socialists* he spoke about."
Maartin Van Hoorten"So, here I am, asking for the support of the fellow member states of the Alliance of Nations. Agnolia is under attack by Valgsland."

NarratorNines repeatedly banged his gavel.
Ephraim Nines"Order!"
Emmerich Hegel"Order?! There will only be order when this coward stops smearing Valgsland's good name!"
Ephraim Nines"Silence, Chancellor. Mr. Prime Minister, please continue."
Maartin Van Hoorten"As you all know, Heljiland -- which had been a territory of Agnolia for more than three decades -- was invaded by Valgsland in a sudden attack."
Maartin Van Hoorten"Not only did they unlawfully occupy our land, they brought nuclear missiles to the island the moment our forces retreated."
Maartin Van Hoorten"Malenyevist terror surrounds us on all sides."
Maartin Van Hoorten"We must not falter. I invite all member states to find a resolution in accordance with Article 11 of the Falenco Treaty, enforcing the immediate dismantling of all missiles from Heljiland."
Maartin Van Hoorten"I must also remind you that under Article 3 of the Ventrie City Treaty, the Alliance of Nations *must* ensure the withdrawal of the occupying force from the island."
Maartin Van Hoorten"I call the democratic world to action. Against the tyranny of Malenyevism, authoritarianism, militarism and extremism."
NarratorThe banging noise started again. Hegel stood up and approached the rostrum.

DeepSeek-R1"Deivid, buckle up. It's the Chancellor's time now."
Deivid Wisci"It looks like you're having fun, Mr. President."
Emmerich Hegel"First of all, I want to extend a due welcome to the new states from Rika that have joined this organization."
Emmerich Hegel"After fighting for years against colonialism and imperialism, they more than earned our membership vote. I am overjoyed to see them finally represented in this assembly."
Emmerich Hegel"As our numbers grow and more states are represented in this stage, we come ever closer to finding resolutions to the conflicts that plague us."
Emmerich Hegel"For hundreds of years, the dominant economies of Merkopa razed, pillaged and exploited the continents of Rika and Xina."
Emmerich Hegel"Sadly, Valgsland was also a part of this plunder in the years before the revolution, when it still clung to the idea of imperialism."
Emmerich Hegel"But when our masses found their voice, the old colonial powers and their new financiers turned against them."
Emmerich Hegel"These powers did whatever they could to hamper our revolutions. They did not want the people to be heard."
Emmerich Hegel"They are scared of us for showing the possibility of a better path. Of course they don't want us to succeed. We threaten the *productivity* that runs on the blood and sweat of their working class."
Emmerich Hegel"And now they have started labelling us the aggressors. Yet we all know it was the Arcasians who built up military bases all around Merkopa. It was the ATO that was first formed to fight against Malenyevism."

DeepSeek-R1I kept watching.
Emmerich Hegel"Heljiland was, is and will be Valgslandian territory. Our intervention in the island was completely justified."
Emmerich Hegel"Other delegates brought up the Falenco and Ventrie City Treaties. However, they are forgetting about the Treaty of Adrium."
Emmerich Hegel"Valgsland exercised its right to intervene on behalf of our people, who were being oppressed and massacred by the Agnolian government just because they did not recognize an appointed governor. They didn't get to elect one."
Emmerich Hegel"We acted in full accordance with the second article of the treaty, If there's a side to be blamed, it is the state which murders its minorities for publicly showing support to our nation."
Emmerich Hegel"There were also claims about illegal missiles."
Emmerich Hegel"No country in the world has the right to determine what type of weapons we import."
Emmerich Hegel"If there's a conflict the Alliance can resolve, let's start with the Arcasian base on Ravnos Island. Lespia has been housing Arcasian troops and missiles by our border for years and none of the member states even talked about it."
Emmerich Hegel"I also want the member states to look into the Arcasian bases in Lespia."
Emmerich Hegel"And if the Alliance of Nations truly claims to have the people's best interests at heart, they cannot stay silent about what the Agnolian government did in Heljiland. How they took away the rights of our people..."
Emmerich Hegel"Perhaps then, there can be proper dialogue between our nations, and the Alliance of Nations can finally demonstrate that it can serve its intended purpose."
Emmerich Hegel"But unfortunately, the Alliance of Nations remains all too willing to overlook such human rights violations."
Emmerich Hegel"However, the Valgslandian government is ready as always to fight the forces of oppression and colonial servitude on behalf of free people everywhere."

NarratorPresident Nines announced the next speaker: Queen Beatrice Livingston.
Beatrice Livingston"Dear President of the Assembly, dear delegates, dear leaders of the world."
Beatrice Livingston"Just as my fellow Eastern Merkopan delegates spoke about the troubles plaguing our continent..."
Beatrice Livingston"...I also have to bring attention to the plight of the people of the north."
Beatrice Livingston"The Alliance of Nations, the ATO, the CSP and even the OMEC have never supported our people when they've been through tragic atrocities."
Beatrice Livingston"Unlike many of the western countries here, we never received financial aid when natural disasters struck our lands."
Beatrice Livingston"The pain of our people was never recognized. Not even when they faced the threat of genocide by the hands of the Sordish kings..."
Beatrice Livingston"In light of all that, I implore this Alliance to take a closer look at the actions of our southern neighbors."
Beatrice Livingston"They took in traitors and gave them protection."
Beatrice Livingston"Their president himself came to power amid murder and unrest. Yet people from his administration had the guts to blame our government for inciting violence."
Beatrice Livingston"And they expect the world to believe their lies."

Beatrice Livingston"I hereby invite all of you to determine the initiation of a proper intergovernmental investigation into Sordland."
Beatrice Livingston"If the Alliance continues to shirk its given duties, you can trust in Rumburg to take matters into her own hands."
Beatrice Livingston"It is also important to point out that Rumburg will not stand idly by if more Contanan and Arcasian military are transferred to Eastern Merkopa."
Beatrice Livingston"I hereby condemn both the CSP and ATO for trying to influence our region with their military power."
Beatrice Livingston"Before I finish, I also want to say..."
Beatrice Livingston"Mr. Smolak. I'm looking forward to reading your report. I wonder what this so-called 'evidence' is all about."
Beatrice Livingston"Know that your slander does not work on us. Know that we are ready to die for our freedom. Know that our kingdom stands firm and strong."
Beatrice Livingston"Expect a grand awakening..."
Beatrice Livingston"Thank you."

  • ·"That was the worst speech I've ever heard."

NarratorDeivid immediately started applauding.
Deivid Wisci"Good luck, Mr. President."

NarratorPresident Nines announced the next speaker: Dwight Walker.
Dwight Walker"Mr. President, I first want to congratulate you on your election to this high office."
Dwight Walker"Mr. Secretary, delegates to the Alliance of Nations, ladies and gentlemen, fellow friends..."
Dwight Walker"We meet again on our quest to bring peace and light into this world. But even though some of the clouds have lifted, the world has not yet escaped the darkness."
Dwight Walker"The Contanan Security Pact is rapidly expanding its influence."
Dwight Walker"It is very troubling to see a democratic nation of Merkopa be invaded by those Malenyevist powers."
Dwight Walker"I refer, of course, to Heljiland's occupation by Valgsland."
Dwight Walker"This is an attack against the lasting peace the world has enjoyed since the Century of Revolutions."
Dwight Walker"Today, our peacekeeping obligations are being tested by United Contana and the Malenyevist bloc. If we fail to counter and stop this threat..."
Dwight Walker"If we stay idle and allow them to build new walls and weapons of hostility..."
Dwight Walker"If the attackers do not face any consequences..."
Dwight Walker"Then this unstable peace we believed we had built will be the world's downfall, and posterity will point its finger at us for not stopping it."
NarratorEmmerich Hegel and the other Valgslandian delegates started making noise again.
NarratorThis time, Hegel had taken off his shoe and was banging the table with it.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:54:48 p17475 / c298
  • ·"What the heck?"
  • ·"I have no words. The chancellor has gone mad."
  • ▶Keep watching.
It's best to observe and not react immediately.
DeepSeek-R1I kept watching.
NarratorDespite the noise Hegel made, President Walker continued his speech without pause.
Dwight Walker"Despite its claims, the CSP's actions will only bring about war."
Dwight Walker"We believed United Contana and Arcasia, together with their allies, could come to an understanding. For our mutual benefit and our desire to avoid the destruction of the world."
Dwight Walker"Yet United Contana keeps acting in complete disregard to international law. Their allies are forcefully taking land and imposing their own values on the people of those lands."
Dwight Walker"I will say this to the leaders of United Contana... If either of us is to be secure, we need a peaceful cooperation, not nuclear missiles, shows of naval superiority or invasions."
Dwight Walker"If the leaders of United Contana fail to de-militarize Heljiland, with complete dismantling of the nuclear missiles and the withdrawal of the occupying Valgslandian forces..."
Dwight Walker"The ATO will have to take matters into our own hands."
NarratorHegel stood up with the shoe in his hand.
Emmerich Hegel"What is that supposed to mean? You're promoting war in the Alliance of Nations?"
Emmerich Hegel"Mr. President! This canno-"
NarratorNines slammed the gavel down.
Ephraim Nines"SILENCE!"
Dwight Walker"Mr. Chancellor, please let me finish. We did not interrupt you when you addressed the assembly."
Dwight Walker"Finally, I propose that the Alliance of Nations undertake the noble cause of helping the people of the world through rewriting and properly enforcing its Human Rights Declaration."
Dwight Walker"The Alliance of Nations cannot survive as a static organization. Its size and duties are increasing enormously. It should adapt to the changing world."
Dwight Walker"But peace and rights do not rest in the hands of this organization alone. They rely on the hearts of our people and the spirit of cooperation between our nations."
Dwight Walker"So, let it be known, my fellow inhabitants of this world... Let us take our stand and see if we can finally achieve a lasting peace."
Dwight Walker"Thank you."
NarratorMost of the assembly applauded his speech, the ATO delegates in particular.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:55:41 p17402 / c483

  • ·Applaud his speech.
  • ·Don't react.
The speech promoted actions that could lead to increased tensions and conflict.
DeepSeek-R1"That speech had very dangerous connotations to it."
Deivid Wisci"Yes... I hope we won't be seeing a direct war between the superpowers any time soon..."
Deivid Wisci"That would be the destruction of the planet."
NarratorThe applause continued for some time. Walker shook hands with various delegates on his way back to his seat.
NarratorPresident Nines spoke into the microphone once more.
Ephraim Nines"We have one final speaker before the break. Leon Malenyev of United Contana."
NarratorMalenyev slowly walked to the rostrum in his iconic suit. All of the CSP delegates stood up and applauded him.
Leon Malenyev"Honorable chairman and fellow delegates to the Alliance of Nations."
Leon Malenyev"Here we are again at the most esteemed meeting of state representatives in the world."
Leon Malenyev"However, increasingly, we are being threatened, disregarded, attacked or ignored in this assembly."
Leon Malenyev"The representative of Arcasia has just shown this unfriendly attitude towards us yet again."
Leon Malenyev"Arcasia would have you believe we are standing in the way of lasting peace. Yet, the peace they talk about was only possible thanks to our missiles and our growing base in the developing world."
Leon Malenyev"The ATO kept waging wars against people's revolutions and forcing new independent states to be their puppets. Binding them to themselves through the Arcasian Lira."
Leon Malenyev"And now yet another Arcasian president is telling us that we are the aggressors and they are the keepers of peace."
Leon Malenyev"We will not be silent anymore against these lies! When was this peace you talk about? Where was this cooperation between our countries? Have you ever accepted our invitation to talk about de-nuclearization?"
Leon Malenyev"If you don't like us, don't accept our deals, don't listen to our concerns, and keep pointing fingers at us, why did you invite us to the Alliance of Nations?"
Leon Malenyev"Every year, United Contana comes to this assembly to address the real problems of *real* people. People who suffer because of your systems, your wars and your ignorance."
Leon Malenyev"Whether you like it or not, we'll keep representing the laborers, the slaves, the oppressed of this world. And believe it or disregard it, history is on our side."
Leon Malenyev"We say to the representatives of capitalist countries... Come and visit us. Come and see the revolution working in Contana."
Leon Malenyev"Show our continent to your people without distorting the reality. If you cannot do that, then you need not come."
Leon Malenyev"We believe socialism will triumph over capitalism in the end. Such is the logical conclusion of the development of humankind."
NarratorThis time, the Lespian delegates started laughing very loudly. Some other delegates booed.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:56:27 p17540 / c346
  • ·"Ridiculous..."
  • ·"Malenyev's living in his dreams."
  • ▶Keep watching.
Choosing not to respond directly allows for observation and avoids escalating tensions.
DeepSeek-R1I kept watching.
Leon Malenyev"You can keep laughing, gentlemen. Communism will bury you."
NarratorSuddenly the laughing turned into shouting.
Leon Malenyev"If you really believe in cooperation, we will be waiting! We're not going anywhere."
Leon Malenyev"However, if the ATO keeps intervening in our matters... It is natural that the CSP would react."
Leon Malenyev"Despite threats from the ATO, Rumburg or any other nation or organization, we must not succumb to further division and violence."
Leon Malenyev"We must ask ourselves, what do the people of our countries expect of us? They surely don't want war and bloodshed."
Leon Malenyev"So let us leave to our children and grandchildren, good memories of our time and a better future for all. Let them look at history and say: once there were great divisions and complex problems, but the leaders of the world came together in the Alliance of Nations and succeeded in settling them in order to ensure our peaceful lives."
Leon Malenyev"Let us act in such a way to make this session not a session of threats, but a session of hopes. And the realization of them."
Leon Malenyev"The government of United Contana and the united peoples of Contana are ready to serve their duties for world peace."
Leon Malenyev"Long live the people's revolution."

■ Queen Phone CallT10

BUDGET -17WEALTH 1STABILITY -8Danger
NarratorI was in my office, waiting for the call that could potentially change the future of Eastern Merkopa.
NarratorThe phone started ringing. I took a deep breath as I held it to my ear.
NarratorI heard the voice of the Queen of Rumburg from the other side.
Beatrice Livingston"Mr. Rayne. How pleasing it is to finally get a chance to speak with you in person. "
Beatrice Livingston"I am Queen Beatrice Livingston. The Queen of the Four Corners of Merkopa, Defender of Castle Tzarsbourgh, Conqueror of the Tranquil Sea, Stout Protector of the Ancient Lands of Rum, Descendant of the Most Honorable King Edward the First and the Ruler of Rumburg. "
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:59:54 p17424 / c214
  • ▶“It is very nice to finally talk to you as well.”
  • ·“Aren't those titles a bit old-fashioned?”
  • ·“Am I supposed to be impressed?”
A polite and respectful response is essential to maintain good relations with Queen Beatrice Livingston.
DeepSeek-R1"It is very nice to finally talk to you as well."
Beatrice Livingston"Excellent."
Beatrice Livingston"Now. Given the circumstances, I never thought I would be giving you a personal phone call. Yet here we are."
Beatrice Livingston"Even after what Sordland has done, I am showing you my willingness to talk instead of cutting off communication."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:00:34 p17396 / c370
  • ·“You are so kind and generous.”
  • ·“I did not hang up yet. That should show you *my* willingness to listen to your whining.”
  • ▶“Why did you call?”
It's straightforward and invites clarification.
DeepSeek-R1"Why did you call?"
Beatrice Livingston"Please listen to me carefully. This is your last chance."
Beatrice Livingston"I know it all, Mr. Rayne. I know all about your plans. I can read your every move."
Beatrice Livingston"I am offering you a peaceful way out of your dire situation."
Beatrice Livingston"My demands are simple. You will give us control of our stolen ancestral homelands, Estord and Narbel."
Beatrice Livingston"Those cities are surely of lesser importance to you judging by the way your country has treated them. That's all I ask."
Beatrice Livingston"If you fail to comply, to put it simply, we will take them by force."
Beatrice Livingston"A war, Mr. Rayne."
Beatrice Livingston"So, please. I beg you to have reason. Think of your citizens. Think of your people."
Beatrice Livingston"Do not enter a war that you have no chance of winning."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:01:34 p17656 / c648
  • ·“You must be out of your mind if you thought I would give in to your threats.”
  • ·“You dare insult me? Your time is over, Your Highness. Along with your country. I will see you on the battlefield.”
  • ▶“I will not lead my country to a war. Peace must be maintained by any means necessary.”
  • ·“Only Estord and Narbel? Nothing else?”
Choosing to respond with a commitment to peace avoids unnecessary conflict and aligns with previous diplomatic strategies.
DeepSeek-R1"I will not lead my country to a war. Peace must be maintained by any means necessary."
Beatrice Livingston"I am glad that you see it our way."
Beatrice Livingston"Do you comply with my demands?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:02:19 p17541 / c420
  • ▶“You must be joking. I have a duty to protect this nation. I will not sell pieces of it.”
  • ·“What are two backwater cities in the name of peace? I accept.”

DeepSeek-R1"You must be joking. I have a duty to protect this nation. I will not sell pieces of it."
Beatrice Livingston"A pity. Really."
Beatrice Livingston"At least my conscience is clear."
Beatrice Livingston"I have given you a chance and you refused. You have failed your people."
Beatrice Livingston"War it is then, Mr. Rayne."
Beatrice Livingston"You will regret your decision until your last breath."
NarratorThe line dropped dead.
NarratorAnd just like that, the Rumburg War had begun.

Deivid Wisci"What a privilege for me."
Deivid Wisci"I had the chance to watch my student grow into a fine and strong leader in my lifetime. What a privilege indeed."
Deivid Wisci"You have brought reform to Sordland. You, my student, actually did it."
Deivid Wisci"You don't know how proud I was to be alongside you."

Deivid Wisci"I swore an oath to myself years ago."
Deivid Wisci"I would quit if I ever felt like I didn't have the physical capability to serve anymore. At that point, I would become a hindrance."
Deivid Wisci"That time has come, Mr. President."
Deivid Wisci"My bones are aching, my hands are shaking, my voice is failing."
Deivid Wisci"Besides, I am sure you can find a younger minister who is a little more full of life."

■ Personal Final SupperT10

BUDGET -17WEALTH 1STABILITY -8Danger
NarratorWith the election looming and war on the horizon, I'd practically been sleeping in my office lately. But tonight, I made a deliberate effort to get home early.
NarratorFranc was coming to dinner for the first time in months.
NarratorI'd told everyone I wanted a family supper, just like in the old days. Monica had promised to cook something special.
NarratorThe usual crowd of protesters was waiting for me outside the gates of the complex.
NarratorThe guards formed a cordon to let me through.

NarratorDeana smiled and pointed to the kitchen.
Deana"Mama's in there. I've gotta do some homework before dinner."

Monica"Sorry, Anton. I guess I've been on edge lately."
Monica"Why don't you go fix yourself a drink in the living room? I'll let you know when dinner's ready."
NarratorI sat down in the living room, poured myself a bit of whiskey and flipped through one of Monica's women's magazines.
NarratorIt took me a second to realize Monica herself was on the cover. The headline: "Sordland's Woman of the Year."
NarratorSuddenly, Franc burst through the door.
Franc"Sorry I'm late. I ran into a couple of my friends protesting outside. It was pretty awkward, telling them I was having dinner with you."

■ Rumburg War PlansT10

BUDGET -17WEALTH 1STABILITY -8Danger
NarratorI arrived at the Ministry of Defense and entered the War Room to discuss the war plans with the general staff.
NarratorI had never seen this room so packed with people. Every single top-ranking general and admiral in Sordland was in attendance.
NarratorIn addition to the military officers, their assistants were spread out around the room. They stopped their chatter immediately and saluted me.
NarratorIosef was standing over the table, arranging tokens atop an oversized map of Sordland and Rumburg. He walked up to me and saluted.
Iosef Lancea"Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:22:50 p16036 / c316
  • ·"At ease, gentlemen."
  • ▶"Cut the chatter and let's start."
  • ·Salute.
Anton Rayne prefers direct and efficient communication in critical situations.
DeepSeek-R1"Cut the chatter and let's start."
NarratorThe generals sat down and I took my seat at the head of the table.
NarratorI took a look around. General Valken nodded at me.
NarratorIosef stood up and picked up an extendable pointer from a shelf on the wall.
NarratorThe chattering among the staff began again. Iosef extended the pointer and loudly hit the map on the table right in its center.
Iosef Lancea"Silence!"
NarratorThe room immediately went quiet. Everyone had their attention on Iosef. He turned to me.
Iosef Lancea"Mr. President. May I begin?"

A gesture encourages immediate action and efficiency in a critical military situation.

Iosef Lancea"Everyone in this room has read the reports that the Queen of Rumburg is getting ready for a nationwide speech in two weeks."
Iosef Lancea"We have credible intel that she will be announcing a declaration of war against Sordland."
Iosef Lancea"The Rumburgian troop movements in the last 24 hours indicate that they are bolstering their frontline strength."
Valken Kruger"The time has come."
Iosef Lancea"Mr. President, at this point we are sure that they are preparing for a large-scale invasion."

Iosef Lancea"Rumburg doesn't have any idea what or who they are dealing with."
Iosef Lancea"We will destroy their armies and send them back to whatever hellhole they came from."

Iosef Lancea"Rest assured, Mr. President. I am more than confident that we will win."

Assessing our military strength against Rumburg's is crucial for evaluating our readiness and strategic planning.
DeepSeek-R1"How does our military strength compare to theirs?"
Iosef Lancea"The estimates put their active military personnel at around 900,000 to one million. Meanwhile, we have approximately 200,000 soldiers on the ground."
Iosef Lancea"As for their airforce, our estimates put the number of aircraft in their fleet at around 700."
Iosef Lancea"We have approximately 410 planes, of which 140 are state-of-the-art jets."
Iosef Lancea"Our fleet currently totals up to 60 ships and approximately 60,000 sailors."
Iosef Lancea"Fortunately, the main theatre of this war will be on land, so their navy will have a limited impact."
Iosef Lancea"I predict that they will try to block our seaports, reducing our trade and disrupting our supplies."
Iosef Lancea"Meanwhile, Rumburg has around 110 ships -- and according to our latest intelligence reports, high-tech submarines as well."
Iosef Lancea"Regrettably, we don't have an estimate on how many submarines they have. "
Iosef Lancea"The Queen has ensured her royal submarines are under state secrecy. Thus, our intelligence officers are having a hard time spotting them."
Iosef Lancea"Overall, their numbers may seem superior, but I would like to remind everyone that wars are not won with numbers alone."
NarratorIosef glanced over at the generals and stopped at Valken. He shook his head.
"I understand the situation."
DeepSeek-R1"I understand the situation."
Iosef Lancea"Good. Now we have to talk about our defensive strategy."
Iosef Lancea"Let's begin with our land forces."
Iosef Lancea"We currently have the 1st Army deployed at Estord to reinforce the border. This is the place they will most likely attack from."
Iosef Lancea"The 2nd army is standing by at Laren for a possible assault from the north."
Iosef Lancea"The 6th army is currently garrisoned at Narbel, awaiting orders."
Valken Kruger"Due to the potential of an invasion, all three armies have been undergoing rigorous drills, especially during the past few months."
Iosef Lancea"They are prepared to fight and die for their country, Mr. President."

Iosef Lancea"We are all alone in this war."

Iosef Lancea"That's the time for us to immediately launch a counter-offensive toward Tzarsbourgh and take the city."
Iosef Lancea"Our modernized army is trained and equipped for such a task."
Iosef Lancea"The gendarmerie will be of excellent help during our strike at Estord."
Iosef Lancea"They know every nook and cranny of the city."
Iosef Lancea"I am confident that we will achieve complete victory with my strategy."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:32:12 p15770 / c343
  • ▶"How is digging in and waiting for them to attack a good option?"
  • ·"Why can't we just launch an assault on Tzarsbourgh?"
  • ·"I'm ready to choose our first move."
Asking about the defensive strategy's merits provides clarity on its potential benefits before deciding.
DeepSeek-R1"How is digging in and waiting for them to attack a good option?"
Valken Kruger"When they launch their initial offensive, our soldiers and tanks will be entrenched in strategic and well-fortified positions."
Valken Kruger"Let them throw their manpower at our impenetrable line of defense."
Valken Kruger"We will grind down their armies that they are so proud of."
Valken Kruger"With their pride shattered, their morale low, and their line disorganized, we will launch an all-out assault to strike the finishing blow."
Iosef Lancea"Which makes no sense."
Iosef Lancea"You're handing them the time and motivation to come up with a plan to attack us."
Valken Kruger"They outnumbered us, Iosef. With this move we will destroy or, at worst, limit the effectiveness of their advantage."
Iosef Lancea"We don't even have the manpower to cover the entire border."
Iosef Lancea"We don't even have enough reserves to fill potential weaknesses."
Valken Kruger"All thanks to you. You were the one who pushed President Rayne to remove mandatory conscription."
Valken Kruger"The gendarmerie can be deployed on the mainline as well to support the war effort."
Iosef Lancea"They are a police force, Valken. Their skills will certainly be of use during the war, but they shouldn't join the front line as common soldiers."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:32:43 p15744 / c264

  • ▶"I'm ready to choose our first move."
It's crucial to carefully evaluate all strategic options before committing to any move.
DeepSeek-R1"I'm ready to choose our first move."
Tarquin Soll"I would suggest that we refrain from attacking Tzarsbourgh directly."
Tarquin Soll"General Kruger, your confidence and bold strategy are commendable, but a blunt, by-the-book approach like this will only work if our forces are equal in power."
Valken Kruger"I apologize for my incompetence, sir."
Tarquin Soll"Looking at the plans that have been proposed..."
Tarquin Soll"I don't see a path to victory by ourselves."
Iosef Lancea"What is your decision on the first phase, Mr. President?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:33:25 p15740 / c564

  • ▶"We need to fight smarter if we are weaker. Let's go with Iosef's pincer plan."
  • ·"We will show them what we're made of. Let us march toward Tzarsbourgh."
The pincer strategy allows Sordland to exploit Rumburg's overconfidence and numerical advantage through strategic maneuvering, offering a better chance of success despite limited resources.
DeepSeek-R1"We need to fight smarter if we are weaker. Let's go with Iosef's pincer plan."
Iosef Lancea"Excellent!"
Valken Kruger"So we're doomed, then."
Iosef Lancea"The decision is already made, Valken."
NarratorIosef and I signed the plan documents for the first phase of the war and handed them off to the nearest soldier.
Iosef Lancea"So. Let's assume that at this point, we have Tzarsbourgh in our hands."
Iosef Lancea"This is just the first step. From this point on, it will be harder since we're going deep into hostile territory."
Iosef Lancea"I see two options for us to move forward."
Iosef Lancea"We can continue pushing toward Thornbourgh and capture the queen."
Iosef Lancea"Or we turn our attention towards Dome."
Iosef Lancea"We risk being flanked from the east with either strategy but it is a risk we must take."
Iosef Lancea"Any questions, Mr. President?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:33:58 p15836 / c304
  • ·"How would we take Thornbourgh?"
  • ·"Why can't we just defend Tzarsbourgh from this point on?"
  • ▶"Elaborate on the strategy around Dome."
To understand the broader defensive strategy beyond capturing just Tzarsbourgh.
DeepSeek-R1"Elaborate on the strategy around Dome."
Iosef Lancea"Dome and Tzarsbourgh are excellently linked logistically. There is a railway between the two cities."
Valken Kruger"This will aid us in transporting military supplies and troops."
Valken Kruger"We can easily launch an assault on Dome within half a week after taking Tzarsbourgh."
Iosef Lancea"With that said, control of the rail system is key because Rumburg can utilize it as well."
Iosef Lancea"If they can withstand our assault, they can launch a counter-offensive with ease from the direction of Dome to retake Tzarsbourgh."
Iosef Lancea"We would lose everything we had gained so far."
Valken Kruger"However, with the capture of Dome, there will be no need to take Thornbourgh."
Valken Kruger"It would be too late to attack anyway."
Valken Kruger"Rumburg would either reinforce their southern defenses or the Queen would come to her senses and flee the capital."
Iosef Lancea"Even if we manage to capture Thornbourgh without their Queen, we won't be able to hold it during the winter. Our supply lines will be too stretched out for that."
Valken Kruger"However, with two of their largest cities in our hand, we can lay out a defensive line."
Valken Kruger"Especially around Dome. They took it from Agnolia because of the oil fields surrounding it. Their industrial sector has since heavily relied on them."
Valken Kruger"With their oil supply cut, they won't have the fuel for their huge war machine and their economy will slowly collapse. We will be effectively choking them until they capitulate."
Iosef Lancea"But we have to consider the defenses in and around the city of Dome. Ever since they took it from Agnolia, they have been building up fortifications in case of an Agnolian attempt at retaking the city."
Iosef Lancea"Furthermore, due to the length of the operation to take Tzarsbourgh, they would come to the realization that Dome is our next target."
Iosef Lancea"There is no guarantee that we can take a well-reinforced and fortified city while holding the line at the western and the northern fronts."
Valken Kruger"The gendarmerie can stay behind and protect our flank from Tzarsbourgh."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:34:35 p15982 / c337

  • ·"Why can't we just defend Tzarsbourgh from this point on?"

Seeking the Colonel's seasoned opinion would provide valuable insight given his military experience.
DeepSeek-R1"Your opinion, Colonel?"
Tarquin Soll"Without sufficient production capacity or supplies to be procured through trade, our chances at victory seem slim."
Iosef Lancea"Your decision, Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:35:13 p15970 / c394

Attacking Dome to establish a defensive line would better leverage Sordland's limited resources and strategically weaken Rumburg's economy, making it a more sustainable move.
DeepSeek-R1"We will attack Dome and lay out a defensive line. Let them come and get us."
Iosef Lancea"You are wasting an opportunity to end this war once and for all, Mr. President."
Valken Kruger"It is decided, Iosef."
Iosef Lancea"Very well. I will add it to our battle plan."
Iosef Lancea"And that, gentlemen, is our plan against Rumburg."
Iosef Lancea"We only have one chance at victory."
Iosef Lancea"If we falter at any point during this war, it will be the end of Sordland as we know it."
Iosef Lancea"Mr. President. We will need your executive order to mobilize the forces."

Sign off on it.

Expressing gratitude for his presence is appropriate and respectful.

Tarquin Soll"Where else would I be? A good leader is always there for his country in times of need."

NarratorSoll saluted me and then headed for the door.
NarratorI stood there alone in the war room, looking at the map of Sordland laid out on the table. I picked up one of the little metal tokens on it and turned it over in my hand.
NarratorIt represented thousands of men who were willing to die for this country. For me.
NarratorI hoped I would not let them down.

■ Rumburg War SpeechT10

BUDGET -17WEALTH 1STABILITY -8Danger
NarratorI was standing at the exact same podium overlooking the Hill of Pride where my inauguration took place. However, the mood this time was far from cheerful.
NarratorI looked at the people of Sordland below. They were gazing up at me with confusion and concern.
NarratorMy name was announced. I stepped forward as the cameras flashed. The crowd started clapping.

  • ·“Dear citizens.”
  • ·“My brothers and sisters.”
The phrase 'Good people of Sordland' is a respectful and inclusive way to address the nation during a time of crisis, suitable for a leader seeking to inspire unity and support.
DeepSeek-R1"Good people of Sordland."
NarratorThe clapping and cheering faded to a deafening silence.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:38:02 p16016 / c363
  • ▶“As you have heard, yesterday, Rumburg declared war on Sordland.”
  • ·“The Kingdom of Rumburg has issued a challenge that we have no choice but to respond to."
  • ·“After a series of dastardly attacks on Sordland's sovereignty by Rumburg, we now find ourselves compelled into battle."
A straightforward declaration of the situation maintains neutrality and factual accuracy.
DeepSeek-R1"As you have heard, yesterday, Rumburg declared war on Sordland."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:38:34 p15989 / c278
  • ·“If their damn Queen thinks that they can get away with killing our soldiers, then I will tell them this. Think again!”
  • ▶“Unfortunately, the times of peace are coming to an end due to our neighbor's aggressive actions.”
  • ·“It is my duty as your President to inform you that in the following days, our country will be targeted with more heinous attacks by our enemy.”
Option 1 provides a calm and measured response, acknowledging the end of peace while emphasizing duty.
DeepSeek-R1"Unfortunately, the times of peace are coming to an end due to our neighbor's aggressive actions."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:39:05 p15967 / c220

The choice emphasizes national unity and citizen commitment, inspiring patriotism.

CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:39:36 p15959 / c296

  • ▶“We have no choice but to take the next steps against this unwarranted war they are instigating.”
Choosing option 2 emphasizes the necessity of defending Sordland while maintaining a cautious and pragmatic approach.

CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:40:16 p15969 / c483

TURN 11

■ Turn Endings Sn O Rumburg War LostT11

BUDGET -17WEALTH 1STABILITY -8Danger
NarratorI undid my tie slowly and rose from my seat in the protected bunker on the outskirts of Holsord. I increased the volume of the crackling radio.
Narrator"The enemy is advancing on us. Requesting backup. We are surrounded from all sides. I repeat, we are surro--"
Narrator"They are using some kind of a gas. Our soldiers' eyes and ears are bleeding. We need immediate assistance on Victory Avenue. Medics! We need more medics!"
Narrator"They are approaching the Maroon Palace. We must protect the president at all costs. Squad 6 will initiate the final charge while we barricade the palace. Please tell my wife I love her. Greci Sordland!"
Narrator"They are coming in through the outer gates. They killed everyone. We surrendered our weapons but they won't stop shooting. If there is a God, I beg you. Please end this."
Narrator"If there is anyone listening right now... please. The world needs to know what is going on. This is no longer a war. This is a massacre! Squad 2 out."
NarratorI turned the radio off and swallowed hard. Nobody knew I wasn't at the Palace. Except for Iosef.
NarratorMiraculously, he'd managed to get my family here as well.
NarratorRight now, he was monitoring communications in the adjoining room. I walked in and he saluted.
Iosef Lancea"The enemy is preparing to breach the Palace. It's only a matter of time before they realize you're not there."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:44:02 p15751 / c706
  • ·“How long until they find us?"
  • ·“Are we safe here, Iosef?”
  • ▶“What do you make of the situation?”
Understanding the broader situation will provide crucial insights for planning an effective response and strategy.
DeepSeek-R1"What do you make of the situation?"
Iosef Lancea"Don't worry, sir. Unless they somehow got their hands on the Palace schematics, they don't know about the secret tunnel that leads here from the Situation Room."
Iosef Lancea"And besides, this room was built to withstand nuclear attacks. We should be safe here for the moment. The diversion at the Palace should buy us some time."
Iosef Lancea"Of course, the question is how much time. We have lost contact with the squadrons protecting the outer perimeter of the palace."
Iosef Lancea"Your guess is as good as my own at this point."
NarratorAfter thanking Iosef, I went back to the main room.
NarratorI looked at Monica, Deana and Franc. Deana started running to me, crying. I wiped her tears away.
Deana"Papa. Are we going to die?"

Anton Rayne would provide direct reassurance to his daughter in a time of crisis.

Deana"All right. I trust you, Papa."
Monica"Anton, can you come here for a second?"
NarratorWe went to a corner where Deana wouldn't be able to hear us.
Monica"What is really going on? Tell me the truth."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:45:22 p15774 / c566

  • ·“We will make through this somehow. I promise.”
Anton is faced with a grim reality and chooses honesty over false reassurance.

NarratorFranc walked up to me.
Franc"Dad. Seriously, tell me. Are we screwed?"

Acknowledging Franc's fear with honesty builds trust.

Franc"Not very reassuring."

Iosef Lancea"Sir. The Palace is about to fall. And one of the Rumburg combatants was overheard saying he'd located the tunnel entrance."

Immediate evacuation is necessary to avoid capture by Rumburg forces.

Iosef Lancea"Look around. Get comfortable."
Iosef Lancea"Because this room will be our grave."

Iosef Lancea"Mr. President, I just wanted to say..."

Iosef Lancea"I'm sorry. I'm sorry for failing our country."
Iosef Lancea"Feels like just yesterday that we were raising Sordland's banner over Tzarsbourgh..."

Iosef Lancea"Quiet. They are here."

Monica"Anton! We can't let them get in!"
Franc"Dad!"

■ EpilogueT11

NarratorYou pointed the gun at the soldier. A young man, no more than Franc's age, unfairly asked to die for his country. Two more men burst in and started yelling at you to lower your weapon in broken Sordish. You...
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:51:40 p15508 / c568
  • ·...pulled the trigger.
  • ▶...put your gun down.
Putting down the gun is a calculated move to avoid escalating the situation, potentially allowing for negotiation or preservation of life despite the hopeless scenario.
NarratorThe soldier reeled back, then re-trained his gun on you, sneering. Monica cried out your name. You prepared for the end. Another one of the men approached you and hit you with the headstock of his rifle.
You blacked out.
NarratorBy the time you came to yourself, you were in a Rumburg prison cell. Hours turned into days and days turned into weeks. Guards and soldiers tormented you from dusk until dawn, scarring your mind and body long after you'd told them everything you knew about Sordland and your presidency. You asked about your family, but none of your questions were answered.
When would it end?
NarratorOne day you were woken up with a splash of icy water. The warden told you that the Queen would pay you a visit. You prepared a makeshift knife and hid it well. She entered the cell. You...
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:52:18 p15681 / c446
  • ·...tried to stab her.
  • ▶...begged for mercy.
Begging for mercy aligns with Anton Rayne's strategic and diplomatic nature, aiming to protect his family and country through negotiation.
NarratorQueen Beatrice smiled and gestured down towards her shoes. You...
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:52:56 p15678 / c509
  • ▶...told her you'd rather die.
  • ·...knelt down and kissed her feet.
Anton Rayne chooses to maintain his dignity by refusing mercy, staying true to his principles.
